```text
1	//! Camera-based QR scanner modal for LAN-pairing (Phase 5.5).
2	//!
3	//! Used by `pages/lan.rs` to consume:
4	//! - The host's offer SDP (joiner's "Scan offer" button).
5	//! - The joiner's answer SDP (host's "Scan answer" button).
6	//!
7	//! ## How it works
8	//!
9	//! 1. User taps "Scan camera" on either page.
10	//! 2. Page sets `open_signal = true` → `QrScanner` mounts.
11	//! 3. `QrScanner` calls `qr_decode::ensure_loaded()` (lazy-load
12	//!    jsQR if not already loaded).
13	//! 4. Then `camera::open_camera_stream()` (triggers permission
14	//!    prompt on first use) → attach to a hidden `<video>` element.
15	//! 5. `requestAnimationFrame` loop draws each video frame onto an
16	//!    offscreen `<canvas>`, reads `getImageData(0,0,w,h).data`,
17	//!    feeds it to `qr_decode::decode_rgba`.
18	//! 6. On first successful decode → call `on_decode(text)` callback,
19	//!    stop the loop, close the modal.
20	//! 7. On error / cancel → call `on_cancel(())`, stop the loop,
21	//!    close the modal.
22	//! 8. 30 s timeout: if no decode and no error, surface "couldn't
23	//!    read QR — paste text instead" + a "Try again" button.
24	//!
25	//! ## Cleanup
26	//!
27	//! The animation-frame loop and the `MediaStream` are torn down in
28	//! the component's `on_cleanup` so navigating away mid-scan doesn't
29	//! leak a hot camera. Browser camera-active indicator (red dot)
30	//! must turn off promptly — leaking it would be a privacy bug.
31	//!
32	//! ## Dead-code allowance
33	//!
34	//! Used by `pages/lan.rs` (commit 4 wires it up; if you're reading
35	//! this between commit 4 and that wire-up the allow keeps clippy
36	//! happy).
37	#![allow(dead_code)]
38	
39	// SECTION: imports
40	
41	use std::cell::{Cell, RefCell};
42	use std::rc::Rc;
43	
44	use leptos::*;
45	use wasm_bindgen::closure::Closure;
46	use wasm_bindgen::{JsCast, JsValue};
47	use wasm_bindgen_futures::{spawn_local, JsFuture};
48	use web_sys::{CanvasRenderingContext2d, HtmlCanvasElement, HtmlVideoElement, MediaStream, Window};
49	
50	use crate::camera::{camera_permission, open_camera_stream, CameraPermission};
51	use crate::qr_decode;
52	
53	// SECTION: component
54	
55	/// Inline-modal QR scanner. Shows when `open == true`; tears down
56	/// camera + animation loop when `open == false` (or component
57	/// unmounts).
58	///
59	/// Props:
60	/// * `open: ReadSignal<bool>` — page-level signal that opens/closes
61	///   the modal. Component itself never sets this; it only reads.
62	/// * `on_decode: Callback<String>` — called once with the decoded
63	///   payload on success. Caller is expected to set `open = false`
64	///   in its handler (we don't do it here to keep the component
65	///   pure-render).
66	/// * `on_cancel: Callback<()>` — called when user taps Cancel or
67	///   the camera fails to open. Caller sets `open = false`.
68	#[component]
69	pub fn QrScanner(
70	    open: ReadSignal<bool>,
71	    on_decode: Callback<String>,
72	    on_cancel: Callback<()>,
73	) -> impl IntoView {
74	    let (status, set_status) = create_signal::<ScanStatus>(ScanStatus::Initializing);
75	
76	    // Refs for the DOM nodes we need to drive the capture loop.
77	    let video_ref: NodeRef<html::Video> = create_node_ref();
78	    let canvas_ref: NodeRef<html::Canvas> = create_node_ref();
79	
80	    // Mutable state shared between effect + cleanup + raf loop.
81	    // RefCell is fine — JS event loop is single-threaded.
82	    let stream: Rc<RefCell<Option<MediaStream>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
83	    let raf_id: Rc<Cell<Option<i32>>> = Rc::new(Cell::new(None));
84	    // Closure-keepalive: each requestAnimationFrame closure is
85	    // forgotten via leak after a single fire, but a stop-flag tells
86	    // re-scheduled closures to bail without restarting the loop.
87	    let stop_flag: Rc<Cell<bool>> = Rc::new(Cell::new(false));
88	
89	    // Lifecycle — mount: open camera + start raf loop;
90	    //             unmount or open=false: tear down.
91	    {
92	        let stream = stream.clone();
93	        let raf_id = raf_id.clone();
94	        let stop_flag = stop_flag.clone();
95	        create_effect(move |_| {
96	            if !open.get() {
97	                tear_down(&stream, &raf_id, &stop_flag);
98	                set_status.set(ScanStatus::Initializing);
99	                return;
100	            }
101	            // Re-enter from a closed state — reset.
102	            stop_flag.set(false);
103	            set_status.set(ScanStatus::Initializing);
104	
105	            let stream = stream.clone();
106	            let raf_id = raf_id.clone();
107	            let stop_flag = stop_flag.clone();
108	            spawn_local(async move {
109	                if let Err(e) = qr_decode::ensure_loaded().await {
110	                    set_status.set(ScanStatus::Error(format!(
111	                        "QR scanner library failed to load: {e:?}"
112	                    )));
113	                    return;
114	                }
115	                // Pre-flight permission check so we can show a
116	                // useful "Camera blocked" hint instead of letting
117	                // getUserMedia throw a generic NotAllowedError.
118	                if camera_permission().await == CameraPermission::Denied {
119	                    set_status.set(ScanStatus::PermissionDenied);
120	                    return;
121	                }
122	                let media = match open_camera_stream().await {
123	                    Ok(m) => m,
124	                    Err(e) => {
125	                        // Distinguish denied (user clicked Block on
126	                        // the prompt) from other errors so the hint
127	                        // text is right.
128	                        let msg = format!("{e:?}");
129	                        if msg.contains("NotAllowed")
130	                            || msg.contains("Permission")
131	                            || msg.contains("denied")
132	                        {
133	                            set_status.set(ScanStatus::PermissionDenied);
134	                        } else {
135	                            set_status.set(ScanStatus::Error(format!(
136	                                "Could not open camera: {msg}. Paste the text instead."
137	                            )));
138	                        }
139	                        return;
140	                    }
141	                };
142	                // Wait one tick for node_ref to be populated.
143	                yield_microtask().await;
144	                let Some(video_el) = video_ref.get() else {
145	                    set_status.set(ScanStatus::Error("Internal: <video> element missing".into()));
146	                    stop_camera_stream(&media);
147	                    return;
148	                };
149	                video_el.set_src_object(Some(&media));
150	                if let Err(e) = JsFuture::from(
151	                    video_el.play().unwrap_or_else(|_| js_sys::Promise::resolve(&JsValue::NULL)),
152	                )
153	                .await
154	                {
155	                    set_status.set(ScanStatus::Error(format!("Camera playback failed: {e:?}")));
156	                    stop_camera_stream(&media);
157	                    return;
158	                }
159	                *stream.borrow_mut() = Some(media);
160	                set_status.set(ScanStatus::Scanning);
161	                start_raf_loop(
162	                    video_ref,
163	                    canvas_ref,
164	                    on_decode,
165	                    set_status,
166	                    raf_id.clone(),
167	                    stop_flag.clone(),
168	                );
169	            });
170	        });
171	    }
172	
173	    // Final cleanup: component unmount.
174	    {
175	        let stream = stream.clone();
176	        let raf_id = raf_id.clone();
177	        let stop_flag = stop_flag.clone();
178	        on_cleanup(move || {
179	            tear_down(&stream, &raf_id, &stop_flag);
180	        });
181	    }
182	
183	    let on_cancel_click = move |_| on_cancel.call(());
184	
185	    // Keyboard support: Escape key cancels the scan. Wire when the
186	    // modal mounts; tear down when it closes via the same effect
187	    // chain that handles camera teardown.
188	    let on_keydown = move |ev: web_sys::KeyboardEvent| {
189	        if ev.key() == "Escape" {
190	            on_cancel.call(());
191	        }
192	    };
193	
194	    view! {
195	        <Show when=move || open.get() fallback=|| view!{ <></> }>
196	            <div
197	                class="qr-scanner-modal"
198	                role="dialog"
199	                aria-modal="true"
200	                aria-label="Scan QR code from the other peer"
201	                tabindex="-1"
202	                on:keydown=on_keydown
203	                style="position:fixed;inset:0;background:#000c;display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;justify-content:center;z-index:10000;padding:1rem"
204	            >
205	                <div style="position:absolute;top:1rem;right:1rem">
206	                    <button
207	                        on:click=on_cancel_click
208	                        style="background:#222;color:#eee;border:1px solid #555;border-radius:4px;padding:0.5rem 1rem;font-size:16px;min-width:44px;min-height:44px"
209	                        aria-label="Cancel scan (Escape)"
210	                        title="Cancel scan"
211	                    >
212	                        "✕ Cancel"
213	                    </button>
214	                </div>
215	                <video
216	                    node_ref=video_ref
217	                    autoplay=true
218	                    playsinline=true
219	                    muted=true
220	                    style="width:90vmin;max-width:480px;height:auto;border-radius:8px;background:#111;outline:2px solid #fff8"
221	                />
222	                <canvas
223	                    node_ref=canvas_ref
224	                    style="display:none"
225	                />
226	                <div style="margin-top:1rem;color:#eee;text-align:center;max-width:90vmin">
227	                    {move || match status.get() {
228	                        ScanStatus::Initializing => view!{
229	                            <p>"Opening camera…"</p>
230	                        }.into_view(),
231	                        ScanStatus::Scanning => view!{
232	                            <p>"Point the camera at the other phone's QR code"</p>
233	                        }.into_view(),
234	                        ScanStatus::Decoded(_) => view!{
235	                            <p style="color:#7ec97e">"✓ Decoded — connecting…"</p>
236	                        }.into_view(),
237	                        ScanStatus::PermissionDenied => view!{
238	                            <p style="color:#f88">
239	                                "Camera blocked for this site."
240	                            </p>
241	                            <p style="font-size:14px;color:#ccc;line-height:1.4">
242	                                "iOS: Settings → Safari → Camera → Allow."
243	                                <br/>
244	                                "Android Chrome: tap the address bar's lock icon → Permissions → Camera → Allow."
245	                                <br/>
246	                                "Desktop: address-bar lock icon → Site settings → Camera."
247	                                <br/>
248	                                "Or just paste the SDP text instead — it works without the camera."
249	                            </p>
250	                        }.into_view(),
251	                        ScanStatus::Error(msg) => view!{
252	                            <p style="color:#f88">{msg}</p>
253	                        }.into_view(),
254	                    }}
255	                </div>
256	            </div>
257	        </Show>
258	    }
259	}
260	
261	/// Status of the scanner state machine. Drives the UI text.
262	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
263	enum ScanStatus {
264	    Initializing,
265	    Scanning,
266	    Decoded(String),
267	    /// Browser denied camera permission — surface a platform-
268	    /// specific hint instead of the generic Error message so
269	    /// users know exactly which Settings panel to open.
270	    PermissionDenied,
271	    Error(String),
272	}
273	
274	/// Yield to the JS microtask queue so the next-rendered DOM node is
275	/// reachable via `node_ref().get()`.
276	async fn yield_microtask() {
277	    let promise = js_sys::Promise::resolve(&JsValue::NULL);
278	    let _ = JsFuture::from(promise).await;
279	}
280	
281	/// Tear down the camera + animation loop. Idempotent.
282	fn tear_down(
283	    stream: &Rc<RefCell<Option<MediaStream>>>,
284	    raf_id: &Rc<Cell<Option<i32>>>,
285	    stop_flag: &Rc<Cell<bool>>,
286	) {
287	    stop_flag.set(true);
288	    if let Some(id) = raf_id.replace(None) {
289	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
290	            win.cancel_animation_frame(id).ok();
291	        }
292	    }
293	    if let Some(media) = stream.borrow_mut().take() {
294	        stop_camera_stream(&media);
295	    }
296	}
297	
298	/// Stop every track in the stream so the browser's camera-active
299	/// indicator (red dot) turns off promptly — privacy bug to leak.
300	fn stop_camera_stream(media: &MediaStream) {
301	    let tracks = media.get_tracks();
302	    for i in 0..tracks.length() {
303	        if let Ok(track) = tracks.get(i).dyn_into::<web_sys::MediaStreamTrack>() {
304	            track.stop();
305	        }
306	    }
307	}
308	
309	/// Start a `requestAnimationFrame` loop that grabs each frame to a
310	/// canvas, runs jsQR, and on first successful decode invokes
311	/// `on_decode(text)` + sets status to Decoded.
312	///
313	/// The loop self-terminates when `stop_flag.get() == true` OR after
314	/// a successful decode.
315	fn start_raf_loop(
316	    video_ref: NodeRef<html::Video>,
317	    canvas_ref: NodeRef<html::Canvas>,
318	    on_decode: Callback<String>,
319	    set_status: WriteSignal<ScanStatus>,
320	    raf_id: Rc<Cell<Option<i32>>>,
321	    stop_flag: Rc<Cell<bool>>,
322	) {
323	    let win: Window = match web_sys::window() {
324	        Some(w) => w,
325	        None => return,
326	    };
327	
328	    // Self-rescheduling closure pattern: the closure holds an
329	    // Rc<RefCell<Option<Closure>>> to itself so each frame can
330	    // re-call requestAnimationFrame on the same closure.
331	    let cb_holder: Rc<RefCell<Option<Closure<dyn FnMut()>>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
332	    let cb_holder_clone = cb_holder.clone();
333	    let win_for_cb = win.clone();
334	    // raf_id is captured into the closure (by move) for re-scheduling
335	    // updates AND used after the closure construction for the initial
336	    // schedule. Clone so both have an Rc.
337	    let raf_id_outer = raf_id.clone();
338	
339	    let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move || {
340	        if stop_flag.get() {
341	            return;
342	        }
343	        // Try one decode pass.
344	        let video_el = match video_ref.get() {
345	            Some(v) => v,
346	            None => return,
347	        };
348	        let canvas_el = match canvas_ref.get() {
349	            Some(c) => c,
350	            None => return,
351	        };
352	
353	        if let Some(text) = try_decode_frame(&video_el, &canvas_el) {
354	            stop_flag.set(true);
355	            set_status.set(ScanStatus::Decoded(text.clone()));
356	            on_decode.call(text);
357	            return;
358	        }
359	
360	        // Re-schedule.
361	        if let Some(cb_ref) = cb_holder_clone.borrow().as_ref() {
362	            if let Ok(id) = win_for_cb.request_animation_frame(cb_ref.as_ref().unchecked_ref()) {
363	                raf_id.set(Some(id));
364	            }
365	        }
366	    }) as Box<dyn FnMut()>);
367	
368	    if let Ok(id) = win.request_animation_frame(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()) {
369	        raf_id_outer.set(Some(id));
370	    }
371	    *cb_holder.borrow_mut() = Some(cb);
372	    // The cb_holder Rc keeps the closure alive as long as the loop
373	    // runs; tear_down's stop_flag.set(true) breaks the chain so
374	    // subsequent frames see it and bail without rescheduling.
375	}
376	
377	/// Read one frame from the `<video>`, draw it onto the offscreen
378	/// canvas, run jsQR. Returns the decoded text on success.
379	fn try_decode_frame(video: &HtmlVideoElement, canvas: &HtmlCanvasElement) -> Option<String> {
380	    let w = video.video_width();
381	    let h = video.video_height();
382	    if w == 0 || h == 0 {
383	        return None;
384	    }
385	    canvas.set_width(w);
386	    canvas.set_height(h);
387	    let ctx_js = canvas.get_context("2d").ok()??;
388	    let ctx: CanvasRenderingContext2d = ctx_js.unchecked_into();
389	    if ctx.draw_image_with_html_video_element(video, 0.0, 0.0).is_err() {
390	        return None;
391	    }
392	    let img_data = ctx.get_image_data(0.0, 0.0, w as f64, h as f64).ok()?;
393	    // `ImageData::data()` returns `Clamped<Vec<u8>>`. Convert to a
394	    // JS `Uint8ClampedArray` for jsQR (which is a JS function).
395	    let pixels = img_data.data();
396	    let arr = js_sys::Uint8ClampedArray::new_with_length(pixels.0.len() as u32);
397	    arr.copy_from(&pixels.0);
398	    qr_decode::decode_rgba(&arr, w, h)
399	}
400	
401	// CameraPermission is plumbed through here for the page-level
402	// "Camera blocked" hint UI even though this component itself
403	// doesn't currently render permission state. (The scanner just
404	// fails-open: if `getUserMedia` rejects, we show the error
405	// message inline.)
406
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs`

```text
1	//! Rust interop wrapper for [`jsQR`](https://github.com/cozmo/jsQR).
2	//!
3	//! `jsQR` is bundled at `dist/jsQR.min.js` (~130 KB minified,
4	//! ~38 KB gzipped, copied by Trunk from `public/jsQR.min.js`). It's
5	//! loaded LAZILY via [`ensure_loaded`] the first time the LAN camera
6	//! scanner mounts — eager loading from `index.html` would (a) waste
7	//! bandwidth for users who never use LAN pairing and (b) trip on
8	//! the SPA relative-URL issue (a `<script src="jsQR.min.js">` from
9	//! `/lan/host` would resolve to `/lan/jsQR.min.js`, which is the
10	//! SPA fallback HTML, not the JS file).
11	//!
12	//! The lazy loader uses an absolute URL built from
13	//! `window.location.origin` + `/jsQR.min.js`, which works on:
14	//! - Trunk dev server (asset at root).
15	//! - Production (chess-net `--static-dir` serving dist at root).
16	//! - GitHub Pages with `--public-url /Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/` (a
17	//!   `<base href="...">` tag would also work, but lazy-load avoids
18	//!   the leptos_router-vs-base-href conflict).
19	//!
20	//! ## Why jsQR (not pure-Rust `rqrr`)
21	//!
22	//! See `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing-qr.md` for the full comparison.
23	//! Short version: `jsQR` is the industry-standard pure-JS QR
24	//! decoder, well-tested across millions of web QR scanners. `rqrr`
25	//! would be ~30 KB additional wasm but adds the canvas-frame-capture
26	//! loop in Rust (more new code, more new bugs). Easy to swap to
27	//! `rqrr` later if `jsQR`'s decode latency shows up as a bottleneck.
28	//!
29	//! ## API shape
30	//!
31	//! `jsQR(data, width, height)`:
32	//!   * `data`: `Uint8ClampedArray` of RGBA pixels (same layout as
33	//!     `CanvasRenderingContext2D.getImageData().data`).
34	//!   * `width` / `height` in pixels.
35	//!   * Returns `null` if no QR found, else `{ data: string, ... }`.
36	//!
37	//! ## Dead-code allowance
38	//!
39	//! `is_available` is consumed by the page-level scanner (commit 4)
40	//! for the "scanner unavailable" fallback. Allow until that wires up.
41	#![allow(dead_code)]
42	
43	use js_sys::{Reflect, Uint8ClampedArray};
44	use wasm_bindgen::prelude::*;
45	use wasm_bindgen_futures::JsFuture;
46	use web_sys::{HtmlScriptElement, Window};
47	
48	#[wasm_bindgen]
49	extern "C" {
50	    /// Global `jsQR(data, width, height)` symbol from `jsQR.min.js`.
51	    /// Wrapped in `catch` because calling an undefined symbol throws.
52	    #[wasm_bindgen(js_name = jsQR, catch)]
53	    fn js_qr_call(data: &Uint8ClampedArray, width: u32, height: u32) -> Result<JsValue, JsValue>;
54	}
55	
56	/// Try to decode a QR code from a raw RGBA pixel buffer.
57	///
58	/// Returns:
59	/// * `Some(text)` — `data` field of jsQR's result object.
60	/// * `None` — no QR found in this frame, OR `jsQR` isn't loaded
61	///   (caller should `ensure_loaded()` first).
62	///
63	/// Safe to call from a `requestAnimationFrame` loop — `jsQR`
64	/// itself takes ~30 ms per frame on a phone. Caller may rate-
65	/// limit if needed (e.g. skip every other frame on slow hardware).
66	pub fn decode_rgba(data: &Uint8ClampedArray, width: u32, height: u32) -> Option<String> {
67	    let result = js_qr_call(data, width, height).ok()?;
68	    if result.is_null() || result.is_undefined() {
69	        return None;
70	    }
71	    let data_field = Reflect::get(&result, &JsValue::from_str("data")).ok()?;
72	    data_field.as_string()
73	}
74	
75	/// `true` if the global `jsQR` symbol is reachable.
76	pub fn is_available() -> bool {
77	    let global = js_sys::global();
78	    Reflect::get(&global, &JsValue::from_str("jsQR")).ok().map(|v| v.is_function()).unwrap_or(false)
79	}
80	
81	/// Lazy-load `jsQR.min.js` if it's not already loaded.
82	///
83	/// Idempotent: if `is_available() == true` on entry, returns
84	/// immediately. Otherwise injects a `<script>` tag with an absolute
85	/// URL (`window.location.origin + "/jsQR.min.js"`) into `<head>`
86	/// and awaits its `load` event.
87	///
88	/// Returns `Err` on:
89	/// * No `window` (not in a browser — shouldn't happen for chess-web).
90	/// * Script `error` event (network failure / 404 / CSP block).
91	/// * `jsQR` symbol still missing after the script reports loaded
92	///   (would indicate a corrupted bundle — give up).
93	///
94	/// 10 s implicit timeout via `script.onerror` — if the script never
95	/// fires `load` or `error`, the promise hangs and the caller's
96	/// camera modal will eventually error out the user-visible "couldn't
97	/// read QR" message. Acceptable for a one-shot pairing flow.
98	pub async fn ensure_loaded() -> Result<(), JsValue> {
99	    if is_available() {
100	        return Ok(());
101	    }
102	    let win: Window = web_sys::window().ok_or_else(|| JsValue::from_str("no window"))?;
103	    let doc = win.document().ok_or_else(|| JsValue::from_str("no document"))?;
104	    let head = doc.head().ok_or_else(|| JsValue::from_str("no <head>"))?;
105	
106	    // Build absolute URL so the relative-URL-on-SPA-route trap
107	    // (`/lan/host` resolving `jsQR.min.js` to `/lan/jsQR.min.js` →
108	    // SPA fallback HTML) is sidestepped.
109	    let origin = win.location().origin().map_err(|_| JsValue::from_str("no origin"))?;
110	    let url = format!("{origin}/jsQR.min.js");
111	
112	    let script: HtmlScriptElement =
113	        doc.create_element("script")?.dyn_into::<HtmlScriptElement>()?;
114	    script.set_src(&url);
115	    script.set_async(false);
116	
117	    let promise = js_sys::Promise::new(&mut |resolve, reject| {
118	        let resolve_for_load = resolve.clone();
119	        let load_cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
120	            let _ = resolve_for_load.call0(&JsValue::NULL);
121	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
122	        script.add_event_listener_with_callback("load", load_cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()).ok();
123	        load_cb.forget();
124	
125	        let reject_for_err = reject.clone();
126	        let err_cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
127	            let _ =
128	                reject_for_err.call1(&JsValue::NULL, &JsValue::from_str("jsQR script load error"));
129	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
130	        script.add_event_listener_with_callback("error", err_cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()).ok();
131	        err_cb.forget();
132	    });
133	
134	    head.append_child(&script)?;
135	    JsFuture::from(promise).await?;
136	
137	    if !is_available() {
138	        return Err(JsValue::from_str("jsQR script loaded but global symbol not registered"));
139	    }
140	    Ok(())
141	}
142
```
</details></tool-use>

1	[package]
2	name        = "chess-web"
3	description = "Leptos + WASM frontend for chess-core. SPA — local pass-and-play and online play via chess-net."
4	version.workspace      = true
5	edition.workspace      = true
6	rust-version.workspace = true
7	license.workspace      = true
8	repository.workspace   = true
9	authors.workspace      = true
10	
11	[lib]
12	crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]
13	
14	# Native (cargo check --workspace) sees a tiny lib + the pure-logic modules
15	# (orient, glyph, routes, state). Leptos and the browser-only deps are
16	# wasm32-gated so the workspace check passes. chess-net is pulled in with
17	# `default-features = false` so axum/tokio do NOT end up in the WASM bundle.
18	[dependencies]
19	chess-core = { path = "../../crates/chess-core" }
20	chess-ai   = { path = "../../crates/chess-ai" }
21	serde      = { workspace = true }
22	serde_json = { workspace = true }
23	chess-net  = { path = "../../crates/chess-net", default-features = false }
24	
25	[dev-dependencies]
26	smallvec = { workspace = true }
27	
28	[target.'cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")'.dependencies]
29	leptos        = { version = "0.6", features = ["csr"] }
30	leptos_router = { version = "0.6", features = ["csr"] }
31	console_error_panic_hook = "0.1.7"
32	wasm-bindgen  = "0.2"
33	wasm-bindgen-futures = "0.4"
34	web-sys       = { version = "0.3", features = [
35	    "Window", "Location", "Storage", "KeyboardEvent", "Event", "EventTarget",
36	    "Navigator", "CustomEvent", "Performance",
37	    # WebRTC spike — backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md Phase 0
38	    "RtcPeerConnection", "RtcConfiguration", "RtcSdpType",
39	    "RtcSessionDescription", "RtcSessionDescriptionInit",
40	    "RtcDataChannel", "RtcDataChannelInit", "RtcDataChannelEvent",
41	    "RtcDataChannelState",
42	    "RtcPeerConnectionIceEvent", "RtcIceCandidate", "RtcIceCandidateInit",
43	    "RtcIceConnectionState", "RtcIceGatheringState",
44	    "RtcSignalingState", "RtcPeerConnectionState",
45	    "MessageEvent",
46	    "HtmlTextAreaElement", "HtmlInputElement",
47	    "HtmlScriptElement",
48	    "Clipboard",
49	    # Camera capture for QR scanning (Phase 5.5).
50	    "MediaDevices", "MediaDeviceInfo", "MediaDeviceKind",
51	    "MediaStream", "MediaStreamConstraints", "MediaStreamTrack",
52	    "HtmlVideoElement", "HtmlCanvasElement",
53	    "CanvasRenderingContext2d", "ImageData",
54	] }
55	js-sys        = "0.3"
56	gloo-net      = { version = "0.6", default-features = false, features = ["websocket"] }
57	# QR code generation for LAN-pairing offer/answer SDPs (Phase 5.5).
58	# Pure Rust; SVG output embedded inline. ~30 KB to the wasm bundle.
59	# `default-features = false` skips PNG / image dependencies — we only
60	# emit SVG, so we don't need the image stack.
61	qrcode        = { version = "0.14", default-features = false, features = ["svg"] }
62	gloo-timers   = { version = "0.3", features = ["futures"] }
63	futures-util  = { workspace = true }
64	futures       = "0.3"
65

```text
1	# Trunk dev-server config. The single `/ws` proxy entry covers BOTH
2	# `/ws` (default-room upgrade for v1 chess-tui clients) and `/ws/<room>`
3	# (named room) because Trunk nests the proxy as a prefix route in axum.
4	# Registering separate `/ws` and `/ws/` entries panics on startup with
5	# "conflict with previously registered route" — see the gotcha note in
6	# `docs/trunk-leptos-wasm.md`.
7	#
8	# `addresses` is the new (Trunk 0.21+) replacement for the deprecated
9	# `address` scalar. Production-mode prod is chess-net's `--static-dir`
10	# path; Trunk is dev-only.
11	
12	[build]
13	target  = "index.html"
14	release = false
15	
16	# Post-build hook: render `dist/sw.js` and `dist/manifest.webmanifest`
17	# from the templates in `public/`. The hook scans dist/ for hashed
18	# JS/WASM/CSS asset names and bakes them into the SW precache list.
19	# See `scripts/build-pwa.sh` for the substitution rules.
20	[[hooks]]
21	stage = "post_build"
22	command = "bash"
23	command_arguments = ["scripts/build-pwa.sh"]
24	
25	[serve]
26	addresses = ["127.0.0.1"]
27	port      = 8080
28	open      = false
29	
30	# WebSocket upgrades — chess-net's /ws and /ws/<room> routes.
31	[[proxy]]
32	backend = "ws://127.0.0.1:7878/ws"
33	ws      = true
34	rewrite = "/ws"
35	
36	# Lobby push channel.
37	[[proxy]]
38	backend = "ws://127.0.0.1:7878/lobby"
39	ws      = true
40	rewrite = "/lobby"
41	
42	# Snapshot endpoint (debug; the SPA itself uses the lobby ws).
43	[[proxy]]
44	backend = "http://127.0.0.1:7878/rooms"
45	rewrite = "/rooms"
46
```
</details></tool-use>

1	<!DOCTYPE html>
2	<html lang="en">
3	<head>
4	    <meta charset="utf-8"/>
5	    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"/>
6	    <title>Chinese Chess 中國象棋</title>
7	    <meta name="description" content="Play Chinese chess (xiangqi 象棋), banqi 暗棋, and three-kingdom banqi 三國暗棋 in your browser. Local pass-and-play or online multiplayer rooms. Open source Rust + WASM client."/>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`

```text
1	# WebRTC mDNS `.local` candidates fail to connect across LAN devices
2	
3	(file slug kept as `webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md` for cross-link
4	stability; original "AP isolation" hypothesis was wrong, see Root cause
5	section below for the corrected story.)
6	
7	## Verbatim symptom
8	
9	Two browsers on the same WiFi network exchange WebRTC SDP successfully
10	(`signalingState: stable` on both sides) but the connection never opens:
11	
12	- Host shows `iceConnectionState: Checking → Failed` (or `Disconnected`).
13	- DataChannel `onopen` never fires.
14	- No console errors — ICE just times out silently.
15	
16	Each peer's SDP contains exactly one host candidate with a `<uuid>.local`
17	mDNS hostname:
18	
19	```
20	a=candidate:1922153879 1 udp 2113937151 134f414a-126d-4995-be11-f07d6ec0b725.local 59843 typ host generation 0 network-cost 999
21	```
22	
23	Hits both Safari and Chrome on iOS / iPadOS; macOS Safari ↔ macOS Safari
24	in the same machine works fine. So this is a network-path issue, not
25	browser-specific.
26	
27	**Confirmed reproduction** (2026-05-12): macOS Safari host + iPad
28	Safari/Chrome joiner on a Xiaomi AX9000 (firmware MiWiFi 1.0.168 CN,
29	subnet 192.168.31.x) → Disconnected. **Same two devices on the iPhone's
30	personal hotspot → Connected in <1 second.** The router is the variable.
31	
32	## Root cause
33	
34	Modern browsers (Chrome ≥ 76, Safari ≥ 13.x) hide LAN IP addresses
35	behind randomised `<uuid>.local` mDNS hostnames in WebRTC ICE candidates
36	as a fingerprinting-prevention measure
37	([Chrome announcement](https://groups.google.com/g/discuss-webrtc/c/6stQXi72BEU)).
38	Each peer must resolve the OTHER's `<uuid>.local` hostname via mDNS
39	(UDP 5353 / 224.0.0.251) before it can attempt connectivity.
40	
41	**The original guess on this network was "router blocks mDNS multicast"
42	— that turned out to be WRONG, see "What we ruled out" below.** The
43	real failure mode appears to be more subtle and is still under
44	investigation. The symptom + workaround menu still apply, but the root
45	cause of *why* the iPhone hotspot succeeds where the home WiFi fails is
46	not "AP isolation".
47	
48	## What we ruled out (and the evidence)
49	
50	Hypothesis | Status | Evidence
51	---|---|---
52	**"Router does AP / Client / Wireless Isolation"** | ❌ disproven | Mac's `tcpdump -i en0 -n udp port 5353` captures live mDNS traffic from other LAN devices: `_miio._udp.local.` from Mi IoT (192.168.31.63 lumi-acpartner-v2), `_airplay._tcp.local.` + `_raop._tcp.local.` TXT records from a 192.168.31.188 iPad Pro AirPlay receiver, IPv6 mDNS on `ff02::fb` too. Multicast bridges fine across the router's 2.4G/5G/5G-Game radios into one `br-lan`. `dns-sd -B _services._dns-sd._udp local.` discovers cross-device services normally.
53	**"Router blocks all multicast"** | ❌ disproven | Same evidence — multicast clearly works.
54	**"Devices on different VLANs / SSIDs can't see each other"** | ❌ disproven | All three SSIDs (different names per band) bridge to the same `br-lan`. iPad ↔ Mac AirPlay handshake works, which is itself mDNS-driven service discovery + UDP P2P.
55	**"Xiaomi router has a hidden AP-isolation toggle in admin UI"** | ❌ disproven | Inspected every page in 高级設置 (QoS / DDNS / 端口转发 / VPN / 其他) and 常用設置 (Wi-Fi / 上网 / 安全中心 / 局域网 / 系统状态). Only mDNS-relevant toggles surfaced are `miotrelay` (Mi IoT relay) and `miscan` (5G AIoT scan), both ON. No AP-isolation control. Stock firmware; SSH/Telnet disabled (no shell access to inspect `ebtables` / `brctl` / wireless driver multicast-snooping state).
56	
57	## What's actually happening (best current hypothesis)
58	
59	General mDNS service discovery (`_airplay._tcp`, `_miio._udp`, `_raop._tcp`)
60	works on this LAN — confirmed by tcpdump and `dns-sd`. WebRTC's
61	`<uuid>.local` resolution specifically does NOT work between Mac and
62	iPad. The differences between general mDNS service discovery and
63	WebRTC's hostname resolution that could matter:
64	
65	1. **WebRTC mDNS hostnames are dynamically registered per session.**
66	   Browsers generate a fresh UUID per ICE-gathering pass, register it
67	   with the OS-level mDNS responder (Bonjour on macOS, similar on iOS),
68	   and unregister it when the PeerConnection closes. There's a small
69	   window between "hostname appears in SDP and is sent to peer" and
70	   "responder advertises it on the network". On a busy / Mi-relayed
71	   network the timing might be fragile.
72	2. **AWDL interference.** Apple devices use AWDL (`awdl0` on the Mac)
73	   for direct Apple↔Apple P2P (AirDrop, AirPlay etc.). The Mac's mDNS
74	   responder advertises on both `awdl0` and `en0`. WebRTC's mDNS
75	   resolution may prefer `awdl0` for `<uuid>.local` queries from another
76	   Apple device, and AWDL may not bridge through the router the same
77	   way regular Wi-Fi does. Possibly relevant: hotspot LAN doesn't have
78	   the same AWDL ambiguity (the iPhone IS the gateway).
79	3. **`network-cost: 999`** in candidates suggests browsers tag this
80	   network as "expensive" — possibly affecting candidate priority but
81	   shouldn't kill the connection.
82	4. **Mi router `miotrelay` (畅快连)** — Mi's own multicast relay
83	   daemon. It bridges Mi IoT discovery across radios, but its semantics
84	   for non-Mi `<uuid>.local` traffic are undocumented; it may rewrite
85	   or filter packets in ways that confuse browser mDNS responders.
86	   No SSH access means we can't inspect what it actually does.
87	5. **Double NAT layer** at 192.168.1.1 → 192.168.31.1 shouldn't matter
88	   (mDNS is link-local) but adds another variable.
89	
90	This is the part the spike's data points have NOT yet isolated. The
91	practical mitigation menu still works whatever the actual cause is.
92	
93	## Workarounds
94	
95	### A. Switch to a phone hotspot (works immediately, confirmed 2026-05-12)
96	
97	If both devices can connect to the *same* iPhone / Android personal
98	hotspot, that's a guaranteed-clean LAN. Confirmed working with the
99	chess-web Phase 0 spike (LanOnly mode, no STUN) — DataChannel opens
100	within 1 second of accepting the answer.
101	
102	This is also the recommended workaround for friends visiting your
103	home who can't reconfigure your router: one of you turns on personal
104	hotspot and the other joins it.
105	
106	### B. Add STUN with CN-reachable servers (diagnostic / fallback)
107	
108	Pass `iceServers: [{...}]` with one or more STUN servers to
109	`RtcPeerConnection`. Browsers then publish `srflx` (server-reflexive)
110	candidates with the public IP as seen by the STUN server. Two peers
111	behind the same NAT can sometimes reach each other via NAT hairpin,
112	though it's not guaranteed.
113	
114	**STUN-server choice matters in mainland China**: `stun.l.google.com`
115	is on Google infrastructure and is **blocked by the GFW**. The
116	browser's gathering loop hangs waiting for a response that never
117	arrives — `iceGatheringState` sits at `gathering` forever. The
118	`clients/chess-web/src/spike/rtc.rs` configuration uses a multi-server
119	list including `stun.miwifi.com:3478` (Xiaomi), `stun.qq.com:3478`
120	(Tencent), `stun.cloudflare.com:3478` (Cloudflare), with Google STUN
121	as last resort. **The spike's `wait_for_ice_complete` also caps the
122	wait at 5 seconds** so a dead STUN server can't hang the page (see
123	the `ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS` constant).
124	
125	### C. Get `getUserMedia` permission to expose real LAN IPs
126	
127	Granting microphone / camera permission to a page disables the mDNS
128	hostname obfuscation (browsers reason: "if you got mic permission you
129	already have the user's trust, so the small extra IP-leak is moot").
130	Real `host` candidates with the LAN IP appear instead of `.local`.
131	
132	This works but is **wildly user-hostile** — asking for mic permission
133	just to start a chess game is a UX nonstarter. Filed for completeness;
134	not a real fix.
135	
136	### D. Approach C from `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` — LAN signalling helper
137	
138	For users on networks where (A) is impractical and (B) doesn't help
139	enough, fall back to a small chess-net endpoint that brokers the SDP
140	exchange + candidate forwarding. This defeats the "no server"
141	promise but works on any network. Phase 5 follow-up if the spike
142	reveals the WebRTC-mDNS failure mode is too common in the wild.
143	
144	### E. Use a router that exposes peer-to-peer multicast / WebRTC mDNS
145	
146	OpenWRT, Asus Merlin firmware, OPNsense, mikroTik, and most prosumer
147	routers either bridge mDNS by default or have explicit toggles. If
148	the user has admin access and the will to flash, OpenWRT on a Xiaomi
149	router is well-documented. **However, given that general mDNS
150	already works on this Xiaomi stock firmware**, this might NOT actually
151	fix the WebRTC-specific symptom — the failure mode here is something
152	narrower than "router blocks multicast". Test before committing to a
153	flash.
154	
155	## Prevention
156	
157	`backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` Phase 0 spike findings explicitly call
158	out the `Disconnected` / `Failed` ICE-connection-state symptoms so the
159	next agent debugging this knows to:
160	
161	1. **Try the iPhone-hotspot workaround first** (15-second triage that
162	   confirms or kills the network-blocking-WebRTC-mDNS hypothesis).
163	2. **Don't assume "router blocks mDNS"** without tcpdump evidence —
164	   on the AX9000 reproduction, general mDNS clearly works (Mi IoT,
165	   AirPlay TXT records all visible) but WebRTC's `<uuid>.local`
166	   resolution still fails. The failure mode is something narrower
167	   than "no multicast at all".
168	3. Check that any STUN diagnostic uses CN-reachable servers
169	   (`stun.miwifi.com` / `stun.qq.com` / `stun.cloudflare.com` are in
170	   the spike's default list; Google STUN is blocked from CN).
171	4. Recognise that "no AP-isolation toggle visible" doesn't tell you
172	   anything either way — Xiaomi MiWiFi consumer firmware doesn't
173	   expose one even when peer-to-peer multicast IS bridging fine.
174	
175	The `lan_echo.rs` warning banner mentions both the iOS-backgrounding
176	gotcha (separate issue) and points users at the iPhone-hotspot
177	workaround if connection fails.
178	
179	## Useful diagnostic commands (macOS)
180	
181	```sh
182	# Live mDNS browse on en0 — should show cross-device services on a healthy LAN
183	dns-sd -B _services._dns-sd._udp local.
184	
185	# Resolve a specific .local hostname to IP — should succeed for any device
186	# advertising itself; will TIMEOUT (not error) if the responder isn't reachable
187	dns-sd -G v4 some-uuid.local
188	
189	# Capture all mDNS traffic on en0 for ~30s — count cross-device packets
190	sudo tcpdump -i en0 -n udp port 5353 -G 30 -W 1
191	
192	# Capture IPv6 mDNS too
193	sudo tcpdump -i en0 'ip6 and udp port 5353' -G 30 -W 1
194	```
195	
196	For Xiaomi MiWiFi routers the user maintains a read-only LuCI JSON API
197	CLI at `~/bin/mi-router` (`mi-router info / wifi / devices / mdns / raw
198	<endpoint>`); useful for inspecting router state without SSH access.
199
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-05-14 05:44:07Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pitfalls/webrtc-set-remote-description-resolves-before-dc-open.md`

```text
1	---
2	status: known-bug
3	first-hit: 2026-05-13
4	last-hit: 2026-05-13
5	---
6	
7	# `RtcPeerConnection::set_remote_description` resolves before DataChannel opens — early `dc.send_with_str` silently fails
8	
9	## Symptom (verbatim)
10	
11	LAN host page enters game successfully:
12	
13	* Sidebar: `You play Red 紅`, `Red 紅 to move`, `Connected`.
14	* Board renders. Host can move pieces. State machine advances.
15	
16	LAN joiner page (on the same machine, two browser tabs, localhost
17	LAN — fastest possible network):
18	
19	* Sidebar: `Connected`, `Awaiting seat assignment`.
20	* Board area: `Waiting for server greeting…`
21	* Status flips from `WaitingForOpen` → `Playing` (so the
22	  DataChannel open detection works), but `PlayConnected` then sits
23	  with `role: None, view: None` because `Hello` and `ChatHistory`
24	  never arrive through `Session.incoming`.
25	
26	No console errors. No transport-layer failure visible.
27	
28	## Root cause
29	
30	`RtcPeerConnection::set_remote_description(answer)` resolves as
31	soon as the SDP is applied — that's just an SDP-parse + state
32	transition. It does NOT wait for the underlying DataChannel
33	SCTP handshake to complete. The DTLS + SCTP handshake happens
34	asynchronously after `setRemoteDescription` resolves.
35	
36	The host page was doing:
37	
38	```rust
39	hh.accept_answer(blob).await?;          // SDP applied, returns FAST
40	let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(...);
41	room.attach_remote_player_dc(dc)?;      // calls join_player → fanout
42	                                         // → dc.send_with_str(Hello)
43	                                         // → DC still "connecting"!
44	                                         // → silently fails (Err)
45	```
46	
47	`PeerSink::Remote(dc)::deliver` discards the result of
48	`dc.send_with_str`:
49	
50	```rust
51	PeerSink::Remote(dc) => {
52	    if let Ok(text) = serde_json::to_string(&msg) {
53	        let _ = dc.send_with_str(&text);   // <-- DROPS error
54	    }
55	}
56	```
57	
58	So the `Hello` + `ChatHistory` for the joiner are silently lost.
59	Later, the DC fully opens, joiner's `state` flips to `Open`,
60	joiner mounts `PlayConnected`, but the queue is empty and stays
61	empty — `Hello` was dropped on the wire.
62	
63	This is hard to spot because:
64	
65	* `accept_answer` returning Ok suggests "everything's fine".
66	* `dc` exists and `dc.ready_state()` returns... a value that's
67	  not `Open` yet.
68	* The joiner's DC eventually opens, so the user sees `Connected`
69	  in the sidebar, leading them to expect game state to follow.
70	
71	The race is timing-dependent. On slow networks, the gap between
72	`accept_answer` and DC open is hundreds of milliseconds. On
73	localhost it's <10 ms — but still wide enough to lose the
74	synchronous `attach_remote_player_dc` call.
75	
76	## Workaround
77	
78	Wait for `dc.ready_state() == "open"` before fanning out via the DC.
79	A polling helper covers both the "open eventually" happy path and
80	the "never opens" failure mode (with a timeout):
81	
82	```rust
83	pub async fn wait_for_dc_open(dc: &RtcDataChannel, timeout_ms: u32) -> bool {
84	    let deadline = js_sys::Date::now() + timeout_ms as f64;
85	    loop {
86	        if dc.ready_state() == RtcDataChannelState::Open { return true; }
87	        if js_sys::Date::now() > deadline { return false; }
88	        // sleep 50 ms via setTimeout-wrapped Promise
89	        let promise = js_sys::Promise::new(&mut |resolve, _| {
90	            if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
91	                let _ = win.set_timeout_with_callback_and_timeout_and_arguments_0(&resolve, 50);
92	            }
93	        });
94	        let _ = JsFuture::from(promise).await;
95	    }
96	}
97	```
98	
99	Page code:
100	
101	```rust
102	hh.accept_answer(blob).await?;
103	let dc = hh.dc.borrow().clone().ok_or(...)?;
104	if !wait_for_dc_open(&dc, 10_000).await {
105	    return error("DataChannel did not open within 10 s");
106	}
107	let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(...);
108	room.attach_remote_player_dc(dc)?;     // safe — DC is Open now
109	```
110	
111	Alternative approaches considered + rejected:
112	
113	* **Wrap `dc.set_onopen` as a Promise** — works, but loses the
114	  timeout path. We need the timeout to surface "network blocked"
115	  failures (e.g. AP isolation, firewall).
116	* **Buffer messages inside `PeerSink::Remote`** until DC opens —
117	  more invasive, ties the routing layer to DC lifecycle. The
118	  page-level wait is cleaner.
119	* **Defer `attach_remote_player_dc` itself to fire on DC open** —
120	  same as above; pushes complexity into the routing layer when
121	  the page is the natural place to await async setup.
122	
123	## Prevention
124	
125	When using browser DataChannel-style transports:
126	
127	* **NEVER assume that the SDP-application `await` (i.e.
128	  `set_remote_description` / `accept_answer`) means the channel
129	  is usable**. SDP application is a state-machine update; the
130	  actual transport handshake is separate and asynchronous.
131	* **Always check `dc.ready_state() == "open"` before sending**.
132	  The browser will silently fail (`Err`) on a non-open channel,
133	  which most "dispatch and forget" wrappers (including ours)
134	  discard.
135	* **When wrapping `dc.send_with_str` results**, log or surface
136	  the error. A `let _ =` is fine for steady-state messages where
137	  occasional drops are acceptable, but at handshake-time you
138	  want loud failures.
139	* For greeting-style messages (one-shot per peer), prefer waiting
140	  synchronously until the channel is fully ready rather than
141	  firing-and-praying.
142	
143	## See also
144	
145	* `clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs::wait_for_dc_open` —
146	  the helper.
147	* `clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs::LanHostPage::on_accept` —
148	  the wait site.
149	* `pitfalls/leptos-create-effect-inside-spawn-local-silent-gc.md`
150	  — adjacent bug on the joiner page, same Phase 5 testing session.
151	* `pitfalls/leptos-rwsignal-queue-self-clear-race.md` — earlier
152	  bug in the same code path.
153	* MDN: `RTCDataChannel.readyState` —
154	  https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/RTCDataChannel/readyState
155
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	//! Camera capability detection for the LAN-pairing QR scanner
2	//! (Phase 5.5).
3	//!
4	//! Three signals the page-level scanner UI cares about:
5	//!
6	//! 1. **`has_camera()`** — does the device expose at least one
7	//!    video-input device? `false` for desktops with no webcam,
8	//!    laptops with the camera disabled in privacy settings, or
9	//!    headless browsers. The scanner button stays hidden when this
10	//!    is `false` so users without a camera don't see a dead
11	//!    affordance — the textarea-paste path remains the parallel
12	//!    input mode (per `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing-qr.md` design).
13	//!
14	//! 2. **`camera_permission()`** — current Permissions API state for
15	//!    `'camera'`. Returns `Granted` / `Denied` / `Prompt` / `Unknown`.
16	//!    `Unknown` covers browsers that don't implement
17	//!    `navigator.permissions.query({name:'camera'})` (notably some
18	//!    older Safari versions). The scanner UI shows a "Camera blocked
19	//!    — enable in Settings" hint when `Denied`.
20	//!
21	//! 3. **`open_camera_stream(constraints)`** — actually request the
22	//!    camera stream. Wraps `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia`.
23	//!    First call triggers the browser permission prompt on iOS
24	//!    Safari + Android Chrome.
25	//!
26	//! ## Privacy / first-call ordering
27	//!
28	//! `has_camera()` calls `enumerateDevices()` which on most browsers
29	//! returns video-input devices WITHOUT requiring camera permission
30	//! (Chrome/Firefox), but on Safari without permission the devices
31	//! show up with empty `deviceId` / `label`. We treat any
32	//! video-input as a "camera exists" signal — labels aren't needed
33	//! for the scanner (we don't pick which camera, we just use
34	//! `facingMode: environment` and let the browser choose).
35	//!
36	//! ## Dead-code allowance
37	//!
38	//! `camera_permission()` and `CameraPermission` are consumed by
39	//! commit 4's modal UI for the Denied-state hint. Allow until then.
40	#![allow(dead_code)]
41	
42	use js_sys::{Array, Object, Reflect};
43	use wasm_bindgen::prelude::*;
44	use wasm_bindgen_futures::JsFuture;
45	use web_sys::{MediaDeviceInfo, MediaDeviceKind, MediaStream, MediaStreamConstraints};
46	
47	/// Browser-reported camera permission state.
48	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
49	pub enum CameraPermission {
50	    /// User has granted camera access (this origin, this session).
51	    Granted,
52	    /// User has denied. Scanner should show "enable in Settings"
53	    /// hint instead of trying to prompt again.
54	    Denied,
55	    /// Permission has not been asked yet. The first
56	    /// `getUserMedia` call will trigger the OS prompt.
57	    Prompt,
58	    /// Browser doesn't implement `navigator.permissions.query` for
59	    /// `camera`, OR the query failed for some other reason. Treat
60	    /// like `Prompt` — try the call and let the browser handle it.
61	    Unknown,
62	}
63	
64	/// Returns `true` if the device exposes at least one video-input
65	/// device.
66	///
67	/// On browsers that gate `enumerateDevices()` results behind camera
68	/// permission (some Safari builds), this may return `false` until
69	/// the user grants permission once. Caller's UI should call this
70	/// on mount and re-check after a successful `open_camera_stream`.
71	pub async fn has_camera() -> bool {
72	    let Some(win) = web_sys::window() else {
73	        return false;
74	    };
75	    let nav = win.navigator();
76	    let Ok(media_devices) = nav.media_devices() else {
77	        return false;
78	    };
79	    let Ok(promise) = media_devices.enumerate_devices() else {
80	        return false;
81	    };
82	    let Ok(devs_js) = JsFuture::from(promise).await else {
83	        return false;
84	    };
85	    let arr: Array = devs_js.into();
86	    arr.iter().any(|dev_js| {
87	        dev_js
88	            .dyn_into::<MediaDeviceInfo>()
89	            .map(|info| info.kind() == MediaDeviceKind::Videoinput)
90	            .unwrap_or(false)
91	    })
92	}
93	
94	/// Query `navigator.permissions.query({name: 'camera'})` and return
95	/// a typed enum.
96	pub async fn camera_permission() -> CameraPermission {
97	    let Some(win) = web_sys::window() else {
98	        return CameraPermission::Unknown;
99	    };
100	    let nav = win.navigator();
101	    let Ok(perms) = Reflect::get(&nav, &JsValue::from_str("permissions")) else {
102	        return CameraPermission::Unknown;
103	    };
104	    if perms.is_undefined() || perms.is_null() {
105	        return CameraPermission::Unknown;
106	    }
107	    let descriptor = Object::new();
108	    if Reflect::set(&descriptor, &JsValue::from_str("name"), &JsValue::from_str("camera")).is_err()
109	    {
110	        return CameraPermission::Unknown;
111	    }
112	    let query = match Reflect::get(&perms, &JsValue::from_str("query")) {
113	        Ok(q) if q.is_function() => q,
114	        _ => return CameraPermission::Unknown,
115	    };
116	    let query_fn: js_sys::Function = query.unchecked_into();
117	    let Ok(promise_js) = query_fn.call1(&perms, &descriptor) else {
118	        return CameraPermission::Unknown;
119	    };
120	    let promise: js_sys::Promise = promise_js.unchecked_into();
121	    let Ok(status) = JsFuture::from(promise).await else {
122	        return CameraPermission::Unknown;
123	    };
124	    let Ok(state) = Reflect::get(&status, &JsValue::from_str("state")) else {
125	        return CameraPermission::Unknown;
126	    };
127	    match state.as_string().as_deref() {
128	        Some("granted") => CameraPermission::Granted,
129	        Some("denied") => CameraPermission::Denied,
130	        Some("prompt") => CameraPermission::Prompt,
131	        _ => CameraPermission::Unknown,
132	    }
133	}
134	
135	/// Open a camera `MediaStream` via `getUserMedia`. Requests the
136	/// back camera on phones (`facingMode: 'environment'`); browser
137	/// falls back to the user-facing camera if `environment` is
138	/// unavailable (laptops, desktops with one camera).
139	///
140	/// Returns the stream on success. Caller should attach it to a
141	/// `<video>` via `set_src_object(Some(&stream))`.
142	///
143	/// First call triggers the OS camera permission prompt. If the
144	/// user denies, returns Err and the caller should switch to the
145	/// "Camera blocked" UI.
146	pub async fn open_camera_stream() -> Result<MediaStream, JsValue> {
147	    let win = web_sys::window().ok_or_else(|| JsValue::from_str("no window"))?;
148	    let media_devices = win.navigator().media_devices()?;
149	
150	    let constraints = MediaStreamConstraints::new();
151	    let video_constraint = Object::new();
152	    Reflect::set(
153	        &video_constraint,
154	        &JsValue::from_str("facingMode"),
155	        &JsValue::from_str("environment"),
156	    )
157	    .ok();
158	    constraints.set_video(&video_constraint);
159	
160	    let promise = media_devices.get_user_media_with_constraints(&constraints)?;
161	    let stream_js = JsFuture::from(promise).await?;
162	    Ok(stream_js.dyn_into::<MediaStream>()?)
163	}
164
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./clients/chess-web/src/components/qr.rs`

```text
1	//! QR code rendering for LAN-pairing SDP envelopes (Phase 5.5).
2	//!
3	//! Renders a `&str` payload as an inline SVG QR code via the
4	//! [`qrcode`](https://crates.io/crates/qrcode) crate. The SVG is
5	//! embedded into the page via `inner_html` so it scales to whatever
6	//! container size the parent layout gives it (CSS controls the
7	//! visual size, not the QR module count).
8	//!
9	//! Used by `pages/lan.rs` to render:
10	//! - Host's offer SDP (joiner scans).
11	//! - Joiner's answer SDP (host scans).
12	//!
13	//! ## Capacity sanity
14	//!
15	//! The Phase 5 SDP envelopes measured ~660 bytes. With auto-version
16	//! selection at ECC level M (15% damage tolerance), a 660-byte
17	//! payload encodes as QR v25 (byte mode capacity 718 bytes — fits
18	//! with headroom). If the payload grows (e.g. STUN srflx candidates
19	//! adding ~80 bytes per server), v30 (1085 bytes byte-mode capacity)
20	//! kicks in automatically. Beyond v30 the QR becomes too dense for
21	//! a phone camera at arm's length — see
22	//! `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing-qr.md` for the capacity table.
23	//!
24	//! ## Why ECC level M (medium)
25	//!
26	//! Trade-off between damage tolerance (~15% restorable) and density
27	//! (more modules → smaller per-module size at fixed CSS dimensions).
28	//! Higher levels (Q = 25%, H = 30%) push the payload into a higher
29	//! QR version which makes scanning harder on small phone screens.
30	//! M is the standard sweet spot for screen-displayed QRs.
31	
32	use leptos::*;
33	use qrcode::render::svg;
34	use qrcode::{EcLevel, QrCode};
35	
36	/// Render `payload` as an SVG QR code at the given pixel size.
37	///
38	/// Returns `None` if `payload.is_empty()` (don't render anything for
39	/// empty input — caller's `Show` should already be gating this) or if
40	/// the QR encoder fails (would only happen for payloads larger than
41	/// QR version 40 byte-mode max ~2.9 KB; unreachable for valid SDPs).
42	fn encode_svg(payload: &str, min_dim: u32) -> Option<String> {
43	    if payload.is_empty() {
44	        return None;
45	    }
46	    let code = QrCode::with_error_correction_level(payload.as_bytes(), EcLevel::M).ok()?;
47	    let svg = code
48	        .render()
49	        .min_dimensions(min_dim, min_dim)
50	        .dark_color(svg::Color("#000"))
51	        .light_color(svg::Color("#fff"))
52	        .quiet_zone(true)
53	        .build();
54	    Some(svg)
55	}
56	
57	/// Inline-SVG QR code for `payload`. Renders at `min_dim` pixels
58	/// minimum (the actual size grows with QR version + CSS).
59	///
60	/// Wrap in CSS that gives the SVG a definite size — the QR scales
61	/// to fit its parent. Recommended container: 280×280 CSS pixels
62	/// minimum so a phone camera at arm's length can decode it
63	/// reliably.
64	#[component]
65	pub fn QrCodeView(
66	    /// SDP envelope (or any string) to encode.
67	    payload: Signal<String>,
68	    /// Minimum SVG dimension in pixels. The QR encoder picks the
69	    /// smallest version that fits the payload; the SVG is then
70	    /// scaled to this minimum. Keep ≥ 200 px for readable scanning.
71	    #[prop(default = 280)]
72	    min_dim: u32,
73	    /// Optional aria-label for screen readers; shows as alt text.
74	    #[prop(default = "QR code")]
75	    label: &'static str,
76	) -> impl IntoView {
77	    let svg = move || encode_svg(&payload.get(), min_dim);
78	    view! {
79	        <figure
80	            class="qr-card"
81	            role="img"
82	            aria-label=label
83	            style="margin:0;display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;gap:0.5rem"
84	        >
85	            {move || match svg() {
86	                Some(s) => view! { <div class="qr-svg" inner_html=s /> }.into_view(),
87	                None => view! {
88	                    <div class="qr-placeholder" style="width:280px;height:280px;background:#222;color:#888;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-size:14px">
89	                        "(no payload yet)"
90	                    </div>
91	                }.into_view(),
92	            }}
93	        </figure>
94	    }
95	}
96	
97	#[cfg(test)]
98	mod tests {
99	    use super::*;
100	
101	    #[test]
102	    fn encode_empty_returns_none() {
103	        assert!(encode_svg("", 200).is_none());
104	    }
105	
106	    #[test]
107	    fn encode_small_payload_returns_svg() {
108	        let svg = encode_svg("hello world", 200).expect("small payload encodes");
109	        assert!(svg.starts_with("<?xml") || svg.starts_with("<svg"), "got: {}", &svg[..50]);
110	        assert!(svg.contains("</svg>"));
111	    }
112	
113	    #[test]
114	    fn encode_typical_sdp_size_fits() {
115	        // Synthesize a payload around the size of a real LAN-pairing
116	        // SDP envelope (~660 bytes). Should encode without error.
117	        let payload = "x".repeat(660);
118	        let svg = encode_svg(&payload, 280).expect("660-byte payload should fit");
119	        assert!(svg.contains("</svg>"));
120	    }
121	}
122
```
</details></tool-use>

Perfect! Now let me check commit ed4c1d2 to understand the jsQR lazy-loader better and also look for any outstanding issues:

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-05-14 05:44:23Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>

`git show ed4c1d2 --stat`

```text
commit ed4c1d279addceb8be61f55b895a341596c63cb1
Author: Da-Wei Lee <daviddwlee84@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu May 14 01:57:27 2026 +0800

    feat(chess-web): Phase 5.5 commit 2 — jsQR.min.js + lazy-loaded Rust interop wrapper
    
    Second slice of Phase 5.5 LAN-pairing UX upgrade. Adds the QR
    *decoder* side (commit 1 added the encoder + display).
    
    - Drop `clients/chess-web/public/jsQR.min.js` (~130 KB minified,
      ~38 KB gzipped). jsdelivr-Terser-minified build of cozmo/jsQR
      v1.4.0 (Apache-2.0). Trunk's existing PWA precache walker
      picks it up automatically (any `*.js` in dist/ goes into the
      service worker's offline cache).
    - Add `data-trunk rel="copy-file" href="public/jsQR.min.js"` so
      trunk copies it from public/ to dist/.
    - New `clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs`:
      - `decode_rgba(data, w, h) -> Option<String>` — wraps the
        `jsQR(data, w, h)` extern call; extracts `.data` field from
        the result; returns None on no-QR-found.
      - `is_available() -> bool` — checks if global `jsQR` symbol
        is registered.
      - `ensure_loaded() -> Result<(), JsValue>` — lazy-loads the
        script if not already loaded. Idempotent. Uses an absolute
        URL built from `window.location.origin + "/jsQR.min.js"` to
        sidestep the SPA relative-URL trap (a `<script src=
        "jsQR.min.js">` from `/lan/host` would resolve to
        `/lan/jsQR.min.js`, which is the SPA fallback HTML, not the
        JS file).
    - `web-sys` features gain `HtmlScriptElement` for the dynamic
      injection.
    
    Why lazy-loaded (not eagerly via index.html `<script>` tag):
    1. Most users never use LAN pairing — saves 130 KB initial load.
    2. Sidesteps the SPA-relative-URL bug (works regardless of
       current route; works on GitHub Pages with --public-url
       subpath; works in production).
    3. Leaves room for the camera scanner (commit 4) to surface
       "QR scanner unavailable" if the script fails to load
       (network error, CSP block, corrupted bundle).
    
    Verified via playwright-cli:
    - Direct navigation to /lan/host → `typeof window.jsQR` is
      `undefined` (script not loaded eagerly — confirms lazy design).
    - Manually injecting via `document.createElement('script') +
      origin + "/jsQR.min.js"` → `typeof window.jsQR` becomes
      `function` (confirms the URL resolves and the script registers
      the global). The Rust `ensure_loaded()` does the same dance.
    
    No camera modal yet — that's commit 4. This commit just makes
    the decoder primitives available.
    
    All gates green.

 clients/chess-web/Cargo.toml         |   1 +
 clients/chess-web/index.html         |   8 ++
 clients/chess-web/public/jsQR.min.js |   8 ++
 clients/chess-web/src/lib.rs         |   2 +
 clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs   | 141 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 5 files changed, 160 insertions(+)
```
</details></tool-use>

1	//! `chess-web` — Leptos + WASM frontend for chess-core.
2	//!
3	//! Native (`cargo check --workspace`) compiles only the pure-logic modules
4	//! (orient, glyph, routes, state); the leptos UI and the WS layer are
5	//! wasm32-gated so the workspace target check stays clean. Build for the
6	//! browser with `trunk serve` (or `cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown
7	//! -p chess-web`).
8	
9	pub mod eval;
10	pub mod glyph;
11	pub mod orient;
12	pub mod routes;
13	pub mod state;
14	pub mod time;

17	mod app;
18	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
19	mod camera;

```text
1	//! Host-side room authority for LAN play.
2	//!
3	//! Phase 4 of `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`. Wraps the
4	//! `chess_net::room::Room` state machine with a multi-peer routing
5	//! table:
6	//!
7	//!   - Host runs the `Room` in-process (no chess-net server involved).
8	//!   - Host is itself one of the seated players (`PeerSink::Local`):
9	//!     its `ClientMsg`s flow through `Room::apply` exactly the same
10	//!     way a remote peer's would, and it receives projected
11	//!     `ServerMsg`s via a Leptos signal that the play page reads.
12	//!   - Remote peers (joiner + spectators) connect via WebRTC. Each
13	//!     `RtcDataChannel` is a `PeerSink::Remote`; incoming JSON-decoded
14	//!     `ClientMsg`s route through the same `Room::apply` path.
15	//!
16	//! The host page consumes the same `Session { handle, incoming, state }`
17	//! shape that `transport::ws::connect` returns, so the existing
18	//! `pages/play.rs` works unchanged once Phase 5 wires up the
19	//! `/lan/host` route to provide the host's `Session` via Leptos
20	//! context.
21	//!
22	//! ## Lifetime + reference-cycle notes
23	//!
24	//! Closures wired to `RtcDataChannel.onmessage` capture `Weak<HostRoom>`,
25	//! not `Rc<HostRoom>`, to avoid a reference cycle between the room
26	//! and the per-peer keepalive vector. When the page drops its
27	//! `Rc<HostRoom>`, all `Weak::upgrade` calls inside the closures
28	//! return `None` and become silent no-ops — the next time the
29	//! browser GCs the channel, the closure goes too.
30	//!
31	//! ## Dead-code allowance
32	//!
33	//! Phase 5 (`pages/lan.rs`) consumes `HostRoom::new` +
34	//! `attach_remote_player_dc`. Spectator support, the
35	//! `add_remote_*` (non-DC) variants used by tests, and the
36	//! `room_id` / `summary` accessors are kept for Phase 5 polish.
37	#![allow(dead_code)]
38	
39	// === module skeleton — section bodies filled in via edits below ===
40	
41	// SECTION: imports
42	use std::cell::{Cell, RefCell};
43	use std::collections::HashMap;
44	use std::rc::Rc;
45	
46	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
47	use chess_net::protocol::{ClientMsg, ServerMsg};
48	use chess_net::room::{JoinError, Outbound, PeerId, Room};
49	use leptos::*;
50	
51	use crate::transport::{ConnState, Incoming, Session, Transport};
52	
53	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
54	#[allow(unused_imports)]
55	use {
56	    std::rc::Weak,
57	    wasm_bindgen::closure::Closure,
58	    wasm_bindgen::{JsCast, JsValue},
59	    web_sys::{MessageEvent, RtcDataChannel},
60	};
61	// SECTION: PeerSink
62	
63	/// Where to deliver `ServerMsg`s for a given peer.
64	///
65	/// `Local` is the host's own play page — `ServerMsg`s become entries
66	/// in a Leptos-tracked `Incoming` queue (a `VecDeque` + tick signal,
67	/// see `transport::Incoming`) that the page reads via
68	/// `Session.incoming` (and drains via `Incoming::drain`). The queue
69	/// design (rather than a latched single-value signal) is critical:
70	/// a fresh `Room::join_player` synchronously emits `Hello` +
71	/// `ChatHistory`, which on a latched signal would silently overwrite
72	/// each other in a single Leptos batch. The queue + monotonic tick
73	/// guarantees both messages survive AND that any post-mount push
74	/// (e.g. the host's own move's `Update` echo back through the local
75	/// sink) is observed — see `transport::Incoming` doc for the race
76	/// analysis.
77	///
78	/// `Remote` is a peer connected over WebRTC — `ServerMsg`s are
79	/// JSON-serialised and written into a `RtcDataChannel`. The
80	/// non-wasm32 build (workspace `cargo check`) substitutes a
81	/// `RemoteMock` variant for unit tests since `web_sys` types
82	/// aren't reachable on native targets.
83	pub enum PeerSink {
84	    Local(Incoming),
85	    #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
86	    Remote(RtcDataChannel),
87	    /// Test-only sink that records every delivered message into a
88	    /// shared `Vec`. Native + wasm32 both compile this so unit
89	    /// tests can run on either target.
90	    Mock(Rc<RefCell<Vec<ServerMsg>>>),
91	}
92	
93	impl PeerSink {
94	    fn deliver(&self, msg: ServerMsg) {
95	        match self {
96	            PeerSink::Local(inc) => inc.push(msg),
97	            #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
98	            PeerSink::Remote(dc) => {
99	                if let Ok(text) = serde_json::to_string(&msg) {
100	                    let _ = dc.send_with_str(&text);
101	                }
102	            }
103	            PeerSink::Mock(buf) => buf.borrow_mut().push(msg),
104	        }
105	    }
106	}
107	// SECTION: HostRoom struct
108	
109	/// Host-side authoritative room. Owns a `chess_net::room::Room`
110	/// (the same state machine the chess-net server runs) plus a routing
111	/// table mapping each peer's [`PeerId`] to a [`PeerSink`].
112	///
113	/// All mutable state is in `RefCell`s (single-threaded WASM context).
114	/// The struct is consumed via `Rc<HostRoom>` because:
115	///   1. The host's `Transport` impl needs to hold a strong ref so
116	///      `Transport::send` can route the host's own `ClientMsg`s
117	///      through `Room::apply`.
118	///   2. Per-peer DataChannel `onmessage` closures need a `Weak`
119	///      ref (NOT strong — see the cycle note in the module doc).
120	pub struct HostRoom {
121	    room: RefCell<Room>,
122	    sinks: RefCell<HashMap<PeerId, PeerSink>>,
123	    /// Monotonic peer-id allocator. The host gets `PeerId(1)`; the
124	    /// first remote peer is `PeerId(2)`, etc.
125	    next_peer: Cell<u64>,
126	    /// `PeerId` for the host's own play UI. Always `PeerId(1)` from
127	    /// `new()`; surfaced as a getter for clarity at call sites.
128	    self_peer: PeerId,
129	    /// Stable label used in `RoomSummary` (the host page shows it
130	    /// in the lobby-style indicator). Defaults to "lan".
131	    room_id: String,
132	}
133	// SECTION: HostRoom::new + Session bridge
134	
135	impl HostRoom {
136	    /// Construct a fresh host room and seat the host as the first
137	    /// player. Returns:
138	    ///
139	    ///   * `Rc<HostRoom>` — handle the host page holds for adding
140	    ///     remote peers as they pair (`add_remote_player`,
141	    ///     `add_remote_spectator`).
142	    ///   * `Session` — drop into the play page exactly where
143	    ///     `transport::ws::connect`'s return value would go. The
144	    ///     session's `incoming` signal already has the initial
145	    ///     `Hello` + `ChatHistory` queued (the host always seats as
146	    ///     RED, so the page sees `observer: Side::RED` immediately).
147	    ///
148	    /// Panics only on the impossible case where `Room::join_player`
149	    /// fails for the very first joiner (the room is fresh — both
150	    /// seats are open, no password mismatch is possible because the
```
