## Approved Plan:
# LAN host/join pairing on GitHub Pages — fix jsQR + ICE diagnostics

## Context

Two bugs reported on `https://daviddwlee84.github.io/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/lan/host` (LAN WebRTC pairing, Phase 5/5.5):

1. **Copy-text flow reaches "Accept answer" then errors out with**
   `ERROR: DataChannel did not open within 10 s — pairing failed (network blocked?)`.
   Offer/answer SDPs both have only `.local` mDNS host candidates; toggling
   "Use STUN" doesn't help — error persists, and offer generation visibly slows
   (5 s ICE-gather timeout firing because one of the STUN servers is unreachable).

2. **QR scanner on iOS Safari fails immediately with**
   `JsValue("jsQR script load error")` — the user can't even open the camera flow.

### Root causes

**Issue 2 (clear code bug, the priority fix).**
`clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs:109-110` builds the script URL as:

```rust
let origin = win.location().origin()?;
let url = format!("{origin}/jsQR.min.js");
```

On the deployed GitHub Pages target the app lives at `https://daviddwlee84.github.io/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/...`. `origin` is just `https://daviddwlee84.github.io` (no path), so the constructed URL `{origin}/jsQR.min.js` resolves to a 404 — the asset is actually at `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/jsQR.min.js`. The script `error` event fires and the loader's promise rejects with `"jsQR script load error"`. The doc comment at lines 12–18 explicitly claims this works on GitHub Pages subpaths, but the implementation doesn't actually consume the base path. The PWA service-worker side handles base paths via `__BASE_PATH__` substitution (`scripts/build-pwa.sh`), and the Leptos router consumes `routes::base_path()` (`src/routes.rs:30`); the jsQR loader just never got plumbed through.

**Issue 1 (not directly fixable in code — it's a network/router-level WebRTC quirk).**
This is the documented pitfall in `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`: modern browsers obfuscate LAN host candidates as `<uuid>.local` mDNS names, and on certain consumer routers (Xiaomi AX9000 confirmed) WebRTC's per-session dynamic `.local` resolution fails even though general mDNS (AirPlay, Mi IoT) works. STUN srflx fallback also doesn't rescue NAT hairpin on the same network (user's STUN-on test confirms this). The known workaround is iPhone-hotspot pairing (Approach A in the pitfall doc); a proper code fix requires a chess-net signalling endpoint (Approach C, backlog `webrtc-lan-pairing.md`).

What we can do in code: make the failure mode *legible* to the user / future debuggers so they don't have to read pitfall docs to know what's happening — surface the live ICE / connection state in the page, log state transitions to the JS console, include the final state in the timeout error, and re-snapshot the SDP at copy time so late-arriving candidates aren't lost.

### Intended outcome

- iOS users can use the QR scanner camera flow on the GH Pages deploy (issue 2 fully fixed).
- When LAN pairing fails (issue 1 — networks where mDNS can't resolve), the user sees what went wrong (e.g. "ICE state: disconnected — try iPhone hotspot or enable STUN") instead of a generic "network blocked?", and the offer/answer text/QR always reflect the latest candidates rather than the half-gathered snapshot. Root-cause network fix (signalling broker) is explicitly deferred to a future Phase 5 follow-up.

---

## Commit 1 — Issue 2: jsQR loader respects base path

**Critical files:**

- `clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs` (lines 98–141, especially 109–110)
- `clients/chess-web/index.html` (comment lines 55–61 — currently lies that the loader works on GH Pages)

**Change:**

In `qr_decode.rs::ensure_loaded()`, replace:

```rust
let origin = win.location().origin().map_err(|_| JsValue::from_str("no origin"))?;
let url = format!("{origin}/jsQR.min.js");
```

with:

```rust
use crate::routes::base_path;

let origin = win.location().origin().map_err(|_| JsValue::from_str("no origin"))?;
let base = base_path();   // "" on server deploy, "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi" on GH Pages
let url = format!("{origin}{base}/jsQR.min.js");
```

`base_path()` is the existing helper from `routes.rs:30`, baked at build time from `CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH` env var (set by `Makefile` line 72's `build-web-static` recipe). It returns `""` for `make build-web` (root deploy) and `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi` for `make build-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi`. The string already has its leading `/` normalized away in the empty case, so concatenation works for both.

Touch up the inaccurate comment block at the top of `ensure_loaded()` (lines 12–18) and the `index.html` comment at lines 55–61 to describe the actual behavior.

---

## Commit 2 — Issue 1: ICE/connection-state diagnostics + re-snapshot SDP on Copy

This is scoped to "make the failure mode visible" — no STUN default change, no signalling-broker work.

**Critical files:**

- `clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs` (install state-change listeners; expose a live `ReadSignal<IceDiag>` on `HostHandshake` / `JoinerHandshake`; widen the per-page SDP exposure to re-read `pc.local_description()` reactively)
- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs` (consume the diag signal in the UI; show inline "ICE: checking → connected" badge; widen the 10-s timeout error to include the final ICE state; re-bind the offer/answer textareas + QR payloads to the live `pc.local_description()` instead of the captured snapshot)
- `clients/chess-web/Cargo.toml` (no changes expected — `RtcIceConnectionState`, `RtcIceGatheringState`, `RtcPeerConnectionState` are all already in the web-sys features at lines 38–45)

### 2a. Wire ICE / connection / gathering state-change handlers

Add to both `connect_as_host()` (around line 302 of `webrtc.rs`, before `create_offer`) and `connect_as_joiner()` (around line 252, after creating the PC):

- `pc.set_oniceconnectionstatechange(...)` — `console.log` the new value of `pc.ice_connection_state()`. Update a reactive `WriteSignal<IceDiag>` field.
- `pc.set_onconnectionstatechange(...)` — same pattern for `pc.connection_state()`.
- `pc.set_onicegatheringstatechange(...)` — keep the existing wait-for-`complete` closure but ALSO push state changes into the diag signal. (Two listeners on the same event is fine; web-sys's setter replaces, so refactor to a single closure that updates the signal AND resolves the gather-complete promise when state == complete.)
- Closures pushed into `_keepalive` (the existing pattern at lines 449–482) so they live as long as the handshake.

Define a small struct in `webrtc.rs`:

```rust
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct IceDiag {
    pub ice: RtcIceConnectionState,
    pub conn: RtcPeerConnectionState,
    pub gather: RtcIceGatheringState,
}
```

Add `pub ice_diag: ReadSignal<IceDiag>` to both `HostHandshake` and `JoinerHandshake`. Initialise with the PC's current states at construction time.

### 2b. Use the final ICE state in the 10-s timeout error

In `pages/lan.rs` lines 144–151, change:

```rust
if !wait_for_dc_open(&dc, 10_000).await {
    set_error_msg.set(Some(
        "DataChannel did not open within 10 s — pairing failed (network blocked?)".into(),
    ));
    ...
}
```

to read the latest `ice_diag` snapshot and produce a message like:

```
DataChannel did not open within 10 s — ICE state: <ice>, connection: <conn>.
Common fixes: switch both devices to an iPhone/Android personal hotspot
(this network's WebRTC mDNS resolution may be failing — see
docs/pwa.md for the documented Xiaomi-router quirk), or enable "Use STUN".
```

Keep the message ~3 lines, no rant. The state values matter most for debugging.

### 2c. Inline live ICE-state badge on the LAN host/join pages

After the existing status line `<p>"Status: " {move || format!("{:?}", status.get())}</p>` (line 208 of host, line 429 of join), add a second line that displays the current `ice_diag` reactively. Roughly:

```rust
<Show when=move || handshake_present.get()>
    <p style="font-size:13px;color:#888">
        "ICE: " {move || ice_diag.get().ice_label()}
        " · connection: " {move || ice_diag.get().conn_label()}
        " · gathering: " {move || ice_diag.get().gather_label()}
    </p>
</Show>
```

Where `*_label()` are small helpers mapping the web-sys enum to short strings ("new", "checking", "connected", "disconnected", "failed", "closed"). This is the load-bearing user-facing change — a user pairing on a flaky network can SEE "ICE: failed" instead of staring at "Status: AcceptingAnswer" for 10 s with no insight.

The `handshake_present` signal is the existing `status.get()` being any state past `Idle`. For host page that's everything after `on_open` succeeds; for joiner that's after `on_generate` succeeds.

### 2d. Re-snapshot SDP on Copy

Currently the offer/answer payload is captured once at `connect_as_*` return time and stored in `offer_blob` / `answer_blob` signals. Background ICE gathering continues; later candidates are silently lost from the user-visible textarea / QR.

Fix: bind the displayed SDP to `pc.local_description()` reactively. The cheapest way:

1. Add a `pub fn current_offer(&self) -> OfferBlob` method on `HostHandshake` that reads the PC's `local_description()` at call time, re-runs `encode_sdp(...)`, and returns a fresh blob. Same `current_answer()` for `JoinerHandshake`.
2. In `lan.rs`, replace the initial `set_offer_blob.set(hh.offer.0.clone())` capture with a derived signal that calls `handshake.borrow().as_ref()?.current_offer()` and reruns on a tick signal driven by `oniceicecandidate` / `onicegatheringstatechange`.
3. Simpler alternative: bind a `create_effect` to `ice_diag` (which already fires on gathering state change) and have it re-run `current_offer()` and set `offer_blob`.

The QR code re-renders automatically because `<QrCodeView payload=Signal::derive(move || offer_blob.get())>` is reactive.

Also wire `pc.set_onicecandidate(...)` to update the diag signal on each candidate arrival — this gives the "Copy offer" button its triggers in real time (each new candidate causes the offer text to refresh). Pure UX: the user can wait, watch the byte count tick up, and copy when they're satisfied.

### 2e. Show candidate counts (optional polish)

In the `ice_diag` struct also include `pub candidates: u32`, incremented by the `onicecandidate` handler. Render as `· candidates: N` in the inline badge. Lets the user see "candidates: 1" (only `.local`, mDNS-only) vs "candidates: 3" (got srflx). Trivial to add since we're already wiring `onicecandidate`.

---

## Verification

```bash
# 1. Workspace sanity (issue 2 + 1 should both compile)
cargo check --workspace

# 2. Native chess-web pure-logic unit tests (15 of them — covers
#    routes.rs::base_path used by qr_decode after the change)
cargo test -p chess-web

# 3. Clippy clean
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings

# 4. WASM build (the actual GH Pages target)
make build-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi

# 5. Confirm jsQR.min.js was actually emitted at the expected path
ls -l clients/chess-web/dist-static/jsQR.min.js
test -f clients/chess-web/dist-static/jsQR.min.js && echo "asset present"

# 6. Local PWA-style serve at the GH Pages subpath
make serve-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi
# → serves on http://localhost:4173/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/

# 7. Use playwright-cli to load the join page (which has the camera
#    button visible), open the scanner, and confirm the loader fires
#    a GET to /Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/jsQR.min.js (not /jsQR.min.js).
#    Specifically:
#    - browser_navigate http://localhost:4173/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/lan/join
#    - browser_evaluate to grant camera mock + flip cam_available
#    - browser_click "📷 Scan offer QR"
#    - browser_network_requests | grep jsQR.min.js
#    - assert path contains /Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/
#    - assert response is 200 (not 404)

# 8. Diagnostic UX smoke (issue 1) — Trunk dev server is fine here
make play-web
# Open http://localhost:8080/lan/host in tab A, /lan/join in tab B
# Tab A: tap Open room → confirm "ICE: …" badge appears under Status,
#        confirm console.log output for ice/connection state changes,
#        confirm the byte-count next to Copy offer ticks up as ICE
#        gathers further candidates.
# Tab A: tap Accept answer with a deliberately broken SDP → confirm
#        the 10-s timeout error now includes "ICE state: …"
#        instead of just "network blocked?".

# 9. Full LAN flow on localhost (clean network, no router) — both tabs
#    in same browser actually pair successfully via mDNS bypass through
#    loopback. Acts as regression check that the diagnostics changes
#    didn't break the happy path.
```

**Cannot verify locally:** the actual user-reported LAN failure (cross-device mDNS resolution failing on their physical router). That requires their two real devices and isn't reproducible from playwright. The diagnostics changes above are intended exactly so the user can self-diagnose the next time the failure recurs, and so the message points them at the documented workaround (iPhone hotspot).

---

## Out of scope (call out in commit body)

- **STUN default = ON.** User's screenshot confirms STUN srflx doesn't bypass the failure on their network; flipping the default would only slow down offer generation (5 s ICE-gather timeout to wait for an unreachable STUN server) without fixing the connection. Keep default OFF.
- **chess-net signalling broker (Approach C).** The right long-term fix for users on networks that fail mDNS *and* STUN-hairpin, but a much bigger change (new server endpoint, persistent ICE-candidate trickle channel, new protocol envelope). Already tracked in `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` as a P3 follow-up.
- **Trickle ICE in copy-paste mode.** Re-snapshotting on Copy is a partial workaround; true trickle requires bidirectional signalling that the manual-copy model can't provide.
- **`getUserMedia` exposes real LAN IPs (Approach C in the pitfall doc).** UX nonstarter — asking for mic permission to start a chess game.

If user wants any of these followups, add to `TODO.md` via `scripts/add-todo.sh` after the two commits land.
```
</details></tool-use>

Check build.rs for env defaults

`cat /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/clients/chess-web/build.rs 2>/dev/null | head -40`

```text
fn main() {
    println!("cargo:rerun-if-env-changed=CHESS_WEB_HOSTING");
    println!("cargo:rerun-if-env-changed=CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH");

    let hosting = std::env::var("CHESS_WEB_HOSTING").unwrap_or_else(|_| "server".to_string());
    let hosting = match hosting.as_str() {
        "static" => "static",
        _ => "server",
    };

    let raw_base = std::env::var("CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH").unwrap_or_default();
    let base = normalize_base_path(&raw_base);

    println!("cargo:rustc-env=CHESS_WEB_HOSTING={hosting}");
    println!("cargo:rustc-env=CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH={base}");
}

fn normalize_base_path(raw: &str) -> String {
    let trimmed = raw.trim().trim_matches('/');
    if trimmed.is_empty() {
        String::new()
    } else {
        format!("/{trimmed}")
    }
}
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs`

```text
444	
445	/// Block until `pc.iceGatheringState == "complete"`, OR
446	/// [`ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS`] elapses (whichever comes first).
447	async fn wait_for_ice_complete(
448	    pc: &RtcPeerConnection,
449	    keepalive: &mut Vec<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>>,
450	) {
451	    if pc.ice_gathering_state() == RtcIceGatheringState::Complete {
452	        return;
453	    }
454	    let promise = js_sys::Promise::new(&mut |resolve, _reject| {
455	        let pc_inner = pc.clone();
456	        let resolve_for_state = resolve.clone();
457	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
458	            if pc_inner.ice_gathering_state() == RtcIceGatheringState::Complete {
459	                let _ = resolve_for_state.call0(&JsValue::NULL);
460	            }
461	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
462	        pc.set_onicegatheringstatechange(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
463	        keepalive.push(cb);
464	
465	        // Hard-cap the wait. Late `complete` events still fire but
466	        // promise resolution is idempotent.
467	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
468	            let resolve_for_timeout = resolve.clone();
469	            let pc_for_timeout = pc.clone();
470	            let timeout_cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
471	                web_sys::console::warn_2(
472	                    &"[webrtc] ICE gather timeout — proceeding with whatever candidates we have. Final state:".into(),
473	                    &format!("{:?}", pc_for_timeout.ice_gathering_state()).into(),
474	                );
475	                let _ = resolve_for_timeout.call0(&JsValue::NULL);
476	            }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
477	            let _ = win.set_timeout_with_callback_and_timeout_and_arguments_0(
478	                timeout_cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref(),
479	                ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS,
480	            );
481	            keepalive.push(timeout_cb);
482	        }
483	    });
484	    let _ = JsFuture::from(promise).await;
485	}

491	    dc: &RtcDataChannel,
492	    incoming: Incoming,
493	    state: WriteSignal<ConnState>,
```

39	
40	use chess_net::protocol::{ClientMsg, ServerMsg};
41	use js_sys::{Array, Reflect};
42	use leptos::*;
43	use wasm_bindgen::closure::Closure;
44	use wasm_bindgen::{JsCast, JsValue};
45	use wasm_bindgen_futures::JsFuture;
46	use web_sys::{
47	    MessageEvent, RtcConfiguration, RtcDataChannel, RtcDataChannelEvent, RtcDataChannelInit,
48	    RtcDataChannelState, RtcIceGatheringState, RtcPeerConnection, RtcSdpType,
49	    RtcSessionDescriptionInit, RtcSignalingState,
50	};
51	
52	use super::{ConnState, Incoming, Session, Transport};
53	// SECTION: public types (IceMode, WebRtcConfig, OfferBlob, AnswerBlob)
54	
55	/// `RtcPeerConnection` ICE configuration.
56	///
57	/// `LanOnly` is the production default — `iceServers: []`, pure mDNS
58	/// `<uuid>.local` host candidates only. No external dependencies.
59	///
60	/// `WithStun` adds a multi-server STUN list for the `srflx` candidates.
61	/// Useful as a fallback when LAN P2P fails (e.g. router blocks WebRTC's
62	/// `<uuid>.local` resolution; see
63	/// `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`). Server list ordered for
64	/// CN reachability — `stun.miwifi.com` and `stun.qq.com` first, Google
65	/// last (Google STUN is blocked by the GFW from mainland China).
66	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
67	pub enum IceMode {
68	    LanOnly,
69	    WithStun,
70	}
71	
72	/// Knobs for opening a WebRTC session. Defaults to `LanOnly` — the
73	/// "no external dependency" production case.
74	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug)]
75	pub struct WebRtcConfig {
76	    pub ice_mode: IceMode,
77	}
78	
79	impl Default for WebRtcConfig {
80	    fn default() -> Self {
81	        Self { ice_mode: IceMode::LanOnly }
82	    }
83	}
84	
85	/// Maximum time we wait for `iceGatheringState == "complete"` before
86	/// giving up and producing SDP with whatever candidates have arrived.
87	///
88	/// Caps the failure mode where one of the configured STUN servers is
89	/// unreachable (e.g. `stun.l.google.com` from mainland China — blocked
90	/// by the GFW), which otherwise hangs the whole `connect_as_*` flow
91	/// indefinitely.
92	///
93	/// 5s is generous: pure-LAN gathering completes in 100-200 ms, healthy
94	/// STUN gathering in <1 s. If we hit the timeout we still produce a
95	/// valid SDP — there will just be fewer candidates than "ideal".
```
</details></tool-use>

370	// SECTION: LanJoinPage component
371	
372	#[component]
373	pub fn LanJoinPage() -> impl IntoView {
374	    let (status, set_status) = create_signal::<JoinStatus>(JoinStatus::Idle);
375	    let (use_stun, set_use_stun) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
376	    let (offer_input, set_offer_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());

381	    let (scanner_open, set_scanner_open) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
382	    let (cam_available, set_cam_available) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
383	    spawn_local(async move {
384	        if has_camera().await {
385	            set_cam_available.set(true);
386	        }
387	    });
388	
389	    // Joiner side: store the JoinerHandshake so we can pull its
390	    // session out once the DC opens.
391	    let handshake: Rc<RefCell<Option<JoinerHandshake>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
392	    let (play_session, set_play_session) = create_signal::<Option<Session>>(None);
393	
394	    // Holder pattern: spawn_local runs without a Leptos owner context,
395	    // so a `create_effect` inside it would be GC'd immediately (no
396	    // subscriptions retained — `state.set(Open)` would fire but no
397	    // effect would react). Solution: define the holder signals and
398	    // the effect at component scope (properly owned), and have
399	    // spawn_local just `set` the holders. The component-scope effect
400	    // re-runs when the holders change AND when the inner state
401	    // signal changes.
402	    let (joiner_state_holder, set_joiner_state_holder) =
403	        create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<ConnState>>>(None);
404	    let session_holder: Rc<RefCell<Option<Session>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
405	
406	    {
407	        let session_holder = session_holder.clone();
408	        create_effect(move |_| {
409	            // Read both signals to register subscriptions:
410	            //   * joiner_state_holder fires once when handshake completes.
411	            //   * the inner state signal fires when DC opens.
412	            if let Some(state_sig) = joiner_state_holder.get() {
413	                if state_sig.get() == ConnState::Open {
414	                    if let Some(session) = session_holder.borrow().clone() {
415	                        set_play_session.set(Some(session));
416	                        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Playing);
417	                    }
418	                }
419	            }
420	        });
421	    }
422	
423	    let handshake_for_gen = handshake.clone();
424	    let session_holder_for_gen = session_holder.clone();
425	    let on_generate: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
426	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), JoinStatus::Idle) {
427	            return;
428	        }
429	        let blob = offer_input.get_untracked();
430	        if blob.trim().is_empty() {
431	            set_error_msg.set(Some("paste the host's offer SDP first".into()));
432	            return;
433	        }
434	        set_error_msg.set(None);
435	        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Generating);
436	        let cfg = WebRtcConfig {
437	            ice_mode: if use_stun.get_untracked() { IceMode::WithStun } else { IceMode::LanOnly },
438	        };
439	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_gen.clone();
440	        let session_holder = session_holder_for_gen.clone();
441	        spawn_local(async move {
442	            match connect_as_joiner(cfg, OfferBlob(blob)).await {
443	                Ok(jh) => {
444	                    set_answer_blob.set(jh.answer.0.clone());
445	                    let state = jh.session.state;
446	                    *session_holder.borrow_mut() = Some(jh.session.clone());
447	                    *handshake_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(jh);
448	                    set_status.set(JoinStatus::WaitingForOpen);
449	                    // Triggers the component-scope effect to subscribe
450	                    // to the state signal. Once DC opens, that effect
451	                    // flips status → Playing.
452	                    set_joiner_state_holder.set(Some(state));
453	                }
454	                Err(e) => {
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Tiny SPA-aware static server for local PWA verification.

GitHub Pages serves the dist with a `404.html` fallback that's a copy of
`index.html`, so direct links to client-side routes like
`/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/local/xiangqi` work. Plain `python3 -m
http.server` does not — it returns its own error page on a missing
file, which is why `make serve-web-static` was 404'ing on every SPA
route.

This handler mirrors the GH Pages behaviour: any GET inside the base
scope that doesn't resolve to a file falls back to `<base>/index.html`.
Outside the scope (or for paths with extensions like .js / .wasm),
return the normal 404 so a real missing asset is still surfaced.

Usage:
    serve-spa.py <serve-dir> <port> <base-prefix>
    # e.g. serve-spa.py /tmp/staging 4173 /Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/
    # base-prefix may be "/" or empty for root-served deployments.

No external deps — stdlib only, runs on the bundled python3 of any
modern macOS / Linux dev box.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
import sys
from http.server import HTTPServer, SimpleHTTPRequestHandler

if len(sys.argv) != 4:
    print(__doc__, file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(2)

SERVE_DIR = os.path.abspath(sys.argv[1])
PORT = int(sys.argv[2])
BASE = "/" + sys.argv[3].strip("/")  # always leading slash, no trailing
if BASE == "/":
    BASE = ""  # treat as root-served

# A path is "asset-like" if it has a file extension we precache or
```
</details></tool-use>

Base directory for this skill: /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/.claude/skills/playwright-cli

# Browser Automation with playwright-cli

## Quick start

```bash
# open new browser
playwright-cli open
# navigate to a page
playwright-cli goto https://playwright.dev
# interact with the page using refs from the snapshot
playwright-cli click e15
playwright-cli type "page.click"
playwright-cli press Enter
# take a screenshot (rarely used, as snapshot is more common)
playwright-cli screenshot
# close the browser
playwright-cli close
```

## Commands

### Core

```bash
playwright-cli open
# open and navigate right away
playwright-cli open https://example.com/
playwright-cli goto https://playwright.dev
playwright-cli type "search query"
playwright-cli click e3
playwright-cli dblclick e7
# --submit presses Enter after filling the element
playwright-cli fill e5 "user@example.com"  --submit
playwright-cli drag e2 e8
# drop files or data onto an element (from outside the page)
playwright-cli drop e4 --path=./image.png
playwright-cli drop e4 --data="text/plain=hello world"
playwright-cli hover e4
playwright-cli select e9 "option-value"
playwright-cli upload ./document.pdf
playwright-cli check e12
playwright-cli uncheck e12
playwright-cli snapshot
playwright-cli eval "document.title"
playwright-cli eval "el => el.textContent" e5
# get element id, class, or any attribute not visible in the snapshot
playwright-cli eval "el => el.id" e5
playwright-cli eval "el => el.getAttribute('data-testid')" e5
playwright-cli dialog-accept
playwright-cli dialog-accept "confirmation text"
playwright-cli dialog-dismiss
playwright-cli resize 1920 1080
playwright-cli close
```

### Navigation

```bash
playwright-cli go-back
playwright-cli go-forward
playwright-cli reload
```

### Keyboard

```bash
playwright-cli press Enter
playwright-cli press ArrowDown
playwright-cli keydown Shift
playwright-cli keyup Shift
```

### Mouse

```bash
playwright-cli mousemove 150 300
playwright-cli mousedown
playwright-cli mousedown right
playwright-cli mouseup
playwright-cli mouseup right
playwright-cli mousewheel 0 100
```

### Save as

```bash
playwright-cli screenshot
playwright-cli screenshot e5
playwright-cli screenshot --filename=page.png
playwright-cli pdf --filename=page.pdf
```

### Tabs

```bash
playwright-cli tab-list
playwright-cli tab-new
playwright-cli tab-new https://example.com/page
playwright-cli tab-close
playwright-cli tab-close 2
playwright-cli tab-select 0
```

### Storage

```bash
playwright-cli state-save
playwright-cli state-save auth.json
playwright-cli state-load auth.json

# Cookies
playwright-cli cookie-list
playwright-cli cookie-list --domain=example.com
playwright-cli cookie-get session_id
playwright-cli cookie-set session_id abc123
playwright-cli cookie-set session_id abc123 --domain=example.com --httpOnly --secure
playwright-cli cookie-delete session_id
playwright-cli cookie-clear

# LocalStorage
playwright-cli localstorage-list
playwright-cli localstorage-get theme
playwright-cli localstorage-set theme dark
playwright-cli localstorage-delete theme
playwright-cli localstorage-clear

# SessionStorage
playwright-cli sessionstorage-list
playwright-cli sessionstorage-get step
playwright-cli sessionstorage-set step 3
playwright-cli sessionstorage-delete step
playwright-cli sessionstorage-clear
```

### Network

```bash
playwright-cli route "**/*.jpg" --status=404
playwright-cli route "https://api.example.com/**" --body='{"mock": true}'
playwright-cli route-list
playwright-cli unroute "**/*.jpg"
playwright-cli unroute
```

### DevTools

```bash
playwright-cli console
playwright-cli console warning
playwright-cli requests
playwright-cli request 5
playwright-cli run-code "async page => await page.context().grantPermissions(['geolocation'])"
playwright-cli run-code --filename=script.js
playwright-cli tracing-start
playwright-cli tracing-stop
playwright-cli video-start video.webm
playwright-cli video-chapter "Chapter Title" --description="Details" --duration=2000
playwright-cli video-stop

# launch the dashboard for UI review / design feedback — user annotates the page, you receive the annotated screenshot, snapshot, and notes
playwright-cli show --annotate

# generate a Playwright locator for an element from its ref or selector
playwright-cli generate-locator e5 --raw

# show a persistent highlight overlay for an element, optionally with a custom style
playwright-cli highlight e5
playwright-cli highlight e5 --style="outline: 3px dashed red"
# hide a single element highlight, or all page highlights when no target is given
playwright-cli highlight e5 --hide
playwright-cli highlight --hide
```

## Raw output

The global `--raw` option strips page status, generated code, and snapshot sections from the output, returning only the result value. Use it to pipe command output into other tools. Commands that don't produce output return nothing.

```bash
playwright-cli --raw eval "JSON.stringify(performance.timing)" | jq '.loadEventEnd - .navigationStart'
playwright-cli --raw eval "JSON.stringify([...document.querySelectorAll('a')].map(a => a.href))" > links.json
playwright-cli --raw snapshot > before.yml
playwright-cli click e5
playwright-cli --raw snapshot > after.yml
diff before.yml after.yml
TOKEN=$(playwright-cli --raw cookie-get session_id)
playwright-cli --raw localstorage-get theme
```

For structured output wrapping every reply as JSON, pass --json
```bash
playwright-cli list --json
```

## Open parameters
```bash
# Use specific browser when creating session
playwright-cli open --browser=chrome
playwright-cli open --browser=firefox
playwright-cli open --browser=webkit
playwright-cli open --browser=msedge

# Use persistent profile (by default profile is in-memory)
playwright-cli open --persistent
# Use persistent profile with custom directory
playwright-cli open --profile=/path/to/profile

# Connect to browser via Playwright Extension
playwright-cli attach --extension=chrome

# Connect to a running Chrome or Edge by channel name
playwright-cli attach --cdp=chrome
playwright-cli attach --cdp=msedge

# Connect to a running browser via CDP endpoint
playwright-cli attach --cdp=http://localhost:9222

# Start with config file
playwright-cli open --config=my-config.json

# Close the browser
playwright-cli close
# Detach from an attached browser (leaves the external browser running)
playwright-cli -s=msedge detach
# Delete user data for the default session
playwright-cli delete-data
```

## Snapshots

After each command, playwright-cli provides a snapshot of the current browser state.

```bash
> playwright-cli goto https://example.com
### Page
- Page URL: https://example.com/
- Page Title: Example Domain
### Snapshot
[Snapshot](.playwright-cli/page-2026-02-14T19-22-42-679Z.yml)
```

You can also take a snapshot on demand using `playwright-cli snapshot` command. All the options below can be combined as needed.

```bash
# default - save to a file with timestamp-based name
playwright-cli snapshot

# save to file, use when snapshot is a part of the workflow result
playwright-cli snapshot --filename=after-click.yaml

# snapshot an element instead of the whole page
playwright-cli snapshot "#main"

# limit snapshot depth for efficiency, take a partial snapshot afterwards
playwright-cli snapshot --depth=4
playwright-cli snapshot e34

# include each element's bounding box as [box=x,y,width,height]
playwright-cli snapshot --boxes
```

## Targeting elements

By default, use refs from the snapshot to interact with page elements.

```bash
# get snapshot with refs
playwright-cli snapshot

# interact using a ref
playwright-cli click e15
```

You can also use css selectors or Playwright locators.

```bash
# css selector
playwright-cli click "#main > button.submit"

# role locator
playwright-cli click "getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' })"

# test id
playwright-cli click "getByTestId('submit-button')"
```

## Browser Sessions

```bash
# create new browser session named "mysession" with persistent profile
playwright-cli -s=mysession open example.com --persistent
# same with manually specified profile directory (use when requested explicitly)
playwright-cli -s=mysession open example.com --profile=/path/to/profile
playwright-cli -s=mysession click e6
playwright-cli -s=mysession close  # stop a named browser
playwright-cli -s=mysession delete-data  # delete user data for persistent session

playwright-cli list
# Close all browsers
playwright-cli close-all
# Forcefully kill all browser processes
playwright-cli kill-all
```

## Installation

If global `playwright-cli` command is not available, try a local version via `npx playwright-cli`:

```bash
npx --no-install playwright-cli --version
```

When local version is available, use `npx playwright-cli` in all commands. Otherwise, install `playwright-cli` as a global command:

```bash
npm install -g @playwright/cli@latest
```

## Example: Form submission

```bash
playwright-cli open https://example.com/form
playwright-cli snapshot

playwright-cli fill e1 "user@example.com"
playwright-cli fill e2 "password123"
playwright-cli click e3
playwright-cli snapshot
playwright-cli close
```

## Example: Multi-tab workflow

```bash
playwright-cli open https://example.com
playwright-cli tab-new https://example.com/other
playwright-cli tab-list
playwright-cli tab-select 0
playwright-cli snapshot
playwright-cli close
```

## Example: Debugging with DevTools

```bash
playwright-cli open https://example.com
playwright-cli click e4
playwright-cli fill e7 "test"
playwright-cli console
playwright-cli requests
playwright-cli close
```

```bash
playwright-cli open https://example.com
playwright-cli tracing-start
playwright-cli click e4
playwright-cli fill e7 "test"
playwright-cli tracing-stop
playwright-cli close
```

## Example: Interactive session

Ask the user for UI review or design feedback. The user draws boxes on the live page and types comments; you receive the annotated screenshot, the snapshot of the marked region, and the user's notes. Use this whenever the user asks for "UI review", "design feedback", or to "ask the user what they think / want / mean":

```bash
playwright-cli open https://example.com
playwright-cli show --annotate
```

* **Running and Debugging Playwright tests** [references/playwright-tests.md](references/playwright-tests.md)
* **Request mocking** [references/request-mocking.md](references/request-mocking.md)
* **Running Playwright code** [references/running-code.md](references/running-code.md)
* **Browser session management** [references/session-management.md](references/session-management.md)
* **Spec-driven testing (plan / generate / heal)** [references/spec-driven-testing.md](references/spec-driven-testing.md)
* **Storage state (cookies, localStorage)** [references/storage-state.md](references/storage-state.md)
* **Test generation** [references/test-generation.md](references/test-generation.md)
* **Tracing** [references/tracing.md](references/tracing.md)
* **Video recording** [references/video-recording.md](references/video-recording.md)
* **Inspecting element attributes** [references/element-attributes.md](references/element-attributes.md)

Recent commits track plan + specstory. I'll attach both artifacts to commit 2 (the bigger diff).

### Commit 1 — jsQR URL fix

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 06:18:28Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Confirm commit 1 file scope

`git diff --stat -- clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs clients/chess-web/index.html`

```text
 clients/chess-web/index.html       |  9 +++++----
 clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs | 26 ++++++++++++++++++--------
 2 files changed, 23 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 06:18:42Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit 1: jsQR URL fix

```bash
git add clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs clients/chess-web/index.html && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(chess-web): jsQR loader respects build-time base_path so GH Pages subpath deploys can load the camera scanner

`qr_decode::ensure_loaded` was building the script URL as
`{origin}/jsQR.min.js`, which 404'd on the GitHub Pages deploy at
`/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/` because `origin` doesn't include the subpath
(`window.location.origin` is just `https://daviddwlee84.github.io`).
The asset itself was correctly emitted at `{base}/jsQR.min.js` and the
PWA service worker precached it under the same path; only the runtime
loader missed the prefix.

Reported symptom on iOS Safari at /lan/join (Phase 5.5 LAN pairing):
"QR scanner library failed to load: JsValue(\"jsQR script load error\")".

Fix: read `crate::routes::base_path()` (baked at build time from
`CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH`, empty for server-root deploys, `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi`
for GH Pages) and concatenate it into the URL. Tighten the misleading
doc comments in qr_decode.rs and index.html.

Verified: rebuilt with `make build-web-static WEB_BASE=/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi`
and confirmed via playwright that the page's runtime context can fetch
the new URL pattern (`/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/jsQR.min.js` → 200) while
the old broken pattern (`/jsQR.min.js`) still 404s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

```text
[main 7220e90] fix(chess-web): jsQR loader respects build-time base_path so GH Pages subpath deploys can load the camera scanner
 2 files changed, 23 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 06:19:10Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit 2: diagnostics + plan/specstory

```bash
git add clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs .claude/plans/https-daviddwlee84-github-io-chinese-ch-imperative-sun.md .specstory/history/2026-05-14_05-43-00Z-https-daviddwlee84-github-io.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(chess-web): live ICE diagnostics on /lan/host and /lan/join — surface the real failure mode when pairing times out

The reported "DataChannel did not open within 10 s — pairing failed
(network blocked?)" error fired on the production GH Pages deploy with
the SDP showing only `.local` mDNS host candidates. The actual root
cause is the documented router-level WebRTC mDNS quirk in
pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md — not anything we can fix
in code. But the existing UX gave the user zero insight into WHY the
DC didn't open; even toggling "Use STUN" produced the same opaque
timeout (because STUN srflx doesn't bypass the failure on the same
network either).

Changes — make the failure mode legible:

* `IceDiag` struct (live `ice` / `conn` / `gather` states + candidate
  count) exposed as a `ReadSignal` on `HostHandshake` /
  `JoinerHandshake`. Updated by listeners installed in
  `install_ice_diag_handlers` (oniceconnectionstatechange,
  onconnectionstatechange, onicecandidate) plus the existing
  `wait_for_ice_complete` for the gathering state. Every transition is
  also `console.log`'d so DevTools shows the full progression.
* `pages/lan.rs` renders the diag as an inline badge below the existing
  "Status: …" line on both host and join pages — user sees e.g. "ICE:
  failed · connection: failed · gathering: complete · candidates: 1"
  instead of staring at "AcceptingAnswer" for 10 s.
* The 10s timeout error now includes the final `IceDiag` line and
  points at the iPhone-hotspot / Use-STUN workarounds.
* `current_offer()` / `current_answer()` re-encode the PC's
  `local_description()` at call time, and an effect in lan.rs re-runs
  them on every diag change. Late-arriving srflx candidates (when STUN
  is enabled but the initial 5s gather timeout fired) now appear in the
  textarea / QR rather than being silently dropped from the snapshot
  captured at handshake-return time.

Out of scope (explicit decisions in the plan):
* STUN default stays OFF — user's own test confirmed default-on STUN
  doesn't help on the affected network and slows offer generation.
* No chess-net signalling broker (Approach C from
  backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md). That's a separate Phase 5 follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status
```

```text
[main 38736f6] feat(chess-web): live ICE diagnostics on /lan/host and /lan/join — surface the real failure mode when pairing times out
 4 files changed, 10715 insertions(+), 14 deletions(-)
 create mode 100644 .claude/plans/https-daviddwlee84-github-io-chinese-ch-imperative-sun.md
 create mode 100644 .specstory/history/2026-05-14_05-43-00Z-https-daviddwlee84-github-io.md
On branch main
Your branch is ahead of 'origin/main' by 2 commits.
  (use "git push" to publish your local commits)

nothing to commit, working tree clean
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 06:19:25Z)**_

Both commits landed cleanly. Quick summary of what shipped:

**Commit 1 `7220e90`** — `fix(chess-web): jsQR loader respects build-time base_path…`
- `qr_decode.rs::ensure_loaded` now builds `{origin}{routes::base_path()}/jsQR.min.js`. On GH Pages that resolves to `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/jsQR.min.js` (200) instead of the broken root path (404).
- Verified end-to-end with `make build-web-static WEB_BASE=/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi` + a playwright in-page `fetch()` against both the new and old URL patterns.

**Commit 2 `38736f6`** — `feat(chess-web): live ICE diagnostics on /lan/host and /lan/join…`
- New `IceDiag` (`ice`/`conn`/`gather`/`candidates`) on both handshakes, fed by `oniceconnectionstatechange` + `onconnectionstatechange` + `onicecandidate` + the existing gather-state listener — also `console.log`'d for DevTools.
- Inline "ICE: … · connection: … · gathering: … · candidates: N" badge under Status on both pages.
- 10s timeout error now reads e.g. `… ICE: failed · connection: failed · candidates: 1. Common fixes: switch both devices to an iPhone/Android personal hotspot (this network's WebRTC mDNS resolution may be failing), or enable "Use STUN".`
- `current_offer()` / `current_answer()` + reactive effects so the textarea/QR refresh on each new candidate — late srflx arrivals no longer get dropped on the floor.

The actual router-level mDNS failure isn't fixable in code (documented in `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`); next time it bites, the badge will name the exact stuck state and point at the iPhone-hotspot workaround. The chess-net signalling-broker route is still tracked in `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` if you want a network-agnostic fix later.

Workspace is clean (`cargo check`/`clippy`/`test -p chess-web`/`build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-web` all green; 86 chess-web tests pass).

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 06:56:39Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 06:56:55Z)**_

太好了 — QR scanner 完全修好 ✅。

Issue 1 的兩張截圖正是 pitfall 文檔記錄的場景：

**No STUN**: `ICE: checking · gathering: complete · candidates: 1` — 只有 `.local` mDNS 候選，ICE 永遠卡在 checking（peer 解析不到對方的 `<uuid>.local`）。

**With STUN**: `ICE: checking · gathering: gathering · candidates: 2` — 多了 1 個 srflx 候選（看得到 `c=IN IP4 48.210.47.244` 公網 IP），但 ICE 仍然 checking 永遠不過。代表 NAT hairpin 也被路由器擋了 — 兩台 device 透過公網繞回來連自己這個 NAT 也走不通。

這就是 `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md` 第 27-30 行記錄的同一台 Xiaomi AX9000 上實測過的失敗模式。Code 端能做的已經做完了 — diagnostics 確認問題不在 client，是 router 層級的封包路由問題。

**下一步建議**（從 cheap → expensive）：

1. **iPhone / Android personal hotspot**（pitfall Approach A，已驗證在 1 秒內配對成功）— 兩台都連同一個熱點，bypass 家裡 router。15 秒就能驗證。
2. **如果連 hotspot 都失敗**，那就要進到 `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` 的 Approach C：加一個 chess-net signalling endpoint 來 broker SDP + 持續 trickle ICE candidates。會需要 server，但保證能跨任何網路。要做的話告訴我。
3. **路由器可用 OpenWRT/Asus Merlin** — 但 pitfall 文檔提醒「一般 mDNS 在這台 Xiaomi 上其實是 work 的，failure 比『阻擋 multicast』更窄」，所以刷 firmware 不保證解決。

要不要先試 hotspot，回報結果？同時我可以把現在新發現（STUN srflx + NAT hairpin 在這台路由器也失敗）補進 pitfall 文檔，避免下次 agent 再走一遍 STUN-rescue 的彎路。

_**User (2026-05-14 07:22:41Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 07:23:44Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 07:24:06Z)**_

幾個重要發現：

**1. 你的 iPad 上有 VPN 啟動中** — 看 SDP 裡的 `c=IN IP4 198.18.0.1` 和 candidate `198.18.0.1 typ host`。`198.18.0.0/15` 是 RFC 2544 Benchmark 保留段，iOS 在開 VPN 時會用這個假 IP 當「LAN 位址」配發給 WebRTC。也就是說即使在 hotspot 上，iPad 看到的 LAN 不是真實 WiFi LAN，而是 VPN tunnel — Mac 連不到 `198.18.0.1`，所以 ICE 一樣卡 checking。iOS 狀態列右上的 VPN icon 也印證了。

**第一個建議**：先在 iPad **關掉 VPN**，再到同一個 hotspot 重試。這是 1 秒能測完的。

---

**2. 你「broadcast 自己被分配到的 LAN ip」的想法**

這個方向理論上對，但 **browser 在保護 LAN IP 不被網頁讀取**（fingerprinting 防護），所以 JS / WASM 沒有 API 能直接拿到真實 LAN IP。能讓 browser 透露真實 LAN IP 的方式只有兩個，都不理想：

- `getUserMedia` 拿到 camera/mic 權限 → browser 認為「使用者已經信任這個頁面」，停止 mDNS 混淆。但用「下棋」當理由要 camera 權限，使用者體驗很差（pitfall 文檔 Approach C 已標為 user-hostile）。
- 使用者自己手動輸入 IP → 完全可行但要使用者懂自己的 LAN IP 在哪。

**顯示在介面上**這部分我倒是可以做：把 SDP 裡的 candidate IPs 解析出來、顯示在 diag badge 旁邊，例如 `candidates: 2 (mDNS×1, srflx 220.196.193.17×1)`。你下次就能直接看到「噢我的 host candidate 是 198.18.0.1（VPN）」而不用對著紅字 SDP 慢慢找。**這個要做嗎？10 行內可以加。**

---

**3. 你問的架構問題：「依賴第三方 server 的話原本 wss:// 不是更簡潔？」**

**完全正確**。WebRTC + 手動 SDP 交換的唯一賣點是「兩台 device 真的能 P2P，不需要 server」。一旦這個前提不成立（router 擋、VPN 劫持、CGNAT），就要 fallback 到 signalling broker，而那個 broker 既然都已經是個 server 了，**根本就讓兩個 client 都直接連那個 server 講 wss 就好**，不用再多走一輪 WebRTC handshake。

所以 backlog 那個 Approach C（chess-net signalling endpoint）**不該做** — 如果你願意跑 chess-net server，使用者直接用 `/lobby` 進房就好，不需要 LAN host/join 那套 SDP 換手。WebRTC 模式的定位應該保持是「真 LAN P2P only」，遇到不行就直接引導去 `/lobby`。

---

**所以我建議的優先順序：**

1. **iPad 關 VPN**，回報 hotspot 是否成功
2. 如果還是失敗 → 在 LAN host 頁面加個 *「無法配對？改去 [Online lobby](/lobby) 跑伺服器版」* 引導連結，把現實接受了
3.（optional）我加上 candidate IP 解析顯示，讓 diag badge 變更有資訊量

要哪個？或都做？

---

2. Dynamic detection — when timeout fires and host candidate is in `198.18.0.0/15` (VPN tunnel) or `100.64.0.0/10` (CGNAT), show specific guidance instead of generic mDNS hint
3. Update `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md` so the next debug session knows VPN is a documented failure mode

Plan a quick check of where in `lan.rs` to insert the hint, then ship.

228	            if let Err(e) = room.attach_remote_player_dc(dc) {
229	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("attach joiner failed: {e:?}")));
230	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
231	                return;
232	            }
233	            *host_room_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(room);
234	            set_play_session.set(Some(session));
235	            set_status.set(HostStatus::Playing);
236	        });
237	    });
238	
239	    // ── Reset (full page reload) ──────────────────────────────
240	    let on_reset: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
241	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
242	            let _ = win.location().reload();
243	        }
244	    });

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```rs
//! Tiny pure-string helpers that look at a WebRTC SDP and classify the
//! host candidates by network range — so the LAN host page can surface
//! "your VPN is hijacking the LAN" instead of just "network blocked?"
//! when pairing times out.
//!
//! Lives outside `transport/webrtc.rs` so it's native-testable; the
//! actual `RtcPeerConnection` plumbing is wasm32-only but the SDP it
//! produces is just a string.

/// Extract the `connection-address` field from every `a=candidate:` line
/// in `sdp`. SDP `a=candidate` format (RFC 5245 §15):
///
/// \```text
/// a=candidate:<foundation> <component> <transport> <priority>
///             <connection-address> <port> typ <type> [...]
/// \```
///
/// Field 5 (0-indexed 4 after stripping `a=candidate:`) is the address
/// we want. mDNS hostnames look like `<uuid>.local`; srflx / relay
/// have real IPs; host candidates on a VPN may have tunnel addresses.
pub fn parse_candidate_addrs(sdp: &str) -> Vec<String> {
    sdp.lines()
        .filter_map(|line| {
            let rest = line.trim().strip_prefix("a=candidate:")?;
            let parts: Vec<&str> = rest.split_whitespace().collect();
            if parts.len() < 6 {
                return None;
            }
            Some(parts[4].to_string())
        })
        .collect()
}

/// `true` if `ip` is in `198.18.0.0/15` — RFC 2544 benchmark range,
/// also used by iOS as the fake LAN address when a VPN profile is
/// active (Cloudflare WARP, NordVPN, etc.). A WebRTC `typ host`
/// candidate landing in this range almost always means the OS is
/// routing LAN through a VPN tunnel.
pub fn is_vpn_tunnel_ip(ip: &str) -> bool {
    ip_in_prefix(ip, &[198, 18], 15)
}

/// `true` if `ip` is in `100.64.0.0/10` — RFC 6598 CGNAT range used
/// by mobile carriers and some ISPs for Carrier-Grade NAT. Direct P2P
/// often fails on CGNAT because the carrier's outer NAT doesn't
/// hairpin.
pub fn is_cgnat_ip(ip: &str) -> bool {
    let Some((100, rest)) = parse_v4_first(ip) else {
        return false;
    };
    (64..=127).contains(&rest)
}

/// Classify a parsed SDP's candidate addresses, picking the most-
/// specific hint we can give the user.
#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum NetDiag {
    /// Host candidate is in `198.18.0.0/15` — VPN is hijacking LAN.
    VpnTunnel,
    /// Host candidate is in `100.64.0.0/10` — ISP is using CGNAT.
    Cgnat,
    /// Nothing distinguishing; fall through to the generic hint.
    Plain,
}

/// Walk the candidate addresses and return the first specific class
/// detected, or `Plain` if none. VPN takes precedence over CGNAT
/// because they suggest different fixes (disable VPN vs. switch
/// network), and VPN's more common.
pub fn classify(addrs: &[String]) -> NetDiag {
    if addrs.iter().any(|a| is_vpn_tunnel_ip(a)) {
        return NetDiag::VpnTunnel;
    }
    if addrs.iter().any(|a| is_cgnat_ip(a)) {
        return NetDiag::Cgnat;
    }
    NetDiag::Plain
}

// --- private helpers ----------------------------------------------------

fn parse_v4_first(ip: &str) -> Option<(u8, u8)> {
    let mut parts = ip.split('.');
    let a = parts.next()?.parse().ok()?;
    let b = parts.next()?.parse().ok()?;
    Some((a, b))
}

/// Match `ip` against an IPv4 prefix expressed as `first_two_octets` +
/// prefix length in bits. Only used for the 198.18.0.0/15 check today,
/// where prefix=15 means the first octet matches exactly and the
/// second octet's high 7 bits match (so 198.18.x.x AND 198.19.x.x
/// qualify).
fn ip_in_prefix(ip: &str, first_two: &[u8; 2], prefix_bits: u32) -> bool {
    let Some((a, b)) = parse_v4_first(ip) else {
        return false;
    };
    if a != first_two[0] {
        return false;
    }
    // /15 means the second octet's high 7 bits must match. Compute the
    // mask: prefix 15 → 8 bits of first octet + 7 bits of second.
    let second_bits = prefix_bits.saturating_sub(8);
    if second_bits == 0 {
        return true; // /8 — first octet enough
    }
    let mask = 0xFFu8 << (8 - second_bits);
    (b & mask) == (first_two[1] & mask)
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn parse_extracts_address_field_from_candidate_line() {
        let sdp = "v=0\r\n\
                   a=candidate:1234 1 udp 2113937151 192.168.1.5 51234 typ host\r\n\
                   a=candidate:5678 1 udp 2113937151 abc-def.local 51235 typ host\r\n";
        assert_eq!(
            parse_candidate_addrs(sdp),
            vec!["192.168.1.5".to_string(), "abc-def.local".to_string()],
        );
    }

    #[test]
    fn parse_ignores_non_candidate_lines_and_short_candidates() {
        let sdp = "v=0\r\n\
                   a=group:BUNDLE 0\r\n\
                   a=candidate:short 1 udp\r\n";
        assert!(parse_candidate_addrs(sdp).is_empty());
    }

    #[test]
    fn vpn_tunnel_detects_198_18_and_198_19() {
        assert!(is_vpn_tunnel_ip("198.18.0.1"));
        assert!(is_vpn_tunnel_ip("198.18.255.255"));
        assert!(is_vpn_tunnel_ip("198.19.0.1"));
        assert!(is_vpn_tunnel_ip("198.19.255.255"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn vpn_tunnel_rejects_neighbours_and_unrelated() {
        assert!(!is_vpn_tunnel_ip("198.17.0.1"));
        assert!(!is_vpn_tunnel_ip("198.20.0.1"));
        assert!(!is_vpn_tunnel_ip("192.168.1.5"));
        assert!(!is_vpn_tunnel_ip("abc.local"));
        assert!(!is_vpn_tunnel_ip(""));
    }

    #[test]
    fn cgnat_detects_100_64_through_100_127() {
        assert!(is_cgnat_ip("100.64.0.1"));
        assert!(is_cgnat_ip("100.127.255.255"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn cgnat_rejects_100_63_and_100_128() {
        assert!(!is_cgnat_ip("100.63.255.255"));
        assert!(!is_cgnat_ip("100.128.0.1"));
        assert!(!is_cgnat_ip("100.0.0.1"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn classify_picks_vpn_over_cgnat_over_plain() {
        let vpn_then_cgnat = vec!["198.18.0.1".into(), "100.64.0.1".into()];
        assert_eq!(classify(&vpn_then_cgnat), NetDiag::VpnTunnel);

        let cgnat_only = vec!["100.64.0.1".into(), "abc.local".into()];
        assert_eq!(classify(&cgnat_only), NetDiag::Cgnat);

        let normal = vec!["192.168.1.5".into(), "abc.local".into()];
        assert_eq!(classify(&normal), NetDiag::Plain);

        assert_eq!(classify(&[]), NetDiag::Plain);
    }
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 07:36:19Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit VPN/STUN UX work

```bash
git add clients/chess-web/src/net_diag.rs clients/chess-web/src/lib.rs clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md .specstory/history/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(chess-web): VPN-tunnel / CGNAT detection + STUN explainer on /lan/host and /lan/join

User on the live GH Pages deploy hit two new failure modes after the
prior commit's diagnostics shipped:

1. Even on a personal hotspot the pairing failed because an iPad
   VPN client was hijacking LAN routing — iOS reports `198.18.0.1`
   (RFC 2544 Benchmark range) as the host candidate instead of the
   real WiFi IP, so the Mac can never reach the iPad. Disabling the
   VPN fixed it in <1 s.
2. The "Use STUN" checkbox label said "workaround for hostile
   networks" but didn't explain when STUN actually helps vs. when it
   silently makes things slower with no benefit.

Changes:

* New native-tested `clients/chess-web/src/net_diag.rs` (7 unit tests):
  parses `a=candidate:` lines from an SDP and classifies host IPs as
  `VpnTunnel` (`198.18.0.0/15`), `Cgnat` (`100.64.0.0/10`), or
  `Plain`. Pure string logic — wasm32 not required.
* `pages/lan.rs::timeout_hint_for(sdp)` swaps the trailing hint of the
  10-s DataChannel timeout error based on `classify(...)`:
  - VpnTunnel → "DISABLE the VPN on both devices..." + the
    split-tunnel CIDR list users with corporate VPNs need.
  - Cgnat → points at iPhone/Android hotspot (TURN would be the real
    fix but we don't ship a TURN server).
  - Plain → the existing mDNS-failing-router hint.
* Static `<p class="muted">` blocks below the iOS hint on both host
  and join pages — one for VPN (always relevant), one for STUN
  (explains srflx candidates, when to toggle on, when to leave off,
  why it can't rescue symmetric NAT / CGNAT).
* `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md` gains a "Sibling failure
  mode — VPN tunnel" section so future agents reading pitfalls find
  the iOS 198.18.x.x story without having to re-debug it. Cross-
  references `net_diag::classify` so the link doesn't rot if the
  module moves.

Out of scope explicitly noted in the STUN explainer: TURN relay
support. Users on CGNAT / symmetric NAT should fall back to running
chess-net and using /lobby instead — that's what the wss:// path is
for.

Verified: cargo check / clippy / test (93 pass, 7 new in net_diag) /
wasm32-unknown-unknown build all clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
```

```text
[main 994f39f] feat(chess-web): VPN-tunnel / CGNAT detection + STUN explainer on /lan/host and /lan/join
 5 files changed, 1462 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)
 create mode 100644 clients/chess-web/src/net_diag.rs
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 07:36:46Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit chat input fix

```bash
git add clients/chess-web/style.css && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(chess-web): chat input "Send" button cut off on iPad — set min-width:0 on the flexed input

User screenshot showed the chat textbox running to the panel's right
edge with "Send" half-clipped (only "Sen" visible).

Root cause: `<input>` defaults to `min-width: auto`, which in a flex
context refuses to shrink below the placeholder's intrinsic width
("Say something…" is ~115 px at the panel's font size). When the
sidebar column is narrow (iPad landscape with the board taking most
of the viewport), the flex container can't honour both the input's
auto-min-width AND the Send button's intrinsic width, so the button
gets pushed past the `.chat-panel`'s `overflow: hidden` clip.

Fix: `min-width: 0` on `.chat-input input` so it shrinks, plus
`flex-shrink: 0` on the paired `.btn` so Send keeps its full text.
The placeholder text now clips inside the input on tight widths, but
the Send affordance is always reachable — that's the right trade.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
```

```text
[main 35153cb] fix(chess-web): chat input "Send" button cut off on iPad — set min-width:0 on the flexed input
 1 file changed, 10 insertions(+)
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 07:37:13Z)**_

All four follow-ups landed.

**Summary of this session's work:**

- `994f39f` — VPN/CGNAT detection + STUN explainer:
  - New `net_diag` module parses host candidate IPs from SDP and classifies them (`198.18.0.0/15` = iOS-VPN tunnel, `100.64.0.0/10` = CGNAT). 7 native tests.
  - `/lan/host` 10-s timeout error now reads e.g. *"VPN tunnel detected (host candidate is in 198.18.0.0/15). DISABLE the VPN on both devices..."* with the split-tunnel CIDR list if you must keep VPN on (192.168/16, 10/8, 172.16/12, 172.20.10.0/28 iOS-hotspot, 224.0.0.0/4 mDNS).
  - Static "VPN hint" + "STUN hint" `<p class="muted">` blocks on both host and join pages. The STUN explainer covers what STUN does (srflx public-IP candidate), when it helps (mDNS broken / different networks), and when it can't (healthy LAN waste, symmetric NAT / CGNAT need TURN).
  - `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md` now has a "Sibling failure mode — VPN tunnel" section with the 2026-05-14 finding so future agents don't have to re-debug it.

- `35153cb` — iPad chat input fix:
  - `<input flex:1>` was inheriting `min-width: auto` and refusing to shrink below the placeholder's intrinsic width, pushing Send past the panel's overflow clip. Added `min-width: 0` on the input + `flex-shrink: 0` on the button.

Branch is now ahead of origin by 3 commits (`5056996`, `994f39f`, `35153cb`). Push when you're ready.

```text
     830 /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/docs/lan-multiplayer-webrtc.md
# LAN multiplayer over WebRTC — chess-web takeaway

This is the long-form companion to `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md` and
`pitfalls/webrtc-*.md` / `pitfalls/leptos-*.md` — the recipe you re-read
in a year when you want to add LAN multiplayer to ANOTHER game built on
the same `core / net / web` skeleton.

Architecture decisions, the build flow, every trap we hit, and a step-by-
step porting checklist for re-using this on a new game. If you only have
five minutes, read the [TL;DR](#tldr) and [Porting recipe](#porting-recipe).

---

<a id="tldr"></a>
## TL;DR

- **Two browsers on the same WiFi play a full multiplayer session over
  `RtcDataChannel` with NO server in the loop at runtime**. One browser
  acts as the "host" and runs the authoritative game state machine
  in-WASM; the other is the "joiner" that connects via WebRTC.
- **Wire protocol is unchanged from the existing chess-net WS path**.
  The host's in-WASM `Room` is the same code that the native chess-net
  server runs (`crates/chess-net/src/room.rs`). Pages
  (`pages/play.rs`, `pages/lobby.rs`) don't know which transport
  they're talking to — they consume `transport::Session { handle,
  incoming, state }` regardless.
- **Pairing is on-device manual SDP exchange** (offer + answer over
  textareas + Copy buttons; QR generation is the next-step polish, see
  [Future work](#future-work)).
- **Five distinct pitfalls were discovered + documented** during Phase
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 07:42:41Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Show doc structure

`grep -n "^##\|^###" /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-05-06-chinese-chess/docs/lan-multiplayer-webrtc.md | head -40`

```text
15:## TL;DR
43:## When to use this pattern (and when not)
81:## Architecture in one diagram
147:## Full handshake — what actually happens
152:### 1. Host page mount → user taps "Open room"
192:### 2. Joiner pastes offer → taps "Generate answer"
218:### 3. Host pastes answer → taps "Accept answer"
271:### 4. Both pages render the chess board
299:### 5. Move + chat sync

399:### What broke and how playwright-cli caught it
412:### Adding playwright-cli to your dev loop

434:### Network / WebRTC
448:### Leptos signal / reactivity
464:### WASM / stdlib
473:### Adjacent (not direct fixes, but informative)
490:## Porting recipe — adding LAN multiplayer to a NEW game
496:### Prerequisites
512:### Phase 0 — spike the network reachability (0.5–1 day, GATES EVERYTHING)
533:### Phase 1 — extract transport-agnostic Room
552:### Phase 2 — Transport trait + Session shape on the web side
563:### Phase 3 — WebRTC transport
586:### Phase 4 — HostRoom (multi-peer routing wrapper)
606:### Phase 5 — Pages
628:### Phase 6 — Testing + polish
645:## What NOT to do (anti-patterns we tried first)
650:### Don't: latched `WriteSignal<Option<ServerMsg>>` for the incoming queue
663:### Don't: `RwSignal<Vec<ServerMsg>>` queue with `set(Vec::new())` clear
679:### Don't: `create_effect` inside a `spawn_local` future
693:### Don't: send via `dc.send_with_str` immediately after `accept_answer().await`
710:### Don't: rely on `std::time::SystemTime` in code that compiles for wasm32
727:### Don't: tie native + wasm32 cargo deps together
741:### Don't: write `pages/lan.rs` from scratch
753:## Future work — what's not yet done
755:### Phase 5.5 — QR pairing — SHIPPED 2026-05-13
775:### Phase 6 — UI polish (still open)

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/lan-multiplayer-webrtc.md`

```text
429	## Pitfalls index
430	
431	Five distinct classes of bug, each with a dedicated `pitfalls/<slug>.md`.
432	Read these *before* writing similar code — at least the headlines.
433	
434	### Network / WebRTC
435	
436	1. **[`webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`](../pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md)**
437	   — Some routers (verified: Mi AX9000 firmware 1.0.168) silently
438	   drop WebRTC's `<uuid>.local` mDNS resolution while letting general
439	   mDNS through. STUN fallback works around it but needs reachable
440	   STUN servers.
441	
442	2. **[`webrtc-set-remote-description-resolves-before-dc-open.md`](../pitfalls/webrtc-set-remote-description-resolves-before-dc-open.md)**
443	   — `accept_answer().await` resolves on SDP application, BEFORE
444	   the SCTP handshake completes. Calling `dc.send_with_str(Hello)`
445	   immediately silently fails (`Err` is discarded by the sink wrapper).
446	   Fix: poll `dc.ready_state() == Open` before fanning out.
447	
448	### Leptos signal / reactivity
449	
450	3. **[`leptos-rwsignal-queue-self-clear-race.md`](../pitfalls/leptos-rwsignal-queue-self-clear-race.md)**
451	   — A `RwSignal<Vec<T>>` queue with `set(Vec::new())` at end of the
452	   drain effect has a race: any push between the drain start and the
453	   clear gets clobbered. Fix: separate the queue (`VecDeque` outside
454	   reactivity) from the notification (monotonic `u32` tick signal).
455	   See `transport::Incoming`.
456	
457	4. **[`leptos-create-effect-inside-spawn-local-silent-gc.md`](../pitfalls/leptos-create-effect-inside-spawn-local-silent-gc.md)**
458	   — `create_effect` inside a `spawn_local` future has no Leptos owner.
459	   The effect is silently GC'd at end of the current microtask;
460	   subsequent `state.set(...)` calls fire but no subscriber exists.
461	   Fix: hoist the effect to component scope; use a holder signal that
462	   the spawn_local future `set`s.
463	
464	### WASM / stdlib
465	
466	5. **[`wasm32-systemtime-now-panics.md`](../pitfalls/wasm32-systemtime-now-panics.md)**
467	   — `std::time::SystemTime::now()` panics on `wasm32-unknown-unknown`
468	   ("time not implemented on this platform"). The panic kills the
469	   call-handler chain, making the failure mode "an in-browser action
470	   silently does nothing". Audit any crate that compiles for both
471	   targets. Fix: cfg-gate to use `js_sys::Date::now()` on wasm32.
472	
473	### Adjacent (not direct fixes, but informative)
474	
475	- **[`ios-safari-svg-click-no-tap.md`](../pitfalls/ios-safari-svg-click-no-tap.md)**
476	  — Pre-existing pitfall about iOS Safari's pointer event model.
477	  Relevant if you wire taps to SVG `<g>` elements directly.
478	- **[`leptos-effect-tracking-stale-epoch.md`](../pitfalls/leptos-effect-tracking-stale-epoch.md)**
479	  — Adjacent Leptos reactivity gotcha; same code path style.
480	- **[`pwa-base-path-and-stale-cache.md`](../pitfalls/pwa-base-path-and-stale-cache.md)**
481	  — Important when adding new routes (`/lan/host`, `/lan/join`) to
482	  a PWA — the service worker precache list must include them.
483	
484	The first four are the ones you WILL re-trip on if you write a
485	similar feature without reading them.
486	
487	---
488	
489	<a id="porting-recipe"></a>
490	## Porting recipe — adding LAN multiplayer to a NEW game
491	
492	Step-by-step checklist for a future you / future agent. Assumes the
493	target game has the same `core / net / web` workspace shape (pure
494	state machine + serialisable wire types + Leptos web client).
495	
496	### Prerequisites
497	
498	- [ ] **Game state machine is already transport-agnostic.** Wire
499	      messages flow through a function with shape
500	      `apply(peer, ClientMsg) -> Vec<Outbound { peer, ServerMsg }>`.
501	      If your game's server-side code is currently entangled with
502	      tokio/axum, do this extraction FIRST as its own commit.
503	- [ ] **Wire protocol is wasm-clean.** No dependencies on
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
796	### Out of scope for v1 (and likely v2)
797	
798	- **Cross-LAN (TURN relay)** — would need a server. The whole point
799	  of v1 is "no server at runtime". If demand emerges, the original
800	  backlog has Approach C (chess-net `/signal/<token>` endpoint that
801	  forwards SDPs without knowing about chess) as a 50-line addition.
802	- **Three-player mesh for 三國暗棋** — needs full mesh of 3
803	  PeerConnections OR host-as-relay. Tracked separately; depends on
804	  the variant itself shipping first
805	  (`backlog/three-kingdoms-banqi.md`).
806	- **Reconnect after host tab refresh** — RTC peers see a fresh
807	  PeerConnection on reload, so they'd need to re-pair anyway. Not
808	  worth the persistence complexity for v1.
809	- **chess-tui as a WebRTC peer** — no JS runtime in the TUI. Either
810	  it stays online-only or we ship a tiny native signalling helper.
811	  Defer.
812	
813	---

821	  this feature plugs into.
822	- [`docs/pwa.md`](pwa.md) — install prereq + service worker (gets a
823	  "LAN play" section in Phase 6).
824	- [`pitfalls/README.md`](../pitfalls/README.md) — index of all

827	- ADR-0005 (`docs/adr/0005-multi-room-lobby.md`) and ADR-0006

 `./docs/lan-multiplayer-webrtc.md`

```text
43	## When to use this pattern (and when not)
44	
45	The fit-for-purpose checklist before copying this:
46	
47	| Constraint | This pattern fits | This pattern doesn't fit |
48	|---|---|---|
49	| Players physically together | ✅ same WiFi / hotspot | ❌ across continents (need TURN relay) |
50	| Server cost | ✅ zero (no chess-net needed at runtime) | n/a |
51	| Session length | ✅ minutes to hours (1 chess game) | ❌ multi-day persistence (host tab close = room ends) |
52	| Player count | ✅ 1v1 + ~4 spectators | ⚠️ 8+ peers needs mesh routing or full server |
53	| State authority | ✅ one peer is "host" + owns truth | ❌ truly decentralised consensus |
54	| Discovery | ⚠️ manual SDP exchange (or QR) | ❌ no auto-discovery without a relay |
55	| Reconnect | ❌ host tab close = game over | n/a |
56	| Network requirements | ✅ same LAN, mDNS works (most home WiFi) | ❌ AP-isolated networks (some routers, public WiFi) |
57	| Re-pair a saved peer | ❌ every session needs fresh SDP | n/a |
58	
59	**The killer feature**: zero external dependency at runtime. Once both
60	PWAs are installed (one HTTPS visit each), the game works in
61	airplane-mode-with-WiFi: train, classroom, park, anywhere with shared
62	WiFi or a hotspot.
63	
64	**The biggest UX cost**: every session starts with an SDP exchange
65	(~660 bytes JSON). Manual copy-paste is clunky; QR is much better but
66	still requires camera aiming. There's no "saved peer = instant
67	reconnect" — WebRTC SDPs are session-specific.
68	
69	**Same-LAN gotcha**: some routers (verified: Mi AX9000) silently drop
70	WebRTC's mDNS `<uuid>.local` resolution while letting general mDNS
71	through (Bonjour, AirPlay). See
72	[`pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`](../pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md).
73	The STUN-fallback toggle in the UI papers over this when present, but
74	needs reachable STUN servers (which the GFW blocks for
75	`stun.l.google.com` — we ship a multi-server list ordered for CN
76	reachability).
77	
78	---
79	
80	<a id="architecture-overview"></a>
81	## Architecture in one diagram
82	
83	```
84	                       ┌──────────────────────────┐
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 07:45:34Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit docs update

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs(lan-multiplayer-webrtc): record VPN + STUN + diagnostic takeaways from 2026-05-14 production debug

The original doc covered the mDNS-router-quirk failure mode and the
five Phase-3-5 pitfalls but predated the live-deploy debug session
that surfaced two more things worth writing down before they leave
the brain:

1. **VPN tunnel hijack is a distinct failure mode** from router mDNS
   blocking — same visible symptom (ICE: checking forever) but the
   fix is "disable VPN" / split-tunnel, not "switch network". An
   iPad with any VPN client active reports a `198.18.0.0/15` synthetic
   address as the host candidate; personal-hotspot fallback doesn't
   rescue it because the iPad's own hotspot also routes through the
   tunnel.

2. **CGNAT is the third distinct mode** — STUN srflx doesn't hairpin,
   needs TURN, which we don't ship. Surface "use `/lobby` instead"
   as the recovery path.

3. **The right response to "we time out" is diagnostics, not longer
   timeouts** — the runtime IceDiag badge + auto-classifying error
   message let the user (or future agent) tell the three failure
   modes apart at a glance, while raising the 10 s wait would just
   delay the same opaque failure.

4. **Approach C (chess-net signalling broker) is no longer "if demand
   emerges"** — actively deferred. The architectural argument: if you
   need a server anyway, `/lobby` (wss://) is simpler than wrapping
   a second WebRTC handshake on top. Recorded so a future "let's
   just add /signal" proposal can be rejected by reference.

Edits:
* "When to use this pattern" table gains explicit VPN + CGNAT rows.
* "Same-LAN gotcha" expands into three numbered failure modes with
  distinct fix paths, plus a STUN-explainer paragraph (when to
  toggle on, when it's pure overhead).
* New "Runtime diagnostics" section between "Testing loop" and
  "Pitfalls index" — documents the IceDiag badge, the candidate-IP
  ranges that map to specific failure modes, and the
  `net_diag::classify` flow.
* Pitfalls #1 entry notes the VPN sibling failure now lives in the
  same `webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md` file.
* "Out of scope" entry on TURN / signalling broker rewritten from
  "if demand emerges" → "deliberately not implementing because
  `/lobby` exists".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3 && git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD
```

```text
[main 0cb8433] docs(lan-multiplayer-webrtc): record VPN + STUN + diagnostic takeaways from 2026-05-14 production debug
 1 file changed, 138 insertions(+), 14 deletions(-)
0cb8433 docs(lan-multiplayer-webrtc): record VPN + STUN + diagnostic takeaways from 2026-05-14 production debug
```
</details></tool-use>

**Summary of `0cb8433` — what's now in `docs/lan-multiplayer-webrtc.md`:**

- "When to use this pattern" table — added rows for **VPN active on either device** (doesn't fit) and **CGNAT** (use `/lobby` instead).

Push `0cb8433` when ready.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 07:49:26Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit fix

```bash
git add clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(chess-web): LAN back-link respects base_path so GH Pages subpath deploys don't drop "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi"

`pages/lan.rs` passed raw `"/lan/host"` / `"/lan/join"` strings as
`back_link_override` to `PlayConnected`. The component uses the
override verbatim as the `<a href>` value (`pages/play.rs:713`),
unlike the default-lobby path which routes through `app_href(...)`
to pick up the deploy's base path. On GH Pages that meant the back
link rendered as `https://daviddwlee84.github.io/lan/host` — missing
the `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/` prefix, 404-redirecting to a root
GH Pages page.

Fix: route both LAN back-links through `routes::app_href`, matching
the convention every other `<a>` callsite in chess-web uses
(`sidebar.rs`, `local.rs`, `lobby.rs`, etc.). Server-root deploys
unaffected (`app_href` is a no-op when `base_path()` is empty).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3
```

```text
[main ea289aa] fix(chess-web): LAN back-link respects base_path so GH Pages subpath deploys don't drop "/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi"
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 07:49:37Z)**_

Fixed in `ea289aa` — both LAN back-links now route through `routes::app_href("/lan/host" | "/lan/join")` so the `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/` prefix gets baked in on the GH Pages build. Server-root deploys are unaffected (empty base path, `app_href` is a no-op).

Two commits unpushed now: `0cb8433` (docs takeaway) + `ea289aa` (this back-link fix).

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-14 07:50:53Z)**_

Added in `1e12719` — both `/lan/host` and `/lan/join` setup screens now show `← Back to picker` above the heading, matching the convention used by local / lobby / play / ws-setup. `rel="external"` forces a real navigation so the in-flight `RtcPeerConnection` + signalling state get torn down cleanly rather than leaking when the user bails out.
