_**User (2026-05-14 07:53:19Z)**_

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. **Primary Request and Intent**:
   The user reported issues on the live GitHub Pages deployment at `https://daviddwlee84.github.io/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/lan/host`:
   - **Issue 1**: LAN WebRTC pairing failed with "ERROR: DataChannel did not open within 10 s — pairing failed (network blocked?)" even with STUN enabled
   - **Issue 2**: QR scanner failed with `JsValue("jsQR script load error")` on iOS Safari
   
   Through iterative debugging, additional requests emerged:
   - After VPN-off fix worked: add VPN hint + IP-CIDR whitelist info to UI
   - Explain when/why STUN helps (user said "我現在也還搞不清楚")
   - Fix iPad chat input where "Send" button was cut off
   - Record takeaways into `docs/`
   - Fix LAN back-link URL missing `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/` base path on GH Pages
   - Add missing "back to picker" link on /lan/host and /lan/join setup screens

2. **Key Technical Concepts**:
   - Rust + WASM + Leptos web client (chess-web crate)
   - WebRTC ICE candidates (mDNS .local, srflx, host types)
   - `RtcPeerConnection`, `RtcDataChannel`, `RtcIceConnectionState`, `RtcPeerConnectionState`, `RtcIceGatheringState`
   - GitHub Pages subpath deployment via `--public-url /Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/`
   - Build-time env var `CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH` baked via `env!()` macro in `routes::base_path()`
   - mDNS multicast (UDP 5353), VPN tunnel addresses (RFC 2544 `198.18.0.0/15`), CGNAT (RFC 6598 `100.64.0.0/10`)
   - Trunk asset bundling with `data-trunk rel="copy-file"` directives
   - Leptos reactive signals (`ReadSignal`, `WriteSignal`, `create_effect`, holder pattern for spawn_local)
   - SPA routing with `<Router base=base_path()>` and `app_href` helper
   - Flexbox `min-width: 0` for shrinkable flex items
   - rustfmt CI requirement

3. **Files and Code Sections**:

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/qr_decode.rs`** (Issue 2 fix):
     Changed `format!("{origin}/jsQR.min.js")` to `format!("{origin}{base}/jsQR.min.js")` where `base = crate::routes::base_path()`. Updated misleading doc comment.

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs`** (ICE diagnostics):
     Added `IceDiag` struct with `Copy + Clone + Debug + PartialEq + Eq` containing `ice`/`conn`/`gather` web-sys enums + `candidates: u32`. Added `IceDiag::initial(pc: &RtcPeerConnection)`. Added `HostHandshake.ice_diag: ReadSignal<IceDiag>` + `current_offer()` method. Added `JoinerHandshake.ice_diag` + `current_answer()` method. New `install_ice_diag_handlers` function wires three event listeners with console logging. Refactored `wait_for_ice_complete` to accept `set_diag: WriteSignal<IceDiag>` and push gather-state updates.

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/net_diag.rs`** (NEW module, native-tested):
     ```rust
     pub fn parse_candidate_addrs(sdp: &str) -> Vec<String> { /* parse a=candidate: lines */ }
     pub fn is_vpn_tunnel_ip(ip: &str) -> bool { /* 198.18.0.0/15 */ }
     pub fn is_cgnat_ip(ip: &str) -> bool { /* 100.64.0.0/10 */ }
     pub enum NetDiag { VpnTunnel, Cgnat, Plain }
     pub fn classify(addrs: &[String]) -> NetDiag { /* VPN > CGNAT > Plain */ }
     ```
     7 unit tests covering parse + classification + range edges.

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/lib.rs`**: Added `pub mod net_diag;` at line ~12 (native-buildable, not gated).

   - **`clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs`**: 
     - Imports: `IceDiag`, `RtcIceConnectionState`, etc., `net_diag::{classify, parse_candidate_addrs, NetDiag}`, `routes::app_href`
     - Helper functions: `ice_label`, `conn_label`, `gather_label`, `diag_line(d: &IceDiag) -> String`, `timeout_hint_for(sdp: &str) -> &'static str`
     - Both host/join pages: `(ice_diag_holder, set_ice_diag_holder) = create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<IceDiag>>>(None)` + `create_effect` for SDP re-snapshot
     - Static `<p class="muted">` blocks: VPN hint + STUN explainer added below iOS hint
     - Inline ICE state badge under Status line
     - Timeout error uses `timeout_hint_for(sdp)`
     - **Latest commits**: 
       - `back_link_override=app_href("/lan/host")` and `app_href("/lan/join")` (was raw `.to_string()`)
       - `<a href=app_href("/") rel="external" class="back-link">"← Back to picker"</a>` added before `<h1>` on both pages

   - **`clients/chess-web/style.css`** (iPad chat fix):
     ```css
     .chat-input input {
         flex: 1;
         min-width: 0;  /* allow shrinking below intrinsic placeholder width */
         ...
     }
     .chat-input .btn {
         flex-shrink: 0;  /* keep Send button at full width */
     }
     ```

   - **`clients/chess-web/index.html`**: Updated comment about jsQR loader URL behavior.

   - **`pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`**: Added "Sibling failure mode — VPN tunnel on either device (confirmed 2026-05-14)" section between "What's actually happening" and "Workarounds", documenting iOS VPN hijacking LAN routing with 198.18.0.0/15 synthetic address. Includes split-tunnel CIDR list and cross-reference to `net_diag::classify`.

   - **`docs/lan-multiplayer-webrtc.md`**: 
     - Added VPN + CGNAT rows to "When to use this pattern" table
     - Expanded "Same-LAN gotcha" into three numbered failure modes (router mDNS / VPN tunnel / CGNAT) + STUN explainer paragraph
     - New "## Runtime diagnostics" section with tables for ICE state values and candidate IPs
     - Updated Pitfalls #1 entry to note VPN sibling
     - Updated "Out of scope" entry to mark TURN/signalling-broker as deliberately NOT implementing (architectural decision: "if you need a server, use chess-net /lobby")

4. **Errors and fixes**:
   - **CI fmt failure** (run 25845379894): Fixed by running `cargo fmt --all` and committing as `5056996` "style(chess-web): cargo fmt fixup for ICE diag commit"
   - **SPA asset paths broken**: Initial `make build-web-static WEB_BASE=Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi` (no leading slash) produced relative asset paths that broke on subroutes. Fixed by using `WEB_BASE=/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi` (matching CI workflow which uses `/${{ github.event.repository.name }}`)
   - **Playwright camera mock didn't trigger has_camera()**: Worked around by directly verifying URL pattern via `fetch()` from page context, confirming `/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi/jsQR.min.js` → 200 vs `/jsQR.min.js` → 404
   - **User's STUN test still failed on hostile network**: Confirmed STUN srflx doesn't bypass mDNS quirk on Xiaomi AX9000-class routers; kept STUN default OFF
   - **VPN was the real culprit on hotspot**: User screenshot showed `198.18.0.1` in SDP and VPN icon in iOS status bar; turning off VPN fixed pairing in <1 second
   - **iPad chat input overflow**: Send button was clipped due to default `min-width: auto` on flex input refusing to shrink below placeholder width; fixed with `min-width: 0` + `flex-shrink: 0`
   - **LAN back-link missing base path**: `back_link_override` accepted raw string used verbatim in `<a href>`; default lobby path used `app_href` but override didn't. Fixed at call sites.

5. **Problem Solving**:
   - Diagnosed three distinct WebRTC LAN failure modes (router mDNS / VPN tunnel / CGNAT) and built classifier
   - Made architectural decision: deliberately don't implement Approach C (chess-net signalling broker) since `/lobby` (wss://) is simpler if a server is needed
   - Architectural insight from user: "依賴一個第三方server的話應該原本的wss://方案更簡潔" — recorded in docs

8. **Current Work**:
   Immediately before the summary request, I completed commit `1e12719` — "fix(chess-web): add 'Back to picker' link on /lan/host and /lan/join setup screens" in response to user message "lan/host & join 也缺返回鍵".

```text
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Code Map: LAN Game State Initialization & Variant Threading

**Initialization Sites (where GameState/RuleSet is currently hardcoded):**

1. **`clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs:258`** — The host-side GameState creation:
   ```rust
   let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(RuleSet::xiangqi(), None, /* hints */ false);
   ```
   This is the critical line where `Variant::Xiangqi` is hardcoded. The host constructs this after the DataChannel opens but BEFORE the joiner connects. The host immediately seats itself and queues `Hello` + `ChatHistory` into the incoming signal.

2. **`crates/chess-net/src/room.rs:156-166`** — The `Room::new` factory:
   ```rust
   pub fn new(rules: RuleSet, password: Option<String>, hints_allowed: bool) -> Self {
       Self {
           state: GameState::new(rules),
           ...
       }
   }
   ```
   Takes a `RuleSet` (which contains the variant enum and per-variant flags like `xiangqi_allow_self_check`, `banqi_seed`, etc.) and calls `GameState::new(rules)` to build the initial board.

3. **`crates/chess-core/src/setup.rs:15-21`** — The `build_initial_state` dispatcher:
   ```rust
   pub fn build_initial_state(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
       match rules.variant {
           Variant::Xiangqi => build_xiangqi(rules),
           Variant::Banqi => build_banqi(rules),
           Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => build_three_kingdom_stub(rules),
       }
   }
   ```
   Banqi shuffles via `ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(rules.banqi_seed.unwrap_or_else(|| rand::thread_rng().next_u64()))` — the seed is part of `RuleSet` and determines the layout deterministically.

---

**Where Variant/Rules Must Be Threaded:**

1. **Host setup page (`LanHostPage`, lines 119–440):** Currently has UI for "Open room" + offer/answer flow but NO variant picker. Must add a form field (dropdown or buttons) for variant + per-variant rule options (xiangqi strict, banqi house flags + optional seed). Store selection in a signal (e.g., `variant_signal`, `rules_params_signal`) and pass to `HostRoom::new()` at line 258.

2. **Join page (`LanJoinPage`, lines 441–717):** Currently receives the offer SDP and generates an answer. The joiner currently has NO visibility into what variant the host chose — it's not communicated until after the DataChannel opens. Must display the variant + rules after DC opens, read from `ServerMsg::Hello` (which already carries `RuleSet`).

3. **WebRTC handshake (`clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs`):** The data channel setup (`install_dc_handlers_for_joiner` / `install_dc_state_handlers`) is variant-agnostic — it just shuttles JSON. No changes needed here; the variant info flows through the chess-net protocol message (`ServerMsg::Hello`).

4. **Routes layer (`clients/chess-web/src/app.rs:46-47`):** Currently routes to `/lan/host` and `/lan/join` with no query params. To support variant picker on the host page, either:
   - Add `?variant=xiangqi|banqi|three-kingdom` + rule params to the route (would need parsing in host page), OR
   - Keep the variant choice in-page (simpler; the host never leaves `/lan/host` until pairing succeeds).

---

**Protocol Envelope (Data Channel Payloads):**

- **Type:** `ServerMsg` and `ClientMsg` enums from `crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs`.
- **Variant already in the envelope:** `ServerMsg::Hello` carries:
  ```rust
  Hello {
      protocol: u16,
      observer: Side,
      rules: RuleSet,  // ← includes variant enum + all per-variant flags
      view: PlayerView,
      hints_allowed: bool,
  }
  ```
  The `rules: RuleSet` field includes `Variant` (enum) + variant-specific fields (`xiangqi_allow_self_check`, `banqi_seed`, `house` flags, etc.). **No new message variant needed** — the protocol already carries everything.

- **Joiner receives variant implicitly:** After the DC opens, the joiner gets `ServerMsg::Hello` from the host and deserializes the full `RuleSet`. The joiner's `Room` is never directly instantiated — it only receives `ServerMsg` updates from the host, so it mirrors the host's board state exactly (including banqi's hidden-tile layout, since the host already shuffled deterministically from the seed).

---

**Banqi Seed Handling (Critical for Correctness):**

- **Shuffle happens on the host only:** `build_banqi()` (line 81–91 of setup.rs) uses `rules.banqi_seed` to initialize a `ChaCha8Rng`, shuffles the 32-piece set, and places them face-down on the board.
- **Seed is part of `RuleSet`:** If the host chooses `RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(house_flags, seed_u64)`, both the host and joiner will have the same seed in their `RuleSet.banqi_seed: Option<u64>` field.
- **Joiner does NOT reconstruct:** The joiner receives no direct board state from the host initially — only the `ServerMsg::Hello` with `rules: RuleSet` (which includes the seed). But the joiner's client-side `Room` never calls `GameState::new()` for banqi. Instead, the joiner only receives `ServerMsg::Update` messages containing `PlayerView` (the host's board projected from each player's viewpoint, with hidden pieces opaque). Crucially, **both sides agree on the hidden layout because the host sent the seed in the `Hello` RuleSet`**, so if the joiner ever needed to reconstruct (e.g., for a local replay), it could call `GameState::new(rules)` with that same seed and get the same layout.
- **Current flow is correct:** The host is the single source of truth; the joiner mirrors its state via `Update` projections. No explicit seed-sharing message is needed — it's already in `Hello.rules.banqi_seed`.

---

**LAN Route Shape:**

- **Current routes:** `/lan/host` (Leptos route, no query params) and `/lan/join` (same).
- **No variant param today:** The routes are hardcoded in `clients/chess-web/src/app.rs:46-47`. If you add variant selection to the host page form (in-page signals), no route changes are needed. If you want the variant in the URL for deep-linking or shareable setup URIs, you'd add a query string parser (similar to the `parse_local_rules` in `routes.rs` for the `/local/:variant` pages) and handle it in `LanHostPage`.

---

**Summary — Extension Points:**

To extend LAN mode to all variants, modify **two sites only:**

1. **Host page form (line 119):** Add variant picker + rule options; store in signals; pass to `HostRoom::new(rules, ...)` instead of hardcoded `RuleSet::xiangqi()`.
2. **Join page display (line 441):** After DC opens and `ServerMsg::Hello` arrives, extract and display `rules` from the message so the joiner sees what they're playing.

The protocol and banqi seed handling are already correct — `ServerMsg::Hello` carries the full `RuleSet` with variant and all flags baked in. No new message types needed.
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me generate the report:

## Inventory of Reusable Components & Logic

**Reusable Pure-Logic Functions in `routes.rs`:**

1. **`parse_local_rules(query_fn) → LocalRulesParams`** (lines 224-271)
   - Parses URL query string (`strict=`, `house=`, `preset=`, `seed=`, `mode=`, etc.) into a typed struct
   - Fully pure; takes closure for flexible query-string sourcing
   - Returns `LocalRulesParams` — a shareable intermediate struct with all rule + AI config

2. **`build_local_query(variant, params) → String`** (lines 276-362)
   - Inverse encoder: `LocalRulesParams` → canonical query string
   - Variant-aware (Xiangqi vs Banqi vs ThreeKingdom have different rules)
   - Pure logic; no state

3. **`build_rule_set(variant, params) → RuleSet`** (lines 378-393)
   - Converts `LocalRulesParams` directly to engine `RuleSet`
   - **Critical**: This is cleanly separable — you can call it directly from a LAN form without URL roundtripping
   - Handles seed-based banqi initialization and Xiangqi casual/strict branching

**Key Intermediate Type:**
- **`LocalRulesParams`** (lines 106-181) — a standalone struct with 13 fields covering all rule choices
  - Contains variant-agnostic flags (`strict`, `house`, `seed`, `mode`) and AI overrides (`ai_difficulty`, `ai_depth`, etc.)
  - **Shareable**: No signals, pure data; can be instantiated directly from UI form signals without URL parsing

---

**UI Structure in `picker.rs`:**

The picker is **NOT a reusable component**. It's heavily inline:
- **XiangqiCard** (lines 158-607): 450-line component with 15+ local signals; builds its own href via `build_local_href()`
- **BanqiCard** (lines 610-712): 100-line variant with 5 signals for house rules + seed
  - Variant + preset selector is **pure UI** — hardcoded buttons ("Purist", "Taiwan", "Aggressive") calling `apply_preset(PRESET_CONSTANT)` closures

**No Extracted Component**: Variant dropdown, rules checkboxes, and preset buttons live inline as JSX. No `VariantSelector` or `RulesForm` component exists.

---

**Display String Helpers:**

There are **no label-generation helpers** in the codebase. The picker uses:
- **Hardcoded strings**: "Taiwan", "Aggressive", "Purist" (lines 658–662)
- **Enum `.as_str()` methods**: `Difficulty::as_str()`, `Strategy::as_str()` (routes.rs lines 289, 294)
- **HouseRules → CSV tokens**: Checked programmatically via bitflags; no reverse lookup from flag → display name exists

---

**State Types for Sharing:**

1. **`LocalRulesParams`** — the sole shareable struct (pure data, no signals)
   - Covers: variant rules, house rule bitflags, seed, play mode, AI config (difficulty, depth, budget, strategy, variation)
   - **Recommendation**: Use directly to drive both picker AND LAN form signals

2. **Signals are NOT shareable** — picker owns 15+ `create_rw_signal()` instances per card
   - To embed rules in LAN setup, clone the **pattern not the signals**: create fresh `RwSignal`s in LAN form, collect them into `LocalRulesParams` on apply

---

**Smallest Extraction for Reuse:**

If you want to avoid duplication:
1. **Extract a `components/rules_form.rs` component** taking:
   - `variant: Signal<Variant>`
   - `on_apply: Callback<LocalRulesParams>`
   - Returns JSX for variant + house rules + seed inputs

2. **Embed in both picker and LAN host**:
   - Picker: collect form into `LocalRulesParams` → call `build_local_href()` to get URL
   - LAN host: collect form into `LocalRulesParams` → call `build_rule_set()` to get `RuleSet` directly (no URL roundtrip)

3. **Share nothing else** — each page owns its own signals and control flow

---

**Critical Separability:**

- `parse_local_rules()` + `build_rule_set()` are **100% decoupled from URLs**
- Call `build_rule_set(Variant::Xiangqi, &params)` directly; the LAN page never needs to generate a URL
- This is the clean cut: picker goes `form → LocalRulesParams → URL → game`, LAN goes `form → LocalRulesParams → RuleSet → HostRoom`
```

1	//! `/lan/host` and `/lan/join` routes — LAN multiplayer over WebRTC.
2	//!
3	//! Phase 5 of `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`. Pairs two browsers
4	//! on the same WiFi via the Phase 3 `transport::webrtc` plumbing
5	//! and the Phase 4 `host_room::HostRoom` authority, then drops the
6	//! resulting `Session` into the existing `pages/play::PlayConnected`
7	//! so the actual game UI is unchanged from chess-net mode.
8	//!
9	//! No QR / camera scanning yet — Phase 5 v1 uses textareas + the
10	//! browser clipboard for the offer/answer exchange. QR is
11	//! deferred (Phase 5.5 if requested).
12	
13	// === module skeleton — section bodies filled in via edits below ===
14	
15	// SECTION: imports
16	use std::cell::RefCell;
17	use std::rc::Rc;
18	
19	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
20	use leptos::*;
21	use wasm_bindgen::JsCast;
22	use wasm_bindgen_futures::spawn_local;
23	use web_sys::HtmlTextAreaElement;
24	
25	use crate::camera::has_camera;
26	use crate::components::qr::QrCodeView;
27	use crate::components::qr_scanner::QrScanner;
28	use crate::config::WsBase;
29	use crate::host_room::HostRoom;
30	use crate::net_diag::{classify, parse_candidate_addrs, NetDiag};
31	use crate::pages::play::PlayConnected;
32	use crate::routes::app_href;
33	use crate::transport::webrtc::{
34	    connect_as_host, connect_as_joiner, wait_for_dc_open, AnswerBlob, HostHandshake, IceDiag,
35	    IceMode, JoinerHandshake, OfferBlob, WebRtcConfig,
36	};
37	use crate::transport::{ConnState, Session};
38	use web_sys::{RtcIceConnectionState, RtcIceGatheringState, RtcPeerConnectionState};
39	
40	// SECTION: ICE diag rendering helpers
41	
42	fn ice_label(s: RtcIceConnectionState) -> &'static str {
43	    match s {
44	        RtcIceConnectionState::New => "new",
45	        RtcIceConnectionState::Checking => "checking",
46	        RtcIceConnectionState::Connected => "connected",
47	        RtcIceConnectionState::Completed => "completed",
48	        RtcIceConnectionState::Failed => "failed",
49	        RtcIceConnectionState::Disconnected => "disconnected",
50	        RtcIceConnectionState::Closed => "closed",
51	        _ => "?",
52	    }
53	}
54	
55	fn conn_label(s: RtcPeerConnectionState) -> &'static str {
56	    match s {
57	        RtcPeerConnectionState::New => "new",
58	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Connecting => "connecting",
59	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Connected => "connected",
60	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Disconnected => "disconnected",
61	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Failed => "failed",
62	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Closed => "closed",
63	        _ => "?",
64	    }
65	}
66	
67	fn gather_label(s: RtcIceGatheringState) -> &'static str {
68	    match s {
69	        RtcIceGatheringState::New => "new",
70	        RtcIceGatheringState::Gathering => "gathering",
71	        RtcIceGatheringState::Complete => "complete",
72	        _ => "?",
73	    }
74	}
75	
76	/// Format an [`IceDiag`] snapshot as a single line for the inline badge.
77	fn diag_line(d: &IceDiag) -> String {
78	    format!(
79	        "ICE: {} · connection: {} · gathering: {} · candidates: {}",
80	        ice_label(d.ice),
81	        conn_label(d.conn),
82	        gather_label(d.gather),
83	        d.candidates,
84	    )
85	}
86	
87	/// Hint string appended to the 10-s DC-open timeout error, varied by
88	/// what we can infer from the host's own SDP candidates. Surfacing
89	/// "your VPN is hijacking the LAN" instead of the generic mDNS hint
90	/// when the host candidate IP betrays a VPN tunnel saves the user
91	/// from blaming the wrong layer.
92	fn timeout_hint_for(sdp: &str) -> &'static str {
93	    match classify(&parse_candidate_addrs(sdp)) {
94	        NetDiag::VpnTunnel => {
95	            "VPN tunnel detected (host candidate is in 198.18.0.0/15). \
96	             DISABLE the VPN on both devices and retry — VPNs route LAN \
97	             traffic through the tunnel and replace your real LAN IP \
98	             with a fake address. If the VPN must stay on, configure \
99	             split-tunnel to bypass 192.168.0.0/16, 10.0.0.0/8, \
100	             172.16.0.0/12, 172.20.10.0/28 (iOS hotspot), and \
101	             224.0.0.0/4 (multicast / mDNS)."
102	        }
103	        NetDiag::Cgnat => {
104	            "CGNAT detected (host candidate is in 100.64.0.0/10). \
105	             Your ISP / carrier is using Carrier-Grade NAT, which often \
106	             blocks direct P2P even with STUN. Try an iPhone/Android \
107	             personal hotspot instead."
108	        }
109	        NetDiag::Plain => {
110	            "Common fixes: switch both devices to an iPhone/Android \
111	             personal hotspot (this network's WebRTC mDNS resolution \
112	             may be failing), or enable \"Use STUN\"."
113	        }
114	    }
115	}
116	// SECTION: LanHostPage component
117	
118	#[component]
119	pub fn LanHostPage() -> impl IntoView {
120	    // ── Page state ────────────────────────────────────────────
121	    // We progress through:
122	    //   Idle → Generating → AwaitingAnswer → AcceptingAnswer →
123	    //   WaitingForChannel → Playing
124	    // Each transition mutates the corresponding signals; the view
125	    // renders accordingly.
126	    let (status, set_status) = create_signal::<HostStatus>(HostStatus::Idle);
127	    let (use_stun, set_use_stun) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
128	    let (offer_blob, set_offer_blob) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
129	    let (answer_input, set_answer_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
130	    let (error_msg, set_error_msg) = create_signal::<Option<String>>(None);
131	
132	    // QR scanner modal state. `cam_available` is set asynchronously
133	    // on mount; default false to keep the Scan-camera button hidden
134	    // until detection completes (no flash-of-button-then-disappear
135	    // for cameraless devices).
136	    let (scanner_open, set_scanner_open) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
137	    let (cam_available, set_cam_available) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
138	    spawn_local(async move {
139	        if has_camera().await {
140	            set_cam_available.set(true);
141	        }
142	    });
143	
144	    // Keep the in-flight HostHandshake alive in the page; closures
145	    // and accept_answer need a stable reference.
146	    let handshake: Rc<RefCell<Option<HostHandshake>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
147	    // The HostRoom + its Session aren't constructed until the
148	    // DataChannel actually opens; on `state == Open` we instantiate
149	    // both and flip status to `Playing`.
150	    let host_room: Rc<RefCell<Option<Rc<HostRoom>>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
```
</details></tool-use>

51	#[component]
52	fn DisplaySettings() -> impl IntoView {
53	    let prefs = expect_context::<Prefs>();
54	    let fx_confetti = prefs.fx_confetti;
55	    let fx_check_banner = prefs.fx_check_banner;
56	    let fx_threat_mode = prefs.fx_threat_mode;
57	    let fx_threat_hover = prefs.fx_threat_hover;
58	    let fx_last_move = prefs.fx_last_move;
59	    // The `<select>` reports value-strings; we map back to ThreatMode
60	    // via `ThreatMode::from_str` (unknown → `Off` default — keeps the
61	    // UI graceful if a future option is added).
62	    let on_mode_change = move |ev: leptos::ev::Event| {
63	        let s = event_target_value(&ev);
64	        fx_threat_mode.set(ThreatMode::from_str(&s));
65	    };
66	    view! {
67	        <details class="picker-settings">
68	            <summary>"⚙ Display settings"</summary>
69	            <div class="fx-toggles">
70	                <label>
71	                    <input
72	                        type="checkbox"
73	                        prop:checked=move || fx_confetti.get()
74	                        on:change=move |ev| fx_confetti.set(event_target_checked(&ev))
75	                    />
76	                    <span>"Victory effects (confetti + banner)"</span>
77	                </label>
78	                <label>
79	                    <input
80	                        type="checkbox"
81	                        prop:checked=move || fx_check_banner.get()
82	                        on:change=move |ev| fx_check_banner.set(event_target_checked(&ev))
83	                    />
84	                    <span>"將軍 / CHECK warning"</span>
85	                </label>
86	                // Threat highlight mode selector. Default `Off` (the
87	                // feature is opt-in per user feedback 2026-05-12 — a
88	                // quiet board out-of-the-box, with `NetLoss` ('被捉')
89	                // labeled as the recommended option for users who flip
90	                // it on). The user-visible labels are bilingual so
91	                // first-time viewers can guess the semantic without
92	                // diving into docs; the option values are the
93	                // lowercase camelCase that `ThreatMode::as_str` writes
94	                // to localStorage.
95	                <label class="threat-mode-row">
96	                    <span>"威脅提示 / Threat highlight:"</span>
97	                    <select
98	                        class="threat-mode-select"
99	                        on:change=on_mode_change
100	                    >
101	                        // `prop:selected` on each option (rather than
102	                        // `prop:value` on the <select>) is the
103	                        // reliable way to bind a <select>'s current
104	                        // choice to a signal in leptos. With
105	                        // `prop:value` the browser sometimes ignores
106	                        // the initial value and shows the first option
107	                        // instead — visually "Off" while the underlying
108	                        // signal is still the recommended default
109	                        // (NetLoss). That mismatch caused the user to
110	                        // believe Threat=Off had been applied while
111	                        // the game still rendered NetLoss rings.
112	                        // See pitfalls/leptos-select-prop-value-vs-prop-selected.md.
113	                        <option
114	                            value="off"
115	                            prop:selected=move || fx_threat_mode.get() == ThreatMode::Off
116	                        >"關閉 / Off"</option>
117	                        <option
118	                            value="attacked"
119	                            prop:selected=move || fx_threat_mode.get() == ThreatMode::Attacked
120	                        >"被攻擊 / Attacked (busy)"</option>
121	                        <option
122	                            value="netLoss"
123	                            prop:selected=move || fx_threat_mode.get() == ThreatMode::NetLoss
124	                        >"被捉 / Net loss (recommended)"</option>
125	                        <option
126	                            value="mateThreat"
127	                            prop:selected=move || fx_threat_mode.get() == ThreatMode::MateThreat
128	                        >"叫殺 / Mate threat"</option>
129	                    </select>
130	                </label>
131	                <label>
132	                    <input
133	                        type="checkbox"
134	                        prop:checked=move || fx_threat_hover.get()
135	                        on:change=move |ev| fx_threat_hover.set(event_target_checked(&ev))
136	                    />
137	                    <span>"Hover preview — show what an opponent could capture if my hovered/selected piece doesn't move"</span>
138	                </label>
139	                // "Highlight latest move" — rings the from/to of the
140	                // most recent move so the user sees what the opponent
141	                // just played without having to scrutinise the board
142	                // diff. Default ON: only ever rings two squares at a
143	                // time so it's low-noise even when always on.
144	                <label>
145	                    <input
146	                        type="checkbox"
147	                        prop:checked=move || fx_last_move.get()
148	                        on:change=move |ev| fx_last_move.set(event_target_checked(&ev))
149	                    />
150	                    <span>"標注最近一手 / Highlight latest move"</span>
151	                </label>
152	            </div>
153	        </details>
154	    }
155	}
156	
157	#[component]
158	fn XiangqiCard() -> impl IntoView {
159	    let strict = create_rw_signal(false);
160	    let mode = create_rw_signal(PlayMode::Pvp);
161	    let player_red = create_rw_signal(true);
162	    // Difficulty signal stores the selected preset (Easy/Normal/Hard).
163	    // When `is_custom` is true, this signal is ignored for depth
164	    // resolution but still feeds the randomness fallback (since
165	    // `Difficulty::default_randomness()` provides per-level defaults
166	    // and there's no neutral "custom" randomness option). We pin the
167	    // underlying value to `Hard` while in Custom mode so the fallback
168	    // is `Randomness::SUBTLE` — power-user appropriate. Users wanting
169	    // a different mix override via the Variation fieldset.
170	    let difficulty = create_rw_signal(Difficulty::Normal);
171	    // Custom is rendered as a 4th radio in the Difficulty fieldset
172	    // — click reveals the Search-depth + Node-budget inputs below
173	    // (they're conditionally mounted via <Show when=is_custom>).
174	    let is_custom = create_rw_signal(false);
175	    let ai_strategy = create_rw_signal(Strategy::default());
176	    // None = "use difficulty default"; Some(_) = explicit override.
177	    // Encoded into the URL via `&variation=`.
178	    let ai_variation = create_rw_signal::<Option<Randomness>>(None);
179	    // Empty = use difficulty default depth; numeric = override (clamped
180	    // 1..=MAX_AI_DEPTH on parse). ONLY emitted to the URL when
181	    // is_custom is true — preset difficulties always defer to
182	    // `Difficulty::default_depth()`.
183	    let ai_depth_text = create_rw_signal(String::new());
184	    // Empty = use the engine's auto-scaled budget (v5:
185	    // node_budget_for_depth(target); v1-v4: flat NODE_BUDGET).
186	    // Numeric = explicit override clamped to MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET. Only
187	    // emitted when is_custom is true.
188	    let ai_budget_text = create_rw_signal(String::new());
189	    // Two independent advanced toggles — see the corresponding fields
190	    // on `LocalRulesParams` for the semantic split:
191	    //   - ai_show_hints → URL `hints=1` → in-game 🧠 toggle button,
192	    //     default hidden, runs analyze from the human's POV when
193	    //     opened (player-friendly).
194	    //   - ai_show_debug → URL `debug=1` → sticky always-on panel
195	    //     showing the AI's POV after each AI move (power-user).
196	    // Both can be enabled together; `pages/local.rs` mounts a single
197	    // `<DebugPanel>` and lets either source feed it.
198	    let ai_show_hints = create_rw_signal(false);
199	    let ai_show_debug = create_rw_signal(false);
200	    // Win-rate display: separate URL flag (`?evalbar=1`). Independent
201	    // of hints/debug — works in both vs-Computer and Pass-and-play
202	    // (samples come from the same hint pump that powers `?hints=1`,
203	    // which is unconditionally enabled when either flag is set).
204	    let ai_show_evalbar = create_rw_signal(false);
205	    // Pass-and-play only: rotate Black piece glyphs 180° so the player
206	    // sitting on the opposite side of the device reads their own pieces
207	    // upright. Coordinate system unchanged.
208	    let mirror_black = create_rw_signal(false);
209	    let href = move || {
210	        let ai_side = if player_red.get() { Side::BLACK } else { Side::RED };
211	        // Custom mode: parse depth + budget; preset modes: leave both None
212	        // so the engine defers to Difficulty::default_depth() and the
213	        // engine-internal node-budget policy.
214	        let (ai_depth, ai_node_budget, effective_difficulty) = if is_custom.get() {
215	            let depth = ai_depth_text
216	                .with(|s| s.trim().parse::<u8>().ok().map(|d| d.clamp(1, MAX_AI_DEPTH)));
217	            let budget = ai_budget_text
218	                .with(|s| s.trim().parse::<u32>().ok().map(|b| b.clamp(1, MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET)));
219	            // Custom rides on top of `Difficulty::Hard` so the
220	            // randomness fallback (when Variation = Default) lands on
221	            // `Randomness::SUBTLE` — the power-user-appropriate
222	            // default. See the comment on `difficulty` above.
223	            (depth, budget, Difficulty::Hard)
224	        } else {
225	            (None, None, difficulty.get())
226	        };
227	        let params = LocalRulesParams {
228	            strict: strict.get(),
229	            mode: mode.get(),
230	            ai_side,
231	            ai_difficulty: effective_difficulty,
232	            ai_strategy: ai_strategy.get(),
233	            ai_variation: ai_variation.get(),
234	            ai_depth,
235	            ai_node_budget,
236	            ai_debug: ai_show_debug.get(),
237	            ai_hints: ai_show_hints.get(),
238	            ai_evalbar: ai_show_evalbar.get(),
239	            mirror: mirror_black.get(),
240	            ..Default::default()
241	        };
242	        app_href(&build_local_href(Variant::Xiangqi, &params))
243	    };
244	    view! {
245	        <div class="variant-card variant-card--form">
246	            <h2>"Xiangqi 象棋"</h2>
247	            <p>"Standard 9×10 Chinese chess."</p>
248	            <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
249	                <legend>"Mode"</legend>
250	                <label class="radio-row">
251	                    <input
252	                        type="radio"
253	                        name="xiangqi-mode"
254	                        prop:checked=move || mode.get() == PlayMode::Pvp
255	                        on:change=move |_| mode.set(PlayMode::Pvp)
256	                    />
257	                    <span>"Pass-and-play — two humans share this device."</span>
258	                </label>
259	                <label class="radio-row">
260	                    <input
261	                        type="radio"
262	                        name="xiangqi-mode"
263	                        prop:checked=move || mode.get() == PlayMode::VsAi
264	                        on:change=move |_| mode.set(PlayMode::VsAi)
265	                    />
266	                    <span>"vs Computer — alpha-beta engine, runs entirely in your browser."</span>
267	                </label>
268	            </fieldset>
269	            <Show when=move || mode.get() == PlayMode::Pvp>
270	                <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
271	                    <legend>"Seat layout"</legend>
272	                    <label class="check-row">
273	                        <input
274	                            type="checkbox"
275	                            prop:checked=move || mirror_black.get()
276	                            on:change=move |ev| mirror_black.set(event_target_checked(&ev))
277	                        />
278	                        <span>"鏡像黑方 — Mirror Black's pieces 180° for a player sitting opposite (phone flat on a table). Captured pieces split to each side's edge."</span>
279	                    </label>
280	                </fieldset>
281	            </Show>
282	            <Show when=move || mode.get() == PlayMode::VsAi>
283	                <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
284	                    <legend>"You play"</legend>
285	                    <label class="radio-row">
286	                        <input
287	                            type="radio"
288	                            name="xiangqi-player-side"
289	                            prop:checked=move || player_red.get()
290	                            on:change=move |_| player_red.set(true)
291	                        />
292	                        <span>"紅 Red — moves first."</span>
293	                    </label>
294	                    <label class="radio-row">
295	                        <input
296	                            type="radio"
297	                            name="xiangqi-player-side"
298	                            prop:checked=move || !player_red.get()
299	                            on:change=move |_| player_red.set(false)
300	                        />
301	                        <span>"黑 Black — AI moves first."</span>
302	                    </label>
303	                </fieldset>
304	                <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
305	                    <legend>"Difficulty"</legend>
306	                    <label class="radio-row">
307	                        <input
308	                            type="radio"
309	                            name="xiangqi-difficulty"
310	                            prop:checked=move || !is_custom.get() && difficulty.get() == Difficulty::Easy
311	                            on:change=move |_| { is_custom.set(false); difficulty.set(Difficulty::Easy); }
312	                        />
313	                        <span>"Easy — depth 1, picks at random from the top three replies."</span>
314	                    </label>
315	                    <label class="radio-row">
316	                        <input
317	                            type="radio"
318	                            name="xiangqi-difficulty"
319	                            prop:checked=move || !is_custom.get() && difficulty.get() == Difficulty::Normal
320	                            on:change=move |_| { is_custom.set(false); difficulty.set(Difficulty::Normal); }
321	                        />
322	                        <span>"Normal — depth 3, mostly plays the best line."</span>
323	                    </label>
324	                    <label class="radio-row">
325	                        <input
326	                            type="radio"
327	                            name="xiangqi-difficulty"
328	                            prop:checked=move || !is_custom.get() && difficulty.get() == Difficulty::Hard
329	                            on:change=move |_| { is_custom.set(false); difficulty.set(Difficulty::Hard); }
330	                        />
331	                        <span>"Hard — depth 4, may take a couple of seconds per move."</span>
332	                    </label>
333	                    // Custom is the 4th radio in the same Difficulty
334	                    // group — selecting it reveals depth + node-budget
335	                    // inputs below. Internally this rides on top of
336	                    // Hard so the Variation fieldset's "Default" still
337	                    // gets a sensible randomness fallback (SUBTLE).
338	                    <label class="radio-row">
339	                        <input
340	                            type="radio"
341	                            name="xiangqi-difficulty"
342	                            prop:checked=move || is_custom.get()
343	                            on:change=move |_| is_custom.set(true)
344	                        />
345	                        <span>"Custom (advanced) — set search depth and node budget directly."</span>
346	                    </label>
347	                    <Show when=move || is_custom.get()>
348	                        <div class="custom-difficulty-inputs">
349	                            <p class="hint">
350	                                "Search depth = how many plies the engine looks ahead. "
351	                                "Node budget = the search bails when this many positions have been visited (lower = faster but shallower realized depth). "
352	                                "Leave both blank to use the engine's depth-scaled defaults; the AI Debug panel reports both reached and target depth."
353	                            </p>
354	                            <label class="custom-input-row">
355	                                <span class="custom-input-label">
356	                                    "Search depth (max " {MAX_AI_DEPTH.to_string()} ")"
357	                                </span>
358	                                <input
359	                                    type="number"
360	                                    inputmode="numeric"
361	                                    min="1"
362	                                    max=MAX_AI_DEPTH.to_string()
363	                                    placeholder="auto (Hard = 4)"
364	                                    class="text-input depth-input"
365	                                    prop:value=move || ai_depth_text.get()
366	                                    on:input=move |ev| ai_depth_text.set(event_target_value(&ev))
367	                                />
368	                            </label>
369	                            <label class="custom-input-row">
370	                                <span class="custom-input-label">
371	                                    "Node budget (max " {MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET.to_string()} ")"
372	                                </span>
373	                                <input
374	                                    type="number"
375	                                    inputmode="numeric"
376	                                    min="1"
377	                                    max=MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET.to_string()
378	                                    placeholder="auto (depth-scaled)"
379	                                    class="text-input depth-input"
380	                                    prop:value=move || ai_budget_text.get()
381	                                    on:input=move |ev| ai_budget_text.set(event_target_value(&ev))
382	                                />
383	                            </label>
384	                            // Variation lives inline here as well, so
385	                            // Custom users get all three preset-equivalent
386	                            // knobs in one place. The standalone
387	                            // "Variation" fieldset below hides while
388	                            // Custom is active (the same `ai_variation`
389	                            // signal feeds both — choices persist when
390	                            // toggling between Custom and a preset).
391	                            <fieldset class="custom-variation">
392	                                <legend>"Variation"</legend>
393	                                <label class="radio-row">
394	                                    <input
395	                                        type="radio"
396	                                        name="xiangqi-variation-custom"
397	                                        prop:checked=move || ai_variation.get().is_none()
398	                                        on:change=move |_| ai_variation.set(None)
399	                                    />
400	                                    <span>"Default — Hard's preset (Subtle, top-3 within ±20 cp)."</span>
401	                                </label>
402	                                <label class="radio-row">
403	                                    <input
404	                                        type="radio"

608	
609	#[component]
610	fn BanqiCard() -> impl IntoView {
611	    let chain = create_rw_signal(false);
612	    let dark = create_rw_signal(false);
613	    let dark_trade = create_rw_signal(false);
614	    let rush = create_rw_signal(false);
615	    let horse = create_rw_signal(false);
616	    let seed_text = create_rw_signal(String::new());
617	
618	    let house = move || {
619	        let mut h = HouseRules::empty();
620	        if chain.get() {
621	            h.insert(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE);
622	        }
623	        if dark.get() {
624	            h.insert(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE);
625	        }
626	        if dark_trade.get() {
627	            h.insert(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE);
628	        }
629	        if rush.get() {
630	            h.insert(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH);
631	        }
632	        if horse.get() {
633	            h.insert(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL);
634	        }
635	        h
636	    };
637	    let apply_preset = move |preset: HouseRules| {
638	        chain.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
639	        dark.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
640	        dark_trade.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE));
641	        rush.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
642	        horse.set(preset.contains(HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL));
643	    };
644	    let href = move || {
645	        let seed = seed_text.with(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok());
646	        let params = LocalRulesParams { house: house(), seed, ..Default::default() };
647	        app_href(&build_local_href(Variant::Banqi, &params))
648	    };
649	
650	    view! {
651	        <div class="variant-card variant-card--form">
652	            <h2>"Banqi 暗棋"</h2>
653	            <p>"Hidden-piece variant on a 4×8 board. Whoever flips first plays that color."</p>
654	            <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
655	                <legend>"Preset"</legend>
656	                <div class="preset-row">
657	                    <button class="btn btn-ghost" type="button"
658	                        on:click=move |_| apply_preset(PRESET_PURIST)>"Purist"</button>
659	                    <button class="btn btn-ghost" type="button"
660	                        on:click=move |_| apply_preset(PRESET_TAIWAN)>"Taiwan"</button>
661	                    <button class="btn btn-ghost" type="button"
662	                        on:click=move |_| apply_preset(PRESET_AGGRESSIVE)>"Aggressive"</button>
663	                </div>
664	            </fieldset>
665	            <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
666	                <legend>"House rules"</legend>
667	                <label class="check-row">
668	                    <input type="checkbox" prop:checked=move || chain.get()
669	                        on:change=move |ev| chain.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
670	                    <span>"連吃 — chain captures along a line"</span>
671	                </label>
672	                <label class="check-row">
673	                    <input type="checkbox" prop:checked=move || dark.get()
674	                        on:change=move |ev| dark.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
675	                    <span>"暗吃 — atomic reveal+capture; on rank-fail your piece stays put (probe)"</span>
676	                </label>
677	                <label class="check-row">
678	                    <input type="checkbox" prop:checked=move || dark_trade.get()
679	                        on:change=move |ev| dark_trade.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
680	                    <span>"暗吃·搏命 — on rank-fail your attacker dies (implies 暗吃)"</span>
681	                </label>
682	                <label class="check-row">
683	                    <input type="checkbox" prop:checked=move || rush.get()
684	                        on:change=move |ev| rush.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
685	                    <span>"車衝 — chariot rays the full board; with a gap, captures any piece"</span>
686	                </label>
687	                <label class="check-row">
688	                    <input type="checkbox" prop:checked=move || horse.get()
689	                        on:change=move |ev| horse.set(event_target_checked(&ev))/>
690	                    <span>"馬斜 — horse adds diagonal one-step moves; diagonal captures any piece"</span>
691	                </label>
692	                <p class="hint">
693	                    "炮快移 is accepted by the engine but not yet wired into move-gen "
694	                    "(see "<code>"TODO.md"</code>")."
695	                </p>
696	            </fieldset>
697	            <fieldset class="card-fieldset">
698	                <legend>"Seed (optional)"</legend>
699	                <input
700	                    type="text"
701	                    inputmode="numeric"
702	                    placeholder="leave blank for random"
703	                    class="text-input"
704	                    prop:value=move || seed_text.get()
705	                    on:input=move |ev| seed_text.set(event_target_value(&ev))
706	                />
707	                <p class="hint">"Same seed = same shuffle, useful for puzzles or rematches."</p>
708	            </fieldset>
709	            <a href=href rel="external" class="btn btn-primary card-cta">"Start Banqi →"</a>
710	        </div>
711	    }
712	}
713

719	            <p>"3-player banqi. Engine still WIP — board renders but moves are gated."</p>
