```text
1	//! Variant-slug parsing for `/local/:variant` plus URL-query rule encoding.
2	//!
3	//! Pure logic — native-testable. The picker page builds a query string from
4	//! its form state via [`build_local_query`]; the local page parses it back
5	//! into a [`RuleSet`] via [`parse_local_rules`] + [`build_rule_set`].
6	
7	use chess_ai::{Difficulty, Randomness, Strategy};
8	use chess_core::piece::Side;
9	use chess_core::rules::{
10	    house, HouseRules, RuleSet, Variant, PRESET_AGGRESSIVE, PRESET_PURIST, PRESET_TAIWAN,
11	};
12	
13	/// Local-page play mode. `Pvp` is the default pass-and-play; `VsAi`
14	/// drives the in-process alpha-beta engine (xiangqi only).
15	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Default)]
16	pub enum PlayMode {
17	    #[default]
18	    Pvp,
19	    VsAi,
20	}
21	
22	pub fn hosting_mode() -> &'static str {
23	    env!("CHESS_WEB_HOSTING")
24	}
25	
26	pub fn is_static_hosting() -> bool {
27	    hosting_mode() == "static"
28	}
29	
30	pub fn base_path() -> &'static str {
31	    env!("CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH")
32	}
33	
34	pub fn router_base() -> Option<&'static str> {
35	    let base = base_path();
36	    if base.is_empty() {
37	        None
38	    } else {
39	        Some(base)
40	    }
41	}
42	
43	pub fn app_href(path: &str) -> String {
44	    app_href_with_base(base_path(), path)
45	}
46	
47	pub fn app_href_with_base(base: &str, path: &str) -> String {
48	    let path = if path.is_empty() { "/" } else { path };
49	    let path = if path.starts_with('/') { path.to_string() } else { format!("/{path}") };
50	    let base = normalize_base_path(base);
51	    if base.is_empty() {
52	        path
53	    } else if path == "/" {
54	        format!("{base}/")
55	    } else {
56	        format!("{base}{path}")
57	    }
58	}
59	
60	fn normalize_base_path(base: &str) -> String {
61	    let trimmed = base.trim().trim_matches('/');
62	    if trimmed.is_empty() {
63	        String::new()
64	    } else {
65	        format!("/{trimmed}")
66	    }
67	}
68	
69	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
70	pub enum WsBaseError {
71	    Empty,
72	    BadScheme,
73	}
74	
75	pub fn normalize_ws_base(raw: &str) -> Result<String, WsBaseError> {
76	    let trimmed = raw.trim().trim_end_matches('/');
77	    if trimmed.is_empty() {
78	        return Err(WsBaseError::Empty);
79	    }
80	    if !(trimmed.starts_with("ws://") || trimmed.starts_with("wss://")) {
81	        return Err(WsBaseError::BadScheme);
82	    }
83	    Ok(trimmed.to_string())
84	}
85	
86	pub fn parse_variant_slug(slug: &str) -> Option<Variant> {
87	    match slug {
88	        "xiangqi" => Some(Variant::Xiangqi),
89	        "banqi" => Some(Variant::Banqi),
90	        "three-kingdom" => Some(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi),
91	        _ => None,
92	    }
93	}
94	
95	pub fn variant_slug(variant: Variant) -> &'static str {
96	    match variant {
97	        Variant::Xiangqi => "xiangqi",
98	        Variant::Banqi => "banqi",
99	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "three-kingdom",
100	    }
101	}
102	
103	/// URL-derived rule choices for `/local/:variant`. Used as an intermediate
104	/// between the picker form and the engine `RuleSet`.
105	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
106	pub struct LocalRulesParams {
107	    /// Xiangqi only. `false` (default) = casual / no self-check filter;
108	    /// `true` = standard rules where leaving your general in check is illegal.
109	    pub strict: bool,
110	    /// Banqi only. Bitflag set; `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` auto-implies
111	    /// `DARK_CAPTURE` after [`house::normalize`] in the parser.
112	    pub house: HouseRules,
113	    /// Banqi only. `None` = engine picks (non-deterministic on native;
114	    /// browser uses `getrandom` JS feature).
115	    pub seed: Option<u64>,
116	    /// Xiangqi only (banqi/three-kingdom ignore). Default `Pvp`.
117	    pub mode: PlayMode,
118	    /// `VsAi` only — which side the AI plays. Default Black (player is Red,
119	    /// the traditional first-mover). Ignored when `mode == Pvp`.
120	    pub ai_side: Side,
121	    /// `VsAi` only — search difficulty. Ignored when `mode == Pvp`.
122	    pub ai_difficulty: Difficulty,
123	    /// `VsAi` only — engine version. Defaults to [`Strategy::default`]
124	    /// (v2 since 2026-05-08). Versioned, switchable, non-overwriting:
125	    /// older strategies stay reachable via `?engine=v1`.
126	    pub ai_strategy: Strategy,
127	    /// `VsAi` only — randomness preset. `None` = use the difficulty
128	    /// default (`Difficulty::default_randomness`); `Some(_)` = explicit
129	    /// override (typically a [`Randomness`] preset like
130	    /// [`Randomness::STRICT`]). URL token: `&variation=strict|subtle|varied|chaotic`.
131	    pub ai_variation: Option<Randomness>,
132	    /// `VsAi` only — search depth override. `None` = use
133	    /// `Difficulty::default_depth` (Easy=1, Normal=3, Hard=4); `Some(N)`
134	    /// = explicit. Capped at `MAX_AI_DEPTH` to keep the worst-case
135	    /// browser response under ~10 s. URL token: `&depth=N`.
136	    pub ai_depth: Option<u8>,
137	    /// `VsAi` only — per-search node-count cap override. `None` = use
138	    /// the engine's default (v5 scales with depth via
139	    /// `chess_ai::search::node_budget_for_depth`; v1-v4 use the flat
140	    /// `NODE_BUDGET = 250_000`). `Some(N)` forces the cap regardless
141	    /// of strategy or depth. Capped at [`MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET`] so a
142	    /// typo'd 9-figure value can't lock the browser tab. URL token:
143	    /// `&budget=N`.
144	    pub ai_node_budget: Option<u32>,
145	    /// `VsAi` only — opens the AI debug panel showing the engine's full
146	    /// scored root-move list, hover-to-highlight on the board, and
147	    /// search metadata (depth, nodes, strategy, randomness). URL token:
148	    /// `&debug=1`. Default off (consumes ~no extra search cost — the
149	    /// scored list is computed regardless; `&debug=1` just exposes it).
150	    pub ai_debug: bool,
151	    /// User-facing alias for [`Self::ai_debug`]: opens the same AI
152	    /// hint / debug panel via the friendlier URL token `&hints=1`.
153	    /// Local mode treats `ai_debug || ai_hints` as the trigger to
154	    /// mount the panel — both flags are functionally equivalent
155	    /// offline (e.g. on the GitHub Pages build). In net mode (`/play/`)
156	    /// neither flag is enough on its own — the server's
157	    /// `hints_allowed` (set at room creation by the first joiner's
158	    /// `?hints=1`) gates the panel for fairness; see `pages/play.rs`.
159	    pub ai_hints: bool,
160	    /// Enables the live AI win-rate display: vertical eval bar attached
161	    /// to the right edge of the board, sidebar `紅 % • 黑 %` badge,
162	    /// per-ply samples cached for the post-game trend chart. Works in
163	    /// both vs-AI and pass-and-play (PvP).
164	    ///
165	    /// Reuses the `chess_ai::analyze` calls the AI move pump and hint
166	    /// pump already make — when this flag is on, the hint pump runs
167	    /// every turn (even without `?hints=1`) so each ply gets sampled.
168	    /// Costs ~100-300 ms WASM per turn at default Hard depth, on top
169	    /// of any existing AI/hint search work.
170	    ///
171	    /// URL token: `&evalbar=1`. Default off.
172	    /// Net mode (`/play/`) currently ignores this flag — see TODO.md
173	    /// "chess-net protocol v6" for the spectator-side broadcast follow-up.
174	    pub ai_evalbar: bool,
175	    /// Xiangqi pass-and-play only. When `true`, Black's piece glyphs are
176	    /// rendered rotated 180° so a player sitting on the opposite side of
177	    /// the device reads their pieces upright. Coordinate system is
178	    /// unchanged. Ignored when `mode == VsAi`, banqi, or three-kingdom.
179	    /// URL token: `&mirror=1`.
180	    pub mirror: bool,
181	}
182	
183	/// Hard cap on user-supplied depth. Picked so that browser WASM v3 Hard
184	/// at depth 8 (~5 s) is the worst tolerable response time. Higher than
185	/// 8 essentially demands iterative deepening + time budget — that's the
186	/// v5 roadmap entry, not a knob to twist.
187	pub const MAX_AI_DEPTH: u8 = 10;
188	
189	/// Hard cap on user-supplied node budget. Picked so the worst-case v5
190	/// search at MAX_AI_DEPTH stays under ~30 s on a typical 2024 laptop
191	/// running WASM (the engine's own auto-scaled cap is 16M, so 64M
192	/// gives the user 4× headroom for "go really deep on a big machine"
193	/// scenarios but still bounds the worst case). The picker's number
194	/// input clamps to this value at parse time.
195	pub const MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET: u32 = 64_000_000;
196	
197	impl Default for LocalRulesParams {
198	    fn default() -> Self {
199	        Self {
200	            strict: false,
201	            house: HouseRules::empty(),
202	            seed: None,
203	            mode: PlayMode::Pvp,
204	            ai_side: Side::BLACK,
205	            ai_difficulty: Difficulty::Normal,
206	            ai_strategy: Strategy::default(),
207	            ai_variation: None,
208	            ai_depth: None,
209	            ai_node_budget: None,
210	            ai_debug: false,
211	            ai_hints: false,
212	            ai_evalbar: false,
213	            mirror: false,
214	        }
215	    }
216	}
217	
218	/// Parse `?strict=1&house=chain,rush&preset=taiwan&seed=42` into a
219	/// [`LocalRulesParams`]. Unknown tokens are silently dropped — this is a
220	/// best-effort decoder for shareable URLs.
221	///
222	/// `house=` overrides `preset=` when both are present; the picker only
223	/// emits `house=` so this just covers manually-typed URLs.
224	pub fn parse_local_rules(get: impl Fn(&str) -> Option<String>) -> LocalRulesParams {
225	    let strict = matches!(get("strict").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true"));
226	    let preset = get("preset").as_deref().and_then(parse_preset_token);
227	    let house_csv = get("house").as_deref().map(parse_house_csv);
228	    let house = house_csv.or(preset).unwrap_or_else(HouseRules::empty);
229	    let seed = get("seed").and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());
230	    let mode = match get("mode").as_deref() {
231	        Some("ai") | Some("vsai") => PlayMode::VsAi,
232	        _ => PlayMode::Pvp,
233	    };
234	    let ai_side = match get("ai").as_deref() {
235	        Some("red") => Side::RED,
236	        _ => Side::BLACK,
237	    };
238	    let ai_difficulty =
239	        get("diff").as_deref().and_then(Difficulty::parse).unwrap_or(Difficulty::Normal);
240	    let ai_strategy = get("engine").as_deref().and_then(Strategy::parse).unwrap_or_default();
241	    let ai_variation = get("variation").as_deref().and_then(Randomness::parse);
242	    let ai_depth = get("depth")
243	        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())
244	        .map(|d| d.clamp(1, MAX_AI_DEPTH as u32) as u8);
245	    let ai_node_budget = get("budget").and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok()).map(|b| {
246	        // Parse as u64 so a typo'd 11-digit value clamps to the cap
247	        // rather than failing to parse and silently falling back to
248	        // None (`u32::parse` overflows above ~4.29B).
249	        b.clamp(1, MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET as u64) as u32
250	    });
251	    let ai_debug = matches!(get("debug").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
252	    let ai_hints = matches!(get("hints").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
253	    let ai_evalbar = matches!(get("evalbar").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
254	    let mirror = matches!(get("mirror").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
255	    LocalRulesParams {
256	        strict,
257	        house: house::normalize(house),
258	        seed,
259	        mode,
260	        ai_side,
261	        ai_difficulty,
262	        ai_strategy,
263	        ai_variation,
264	        ai_depth,
265	        ai_node_budget,
266	        ai_debug,
267	        ai_hints,
268	        ai_evalbar,
269	        mirror,
270	    }
271	}
272	
273	/// Inverse of [`parse_local_rules`] — emits a stable canonical query string
274	/// (no leading `?`). Empty result means "use defaults" — the picker drops the
275	/// `?` entirely so `/local/xiangqi` with no query is the default-casual URL.
276	pub fn build_local_query(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
277	    let mut parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
278	    match variant {
279	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
280	            if params.strict {
281	                parts.push("strict=1".to_string());
282	            }
283	            if params.mode == PlayMode::VsAi {
284	                parts.push("mode=ai".to_string());
285	                parts.push(format!(
286	                    "ai={}",
287	                    if params.ai_side == Side::RED { "red" } else { "black" }
288	                ));
289	                parts.push(format!("diff={}", params.ai_difficulty.as_str()));
290	                // Only emit engine= when the user picked a non-default
291	                // strategy. Keeps the canonical short URL unchanged for
292	                // the recommended setup.
293	                if params.ai_strategy != Strategy::default() {
294	                    parts.push(format!("engine={}", params.ai_strategy.as_str()));
295	                }
296	                // Only emit variation= when the user explicitly overrode
297	                // the difficulty default (and the override is one of the
298	                // known presets — custom Randomness values can't be
299	                // round-tripped through the URL).
300	                if let Some(name) = params.ai_variation.and_then(|r| r.preset_name()) {
301	                    parts.push(format!("variation={}", name));
302	                }
303	                // Only emit depth= when the user overrode the difficulty's
304	                // default. Allows the canonical short URL for the
305	                // recommended setup to stay short.
306	                if let Some(d) = params.ai_depth {
307	                    parts.push(format!("depth={}", d));
308	                }
309	                // Only emit budget= when the user explicitly overrode
310	                // the engine's default node-count cap. Keeps the
311	                // canonical "I'm using a preset difficulty" URL short.
312	                if let Some(b) = params.ai_node_budget {
313	                    parts.push(format!("budget={}", b));
314	                }
315	                // Debug panel toggle (vs-AI only — debug shows the AI's
316	                // own POV cached after each AI move; meaningless in PvP
317	                // where there's no AI move pump to fill the cache).
318	                if params.ai_debug {
319	                    parts.push("debug=1".to_string());
320	                }
321	            }
322	            // Hint mode toggle is emitted REGARDLESS of mode — hints
323	            // work in both vs-AI (analysis from human's POV when it's
324	            // their turn) AND pass-and-play (analysis from current
325	            // side-to-move's POV — both humans can ask the bot for
326	            // advice on every move). If we only emitted hints=1 inside
327	            // the VsAi block above, the picker's PvP-mode + checked
328	            // hints box would silently drop the flag and the player
329	            // would see no panel after clicking Start.
330	            if params.ai_hints {
331	                parts.push("hints=1".to_string());
332	            }
333	            // Win-rate display flag — same reason as hints=1 above:
334	            // works in both vs-AI and PvP, so emitted unconditionally
335	            // (when set). The local mode pages then thread it through
336	            // the same hint pump that powers `?hints=1`.
337	            if params.ai_evalbar {
338	                parts.push("evalbar=1".to_string());
339	            }
340	            // Mirror is pass-and-play only — vs-AI has no opponent on
341	            // the far side of the device, so it would just confuse the
342	            // single human. We still allow `?mirror=1` to round-trip
343	            // when set even with mode=ai (so URL hand-edits don't get
344	            // silently dropped on parse), but the picker only offers
345	            // the checkbox when mode == Pvp.
346	            if params.mirror && params.mode == PlayMode::Pvp {
347	                parts.push("mirror=1".to_string());
348	            }
349	        }
350	        Variant::Banqi => {
351	            let csv = house_csv(params.house);
352	            if !csv.is_empty() {
353	                parts.push(format!("house={}", csv));
354	            }
355	            if let Some(seed) = params.seed {
356	                parts.push(format!("seed={}", seed));
357	            }
358	        }
359	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {}
360	    }
361	    parts.join("&")
362	}
363	
364	/// Build the full path for the picker's "Start" link given the current form.
365	/// Always returns `"/local/<slug>"` with the query string appended only when
366	/// non-empty.
367	pub fn build_local_href(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
368	    let q = build_local_query(variant, params);
369	    if q.is_empty() {
370	        format!("/local/{}", variant_slug(variant))
371	    } else {
372	        format!("/local/{}?{}", variant_slug(variant), q)
373	    }
374	}
375	
376	/// Convert parsed [`LocalRulesParams`] into the engine `RuleSet` for the
377	/// chosen variant. The picker → URL → page flow is the only call site.
378	pub fn build_rule_set(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> RuleSet {
379	    match variant {
380	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
381	            if params.strict {
382	                RuleSet::xiangqi()
383	            } else {
384	                RuleSet::xiangqi_casual()
385	            }
386	        }
387	        Variant::Banqi => match params.seed {
388	            Some(seed) => RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(params.house, seed),
389	            None => RuleSet::banqi(params.house),
390	        },
391	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => RuleSet::three_kingdom(),
392	    }
393	}
394	
395	fn parse_house_csv(s: &str) -> HouseRules {
396	    let mut out = HouseRules::empty();
397	    for tok in s.split(',').map(str::trim).filter(|t| !t.is_empty()) {
398	        if let Some(flag) = parse_house_token(tok) {
399	            out.insert(flag);
400	        }
401	    }
402	    out
403	}
404	
405	fn house_csv(rules: HouseRules) -> String {
406	    let mut parts: Vec<&'static str> = Vec::new();
407	    for (flag, tok) in HOUSE_TOKENS {
408	        if rules.contains(*flag) {
409	            parts.push(tok);
410	        }
411	    }
412	    parts.join(",")
413	}
414	
415	fn parse_house_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
416	    HOUSE_TOKENS
417	        .iter()
418	        .chain(HOUSE_TOKEN_ALIASES.iter())
419	        .find(|(_, t)| t.eq_ignore_ascii_case(tok))
420	        .map(|(f, _)| *f)
421	}
422	
423	fn parse_preset_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
424	    match tok.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
425	        "purist" => Some(PRESET_PURIST),
426	        "taiwan" => Some(PRESET_TAIWAN),
427	        "aggressive" => Some(PRESET_AGGRESSIVE),
428	        _ => None,
429	    }
430	}
431	
432	const HOUSE_TOKENS: &[(HouseRules, &str)] = &[
433	    (HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, "chain"),
434	    (HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, "dark"),
435	    (HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH, "rush"),
436	    (HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, "horse"),
437	    (HouseRules::CANNON_FAST_MOVE, "cannon"),
438	    (HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE, "dark-trade"),
439	];
440	
441	/// Aliases recognised on parse only (older URLs / pre-rename snapshots).
442	/// Encoding uses the canonical `HOUSE_TOKENS` only.
443	const HOUSE_TOKEN_ALIASES: &[(HouseRules, &str)] = &[(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, "dark-chain")];
444	
445	#[cfg(test)]
446	mod tests {
447	    use super::*;
448	
449	    fn from_pairs<'a>(pairs: &'a [(&'a str, &'a str)]) -> impl Fn(&str) -> Option<String> + 'a {
450	        move |k: &str| pairs.iter().find(|(pk, _)| *pk == k).map(|(_, v)| v.to_string())
451	    }
452	
453	    #[test]
454	    fn known_slugs_parse() {
455	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("xiangqi"), Some(Variant::Xiangqi)));
456	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("banqi"), Some(Variant::Banqi)));
457	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("three-kingdom"), Some(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi)));
458	    }
459	
460	    #[test]
461	    fn unknown_slug_returns_none() {
462	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("chess").is_none());
463	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("").is_none());
464	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("XIANGQI").is_none());
465	    }
466	
467	    #[test]
468	    fn slug_round_trip() {
469	        for v in [Variant::Xiangqi, Variant::Banqi, Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi] {
470	            assert_eq!(parse_variant_slug(variant_slug(v)), Some(v));
471	        }
472	    }
473	
474	    #[test]
475	    fn xiangqi_default_is_casual() {
476	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[]));
477	        assert!(!p.strict);
478	        let r = build_rule_set(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
479	        assert!(r.xiangqi_allow_self_check);
480	    }
481	
482	    #[test]
483	    fn xiangqi_strict_query_yields_strict_ruleset() {
484	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("strict", "1")]));
485	        assert!(p.strict);
486	        let r = build_rule_set(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
487	        assert!(!r.xiangqi_allow_self_check);
488	    }
489	
490	    #[test]
491	    fn banqi_house_csv_round_trips() {
492	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "chain,rush"), ("seed", "42")]));
493	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
494	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
495	        assert_eq!(p.seed, Some(42));
496	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Banqi, &p);
497	        assert!(q.contains("house=chain,rush"));
498	        assert!(q.contains("seed=42"));
499	    }
500	
501	    #[test]
502	    fn banqi_dark_capture_token_parses() {
503	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "dark")]));
504	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
505	    }
506	
507	    #[test]
508	    fn banqi_dark_chain_alias_still_parses_to_dark_capture() {
509	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "dark-chain")]));
510	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
511	    }
512	
513	    #[test]
514	    fn banqi_dark_trade_implies_dark_capture() {
515	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "dark-trade")]));
516	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE));
517	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
518	    }
519	
520	    #[test]
521	    fn banqi_preset_taiwan_picks_chain_and_rush() {
522	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("preset", "taiwan")]));
523	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
524	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
525	    }
526	
527	    #[test]
528	    fn banqi_house_overrides_preset_when_both_set() {
529	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("preset", "taiwan"), ("house", "")]));
530	        // Empty house= still wins → no flags.
531	        assert!(p.house.is_empty());
532	    }
533	
534	    #[test]
535	    fn unknown_house_token_is_dropped_silently() {
536	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "chain,bogus,rush")]));
537	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
538	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
539	    }
540	
541	    #[test]
542	    fn build_local_href_skips_query_when_defaults() {
543	        let p = LocalRulesParams::default();
544	        assert_eq!(build_local_href(Variant::Xiangqi, &p), "/local/xiangqi");
545	        assert_eq!(build_local_href(Variant::Banqi, &p), "/local/banqi");
546	    }
547	
548	    #[test]
549	    fn app_href_adds_base_path() {
550	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("", "/local/xiangqi"), "/local/xiangqi");
551	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("/", "/local/xiangqi"), "/local/xiangqi");
552	        assert_eq!(
553	            app_href_with_base("/chinese-chess", "/local/xiangqi"),
554	            "/chinese-chess/local/xiangqi"
555	        );
556	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("chinese-chess", "lobby"), "/chinese-chess/lobby");
557	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("/chinese-chess/", "/"), "/chinese-chess/");
558	        assert_eq!(
559	            app_href_with_base("/chinese-chess", "/local/banqi?seed=7"),
560	            "/chinese-chess/local/banqi?seed=7"
561	        );
562	    }
563	
564	    #[test]
565	    fn ws_base_normalizes_websocket_urls_only() {
566	        assert_eq!(
567	            normalize_ws_base(" wss://example.com/ws-root/ "),
568	            Ok("wss://example.com/ws-root".to_string())
569	        );
570	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base("ws://127.0.0.1:7878"), Ok("ws://127.0.0.1:7878".to_string()));
571	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base(""), Err(WsBaseError::Empty));
572	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base("https://example.com"), Err(WsBaseError::BadScheme));
573	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base("/ws"), Err(WsBaseError::BadScheme));
574	    }
575	
576	    #[test]
577	    fn build_local_href_includes_query_when_non_default() {
578	        let p = LocalRulesParams { strict: true, ..Default::default() };
579	        assert_eq!(build_local_href(Variant::Xiangqi, &p), "/local/xiangqi?strict=1");
580	        let p = LocalRulesParams {
581	            house: HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE | HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH,
582	            seed: Some(7),
583	            ..Default::default()
584	        };
585	        assert_eq!(build_local_href(Variant::Banqi, &p), "/local/banqi?house=chain,rush&seed=7");
586	    }
587	
588	    #[test]
589	    fn xiangqi_vs_ai_query_round_trips() {
590	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("ai", "red"), ("diff", "hard")]));
591	        assert_eq!(p.mode, PlayMode::VsAi);
592	        assert_eq!(p.ai_side, Side::RED);
593	        assert_eq!(p.ai_difficulty, Difficulty::Hard);
594	        assert_eq!(p.ai_strategy, Strategy::default(), "missing engine= → default");
595	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
596	        assert!(q.contains("mode=ai"));
597	        assert!(q.contains("ai=red"));
598	        assert!(q.contains("diff=hard"));
599	        assert!(!q.contains("engine="), "default strategy should be omitted from query");
600	    }
601	
602	    #[test]
603	    fn xiangqi_engine_v1_round_trips() {
604	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[
605	            ("mode", "ai"),
606	            ("ai", "black"),
607	            ("diff", "normal"),
608	            ("engine", "v1"),
609	        ]));
610	        assert_eq!(p.ai_strategy, Strategy::MaterialV1);
611	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
612	        assert!(q.contains("engine=v1"), "non-default strategy must round-trip; got {:?}", q);
613	    }
614	
615	    #[test]
616	    fn xiangqi_engine_v2_alias_parses() {
617	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("engine", "material-pst")]));
618	        assert_eq!(p.ai_strategy, Strategy::MaterialPstV2);
619	    }
620	
621	    #[test]
622	    fn xiangqi_engine_v3_alias_parses() {
623	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[
624	            ("mode", "ai"),
625	            ("engine", "material-king-safety-pst"),
626	        ]));
627	        assert_eq!(p.ai_strategy, Strategy::MaterialKingSafetyPstV3);
628	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("engine", "king-safety")]));
629	        assert_eq!(p.ai_strategy, Strategy::MaterialKingSafetyPstV3);
630	    }
631	
632	    #[test]
633	    fn xiangqi_engine_unknown_token_falls_back_to_default() {
634	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("engine", "v999")]));
635	        assert_eq!(p.ai_strategy, Strategy::default());
636	    }
637	
638	    #[test]
639	    fn xiangqi_variation_round_trips() {
640	        for (token, expected) in &[
641	            ("strict", Randomness::STRICT),
642	            ("subtle", Randomness::SUBTLE),
643	            ("varied", Randomness::VARIED),
644	            ("chaotic", Randomness::CHAOTIC),
645	        ] {
646	            let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("variation", token)]));
647	            assert_eq!(p.ai_variation, Some(*expected));
648	            let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
649	            assert!(
650	                q.contains(&format!("variation={}", token)),
651	                "round-trip failed for {}: {}",
652	                token,
653	                q
654	            );
655	        }
656	    }
657	
658	    #[test]
659	    fn xiangqi_variation_omitted_when_default() {
660	        let p = LocalRulesParams { mode: PlayMode::VsAi, ..Default::default() };
661	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
662	        assert!(
663	            !q.contains("variation="),
664	            "default (None) variation should not be emitted; got {}",
665	            q
666	        );
667	    }
668	
669	    #[test]
670	    fn xiangqi_variation_unknown_token_falls_back_to_none() {
671	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("variation", "garbage")]));

```text
1	//! Pure-logic helpers for the client's view of a game. Native-testable —
2	//! no Leptos signals or browser deps live here.
3	
4	use chess_core::board::{Board, BoardShape};
5	use chess_core::coord::Square;
6	use chess_core::moves::Move;
7	use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, Side};
8	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
9	use chess_core::state::{GameState, GameStatus};
10	use chess_core::view::{PlayerView, VisibleCell};
11	
12	/// Role assigned by the server on `Hello` (player) or `Spectating`
13	/// (read-only). Drives whether the play page renders move/resign/rematch
14	/// affordances and whether the chat input is enabled.
15	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
16	pub enum ClientRole {
17	    Player(Side),
18	    Spectator,
19	}
20	
21	impl ClientRole {
22	    pub fn is_player(self) -> bool {
23	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Player(_))
24	    }
25	
26	    pub fn is_spectator(self) -> bool {
27	        matches!(self, ClientRole::Spectator)
28	    }
29	
30	    pub fn observer(self) -> Side {
31	        // Spectators render from RED's POV — matches what chess-net's
32	        // `broadcast_update` projects for spectator updates.
33	        match self {
34	            ClientRole::Player(s) => s,
35	            ClientRole::Spectator => Side::RED,
36	        }
37	    }
38	}
39	
40	/// Trim a vec from the front so it holds at most `max` entries. Used for
41	/// the per-page chat ring buffer that mirrors the server's 50-line cap.
42	pub fn truncate_front<T>(buf: &mut Vec<T>, max: usize) {
43	    if buf.len() > max {
44	        let drop_count = buf.len() - max;
45	        buf.drain(0..drop_count);
46	    }
47	}
48	
49	/// Find the legal `Move` (if any) whose origin is `from` and whose final
50	/// destination is `to`. Reveal moves match when `from == to == at`.
51	pub fn find_move(view: &PlayerView, from: Square, to: Square) -> Option<Move> {
52	    view.legal_moves.iter().find(|m| matches_endpoints(m, from, to)).cloned()
53	}
54	
55	fn matches_endpoints(mv: &Move, from: Square, to: Square) -> bool {
56	    if mv.origin_square() != from {
57	        return false;
58	    }
59	    match mv.to_square() {
60	        Some(t) => t == to,
61	        None => matches!(mv, Move::Reveal { at, .. } if *at == to),
62	    }
63	}
64	
65	/// All legal destination squares for a piece on `from` (used to render
66	/// move dots). In banqi 連吃 chain mode the engine adds
67	/// `Move::EndChain { at: chain_lock }` to `legal_moves`; that move has
68	/// `origin == chain_lock` and `to == None`, and is intentionally NOT a
69	/// movement target — it's the explicit "release the lock" gesture
70	/// triggered by clicking the locked piece. Excluding it here keeps
71	/// the dot/ring layer accurate during chain mode (and crucially keeps
72	/// this closure from panicking, which would freeze the leptos DOM
73	/// updates and leave stale markers on the board).
74	pub fn legal_targets(view: &PlayerView, from: Square) -> Vec<Square> {
75	    view.legal_moves
76	        .iter()
77	        .filter(|m| m.origin_square() == from)
78	        .filter_map(|m| match m {
79	            Move::Reveal { at, .. } => Some(*at),
80	            Move::EndChain { .. } => None,
81	            other => other.to_square(),
82	        })
83	        .collect()
84	}
85	
86	/// True if `view.chain_lock` is set and the click on `sq` should commit
87	/// `Move::EndChain` (e.g. user clicks the locked piece itself to release
88	/// the chain). Otherwise the click should attempt a further capture.
89	pub fn end_chain_move(view: &PlayerView) -> Option<Move> {
90	    view.chain_lock.map(|at| Move::EndChain { at })
91	}
92	
93	/// Sort order for the sidebar "captured pieces" panel.
94	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Default)]
95	pub enum CapturedSort {
96	    /// Chronological — order returned by `GameState::captured_pieces()`.
97	    #[default]
98	    Time,
99	    /// Largest piece first (General > Advisor > Elephant > Chariot >
100	    /// Horse > Cannon > Soldier).
101	    Rank,
102	}
103	
104	impl CapturedSort {
105	    pub fn toggled(self) -> Self {
106	        match self {
107	            CapturedSort::Time => CapturedSort::Rank,
108	            CapturedSort::Rank => CapturedSort::Time,
109	        }
110	    }
111	
112	    pub fn label(self) -> &'static str {
113	        match self {
114	            CapturedSort::Time => "time",
115	            CapturedSort::Rank => "rank",
116	        }
117	    }
118	}
119	
120	/// Rank value used by `CapturedSort::Rank`. Higher = stronger piece.
121	pub fn piece_rank_value(kind: PieceKind) -> u8 {
122	    match kind {
123	        PieceKind::General => 6,
124	        PieceKind::Advisor => 5,
125	        PieceKind::Elephant => 4,
126	        PieceKind::Chariot => 3,
127	        PieceKind::Horse => 2,
128	        PieceKind::Cannon => 1,
129	        PieceKind::Soldier => 0,
130	    }
131	}
132	
133	/// Split `pieces` (chronological from the engine) into per-side rows
134	/// for the sidebar panel, sorting each row according to `sort`.
135	pub fn split_and_sort_captured(pieces: &[Piece], sort: CapturedSort) -> (Vec<Piece>, Vec<Piece>) {
136	    let mut red: Vec<Piece> = pieces.iter().filter(|p| p.side == Side::RED).copied().collect();
137	    let mut black: Vec<Piece> = pieces.iter().filter(|p| p.side == Side::BLACK).copied().collect();
138	    if sort == CapturedSort::Rank {
139	        red.sort_by_key(|p| std::cmp::Reverse(piece_rank_value(p.kind)));
140	        black.sort_by_key(|p| std::cmp::Reverse(piece_rank_value(p.kind)));
141	    }
142	    (red, black)
143	}
144	
145	/// Reconstruct a (best-effort) `GameState` from a `PlayerView` for the
146	/// sole purpose of running `chess_ai::analyze` client-side in Net mode
147	/// (debug overlay).
148	///
149	/// **Xiangqi only** — banqi has hidden tiles which we can't reveal
150	/// without cheating, so we refuse to reconstruct (returns `None`).
151	/// Three-Kingdom too.
152	///
153	/// The reconstructed state is good enough for `chess_ai::analyze` (the
154	/// search reads `board`, `side_to_move`, `legal_moves`, `make_move`,
155	/// `unmake_move`) but is NOT a faithful replay — `history` is empty,
156	/// `no_progress_plies` is 0, `chain_lock` mirrors the view, etc. Don't
157	/// try to roundtrip this back to a server.
158	pub fn reconstruct_xiangqi_state_for_analysis(view: &PlayerView) -> Option<GameState> {
159	    if !matches!(view.shape, BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10) {
160	        // Banqi / three-kingdom: hidden info or unsupported. The chess-ai
161	        // engine is xiangqi-only anyway.
162	        return None;
163	    }
164	    if !matches!(view.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
165	        // analyze() returns None on terminated games anyway.
166	        return None;
167	    }
168	    // Build a fresh xiangqi-casual state and overwrite its board.
169	    // Xiangqi rules don't mutate per-game; only the board + side-to-move
170	    // matter for search.
171	    let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
172	    let mut board = Board::new(view.shape);
173	    for (idx, cell) in view.cells.iter().enumerate() {
174	        let sq = Square(idx as u16);
175	        let pos = match cell {
176	            VisibleCell::Empty => None,
177	            VisibleCell::Hidden => return None, // shouldn't happen for xiangqi
178	            VisibleCell::Revealed(p) => Some(*p),
179	        };
180	        board.set(sq, pos);
181	    }
182	    state.board = board;
183	    state.side_to_move = view.side_to_move;
184	    state.status = view.status;
185	    state.chain_lock = view.chain_lock;
186	    Some(state)
187	}
188	
189	/// Compute the squares a 'what-if' threat-hover preview should ring,
190	/// given that the user is hovering one of their own pieces at
191	/// `hovered_sq`.
192	///
193	/// The hover preview answers: "which of my OTHER pieces would
194	/// become newly vulnerable if I moved this hovered piece away?"
195	/// Computed as the delta between `attacked_pieces(observer)` before
196	/// and after removing the hovered piece from the board:
197	///
198	/// ```text
199	/// hover_squares = attacked_after_removal − attacked_before_removal
200	/// ```
201	///
202	/// This gives the user a 'this piece is currently defending these
203	/// others — moving it exposes them' nudge that the static
204	/// `view.threats.attacked` list never surfaces (those pieces are
205	/// safe right now, until the defender moves).
206	///
207	/// Returns empty when:
208	/// * `view` reconstructs to a state we can't analyse (banqi /
209	///   three-kingdom — see `reconstruct_xiangqi_state_for_analysis`),
210	/// * the hovered square doesn't host one of `observer`'s revealed
211	///   pieces, or
212	/// * the game is finished.
213	///
214	/// Compute cost: two `attacked_pieces` calls (~2 ms on 9×10), safe
215	/// to run on every hover change. We do clone the board for the
216	/// remove-and-recompute step, but a 9×10 board clone is sub-µs.
217	pub fn hover_threat_squares(
218	    view: &PlayerView,
219	    observer: chess_core::piece::Side,
220	    hovered_sq: Square,
221	) -> Vec<Square> {
222	    // The reconstructed state is xiangqi-only by design; banqi would
223	    // need hidden info we can't legitimately recover client-side.
224	    let Some(state) = reconstruct_xiangqi_state_for_analysis(view) else {
225	        return Vec::new();
226	    };
227	    // Only meaningful when hovering one of the observer's own pieces.
228	    let Some(pos) = state.board.get(hovered_sq) else { return Vec::new() };
229	    if pos.piece.side != observer {
230	        return Vec::new();
231	    }
232	    // Already-finished game has no "next turn" to ask about.
233	    if !matches!(state.status, GameStatus::Ongoing) {
234	        return Vec::new();
235	    }
236	    let before: std::collections::HashSet<Square> =
237	        state.attacked_pieces(observer).into_iter().collect();
238	    let mut without = state.clone();
239	    without.board.set(hovered_sq, None);
240	    without
241	        .attacked_pieces(observer)
242	        .into_iter()
243	        .filter(|sq| !before.contains(sq) && *sq != hovered_sq)
244	        .collect()
245	}
246	
247	#[cfg(test)]
248	mod tests {
249	    use super::*;
250	    use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
251	    use chess_core::state::GameState;
252	
253	    #[test]
254	    fn find_move_locates_xiangqi_step() {
255	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
256	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
257	        // Red soldier at file 0 rank 3 should have a step forward to rank 4.
258	        let from = Square(3 * 9); // rank 3, file 0
259	        let to = Square(4 * 9); // rank 4, file 0
260	        let mv = find_move(&view, from, to);
261	        assert!(matches!(mv, Some(Move::Step { .. })), "expected step from soldier, got {:?}", mv);
262	    }
263	
264	    #[test]
265	    fn legal_targets_for_chariot_in_corner() {
266	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
267	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
268	        // Red chariot at (file 0, rank 0) — Square(0). Should have several
269	        // legal destinations along its file (rank moves blocked by horse).
270	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, Square(0));
271	        assert!(!targets.is_empty(), "chariot at corner must have legal moves");
272	    }
273	
274	    #[test]
275	    fn legal_targets_empty_for_non_piece_square() {
276	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
277	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
278	        // Square(5*9 + 4) — a river-ish empty square. Empty piece → no targets.
279	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, Square(5 * 9 + 4));
280	        assert!(targets.is_empty());
281	    }
282	
283	    #[test]
284	    fn truncate_front_drops_oldest_when_over_cap() {
285	        let mut v = vec![1, 2, 3, 4, 5];
286	        truncate_front(&mut v, 3);
287	        assert_eq!(v, vec![3, 4, 5]);
288	    }
289	
290	    #[test]
291	    fn truncate_front_noop_when_under_cap() {
292	        let mut v = vec![1, 2];
293	        truncate_front(&mut v, 5);
294	        assert_eq!(v, vec![1, 2]);
295	    }
296	
297	    #[test]
298	    fn client_role_observer_defaults_red_for_spectator() {
299	        assert_eq!(ClientRole::Player(Side::BLACK).observer(), Side::BLACK);
300	        assert_eq!(ClientRole::Spectator.observer(), Side::RED);
301	        assert!(ClientRole::Spectator.is_spectator());
302	        assert!(ClientRole::Player(Side::RED).is_player());
303	    }
304	
305	    #[test]
306	    fn end_chain_move_returns_some_when_chain_lock_set() {
307	        use chess_core::board::Board;
308	        use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
309	        use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare};
310	        use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
311	        use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
312	        use smallvec::smallvec;
313	
314	        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
315	        let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
316	        for sq in squares {
317	            state.board.set(sq, None);
318	        }
319	        state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
320	        let _ = Board::new(state.board.shape()); // sanity
321	
322	        let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
323	        let s1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
324	        let s2 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(3));
325	        state.board.set(h, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse))));
326	        state
327	            .board
328	            .set(s1, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier))));
329	        state
330	            .board
331	            .set(s2, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier))));
332	
333	        let cap = Move::Capture {
334	            from: h,
335	            to: s1,
336	            captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
337	        };
338	        state.make_move(&cap).unwrap();
339	        assert!(state.chain_lock.is_some());
340	
341	        let view = chess_core::view::PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
342	        assert_eq!(view.chain_lock, Some(s1));
343	        assert!(matches!(end_chain_move(&view), Some(Move::EndChain { at }) if at == s1));
344	    }
345	
346	    #[test]
347	    fn legal_targets_in_chain_mode_excludes_end_chain_and_does_not_panic() {
348	        // Regression for the "stale dots after chain capture" bug:
349	        // `legal_targets` was hitting an `unreachable!()` arm for
350	        // `Move::EndChain { at }` (whose `to_square()` returns None
351	        // and which doesn't match the `Reveal` arm). The panic froze
352	        // leptos DOM updates, leaving the previous chain step's
353	        // markers on screen. Now EndChain is filtered out here and
354	        // the closure returns a clean target list.
355	        use chess_core::coord::{File, Rank, Square};
356	        use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare};
357	        use chess_core::rules::{HouseRules, RuleSet};
358	        use chess_core::state::{GameState, SideAssignment};
359	        use smallvec::smallvec;
360	
361	        let mut state = GameState::new(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, 0));
362	        let squares: Vec<Square> = state.board.squares().collect();
363	        for sq in squares {
364	            state.board.set(sq, None);
365	        }
366	        state.side_assignment = Some(SideAssignment { mapping: smallvec![Side::RED, Side::BLACK] });
367	        let h = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(1));
368	        let s1 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(2));
369	        let s2 = state.board.sq(File(1), Rank(3));
370	        state.board.set(h, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse))));
371	        state
372	            .board
373	            .set(s1, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier))));
374	        state
375	            .board
376	            .set(s2, Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier))));
377	        // Capture s1, landing on s1 with chain mode active.
378	        let cap = Move::Capture {
379	            from: h,
380	            to: s1,
381	            captured: Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
382	        };
383	        state.make_move(&cap).unwrap();
384	        assert_eq!(state.chain_lock, Some(s1));
385	
386	        let view = chess_core::view::PlayerView::project(&state, Side::RED);
387	        // Confirm the engine actually emitted EndChain alongside the next-hop capture.
388	        assert!(view.legal_moves.iter().any(|m| matches!(m, Move::EndChain { .. })));
389	
390	        // legal_targets must not panic and must not list the locked
391	        // square (where EndChain "lives") as a movement target.
392	        let targets = legal_targets(&view, s1);
393	        assert!(targets.contains(&s2), "next hop s2 must be a legal target");
394	        assert!(!targets.contains(&s1), "EndChain's `at` must NOT show up as a target dot");
395	    }
396	
397	    #[test]
398	    fn end_chain_move_returns_none_when_no_chain_lock() {
399	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual());
400	        let view = chess_core::view::PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
401	        assert!(end_chain_move(&view).is_none());
402	    }
403	
404	    #[test]
405	    fn captured_sort_time_preserves_chronological_order() {
406	        use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind};
407	        let chronological = vec![
408	            Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Soldier),
409	            Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Cannon),
410	            Piece::new(Side::BLACK, PieceKind::Horse),
411	            Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot),
412	        ];
413	        let (red, black) = split_and_sort_captured(&chronological, CapturedSort::Time);
414	        assert_eq!(
415	            red.iter().map(|p| p.kind).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
416	            vec![PieceKind::Cannon, PieceKind::Chariot]
417	        );
418	        assert_eq!(
419	            black.iter().map(|p| p.kind).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
420	            vec![PieceKind::Soldier, PieceKind::Horse]
421	        );
422	    }
423	
424	    #[test]
425	    fn captured_sort_rank_orders_largest_first() {
426	        use chess_core::piece::{Piece, PieceKind};
427	        let chronological = vec![
428	            Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Soldier),
429	            Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Chariot),
430	            Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::Horse),
431	            Piece::new(Side::RED, PieceKind::General),
432	        ];
433	        let (red, _black) = split_and_sort_captured(&chronological, CapturedSort::Rank);
434	        assert_eq!(
435	            red.iter().map(|p| p.kind).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
436	            vec![PieceKind::General, PieceKind::Chariot, PieceKind::Horse, PieceKind::Soldier]
437	        );
438	    }
439	
440	    #[test]
441	    fn captured_sort_toggle_round_trips() {
442	        assert_eq!(CapturedSort::Time.toggled(), CapturedSort::Rank);
443	        assert_eq!(CapturedSort::Rank.toggled(), CapturedSort::Time);
444	    }
445	
446	    /// Hover-preview produces an empty list for an opening xiangqi
447	    /// position regardless of which own piece is hovered: opening
448	    /// has no piece in danger right now, AND removing any single own
449	    /// piece doesn't expose anything else (the legality filter
450	    /// already prevented every fragile setup). Confirms the helper
451	    /// returns silently rather than e.g. listing every own piece.
452	    #[test]
453	    fn hover_preview_empty_in_opening() {
454	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
455	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
456	        // Pick a corner chariot — surely defending nothing important.
457	        let chariot_sq = Square(0);
458	        let hover = hover_threat_squares(&view, Side::RED, chariot_sq);
459	        assert!(hover.is_empty(), "opening hover should be empty, got {:?}", hover);
460	    }
461	
462	    /// Hover-preview against an opponent piece returns empty —
463	    /// hovering the wrong color is a no-op (saves the renderer from
464	    /// painting bogus rings).
465	    #[test]
466	    fn hover_preview_empty_for_opponent_piece() {
467	        let state = GameState::new(RuleSet::xiangqi());
468	        let view = PlayerView::project(&state, state.side_to_move);
469	        // Black corner chariot — observer is RED, so this is opponent.
470	        let black_chariot = Square(9 * 9); // file 0 rank 9
471	        let hover = hover_threat_squares(&view, Side::RED, black_chariot);
472	        assert!(hover.is_empty());
473	    }
474	}

150	    let host_room: Rc<RefCell<Option<Rc<HostRoom>>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
151	    let (play_session, set_play_session) = create_signal::<Option<Session>>(None);
152	    // Holder for the handshake's live ICE diag signal so the inline
153	    // status badge can render it reactively. None before Open room
154	    // succeeds; Some thereafter.
155	    let (ice_diag_holder, set_ice_diag_holder) = create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<IceDiag>>>(None);
156	
157	    // Re-snapshot the offer SDP whenever the diag signal fires (every
158	    // `oniceicecandidate` / connection / gathering state change). Catches
159	    // late-arriving srflx candidates that wouldn't be in the half-
160	    // gathered SDP captured at connect_as_host return time. See
161	    // HostHandshake::current_offer for the rationale.
162	    {
163	        let handshake = handshake.clone();
164	        create_effect(move |_| {
165	            if let Some(sig) = ice_diag_holder.get() {
166	                let _ = sig.get(); // subscribe to inner changes
167	                if let Some(hh) = handshake.borrow().as_ref() {
168	                    set_offer_blob.set(hh.current_offer().0);
169	                }
170	            }
171	        });
172	    }
173	
174	    // ── Open room (generate offer) ────────────────────────────
175	    let handshake_for_open = handshake.clone();
176	    let on_open: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
177	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), HostStatus::Idle) {
178	            return;
179	        }
180	        set_error_msg.set(None);
181	        set_status.set(HostStatus::Generating);
182	        let cfg = WebRtcConfig {
183	            ice_mode: if use_stun.get_untracked() { IceMode::WithStun } else { IceMode::LanOnly },
184	        };
185	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_open.clone();
186	        spawn_local(async move {
187	            match connect_as_host(cfg).await {
188	                Ok(hh) => {
189	                    set_offer_blob.set(hh.offer.0.clone());
190	                    set_ice_diag_holder.set(Some(hh.ice_diag));
191	                    *handshake_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(hh);
192	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::AwaitingAnswer);
193	                }
194	                Err(e) => {
195	                    set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("connect_as_host failed: {e:?}")));
196	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
197	                }
198	            }
199	        });
200	    });
201	
202	    // ── Accept answer ──────────────────────────────────────────
203	    let handshake_for_accept = handshake.clone();
204	    let host_room_slot = host_room.clone();
205	    let on_accept: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
206	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), HostStatus::AwaitingAnswer) {
207	            return;
208	        }
209	        let blob = answer_input.get_untracked();
210	        if blob.trim().is_empty() {
211	            set_error_msg.set(Some("paste the joiner's answer SDP first".into()));
212	            return;
213	        }
214	        set_error_msg.set(None);
215	        set_status.set(HostStatus::AcceptingAnswer);
216	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_accept.clone();
217	        let host_room_slot = host_room_slot.clone();
218	        spawn_local(async move {
219	            let hh = match handshake_slot.borrow_mut().take() {
220	                Some(hh) => hh,
221	                None => {
222	                    set_error_msg.set(Some("no handshake in flight".into()));
223	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
224	                    return;
225	                }
226	            };
227	            if let Err(e) = hh.accept_answer(AnswerBlob(blob)).await {
228	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("accept_answer failed: {e:?}")));
229	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
230	                return;
231	            }
232	            // CRITICAL: `accept_answer` resolves as soon as
233	            // `setRemoteDescription` returns — BEFORE the SCTP
234	            // handshake completes. If we call `attach_remote_player_dc`
235	            // immediately, the `dc.send_with_str(...)` for Hello +
236	            // ChatHistory silently fails (DC ready_state is still
237	            // "connecting"). Wait for actual DC open first.
238	            let dc = match hh.dc.borrow().clone() {
239	                Some(d) => d,
240	                None => {
241	                    set_error_msg.set(Some("DataChannel slot is empty".into()));
242	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
243	                    return;
244	                }
245	            };
246	            let ice_diag = hh.ice_diag;
247	            if !wait_for_dc_open(&dc, 10_000).await {
248	                let d = ice_diag.get_untracked();
249	                let sdp = hh.pc.local_description().map(|desc| desc.sdp()).unwrap_or_default();
250	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!(
251	                    "DataChannel did not open within 10 s — {}. {}",
252	                    diag_line(&d),
253	                    timeout_hint_for(&sdp),
254	                )));
255	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
256	                return;
257	            }
258	            let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(RuleSet::xiangqi(), None, /* hints */ false);
259	            if let Err(e) = room.attach_remote_player_dc(dc) {
260	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("attach joiner failed: {e:?}")));
261	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
262	                return;
263	            }
264	            *host_room_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(room);
265	            set_play_session.set(Some(session));
266	            set_status.set(HostStatus::Playing);
267	        });
268	    });
269	
270	    // ── Reset (full page reload) ──────────────────────────────
271	    let on_reset: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
272	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
273	            let _ = win.location().reload();
274	        }
275	    });
276	
277	    view! {
278	        <div class="lan-page" style="max-width:720px;margin:1.5rem auto;padding:1rem">

280	                when=move || matches!(status.get(), HostStatus::Playing)

292	                         address (often 198.18.x.x) and route LAN traffic through the tunnel, \
293	                         which breaks WebRTC's direct device discovery. If the VPN must stay on, \
294	                         configure split-tunnel to bypass: 192.168.0.0/16 + 10.0.0.0/8 + \
295	                         172.16.0.0/12 (private LANs), 172.20.10.0/28 (iOS hotspot), and \

441	pub fn LanJoinPage() -> impl IntoView {
442	    let (status, set_status) = create_signal::<JoinStatus>(JoinStatus::Idle);
443	    let (use_stun, set_use_stun) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
444	    let (offer_input, set_offer_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
445	    let (answer_blob, set_answer_blob) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
446	    let (error_msg, set_error_msg) = create_signal::<Option<String>>(None);
447	
448	    // QR scanner modal state. See LanHostPage for the same pattern.
449	    let (scanner_open, set_scanner_open) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
450	    let (cam_available, set_cam_available) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
451	    spawn_local(async move {
452	        if has_camera().await {
453	            set_cam_available.set(true);
454	        }
455	    });
456	
457	    // Joiner side: store the JoinerHandshake so we can pull its
458	    // session out once the DC opens.
459	    let handshake: Rc<RefCell<Option<JoinerHandshake>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
460	    let (play_session, set_play_session) = create_signal::<Option<Session>>(None);
461	    // Diag-signal holder mirroring the host page. Populated once
462	    // `connect_as_joiner` resolves; drives the inline ICE-state badge
463	    // until the joiner's `handshake` is dropped (at which point the
464	    // PC's event handlers stop firing).
465	    let (ice_diag_holder, set_ice_diag_holder) = create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<IceDiag>>>(None);
466	
467	    // Re-snapshot the answer SDP on every diag fire (candidates,
468	    // connection state, gathering). Mirrors the host page; see
469	    // JoinerHandshake::current_answer.
470	    {
471	        let handshake = handshake.clone();
472	        create_effect(move |_| {
473	            if let Some(sig) = ice_diag_holder.get() {
474	                let _ = sig.get();
475	                if let Some(jh) = handshake.borrow().as_ref() {
476	                    set_answer_blob.set(jh.current_answer().0);
477	                }
478	            }
479	        });
480	    }
481	
482	    // Holder pattern: spawn_local runs without a Leptos owner context,
483	    // so a `create_effect` inside it would be GC'd immediately (no
484	    // subscriptions retained — `state.set(Open)` would fire but no
485	    // effect would react). Solution: define the holder signals and
486	    // the effect at component scope (properly owned), and have
487	    // spawn_local just `set` the holders. The component-scope effect
488	    // re-runs when the holders change AND when the inner state
489	    // signal changes.
490	    let (joiner_state_holder, set_joiner_state_holder) =
491	        create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<ConnState>>>(None);
492	    let session_holder: Rc<RefCell<Option<Session>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
493	
494	    {
495	        let session_holder = session_holder.clone();
496	        create_effect(move |_| {
497	            // Read both signals to register subscriptions:
498	            //   * joiner_state_holder fires once when handshake completes.
499	            //   * the inner state signal fires when DC opens.
500	            if let Some(state_sig) = joiner_state_holder.get() {
501	                if state_sig.get() == ConnState::Open {
502	                    if let Some(session) = session_holder.borrow().clone() {
503	                        set_play_session.set(Some(session));
504	                        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Playing);
505	                    }
506	                }
507	            }
508	        });
509	    }
510	
511	    let handshake_for_gen = handshake.clone();
512	    let session_holder_for_gen = session_holder.clone();
513	    let on_generate: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
514	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), JoinStatus::Idle) {
515	            return;
516	        }
517	        let blob = offer_input.get_untracked();
518	        if blob.trim().is_empty() {
519	            set_error_msg.set(Some("paste the host's offer SDP first".into()));
520	            return;
521	        }
522	        set_error_msg.set(None);
523	        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Generating);
524	        let cfg = WebRtcConfig {
525	            ice_mode: if use_stun.get_untracked() { IceMode::WithStun } else { IceMode::LanOnly },
526	        };
527	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_gen.clone();
528	        let session_holder = session_holder_for_gen.clone();
529	        spawn_local(async move {
530	            match connect_as_joiner(cfg, OfferBlob(blob)).await {
531	                Ok(jh) => {
532	                    set_answer_blob.set(jh.answer.0.clone());
533	                    let state = jh.session.state;
534	                    set_ice_diag_holder.set(Some(jh.ice_diag));
535	                    *session_holder.borrow_mut() = Some(jh.session.clone());
536	                    *handshake_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(jh);
537	                    set_status.set(JoinStatus::WaitingForOpen);
538	                    // Triggers the component-scope effect to subscribe
539	                    // to the state signal. Once DC opens, that effect
540	                    // flips status → Playing.
541	                    set_joiner_state_holder.set(Some(state));
542	                }
543	                Err(e) => {
544	                    set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("connect_as_joiner failed: {e:?}")));
545	                    set_status.set(JoinStatus::Idle);
546	                }
547	            }
548	        });
549	    });
550	
551	    let on_reset: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
552	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
553	            let _ = win.location().reload();
554	        }
555	    });
556	
557	    view! {
558	        <div class="lan-page" style="max-width:720px;margin:1.5rem auto;padding:1rem">
559	            <Show
560	                when=move || matches!(status.get(), JoinStatus::Playing)
561	                fallback=move || view! {
562	                    <a href=app_href("/") rel="external" class="back-link">"← Back to picker"</a>
563	                    <h1>"LAN join (WebRTC)"</h1>
564	                    <p class="muted">
565	                        "Paste the host's offer SDP, generate an answer, send it back to the host."
566	                    </p>
567	                    <p class="muted">
568	                        "VPN hint: disable any VPN (Cloudflare WARP, NordVPN, etc.) on BOTH \
569	                         devices before pairing. VPNs replace the real LAN IP with a tunnel \
570	                         address (often 198.18.x.x) and route LAN traffic through the tunnel, \
571	                         which breaks WebRTC's direct device discovery. If the VPN must stay on, \
572	                         configure split-tunnel to bypass: 192.168.0.0/16 + 10.0.0.0/8 + \
573	                         172.16.0.0/12 (private LANs), 172.20.10.0/28 (iOS hotspot), and \
574	                         224.0.0.0/4 (multicast / mDNS)."
575	                    </p>
576	                    <p class="muted">
577	                        "STUN hint: \"Use STUN\" adds public-IP \"srflx\" candidates so peers on \
578	                         different networks (or behind a router that breaks LAN mDNS) can still \
579	                         find each other. Skip it on a healthy same-LAN setup. Must match the \
580	                         host's setting. Doesn't help with symmetric NAT / CGNAT."
581	                    </p>
582	                    <p>
583	                        <button on:click=move |_| on_reset.call(())>"Reset"</button>
584	                        <label style="margin-left:1rem;font-size:13px">
585	                            <input
586	                                type="checkbox"
587	                                prop:checked=move || use_stun.get()
588	                                disabled=move || !matches!(status.get(), JoinStatus::Idle)
589	                                on:change=move |ev| {
590	                                    let v = ev.target()
```
</details></tool-use>
