```text
1	//! Host-side room authority for LAN play.
2	//!
3	//! Phase 4 of `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`. Wraps the
4	//! `chess_net::room::Room` state machine with a multi-peer routing
5	//! table:
6	//!
7	//!   - Host runs the `Room` in-process (no chess-net server involved).
8	//!   - Host is itself one of the seated players (`PeerSink::Local`):
9	//!     its `ClientMsg`s flow through `Room::apply` exactly the same
10	//!     way a remote peer's would, and it receives projected
11	//!     `ServerMsg`s via a Leptos signal that the play page reads.
12	//!   - Remote peers (joiner + spectators) connect via WebRTC. Each
13	//!     `RtcDataChannel` is a `PeerSink::Remote`; incoming JSON-decoded
14	//!     `ClientMsg`s route through the same `Room::apply` path.
15	//!
16	//! The host page consumes the same `Session { handle, incoming, state }`
17	//! shape that `transport::ws::connect` returns, so the existing
18	//! `pages/play.rs` works unchanged once Phase 5 wires up the
19	//! `/lan/host` route to provide the host's `Session` via Leptos
20	//! context.
21	//!
22	//! ## Lifetime + reference-cycle notes
23	//!
24	//! Closures wired to `RtcDataChannel.onmessage` capture `Weak<HostRoom>`,
25	//! not `Rc<HostRoom>`, to avoid a reference cycle between the room
26	//! and the per-peer keepalive vector. When the page drops its
27	//! `Rc<HostRoom>`, all `Weak::upgrade` calls inside the closures
28	//! return `None` and become silent no-ops — the next time the
29	//! browser GCs the channel, the closure goes too.
30	//!
31	//! ## Dead-code allowance
32	//!
33	//! Phase 5 (`pages/lan.rs`) consumes `HostRoom::new` +
34	//! `attach_remote_player_dc`. Spectator support, the
35	//! `add_remote_*` (non-DC) variants used by tests, and the
36	//! `room_id` / `summary` accessors are kept for Phase 5 polish.
37	#![allow(dead_code)]
38	
39	// === module skeleton — section bodies filled in via edits below ===
40	
41	// SECTION: imports
42	use std::cell::{Cell, RefCell};
43	use std::collections::HashMap;
44	use std::rc::Rc;
45	
46	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
47	use chess_net::protocol::{ClientMsg, ServerMsg};
48	use chess_net::room::{JoinError, Outbound, PeerId, Room};
49	use leptos::*;
50	
51	use crate::transport::{ConnState, Incoming, Session, Transport};
52	
53	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
54	#[allow(unused_imports)]
55	use {
56	    std::rc::Weak,
57	    wasm_bindgen::closure::Closure,
58	    wasm_bindgen::{JsCast, JsValue},
59	    web_sys::{MessageEvent, RtcDataChannel},
60	};
61	// SECTION: PeerSink
62	
63	/// Where to deliver `ServerMsg`s for a given peer.
64	///
65	/// `Local` is the host's own play page — `ServerMsg`s become entries
66	/// in a Leptos-tracked `Incoming` queue (a `VecDeque` + tick signal,
67	/// see `transport::Incoming`) that the page reads via
68	/// `Session.incoming` (and drains via `Incoming::drain`). The queue
69	/// design (rather than a latched single-value signal) is critical:
70	/// a fresh `Room::join_player` synchronously emits `Hello` +
71	/// `ChatHistory`, which on a latched signal would silently overwrite
72	/// each other in a single Leptos batch. The queue + monotonic tick
73	/// guarantees both messages survive AND that any post-mount push
74	/// (e.g. the host's own move's `Update` echo back through the local
75	/// sink) is observed — see `transport::Incoming` doc for the race
76	/// analysis.
77	///
78	/// `Remote` is a peer connected over WebRTC — `ServerMsg`s are
79	/// JSON-serialised and written into a `RtcDataChannel`. The
80	/// non-wasm32 build (workspace `cargo check`) substitutes a
81	/// `RemoteMock` variant for unit tests since `web_sys` types
82	/// aren't reachable on native targets.
83	pub enum PeerSink {
84	    Local(Incoming),
85	    #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
86	    Remote(RtcDataChannel),
87	    /// Test-only sink that records every delivered message into a
88	    /// shared `Vec`. Native + wasm32 both compile this so unit
89	    /// tests can run on either target.
90	    Mock(Rc<RefCell<Vec<ServerMsg>>>),
91	}
92	
93	impl PeerSink {
94	    fn deliver(&self, msg: ServerMsg) {
95	        match self {
96	            PeerSink::Local(inc) => inc.push(msg),
97	            #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
98	            PeerSink::Remote(dc) => {
99	                if let Ok(text) = serde_json::to_string(&msg) {
100	                    let _ = dc.send_with_str(&text);
101	                }
102	            }
103	            PeerSink::Mock(buf) => buf.borrow_mut().push(msg),
104	        }
105	    }
106	}
107	// SECTION: HostRoom struct
108	
109	/// Host-side authoritative room. Owns a `chess_net::room::Room`
110	/// (the same state machine the chess-net server runs) plus a routing
111	/// table mapping each peer's [`PeerId`] to a [`PeerSink`].
112	///
113	/// All mutable state is in `RefCell`s (single-threaded WASM context).
114	/// The struct is consumed via `Rc<HostRoom>` because:
115	///   1. The host's `Transport` impl needs to hold a strong ref so
116	///      `Transport::send` can route the host's own `ClientMsg`s
117	///      through `Room::apply`.
118	///   2. Per-peer DataChannel `onmessage` closures need a `Weak`
119	///      ref (NOT strong — see the cycle note in the module doc).
120	pub struct HostRoom {
121	    room: RefCell<Room>,
122	    sinks: RefCell<HashMap<PeerId, PeerSink>>,
123	    /// Monotonic peer-id allocator. The host gets `PeerId(1)`; the
124	    /// first remote peer is `PeerId(2)`, etc.
125	    next_peer: Cell<u64>,
126	    /// `PeerId` for the host's own play UI. Always `PeerId(1)` from
127	    /// `new()`; surfaced as a getter for clarity at call sites.
128	    self_peer: PeerId,
129	    /// Stable label used in `RoomSummary` (the host page shows it
130	    /// in the lobby-style indicator). Defaults to "lan".
131	    room_id: String,
132	}
133	// SECTION: HostRoom::new + Session bridge
134	
135	impl HostRoom {
136	    /// Construct a fresh host room and seat the host as the first
137	    /// player. Returns:
138	    ///
139	    ///   * `Rc<HostRoom>` — handle the host page holds for adding
140	    ///     remote peers as they pair (`add_remote_player`,
141	    ///     `add_remote_spectator`).
142	    ///   * `Session` — drop into the play page exactly where
143	    ///     `transport::ws::connect`'s return value would go. The
144	    ///     session's `incoming` signal already has the initial
145	    ///     `Hello` + `ChatHistory` queued (the host always seats as
146	    ///     RED, so the page sees `observer: Side::RED` immediately).
147	    ///
148	    /// Panics only on the impossible case where `Room::join_player`
149	    /// fails for the very first joiner (the room is fresh — both
150	    /// seats are open, no password mismatch is possible because the
151	    /// password was set on the same line).
152	    pub fn new(
153	        rules: RuleSet,
154	        password: Option<String>,
155	        hints_allowed: bool,
156	    ) -> (Rc<Self>, Session) {
157	        let mut room = Room::new(rules, password, hints_allowed);
158	        let self_peer = PeerId(1);
159	        let incoming = Incoming::new();
160	        let (state, _set_state) = create_signal(ConnState::Open);
161	
162	        // Insert host's sink BEFORE join_player so the Hello +
163	        // ChatHistory the room emits queue into the host's own
164	        // incoming Incoming — same VecDeque, processed in arrival
165	        // order by the play page's draining effect (which reads
166	        // every pending message via Incoming::drain on each tick).
167	        let mut sinks: HashMap<PeerId, PeerSink> = HashMap::new();
168	        sinks.insert(self_peer, PeerSink::Local(incoming.clone()));
169	
170	        let (_side, outbound) =
171	            room.join_player(self_peer).expect("fresh room always seats the first joiner");
172	        for ob in outbound {
173	            if let Some(sink) = sinks.get(&ob.peer) {
174	                sink.deliver(ob.msg);
175	            }
176	        }
177	
178	        let host = Rc::new(Self {
179	            room: RefCell::new(room),
180	            sinks: RefCell::new(sinks),
181	            next_peer: Cell::new(2),
182	            self_peer,
183	            room_id: "lan".into(),
184	        });
185	
186	        let handle: Rc<dyn Transport> =
187	            Rc::new(HostSelfTransport { host: host.clone(), self_peer });
188	        let session = Session { handle, incoming, state };
189	        (host, session)
190	    }
191	
192	    /// `PeerId` assigned to the host's own play UI. Stable across
193	    /// the room's lifetime.
194	    pub fn self_peer(&self) -> PeerId {
195	        self.self_peer
196	    }
197	
198	    /// Allocate the next `PeerId` for a newly-arriving remote peer.
199	    fn alloc_peer(&self) -> PeerId {
200	        let id = self.next_peer.get();
201	        self.next_peer.set(id + 1);
202	        PeerId(id)
203	    }
204	}
205	// SECTION: HostSelfTransport (impl Transport for the host's own page)
206	
207	/// `Transport` impl that routes the host's own `ClientMsg`s through
208	/// the in-process `Room::apply` instead of a network socket.
209	struct HostSelfTransport {
210	    host: Rc<HostRoom>,
211	    self_peer: PeerId,
212	}
213	
214	impl Transport for HostSelfTransport {
215	    fn send(&self, msg: ClientMsg) -> bool {
216	        self.host.handle_self_send(self.self_peer, msg);
217	        true
218	    }
219	}
220	// SECTION: add_remote_player / add_remote_spectator
221	
222	impl HostRoom {
223	    /// Insert a remote peer with a player-side seat.
224	    ///
225	    /// `sink` should normally be `PeerSink::Remote(dc)` for production
226	    /// (the page just got a `RtcDataChannel` from
227	    /// `HostHandshake::accept_answer` succeeding); tests pass
228	    /// `PeerSink::Mock(...)` instead.
229	    ///
230	    /// Wires the DataChannel's `onmessage` to route incoming
231	    /// `ClientMsg`s through `Room::apply` (production only — the
232	    /// `dc_for_handlers` arg passes the channel through to the
233	    /// handler installer; tests skip it). On error (room full),
234	    /// the sink is removed and the error returned.
235	    pub fn add_remote_player(self: &Rc<Self>, sink: PeerSink) -> Result<PeerId, JoinError> {
236	        let peer = self.alloc_peer();
237	        self.sinks.borrow_mut().insert(peer, sink);
238	        let outbound = match self.room.borrow_mut().join_player(peer) {
239	            Ok((_side, ob)) => ob,
240	            Err(e) => {
241	                self.sinks.borrow_mut().remove(&peer);
242	                return Err(e);
243	            }
244	        };
245	        self.fanout(outbound);
246	        Ok(peer)
247	    }
248	
249	    /// Insert a remote peer as a read-only spectator. `max` is the
250	    /// per-room spectator cap (the v1 plan says 4).
251	    pub fn add_remote_spectator(
252	        self: &Rc<Self>,
253	        sink: PeerSink,
254	        max: usize,
255	    ) -> Result<PeerId, JoinError> {
256	        let peer = self.alloc_peer();
257	        self.sinks.borrow_mut().insert(peer, sink);
258	        let outbound = match self.room.borrow_mut().join_spectator(peer, max) {
259	            Ok(ob) => ob,
260	            Err(e) => {
261	                self.sinks.borrow_mut().remove(&peer);
262	                return Err(e);
263	            }
264	        };
265	        self.fanout(outbound);
266	        Ok(peer)
267	    }
268	
269	    /// Wasm-only convenience: take a freshly-opened `RtcDataChannel`
270	    /// from a completed `HostHandshake`, install onmessage routing
271	    /// to `Room::apply`, and seat the peer as a player.
272	    #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
273	    pub fn attach_remote_player_dc(
274	        self: &Rc<Self>,
275	        dc: RtcDataChannel,
276	    ) -> Result<PeerId, JoinError> {
277	        let peer = self.add_remote_player(PeerSink::Remote(dc.clone()))?;
278	        install_remote_dc_handlers(self, peer, &dc);
279	        Ok(peer)
280	    }
281	
282	    #[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
283	    pub fn attach_remote_spectator_dc(
284	        self: &Rc<Self>,
285	        dc: RtcDataChannel,
286	        max: usize,
287	    ) -> Result<PeerId, JoinError> {
288	        let peer = self.add_remote_spectator(PeerSink::Remote(dc.clone()), max)?;
289	        install_remote_dc_handlers(self, peer, &dc);
290	        Ok(peer)
291	    }
292	}
293	// SECTION: handle_self_send / handle_remote_msg / fanout
294	
295	impl HostRoom {
296	    /// Route the host's own `ClientMsg` (from the play page's
297	    /// `Transport::send`) through the room.
298	    fn handle_self_send(&self, peer: PeerId, msg: ClientMsg) {
299	        let outbound = self.room.borrow_mut().apply(peer, msg);
300	        self.fanout(outbound);
301	    }
302	
303	    /// Route a remote peer's incoming `ClientMsg` through the room.
304	    /// Called from the per-DataChannel `onmessage` closure.
305	    fn handle_remote_msg(&self, peer: PeerId, msg: ClientMsg) {
306	        let outbound = self.room.borrow_mut().apply(peer, msg);
307	        self.fanout(outbound);
308	    }
309	
310	    /// Deliver each `Outbound` to its target peer's sink. Outbounds
311	    /// for unknown peers (e.g. peer disconnected mid-broadcast) are
312	    /// silently dropped.
313	    fn fanout(&self, outbound: Vec<Outbound>) {
314	        let sinks = self.sinks.borrow();
315	        for ob in outbound {
316	            if let Some(sink) = sinks.get(&ob.peer) {
317	                sink.deliver(ob.msg);
318	            }
319	        }
320	    }
321	
322	    /// Stable label used in `RoomSummary` (the host page may show
323	    /// the room state in a sidebar widget).
324	    pub fn room_id(&self) -> &str {
325	        &self.room_id
326	    }
327	
328	    /// Snapshot of the underlying room's [`chess_net::RoomSummary`].
329	    /// Useful for the host page's sidebar / future lobby UI.
330	    pub fn summary(&self) -> chess_net::RoomSummary {
331	        self.room.borrow().summary(&self.room_id)
332	    }
333	}
334	// SECTION: peer disconnect handling
335	
336	impl HostRoom {
337	    /// Remove a peer from the routing table + room. Returns the
338	    /// outbound list `Room::leave` produces (e.g. mid-game
339	    /// "opponent disconnected" notice to the surviving seat). The
340	    /// caller is responsible for invoking this from a DataChannel
341	    /// `onclose` event — production wires it via
342	    /// `install_remote_dc_handlers`.
343	    pub fn drop_peer(&self, peer: PeerId) {
344	        let outbound = self.room.borrow_mut().leave(peer);
345	        self.fanout(outbound);
346	        self.sinks.borrow_mut().remove(&peer);
347	    }
348	}
349	// SECTION: install_remote_dc_handlers (Weak<HostRoom> closures)
350	
351	/// Wire a remote peer's `RtcDataChannel.onmessage` to route incoming
352	/// `ClientMsg` JSON through `Room::apply`, and `onclose` to drop the
353	/// peer.
354	///
355	/// Closures capture `Weak<HostRoom>` (NOT `Rc`) so dropping the
356	/// page's strong references actually frees the room — without
357	/// `Weak`, the closures-in-keepalive-vec → `Rc<HostRoom>` cycle
358	/// would leak forever. Once the room drops, `Weak::upgrade` returns
359	/// `None` and any in-flight callback is a silent no-op.
360	///
361	/// Closures are `cb.forget()`-leaked: the only way to cancel them
362	/// would be `dc.set_onmessage(None)`, but the room dropping already
363	/// makes them harmless. For Phase 4 this is acceptable; Phase 5+
364	/// could add explicit teardown if memory pressure ever shows up.
365	#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]
366	fn install_remote_dc_handlers(host: &Rc<HostRoom>, peer: PeerId, dc: &RtcDataChannel) {
367	    {
368	        let weak = Rc::downgrade(host);
369	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |ev: JsValue| {
370	            let ev: MessageEvent = ev.unchecked_into();
371	            let text = match ev.data().as_string() {
372	                Some(t) => t,
373	                None => return,
374	            };
375	            let msg: ClientMsg = match serde_json::from_str(&text) {
376	                Ok(m) => m,
377	                Err(_) => return,
378	            };
379	            if let Some(host) = weak.upgrade() {
380	                host.handle_remote_msg(peer, msg);
381	            }
382	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
383	        dc.set_onmessage(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
384	        cb.forget();
385	    }
386	    {
387	        let weak = Rc::downgrade(host);
388	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |_ev: JsValue| {
389	            if let Some(host) = weak.upgrade() {
390	                host.drop_peer(peer);
391	            }
392	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
393	        dc.set_onclose(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
394	        cb.forget();
395	    }
396	}
397	// SECTION: tests
398	//
399	// HostRoom is wasm32-only because of the Leptos `WriteSignal` /
400	// `ReadSignal` dependency on the host's `Session` bridge. Native
401	// `cargo test --workspace` therefore cannot exercise this module
402	// directly; the meaningful test coverage lives elsewhere:
403	//
404	// * `chess-net::room::tests` (in the workspace) has 17 unit tests
405	//   covering every `Room::apply` branch (move / resign / chat /
406	//   rematch / leave / spectator gates / ...). HostRoom is a thin
407	//   routing wrapper on top of that; the moment a `ClientMsg`
408	//   reaches `Room::apply`, behaviour is identical to the chess-net
409	//   server. So the room-side correctness is well-covered.
410	//
411	// * The Phase 5 `/lan/host` and `/lan/join` pages exercise the
412	//   wasm-specific glue (DataChannel routing, Local-sink Hello
413	//   delivery, peer disconnect notifications) end-to-end on real
414	//   devices.
415	//
416	// If we ever need native unit coverage of HostRoom itself, the
417	// cleanest split is to move the pure-routing `handle_remote_msg` /
418	// `fanout` / `drop_peer` methods + `PeerSink::Mock` into a
419	// `host_room/router.rs` submodule that has no Leptos dependency,
420	// leaving the `Session` bridge in a wasm32-only `host_room/
421	// session.rs`. Deferred until a routing-bug-without-an-obvious-fix
422	// shows up.
423
```

```text
1	//! `/lan/host` and `/lan/join` routes — LAN multiplayer over WebRTC.
2	//!
3	//! Phase 5 of `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`. Pairs two browsers
4	//! on the same WiFi via the Phase 3 `transport::webrtc` plumbing
5	//! and the Phase 4 `host_room::HostRoom` authority, then drops the
6	//! resulting `Session` into the existing `pages/play::PlayConnected`
7	//! so the actual game UI is unchanged from chess-net mode.
8	//!
9	//! No QR / camera scanning yet — Phase 5 v1 uses textareas + the
10	//! browser clipboard for the offer/answer exchange. QR is
11	//! deferred (Phase 5.5 if requested).
12	
13	// === module skeleton — section bodies filled in via edits below ===
14	
15	// SECTION: imports
16	use std::cell::RefCell;
17	use std::rc::Rc;
18	
19	use chess_core::rules::RuleSet;
20	use leptos::*;
21	use wasm_bindgen::JsCast;
22	use wasm_bindgen_futures::spawn_local;
23	use web_sys::HtmlTextAreaElement;
24	
25	use crate::camera::has_camera;
26	use crate::components::qr::QrCodeView;
27	use crate::components::qr_scanner::QrScanner;
28	use crate::config::WsBase;
29	use crate::host_room::HostRoom;
30	use crate::net_diag::{classify, parse_candidate_addrs, NetDiag};
31	use crate::pages::play::PlayConnected;
32	use crate::routes::app_href;
33	use crate::transport::webrtc::{
34	    connect_as_host, connect_as_joiner, wait_for_dc_open, AnswerBlob, HostHandshake, IceDiag,
35	    IceMode, JoinerHandshake, OfferBlob, WebRtcConfig,
36	};
37	use crate::transport::{ConnState, Session};
38	use web_sys::{RtcIceConnectionState, RtcIceGatheringState, RtcPeerConnectionState};
39	
40	// SECTION: ICE diag rendering helpers
41	
42	fn ice_label(s: RtcIceConnectionState) -> &'static str {
43	    match s {
44	        RtcIceConnectionState::New => "new",
45	        RtcIceConnectionState::Checking => "checking",
46	        RtcIceConnectionState::Connected => "connected",
47	        RtcIceConnectionState::Completed => "completed",
48	        RtcIceConnectionState::Failed => "failed",
49	        RtcIceConnectionState::Disconnected => "disconnected",
50	        RtcIceConnectionState::Closed => "closed",
51	        _ => "?",
52	    }
53	}
54	
55	fn conn_label(s: RtcPeerConnectionState) -> &'static str {
56	    match s {
57	        RtcPeerConnectionState::New => "new",
58	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Connecting => "connecting",
59	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Connected => "connected",
60	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Disconnected => "disconnected",
61	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Failed => "failed",
62	        RtcPeerConnectionState::Closed => "closed",
63	        _ => "?",
64	    }
65	}
66	
67	fn gather_label(s: RtcIceGatheringState) -> &'static str {
68	    match s {
69	        RtcIceGatheringState::New => "new",
70	        RtcIceGatheringState::Gathering => "gathering",
71	        RtcIceGatheringState::Complete => "complete",
72	        _ => "?",
73	    }
74	}
75	
76	/// Format an [`IceDiag`] snapshot as a single line for the inline badge.
77	fn diag_line(d: &IceDiag) -> String {
78	    format!(
79	        "ICE: {} · connection: {} · gathering: {} · candidates: {}",
80	        ice_label(d.ice),
81	        conn_label(d.conn),
82	        gather_label(d.gather),
83	        d.candidates,
84	    )
85	}
86	
87	/// Hint string appended to the 10-s DC-open timeout error, varied by
88	/// what we can infer from the host's own SDP candidates. Surfacing
89	/// "your VPN is hijacking the LAN" instead of the generic mDNS hint
90	/// when the host candidate IP betrays a VPN tunnel saves the user
91	/// from blaming the wrong layer.
92	fn timeout_hint_for(sdp: &str) -> &'static str {
93	    match classify(&parse_candidate_addrs(sdp)) {
94	        NetDiag::VpnTunnel => {
95	            "VPN tunnel detected (host candidate is in 198.18.0.0/15). \
96	             DISABLE the VPN on both devices and retry — VPNs route LAN \
97	             traffic through the tunnel and replace your real LAN IP \
98	             with a fake address. If the VPN must stay on, configure \
99	             split-tunnel to bypass 192.168.0.0/16, 10.0.0.0/8, \
100	             172.16.0.0/12, 172.20.10.0/28 (iOS hotspot), and \
101	             224.0.0.0/4 (multicast / mDNS)."
102	        }
103	        NetDiag::Cgnat => {
104	            "CGNAT detected (host candidate is in 100.64.0.0/10). \
105	             Your ISP / carrier is using Carrier-Grade NAT, which often \
106	             blocks direct P2P even with STUN. Try an iPhone/Android \
107	             personal hotspot instead."
108	        }
109	        NetDiag::Plain => {
110	            "Common fixes: switch both devices to an iPhone/Android \
111	             personal hotspot (this network's WebRTC mDNS resolution \
112	             may be failing), or enable \"Use STUN\"."
113	        }
114	    }
115	}
116	// SECTION: LanHostPage component
117	
118	#[component]
119	pub fn LanHostPage() -> impl IntoView {
120	    // ── Page state ────────────────────────────────────────────
121	    // We progress through:
122	    //   Idle → Generating → AwaitingAnswer → AcceptingAnswer →
123	    //   WaitingForChannel → Playing
124	    // Each transition mutates the corresponding signals; the view
125	    // renders accordingly.
126	    let (status, set_status) = create_signal::<HostStatus>(HostStatus::Idle);
127	    let (use_stun, set_use_stun) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
128	    let (offer_blob, set_offer_blob) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
129	    let (answer_input, set_answer_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
130	    let (error_msg, set_error_msg) = create_signal::<Option<String>>(None);
131	
132	    // QR scanner modal state. `cam_available` is set asynchronously
133	    // on mount; default false to keep the Scan-camera button hidden
134	    // until detection completes (no flash-of-button-then-disappear
135	    // for cameraless devices).
136	    let (scanner_open, set_scanner_open) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
137	    let (cam_available, set_cam_available) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
138	    spawn_local(async move {
139	        if has_camera().await {
140	            set_cam_available.set(true);
141	        }
142	    });
143	
144	    // Keep the in-flight HostHandshake alive in the page; closures
145	    // and accept_answer need a stable reference.
146	    let handshake: Rc<RefCell<Option<HostHandshake>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
147	    // The HostRoom + its Session aren't constructed until the
148	    // DataChannel actually opens; on `state == Open` we instantiate
149	    // both and flip status to `Playing`.
150	    let host_room: Rc<RefCell<Option<Rc<HostRoom>>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
151	    let (play_session, set_play_session) = create_signal::<Option<Session>>(None);
152	    // Holder for the handshake's live ICE diag signal so the inline
153	    // status badge can render it reactively. None before Open room
154	    // succeeds; Some thereafter.
155	    let (ice_diag_holder, set_ice_diag_holder) = create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<IceDiag>>>(None);
156	
157	    // Re-snapshot the offer SDP whenever the diag signal fires (every
158	    // `oniceicecandidate` / connection / gathering state change). Catches
159	    // late-arriving srflx candidates that wouldn't be in the half-
160	    // gathered SDP captured at connect_as_host return time. See
161	    // HostHandshake::current_offer for the rationale.
162	    {
163	        let handshake = handshake.clone();
164	        create_effect(move |_| {
165	            if let Some(sig) = ice_diag_holder.get() {
166	                let _ = sig.get(); // subscribe to inner changes
167	                if let Some(hh) = handshake.borrow().as_ref() {
168	                    set_offer_blob.set(hh.current_offer().0);
169	                }
170	            }
171	        });
172	    }
173	
174	    // ── Open room (generate offer) ────────────────────────────
175	    let handshake_for_open = handshake.clone();
176	    let on_open: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
177	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), HostStatus::Idle) {
178	            return;
179	        }
180	        set_error_msg.set(None);
181	        set_status.set(HostStatus::Generating);
182	        let cfg = WebRtcConfig {
183	            ice_mode: if use_stun.get_untracked() { IceMode::WithStun } else { IceMode::LanOnly },
184	        };
185	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_open.clone();
186	        spawn_local(async move {
187	            match connect_as_host(cfg).await {
188	                Ok(hh) => {
189	                    set_offer_blob.set(hh.offer.0.clone());
190	                    set_ice_diag_holder.set(Some(hh.ice_diag));
191	                    *handshake_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(hh);
192	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::AwaitingAnswer);
193	                }
194	                Err(e) => {
195	                    set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("connect_as_host failed: {e:?}")));
196	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
197	                }
198	            }
199	        });
200	    });
201	
202	    // ── Accept answer ──────────────────────────────────────────
203	    let handshake_for_accept = handshake.clone();
204	    let host_room_slot = host_room.clone();
205	    let on_accept: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
206	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), HostStatus::AwaitingAnswer) {
207	            return;
208	        }
209	        let blob = answer_input.get_untracked();
210	        if blob.trim().is_empty() {
211	            set_error_msg.set(Some("paste the joiner's answer SDP first".into()));
212	            return;
213	        }
214	        set_error_msg.set(None);
215	        set_status.set(HostStatus::AcceptingAnswer);
216	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_accept.clone();
217	        let host_room_slot = host_room_slot.clone();
218	        spawn_local(async move {
219	            let hh = match handshake_slot.borrow_mut().take() {
220	                Some(hh) => hh,
221	                None => {
222	                    set_error_msg.set(Some("no handshake in flight".into()));
223	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
224	                    return;
225	                }
226	            };
227	            if let Err(e) = hh.accept_answer(AnswerBlob(blob)).await {
228	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("accept_answer failed: {e:?}")));
229	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
230	                return;
231	            }
232	            // CRITICAL: `accept_answer` resolves as soon as
233	            // `setRemoteDescription` returns — BEFORE the SCTP
234	            // handshake completes. If we call `attach_remote_player_dc`
235	            // immediately, the `dc.send_with_str(...)` for Hello +
236	            // ChatHistory silently fails (DC ready_state is still
237	            // "connecting"). Wait for actual DC open first.
238	            let dc = match hh.dc.borrow().clone() {
239	                Some(d) => d,
240	                None => {
241	                    set_error_msg.set(Some("DataChannel slot is empty".into()));
242	                    set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
243	                    return;
244	                }
245	            };
246	            let ice_diag = hh.ice_diag;
247	            if !wait_for_dc_open(&dc, 10_000).await {
248	                let d = ice_diag.get_untracked();
249	                let sdp = hh.pc.local_description().map(|desc| desc.sdp()).unwrap_or_default();
250	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!(
251	                    "DataChannel did not open within 10 s — {}. {}",
252	                    diag_line(&d),
253	                    timeout_hint_for(&sdp),
254	                )));
255	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
256	                return;
257	            }
258	            let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(RuleSet::xiangqi(), None, /* hints */ false);
259	            if let Err(e) = room.attach_remote_player_dc(dc) {
260	                set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("attach joiner failed: {e:?}")));
261	                set_status.set(HostStatus::Idle);
262	                return;
263	            }
264	            *host_room_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(room);
265	            set_play_session.set(Some(session));
266	            set_status.set(HostStatus::Playing);
267	        });
268	    });
269	
270	    // ── Reset (full page reload) ──────────────────────────────
271	    let on_reset: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
272	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
273	            let _ = win.location().reload();
274	        }
275	    });
276	
277	    view! {
278	        <div class="lan-page" style="max-width:720px;margin:1.5rem auto;padding:1rem">
279	            <Show
280	                when=move || matches!(status.get(), HostStatus::Playing)
281	                fallback=move || view! {
282	                    <a href=app_href("/") rel="external" class="back-link">"← Back to picker"</a>
283	                    <h1>"LAN host (WebRTC)"</h1>
284	                    <p class="muted">
285	                        "iOS hint: do not switch apps after tapping Open room. iOS Safari pauses \
286	                         WebRTC when the page is backgrounded. If you must AirDrop the offer, \
287	                         keep this Safari tab in the foreground (split-view works)."
288	                    </p>
289	                    <p class="muted">
290	                        "VPN hint: disable any VPN (Cloudflare WARP, NordVPN, etc.) on BOTH \
291	                         devices before pairing. VPNs replace the real LAN IP with a tunnel \
292	                         address (often 198.18.x.x) and route LAN traffic through the tunnel, \
293	                         which breaks WebRTC's direct device discovery. If the VPN must stay on, \
294	                         configure split-tunnel to bypass: 192.168.0.0/16 + 10.0.0.0/8 + \
295	                         172.16.0.0/12 (private LANs), 172.20.10.0/28 (iOS hotspot), and \
296	                         224.0.0.0/4 (multicast / mDNS)."
297	                    </p>
298	                    <p class="muted">
299	                        "STUN hint: enabling \"Use STUN\" asks public servers (Miwifi / Tencent / \
300	                         Cloudflare / Google) to tell the browser its own public IP and adds it \
301	                         as an extra \"srflx\" candidate. Turn it ON when: (a) same LAN but \
302	                         mDNS resolution is broken (some routers), or (b) the two devices are on \
303	                         different networks. Leave it OFF on a healthy same-LAN setup — mDNS \
304	                         works directly and STUN just adds up to 5 s of gather delay. STUN can't \
305	                         rescue symmetric NAT / CGNAT (mobile carriers, hotel WiFi); those would \
306	                         need a TURN relay (not shipped — fall back to running chess-net and \
307	                         using /lobby instead)."
308	                    </p>
309	                    <p>
310	                        <button
311	                            on:click=move |_| on_open.call(())
312	                            disabled=move || !matches!(status.get(), HostStatus::Idle)
313	                        >
314	                            "1. Open room"
315	                        </button>
316	                        <button on:click=move |_| on_reset.call(()) style="margin-left:0.5rem">
317	                            "Reset"
318	                        </button>
319	                        <label style="margin-left:1rem;font-size:13px">
320	                            <input
321	                                type="checkbox"
322	                                prop:checked=move || use_stun.get()
323	                                disabled=move || !matches!(status.get(), HostStatus::Idle)
324	                                on:change=move |ev| {
325	                                    let v = ev.target()
326	                                        .and_then(|t| t.dyn_into::<web_sys::HtmlInputElement>().ok())
327	                                        .map(|el| el.checked())
328	                                        .unwrap_or(false);
329	                                    set_use_stun.set(v);
330	                                }
331	                            />
332	                            " Use STUN (workaround for hostile networks)"
333	                        </label>
334	                    </p>
335	                    <p>"Status: " {move || format!("{:?}", status.get())}</p>
336	                    <Show when=move || ice_diag_holder.with(|h| h.is_some())>
337	                        <p style="font-size:13px;color:#888;margin-top:-0.5rem">
338	                            {move || ice_diag_holder.get()
339	                                .map(|sig| diag_line(&sig.get()))
340	                                .unwrap_or_default()}
341	                        </p>
342	                    </Show>
343	                    <Show when=move || !offer_blob.with(|s| s.is_empty())>
344	                        <p>"Have the joiner scan this QR — or copy the text and AirDrop / Messages it:"</p>
345	                        <QrCodeView
346	                            payload=Signal::derive(move || offer_blob.get())
347	                            label="LAN host offer SDP"
348	                        />
349	                        <p style="margin-top:0.75rem">"Or copy as text:"</p>
350	                        <textarea
351	                            rows="6"
352	                            readonly=true
353	                            style="width:100%;font-family:monospace;font-size:12px"
354	                            prop:value=move || offer_blob.get()
355	                        />
356	                        <p>
357	                            <button on:click=move |_| {
358	                                let blob = offer_blob.get();
359	                                if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
360	                                    let _ = win.navigator().clipboard().write_text(&blob);
361	                                }
362	                            }>
363	                                "Copy offer"
364	                            </button>
365	                            <span style="margin-left:0.5rem;font-size:12px;color:#666">
366	                                "Bytes: " {move || offer_blob.with(|s| s.len())}
367	                            </span>
368	                        </p>
369	                        <p>"2. Get the joiner's answer back. Either scan their QR with the camera, or paste their text below:"</p>
370	                        <Show when=move || cam_available.get()>
371	                            <p style="margin:0.25rem 0 0.5rem 0">
372	                                <button on:click=move |_| set_scanner_open.set(true)>
373	                                    "📷 Scan answer QR"
374	                                </button>
375	                            </p>
376	                        </Show>
377	                        <textarea
378	                            rows="6"
379	                            style="width:100%;font-family:monospace;font-size:12px"
380	                            on:input=move |ev| {
381	                                let v = ev.target()
382	                                    .and_then(|t| t.dyn_into::<HtmlTextAreaElement>().ok())
383	                                    .map(|el| el.value())
384	                                    .unwrap_or_default();
385	                                set_answer_input.set(v);
386	                            }
387	                            prop:value=move || answer_input.get()
388	                        />
389	                        <button
390	                            on:click=move |_| on_accept.call(())
391	                            disabled=move || !matches!(status.get(), HostStatus::AwaitingAnswer)
392	                        >
393	                            "3. Accept answer"
394	                        </button>
395	                    </Show>
396	                    <QrScanner
397	                        open=scanner_open
398	                        on_decode=Callback::new(move |text: String| {
399	                            set_answer_input.set(text);
400	                            set_scanner_open.set(false);
401	                            // Auto-fire Accept (only if we're in the right state).
402	                            if matches!(status.get_untracked(), HostStatus::AwaitingAnswer) {
403	                                on_accept.call(());
404	                            }
405	                        })
406	                        on_cancel=Callback::new(move |_| set_scanner_open.set(false))
407	                    />
408	                    {move || error_msg.get().map(|e| view! {
409	                        <p style="color:#c33;margin-top:1rem">"ERROR: " {e}</p>
410	                    })}
411	                }
412	            >
413	                {move || play_session.get().map(|s| view! {
414	                    <PlayConnected
415	                        ws_base=lan_dummy_ws_base()
416	                        room="lan-host".to_string()
417	                        password=None
418	                        watch_only=false
419	                        debug_enabled=false
420	                        hints_requested=false
421	                        injected_session=s
422	                        back_link_override=app_href("/lan/host")
423	                    />
424	                })}
425	            </Show>
426	        </div>
427	    }
428	}
429	
430	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
431	enum HostStatus {
432	    Idle,
433	    Generating,
434	    AwaitingAnswer,
435	    AcceptingAnswer,
436	    Playing,
437	}
438	// SECTION: LanJoinPage component
439	
440	#[component]
441	pub fn LanJoinPage() -> impl IntoView {
442	    let (status, set_status) = create_signal::<JoinStatus>(JoinStatus::Idle);
443	    let (use_stun, set_use_stun) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
444	    let (offer_input, set_offer_input) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
445	    let (answer_blob, set_answer_blob) = create_signal::<String>(String::new());
446	    let (error_msg, set_error_msg) = create_signal::<Option<String>>(None);
447	
448	    // QR scanner modal state. See LanHostPage for the same pattern.
449	    let (scanner_open, set_scanner_open) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
450	    let (cam_available, set_cam_available) = create_signal::<bool>(false);
451	    spawn_local(async move {
452	        if has_camera().await {
453	            set_cam_available.set(true);
454	        }
455	    });
456	
457	    // Joiner side: store the JoinerHandshake so we can pull its
458	    // session out once the DC opens.
459	    let handshake: Rc<RefCell<Option<JoinerHandshake>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
460	    let (play_session, set_play_session) = create_signal::<Option<Session>>(None);
461	    // Diag-signal holder mirroring the host page. Populated once
462	    // `connect_as_joiner` resolves; drives the inline ICE-state badge
463	    // until the joiner's `handshake` is dropped (at which point the
464	    // PC's event handlers stop firing).
465	    let (ice_diag_holder, set_ice_diag_holder) = create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<IceDiag>>>(None);
466	
467	    // Re-snapshot the answer SDP on every diag fire (candidates,
468	    // connection state, gathering). Mirrors the host page; see
469	    // JoinerHandshake::current_answer.
470	    {
471	        let handshake = handshake.clone();
472	        create_effect(move |_| {
473	            if let Some(sig) = ice_diag_holder.get() {
474	                let _ = sig.get();
475	                if let Some(jh) = handshake.borrow().as_ref() {
476	                    set_answer_blob.set(jh.current_answer().0);
477	                }
478	            }
479	        });
480	    }
481	
482	    // Holder pattern: spawn_local runs without a Leptos owner context,
483	    // so a `create_effect` inside it would be GC'd immediately (no
484	    // subscriptions retained — `state.set(Open)` would fire but no
485	    // effect would react). Solution: define the holder signals and
486	    // the effect at component scope (properly owned), and have
487	    // spawn_local just `set` the holders. The component-scope effect
488	    // re-runs when the holders change AND when the inner state
489	    // signal changes.
490	    let (joiner_state_holder, set_joiner_state_holder) =
491	        create_signal::<Option<ReadSignal<ConnState>>>(None);
492	    let session_holder: Rc<RefCell<Option<Session>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
493	
494	    {
495	        let session_holder = session_holder.clone();
496	        create_effect(move |_| {
497	            // Read both signals to register subscriptions:
498	            //   * joiner_state_holder fires once when handshake completes.
499	            //   * the inner state signal fires when DC opens.
500	            if let Some(state_sig) = joiner_state_holder.get() {
501	                if state_sig.get() == ConnState::Open {
502	                    if let Some(session) = session_holder.borrow().clone() {
503	                        set_play_session.set(Some(session));
504	                        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Playing);
505	                    }
506	                }
507	            }
508	        });
509	    }
510	
511	    let handshake_for_gen = handshake.clone();
512	    let session_holder_for_gen = session_holder.clone();
513	    let on_generate: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
514	        if !matches!(status.get_untracked(), JoinStatus::Idle) {
515	            return;
516	        }
517	        let blob = offer_input.get_untracked();
518	        if blob.trim().is_empty() {
519	            set_error_msg.set(Some("paste the host's offer SDP first".into()));
520	            return;
521	        }
522	        set_error_msg.set(None);
523	        set_status.set(JoinStatus::Generating);
524	        let cfg = WebRtcConfig {
525	            ice_mode: if use_stun.get_untracked() { IceMode::WithStun } else { IceMode::LanOnly },
526	        };
527	        let handshake_slot = handshake_for_gen.clone();
528	        let session_holder = session_holder_for_gen.clone();
529	        spawn_local(async move {
530	            match connect_as_joiner(cfg, OfferBlob(blob)).await {
531	                Ok(jh) => {
532	                    set_answer_blob.set(jh.answer.0.clone());
533	                    let state = jh.session.state;
534	                    set_ice_diag_holder.set(Some(jh.ice_diag));
535	                    *session_holder.borrow_mut() = Some(jh.session.clone());
536	                    *handshake_slot.borrow_mut() = Some(jh);
537	                    set_status.set(JoinStatus::WaitingForOpen);
538	                    // Triggers the component-scope effect to subscribe
539	                    // to the state signal. Once DC opens, that effect
540	                    // flips status → Playing.
541	                    set_joiner_state_holder.set(Some(state));
542	                }
543	                Err(e) => {
544	                    set_error_msg.set(Some(format!("connect_as_joiner failed: {e:?}")));
545	                    set_status.set(JoinStatus::Idle);
546	                }
547	            }
548	        });
549	    });
550	
551	    let on_reset: Callback<()> = Callback::new(move |_: ()| {
552	        if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
553	            let _ = win.location().reload();
554	        }
555	    });
556	
557	    view! {
558	        <div class="lan-page" style="max-width:720px;margin:1.5rem auto;padding:1rem">
559	            <Show
560	                when=move || matches!(status.get(), JoinStatus::Playing)
561	                fallback=move || view! {
562	                    <a href=app_href("/") rel="external" class="back-link">"← Back to picker"</a>
563	                    <h1>"LAN join (WebRTC)"</h1>
564	                    <p class="muted">
565	                        "Paste the host's offer SDP, generate an answer, send it back to the host."
566	                    </p>
567	                    <p class="muted">
568	                        "VPN hint: disable any VPN (Cloudflare WARP, NordVPN, etc.) on BOTH \
569	                         devices before pairing. VPNs replace the real LAN IP with a tunnel \
570	                         address (often 198.18.x.x) and route LAN traffic through the tunnel, \
571	                         which breaks WebRTC's direct device discovery. If the VPN must stay on, \
572	                         configure split-tunnel to bypass: 192.168.0.0/16 + 10.0.0.0/8 + \
573	                         172.16.0.0/12 (private LANs), 172.20.10.0/28 (iOS hotspot), and \
574	                         224.0.0.0/4 (multicast / mDNS)."
575	                    </p>
576	                    <p class="muted">
577	                        "STUN hint: \"Use STUN\" adds public-IP \"srflx\" candidates so peers on \
578	                         different networks (or behind a router that breaks LAN mDNS) can still \
579	                         find each other. Skip it on a healthy same-LAN setup. Must match the \
580	                         host's setting. Doesn't help with symmetric NAT / CGNAT."
581	                    </p>
582	                    <p>
583	                        <button on:click=move |_| on_reset.call(())>"Reset"</button>
584	                        <label style="margin-left:1rem;font-size:13px">
585	                            <input
586	                                type="checkbox"
587	                                prop:checked=move || use_stun.get()
588	                                disabled=move || !matches!(status.get(), JoinStatus::Idle)
589	                                on:change=move |ev| {
590	                                    let v = ev.target()
591	                                        .and_then(|t| t.dyn_into::<web_sys::HtmlInputElement>().ok())
592	                                        .map(|el| el.checked())
593	                                        .unwrap_or(false);
594	                                    set_use_stun.set(v);
595	                                }
596	                            />
597	                            " Use STUN (must match host's setting)"
598	                        </label>
599	                    </p>
600	                    <p>"Status: " {move || format!("{:?}", status.get())}</p>
601	                    <Show when=move || ice_diag_holder.with(|h| h.is_some())>
602	                        <p style="font-size:13px;color:#888;margin-top:-0.5rem">
603	                            {move || ice_diag_holder.get()
604	                                .map(|sig| diag_line(&sig.get()))
605	                                .unwrap_or_default()}
606	                        </p>
607	                    </Show>
608	                    <p>"1. Get the host's offer. Either scan their QR with the camera, or paste their text below:"</p>
609	                    <Show when=move || cam_available.get()>
610	                        <p style="margin:0.25rem 0 0.5rem 0">
611	                            <button
612	                                on:click=move |_| set_scanner_open.set(true)
613	                                disabled=move || !matches!(status.get(), JoinStatus::Idle)
614	                            >
615	                                "📷 Scan offer QR"
616	                            </button>
617	                        </p>
618	                    </Show>
619	                    <textarea
620	                        rows="6"
621	                        style="width:100%;font-family:monospace;font-size:12px"
622	                        on:input=move |ev| {
623	                            let v = ev.target()
624	                                .and_then(|t| t.dyn_into::<HtmlTextAreaElement>().ok())
625	                                .map(|el| el.value())
626	                                .unwrap_or_default();
627	                            set_offer_input.set(v);
628	                        }
629	                        prop:value=move || offer_input.get()
630	                    />
631	                    <button
632	                        on:click=move |_| on_generate.call(())
633	                        disabled=move || !matches!(status.get(), JoinStatus::Idle)
634	                    >
635	                        "2. Generate answer"
636	                    </button>
637	                    <QrScanner
638	                        open=scanner_open
639	                        on_decode=Callback::new(move |text: String| {
640	                            set_offer_input.set(text);
641	                            set_scanner_open.set(false);
642	                            // Auto-fire Generate answer if still idle.
643	                            if matches!(status.get_untracked(), JoinStatus::Idle) {
644	                                on_generate.call(());
645	                            }
646	                        })
647	                        on_cancel=Callback::new(move |_| set_scanner_open.set(false))
648	                    />
649	                    <Show when=move || !answer_blob.with(|s| s.is_empty())>
650	                        <p>"Show this QR back to the host — or copy + AirDrop the text:"</p>
651	                        <QrCodeView
652	                            payload=Signal::derive(move || answer_blob.get())
653	                            label="LAN joiner answer SDP"
654	                        />
655	                        <p style="margin-top:0.75rem">"Or copy as text:"</p>
656	                        <textarea
657	                            rows="6"
658	                            readonly=true
659	                            style="width:100%;font-family:monospace;font-size:12px"
660	                            prop:value=move || answer_blob.get()
661	                        />
662	                        <p>
663	                            <button on:click=move |_| {
664	                                let blob = answer_blob.get();
665	                                if let Some(win) = web_sys::window() {
666	                                    let _ = win.navigator().clipboard().write_text(&blob);
667	                                }
668	                            }>
669	                                "Copy answer"
670	                            </button>
671	                            <span style="margin-left:0.5rem;font-size:12px;color:#666">
672	                                "Bytes: " {move || answer_blob.with(|s| s.len())}
673	                            </span>
674	                        </p>
675	                        <p class="muted" style="font-size:13px">
676	                            "Waiting for host to accept the answer + the DataChannel to open. \
677	                             Once host taps 'Accept answer' on their side, this page should \
678	                             flip to the game view."
679	                        </p>
680	                    </Show>
681	                    {move || error_msg.get().map(|e| view! {
682	                        <p style="color:#c33;margin-top:1rem">"ERROR: " {e}</p>
683	                    })}
684	                }
685	            >
686	                {move || play_session.get().map(|s| view! {
687	                    <PlayConnected
688	                        ws_base=lan_dummy_ws_base()
689	                        room="lan-join".to_string()
690	                        password=None
691	                        watch_only=false
692	                        debug_enabled=false
693	                        hints_requested=false
694	                        injected_session=s
695	                        back_link_override=app_href("/lan/join")
696	                    />
697	                })}
698	            </Show>
699	        </div>
700	    }
701	}
702	
703	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
704	enum JoinStatus {
705	    Idle,
706	    Generating,
707	    WaitingForOpen,
708	    Playing,
709	}
710	// SECTION: shared UI helpers (textarea + Copy button + link back)
711	
712	/// LAN play has no chess-net `WsBase` — but `PlayConnected` still
713	/// requires one for the OnlineSidebar's "back to lobby" path
714	/// (which we override anyway via `back_link_override`). Construct
715	/// a stub `WsBase` whose `base` is empty + `persist_in_urls = false`
716	/// so any code path that DID happen to use it for URL building
717	/// produces something obviously LAN-shaped.
718	fn lan_dummy_ws_base() -> WsBase {
719	    WsBase { base: String::new(), persist_in_urls: false }
720	}
721
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs`

```text
1	//! WebRTC transport for chess-net protocol.
2	//!
3	//! Production successor to the Phase 0 spike in
4	//! `clients/chess-web/src/spike/rtc.rs`. Same underlying plumbing
5	//! (`web_sys::RtcPeerConnection` + reliable+ordered DataChannel +
6	//! mDNS-only ICE), but exposes a clean API matching `transport::ws`:
7	//!
8	//! * `connect_as_joiner(cfg, offer) -> JoinerHandshake` — the
9	//!   joiner consumes the host's offer SDP and produces an answer SDP.
10	//!   Returns the same [`Session`] shape as `transport::ws::connect`,
11	//!   so `pages/play.rs` and `pages/lobby.rs` work unchanged.
12	//! * `connect_as_host(cfg) -> HostHandshake` — the host generates an
13	//!   offer SDP and waits for the joiner's answer. Exposes the raw
14	//!   PeerConnection + DataChannel handles so Phase 4's
15	//!   `host_room.rs` can wrap them with multi-peer `Room` routing.
16	//!
17	//! Out of scope for Phase 3:
18	//! * QR generation / camera scanning (Phase 5).
19	//! * Multi-peer host (`host_room.rs` — Phase 4).
20	//! * Auto-reconnect / signalling fallback (Approach C — P3 follow-up).
21	//!
22	//! Backlog: `backlog/webrtc-lan-pairing.md`. Pitfalls:
23	//! `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`.
24	//!
25	//! ## Dead-code allowance
26	//!
27	//! Phase 4 (`host_room.rs`) and Phase 5 (`pages/lan.rs`) consume
28	//! the public surface of this module — `connect_as_*`, `OfferBlob`,
29	//! `AnswerBlob`, `WebRtcConfig`, `IceMode`. Some helpers (the spike's
30	//! `Mock` sink path, future spectator attach, etc.) remain unused;
31	//! kept for completeness pending Phase 5 polish.
32	#![allow(dead_code)]
33	
34	// === module skeleton — section bodies filled in via edits below ===
35	
36	// SECTION: imports
37	use std::cell::RefCell;
38	use std::rc::Rc;
39	
40	use chess_net::protocol::{ClientMsg, ServerMsg};
41	use js_sys::{Array, Reflect};
42	use leptos::*;
43	use wasm_bindgen::closure::Closure;
44	use wasm_bindgen::{JsCast, JsValue};
45	use wasm_bindgen_futures::JsFuture;
46	use web_sys::{
47	    MessageEvent, RtcConfiguration, RtcDataChannel, RtcDataChannelEvent, RtcDataChannelInit,
48	    RtcDataChannelState, RtcIceConnectionState, RtcIceGatheringState, RtcPeerConnection,
49	    RtcPeerConnectionState, RtcSdpType, RtcSessionDescriptionInit, RtcSignalingState,
50	};
51	
52	use super::{ConnState, Incoming, Session, Transport};
53	// SECTION: public types (IceMode, WebRtcConfig, OfferBlob, AnswerBlob)
54	
55	/// `RtcPeerConnection` ICE configuration.
56	///
57	/// `LanOnly` is the production default — `iceServers: []`, pure mDNS
58	/// `<uuid>.local` host candidates only. No external dependencies.
59	///
60	/// `WithStun` adds a multi-server STUN list for the `srflx` candidates.
61	/// Useful as a fallback when LAN P2P fails (e.g. router blocks WebRTC's
62	/// `<uuid>.local` resolution; see
63	/// `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md`). Server list ordered for
64	/// CN reachability — `stun.miwifi.com` and `stun.qq.com` first, Google
65	/// last (Google STUN is blocked by the GFW from mainland China).
66	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
67	pub enum IceMode {
68	    LanOnly,
69	    WithStun,
70	}
71	
72	/// Knobs for opening a WebRTC session. Defaults to `LanOnly` — the
73	/// "no external dependency" production case.
74	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug)]
75	pub struct WebRtcConfig {
76	    pub ice_mode: IceMode,
77	}
78	
79	impl Default for WebRtcConfig {
80	    fn default() -> Self {
81	        Self { ice_mode: IceMode::LanOnly }
82	    }
83	}
84	
85	/// Maximum time we wait for `iceGatheringState == "complete"` before
86	/// giving up and producing SDP with whatever candidates have arrived.
87	///
88	/// Caps the failure mode where one of the configured STUN servers is
89	/// unreachable (e.g. `stun.l.google.com` from mainland China — blocked
90	/// by the GFW), which otherwise hangs the whole `connect_as_*` flow
91	/// indefinitely.
92	///
93	/// 5s is generous: pure-LAN gathering completes in 100-200 ms, healthy
94	/// STUN gathering in <1 s. If we hit the timeout we still produce a
95	/// valid SDP — there will just be fewer candidates than "ideal".
96	const ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS: i32 = 5000;
97	
98	/// Typed wrapper around the host's offer SDP blob (JSON envelope of
99	/// `{type:"offer", sdp:"..."}`). Prevents accidentally passing an
100	/// answer where an offer is expected.
101	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
102	pub struct OfferBlob(pub String);
103	
104	/// Typed wrapper around the joiner's answer SDP blob.
105	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
106	pub struct AnswerBlob(pub String);
107	
108	/// Live diagnostic snapshot of the underlying RTCPeerConnection. Exposed
109	/// to the LAN host/join pages so they can show users WHY pairing is
110	/// stuck (e.g. "ICE: failed, candidates: 1") instead of just timing out
111	/// with no insight. Updated by listeners installed in
112	/// `install_ice_diag_handlers` + the gather-state listener inside
113	/// `wait_for_ice_complete`.
114	///
115	/// The cross-device LAN failure documented in
116	/// `pitfalls/webrtc-mdns-lan-ap-isolation.md` shows up here as
117	/// `ice = Checking` flipping to `Disconnected`/`Failed` while the page
118	/// is staring at the 10 s DC-open wait.
119	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
120	pub struct IceDiag {
121	    pub ice: RtcIceConnectionState,
122	    pub conn: RtcPeerConnectionState,
123	    pub gather: RtcIceGatheringState,
124	    /// Count of non-null `icecandidate` events fired since handler
125	    /// installation. A pure-`.local`-only offer typically lands at 1;
126	    /// healthy STUN adds 1–3 srflx candidates on top.
127	    pub candidates: u32,
128	}
129	
130	impl IceDiag {
131	    pub fn initial(pc: &RtcPeerConnection) -> Self {
132	        Self {
133	            ice: pc.ice_connection_state(),
134	            conn: pc.connection_state(),
135	            gather: pc.ice_gathering_state(),
136	            candidates: 0,
137	        }
138	    }
139	}
140	// SECTION: WebRtcTransport (impl Transport)
141	
142	/// `Transport` impl backed by a single `RtcDataChannel`. Shared by both
143	/// joiner and host sides — the same DataChannel just carries different
144	/// payloads in each direction (joiner sends `ClientMsg`, receives
145	/// `ServerMsg`; host the inverse, but Phase 4 wraps it differently
146	/// rather than reusing this trait directly for the host send path).
147	pub struct WebRtcTransport {
148	    /// `Rc<RefCell<...>>` so the DataChannel reference can be late-bound
149	    /// (joiner side discovers it via `ondatachannel` when the host's
150	    /// channel arrives, after `connect_as_joiner` returns).
151	    dc: Rc<RefCell<Option<RtcDataChannel>>>,
152	}
153	
154	impl Transport for WebRtcTransport {
155	    fn send(&self, msg: ClientMsg) -> bool {
156	        let dc = self.dc.borrow();
157	        let dc = match dc.as_ref() {
158	            Some(dc) => dc,
159	            None => return false,
160	        };
161	        if dc.ready_state() != RtcDataChannelState::Open {
162	            return false;
163	        }
164	        let body = match serde_json::to_string(&msg) {
165	            Ok(s) => s,
166	            Err(_) => return false,
167	        };
168	        dc.send_with_str(&body).is_ok()
169	    }
170	}
171	// SECTION: HostHandshake
172	
173	/// What `connect_as_host` returns.
174	///
175	/// Phase 4 (`host_room.rs`) consumes this: it owns the `Room` state
176	/// machine, holds the `pc` + `dc` references, and routes incoming
177	/// `ClientMsg`s through `Room::apply` to produce per-peer `ServerMsg`
178	/// fan-outs. Phase 3 just exposes the raw handles + the
179	/// offer/accept-answer dance.
180	///
181	/// Note: there's no `Session` here. The host doesn't consume the
182	/// `Transport` trait directly — it operates one level lower because
183	/// it needs to tag incoming bytes by `PeerId` and demultiplex
184	/// outgoing bytes back to specific peers' DataChannels (Phase 4
185	/// adds spectator channels).
186	pub struct HostHandshake {
187	    pub pc: RtcPeerConnection,
188	    /// Slot for the host-side DataChannel. Populated synchronously
189	    /// during `connect_as_host` (the host creates the channel before
190	    /// generating the offer); consumers may `clone()` to drive it.
191	    pub dc: Rc<RefCell<Option<RtcDataChannel>>>,
192	    pub offer: OfferBlob,
193	    /// Reactive view of the joiner's connection state. `Open` once
194	    /// the DataChannel handshake completes; `Closed`/`Error` on
195	    /// failure. Phase 4's `host_room.rs` watches this to know when
196	    /// to emit `Hello` / detect peer disconnect.
197	    pub state: ReadSignal<ConnState>,
198	    /// Live ICE / connection / gathering snapshot. The LAN host page
199	    /// shows this as a status badge and reads it when the DC-open
200	    /// timeout fires so the error message can name the actual stuck
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs`

```text
200	    /// timeout fires so the error message can name the actual stuck
201	    /// state instead of a generic "network blocked?".
202	    pub ice_diag: ReadSignal<IceDiag>,
203	    /// Wasm-bindgen closures kept alive for the connection's
204	    /// lifetime. Drop the `HostHandshake` and they all get freed.
205	    _keepalive: Vec<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>>,
206	}
207	
208	impl HostHandshake {
209	    /// Apply the joiner's answer SDP to complete the handshake.
210	    ///
211	    /// Pre-flights `signalingState` so we surface "the joiner's
212	    /// answer is stale" / "you accepted twice" before throwing the
213	    /// browser's cryptic `Called in wrong state` error. See
214	    /// `pitfalls/...` (the iOS-backgrounding pitfall, when written).
215	    pub async fn accept_answer(&self, answer: AnswerBlob) -> Result<(), JsValue> {
216	        let signaling = self.pc.signaling_state();
217	        if signaling != RtcSignalingState::HaveLocalOffer {
218	            return Err(JsValue::from_str(&format!(
219	                "PeerConnection signaling state is `{signaling:?}` (expected `HaveLocalOffer`). \
220	                 The host's offer may have been replaced (multiple Start hosting taps), \
221	                 the page was paused (iOS Safari backgrounding tears down RTC), or this \
222	                 answer was already accepted."
223	            )));
224	        }
225	        let answer_sdp = decode_answer(&answer)?;
226	        let answer_desc = RtcSessionDescriptionInit::new(RtcSdpType::Answer);
227	        answer_desc.set_sdp(&answer_sdp);
228	        JsFuture::from(self.pc.set_remote_description(&answer_desc)).await?;
229	        Ok(())
230	    }
231	
232	    /// Re-encode `pc.local_description()` as an offer blob right now.
233	    ///
234	    /// `connect_as_host` returns once `wait_for_ice_complete` resolves,
235	    /// but the browser may continue adding ICE candidates in the
236	    /// background (especially trickle-srflx if STUN is enabled and the
237	    /// initial 5s gather timeout fired). The page's "Copy offer" button
238	    /// calls this so the textarea / QR reflect those late arrivals
239	    /// instead of the half-gathered snapshot frozen at handshake time.
240	    pub fn current_offer(&self) -> OfferBlob {
241	        let sdp = self.pc.local_description().map(|d| d.sdp()).unwrap_or_default();
242	        OfferBlob(encode_sdp("offer", &sdp))
243	    }
244	}
245	// SECTION: JoinerHandshake (return value of connect_as_joiner)
246	
247	/// What `connect_as_joiner` returns.
248	///
249	/// `session` is a normal [`Session`] — drop it into `pages/play.rs`
250	/// or `pages/lobby.rs` exactly where the WebSocket `Session` would
251	/// have gone.
252	///
253	/// `answer` is the SDP blob to deliver back to the host out-of-band
254	/// (paste into a textarea / scan via QR / whatever the higher-level
255	/// flow uses).
256	pub struct JoinerHandshake {
257	    pub session: Session,
258	    pub answer: AnswerBlob,
259	    /// `RtcPeerConnection` reference — kept alive in the page so the
260	    /// connection survives. Wraps in `Rc<RefCell<...>>` to match the
261	    /// host side (if a future caller wants to e.g. close it
262	    /// proactively).
263	    pub pc: Rc<RtcPeerConnection>,
264	    /// Same role as [`HostHandshake::ice_diag`] — exposed to the LAN
265	    /// join page for diagnostic UI / late-candidate-aware Copy.
266	    pub ice_diag: ReadSignal<IceDiag>,
267	    _keepalive: Vec<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>>,
268	}
269	
270	impl JoinerHandshake {
271	    /// Re-encode `pc.local_description()` as an answer blob right now.
272	    /// See [`HostHandshake::current_offer`] for the rationale.
273	    pub fn current_answer(&self) -> AnswerBlob {
274	        let sdp = self.pc.local_description().map(|d| d.sdp()).unwrap_or_default();
275	        AnswerBlob(encode_sdp("answer", &sdp))
276	    }
277	}
278	// SECTION: connect_as_joiner factory
279	
280	/// Open a WebRTC session as the joiner.
281	///
282	/// Steps:
283	/// 1. Create `RtcPeerConnection` with `cfg.ice_mode`.
284	/// 2. Wire `ondatachannel` so we receive the host's DataChannel.
285	/// 3. Apply the host's offer SDP via `setRemoteDescription`.
286	/// 4. Generate an answer SDP via `createAnswer` + `setLocalDescription`.
287	/// 5. Wait up to [`ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS`] for ICE gathering.
288	/// 6. Return the answer SDP + a [`Session`] backed by the DataChannel.
289	pub async fn connect_as_joiner(
290	    cfg: WebRtcConfig,
291	    offer: OfferBlob,
292	) -> Result<JoinerHandshake, JsValue> {
293	    let pc = new_peer_connection(cfg.ice_mode)?;
294	    let pc_rc = Rc::new(pc);
295	    let dc_holder: Rc<RefCell<Option<RtcDataChannel>>> = Rc::new(RefCell::new(None));
296	    let mut keepalive: Vec<Closure<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>> = Vec::new();
297	
298	    // `incoming` is a queue (push from DC onmessage, page drains via
299	    // tick signal). See `transport::Incoming` doc for the rationale.
300	    let incoming = Incoming::new();
301	    let (state, set_state) = create_signal(ConnState::Connecting);
302	    let (ice_diag, set_ice_diag) = create_signal(IceDiag::initial(&pc_rc));
303	    install_ice_diag_handlers(&pc_rc, set_ice_diag, &mut keepalive);
304	
305	    // The DataChannel arrives on `ondatachannel` after the host's
306	    // SDP is applied. Wire it up before we set the remote description
307	    // so we don't miss the event.
308	    {
309	        let dc_holder = dc_holder.clone();
310	        let incoming_for_dc = incoming.clone();
311	        let cb = Closure::wrap(Box::new(move |ev: JsValue| {
312	            let ev: RtcDataChannelEvent = ev.unchecked_into();
313	            let dc = ev.channel();
314	            install_dc_handlers_for_joiner(&dc, incoming_for_dc.clone(), set_state);
315	            *dc_holder.borrow_mut() = Some(dc);
316	        }) as Box<dyn FnMut(JsValue)>);
317	        pc_rc.set_ondatachannel(Some(cb.as_ref().unchecked_ref()));
318	        keepalive.push(cb);
319	    }
320	
321	    let offer_sdp = decode_offer(&offer)?;
322	    let offer_desc = RtcSessionDescriptionInit::new(RtcSdpType::Offer);
323	    offer_desc.set_sdp(&offer_sdp);
324	    JsFuture::from(pc_rc.set_remote_description(&offer_desc)).await?;
325	
326	    let answer = JsFuture::from(pc_rc.create_answer()).await?;
327	    let answer_desc: RtcSessionDescriptionInit = answer.unchecked_into();
328	    JsFuture::from(pc_rc.set_local_description(&answer_desc)).await?;
329	    wait_for_ice_complete(&pc_rc, set_ice_diag, &mut keepalive).await;
330	
331	    let sdp = pc_rc.local_description().map(|d| d.sdp()).unwrap_or_default();
332	    let answer_blob = AnswerBlob(encode_sdp("answer", &sdp));
333	
334	    let handle: Rc<dyn Transport> = Rc::new(WebRtcTransport { dc: dc_holder });
335	    let session = Session { handle, incoming, state };
336	    Ok(JoinerHandshake { session, answer: answer_blob, pc: pc_rc, ice_diag, _keepalive: keepalive })
337	}
338	// SECTION: connect_as_host factory
339	
340	/// Open a WebRTC session as the host.
341	///
342	/// Steps:
343	/// 1. Create `RtcPeerConnection` with `cfg.ice_mode`.
344	/// 2. Create the DataChannel (host always initiates; joiner discovers
345	///    via `ondatachannel`).
346	/// 3. Generate an offer SDP via `createOffer` + `setLocalDescription`.
347	/// 4. Wait up to [`ICE_GATHER_TIMEOUT_MS`] for ICE gathering.
348	/// 5. Return the offer SDP. Caller delivers it out-of-band, then calls
349	///    `HostHandshake::accept_answer` with the joiner's response.
```
</details></tool-use>
