<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs`

```text
1	//! Wire protocol between `chess-net-server` and clients.
2	//!
3	//! Frames are JSON text frames, one message per frame. Tagged enums via
4	//! `#[serde(tag = "type")]` keep the schema obvious in `wscat`/`jq`.
5	//!
6	//! All payload types come straight from `chess-core` and already derive
7	//! `Serialize/Deserialize`, including the proptest-validated no-leak
8	//! projection in `PlayerView` (hidden banqi pieces stay opaque on the wire).
9	
10	use chess_core::moves::Move;
11	use chess_core::piece::Side;
12	use chess_core::rules::{RuleSet, Variant};
13	use chess_core::view::PlayerView;
14	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
15	
16	/// Bumped whenever the wire schema changes incompatibly.
17	///
18	/// v1 → v2: added `ServerMsg::Rooms` and `ClientMsg::ListRooms` for the
19	/// multi-room lobby. Old (v1) clients still work against a v2 server in
20	/// the default room "main" — the new message variants are additive.
21	///
22	/// v2 → v3: added `ServerMsg::{Spectating, ChatHistory, Chat}` and
23	/// `ClientMsg::Chat` for read-only spectators + in-room chat. v2 clients
24	/// keep working as players (they never request spectator role; the new
25	/// chat variants are additive). `RoomSummary` gains a `spectators` field
26	/// with `#[serde(default)]` so v2 lobby clients deserializing a v3 server
27	/// snapshot ignore the new column.
28	///
29	/// v3 → v4: added `PlayerView.in_check: bool` so clients can render a
30	/// "將軍 / CHECK" warning without having to recompute it from the board.
31	/// Wire-compatible — `#[serde(default)]` on the new field means a v3
32	/// client deserializing a v4 message reads `in_check = false` and a v4
33	/// client deserializing a v3 message gets the same default. The bump is
34	/// for documentation, not enforcement; the handshake still uses lenient
35	/// equality. See ADR-0007.
36	///
37	/// v4 → v5: added `hints_allowed: bool` to `RoomSummary`, `Hello`, and
38	/// `Spectating` so clients can decide whether to mount the AI debug /
39	/// hint panel for a given room. Set at room creation time from the
40	/// first joiner's `?hints=1` query parameter, frozen for the room's
41	/// lifetime (same pattern as `password`). Closes the previous
42	/// client-only-`?debug=1` cheat hole — net-mode clients now refuse to
43	/// mount the panel unless the server says hints are allowed in this
44	/// room. Wire-compatible: `#[serde(default)]` on every new field means
45	/// v4 clients deserializing v5 messages see `hints_allowed = false`,
46	/// and v5 clients deserializing v4 messages also see `false`. Local
47	/// (offline / GitHub Pages) `?debug=1` and `?hints=1` are unaffected
48	/// — they bypass the server entirely.
49	pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: u16 = 5;
50	
51	/// Server → client.
52	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
53	#[serde(tag = "type")]
54	pub enum ServerMsg {
55	    /// Sent once per client right after side assignment. Carries the variant
56	    /// (so the client knows what to render) plus the initial `PlayerView`.
57	    /// `hints_allowed` (v5+) reflects whether the room was created with
58	    /// `?hints=1`; clients consult this to decide whether to mount the
59	    /// AI debug / hint panel.
60	    Hello {
61	        protocol: u16,
62	        observer: Side,
63	        rules: RuleSet,
64	        view: PlayerView,
65	        #[serde(default)]
66	        hints_allowed: bool,
67	    },
68	    /// Sent to every seated client after each committed move.
69	    Update { view: PlayerView },
70	    /// Illegal move, room full, malformed message, opponent disconnect, etc.
71	    /// Non-fatal by default — clients should display and keep the connection.
72	    Error { message: String },
73	    /// Pushed on the lobby socket only — initial snapshot on connect, then
74	    /// again whenever any room's state changes (seat insertion / removal /
75	    /// game finish / GC). Game sockets never receive this variant.
76	    Rooms { rooms: Vec<RoomSummary> },
77	    /// Spectator-side counterpart to `Hello` — no `observer` because
78	    /// spectators are not seated. Carries the same `rules` + initial `view`
79	    /// (projected from `Side::RED`'s perspective so banqi hidden tiles stay
80	    /// hidden). Sent immediately after a `?role=spectator` connection
81	    /// upgrades successfully. `hints_allowed` (v5+) — see [`Hello`].
82	    Spectating {
83	        protocol: u16,
84	        rules: RuleSet,
85	        view: PlayerView,
86	        #[serde(default)]
87	        hints_allowed: bool,
88	    },
89	    /// Pushed once right after `Hello` / `Spectating` with the room's chat
90	    /// ring buffer (≤50 lines). Empty `lines` for a fresh room.
91	    ChatHistory { lines: Vec<ChatLine> },
92	    /// Pushed live to every recipient (seats + spectators) on each new chat
93	    /// line. `from` is currently always a player's `Side`; system messages
94	    /// (player joined / left) are deferred — see
95	    /// `backlog/chess-net-system-messages.md`.
96	    Chat { line: ChatLine },
97	}
98	
99	/// Client → server.
100	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
101	#[serde(tag = "type")]
102	pub enum ClientMsg {
103	    /// Submit a move. For `Move::Reveal`, send `revealed: None` — the server
104	    /// fills in the piece identity locally; the wire form stays `None`.
105	    Move { mv: Move },
106	    /// Resign — ends the game with `WinReason::Resignation` for the opponent.
107	    Resign,
108	    /// Request a rematch. Only valid after the current game has ended
109	    /// (Won / Drawn). Server resets the game when both sides have requested
110	    /// and re-sends `Hello` to each. Same observer assignment, same rules,
111	    /// fresh `GameState`.
112	    Rematch,
113	    /// Lobby-only: forces a fresh `Rooms` snapshot to the requester. The
114	    /// server pushes `Rooms` automatically on state changes; this is the
115	    /// manual-refresh button (`r` in the TUI lobby).
116	    ListRooms,
117	    /// Send a chat line. Server enforces players-only (spectators get
118	    /// `Error{"spectators cannot chat"}`), trims to ≤256 chars after stripping
119	    /// control chars, stamps `ts_ms` server-side, and broadcasts the result
120	    /// to every seat + spectator as `ServerMsg::Chat`.
121	    Chat { text: String },
122	}
123	
124	/// One chat line as it appears on the wire and in the per-room ring buffer.
125	/// `ts_ms` is unix milliseconds set server-side so clients don't have to
126	/// agree on a clock.
127	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
128	pub struct ChatLine {
129	    pub from: Side,
130	    pub text: String,
131	    pub ts_ms: u64,
132	}
133	
134	/// One row in the lobby browser. Mirrors what's interesting to a player
135	/// deciding whether to join: variant for setting expectations, seat count
136	/// for "is this room joinable?", `has_password` for the lock icon, and
137	/// `status` so finished rooms stand out from active games.
138	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
139	pub struct RoomSummary {
140	    pub id: String,
141	    pub variant: String,
142	    pub seats: u8,
143	    /// Watching-only clients connected via `?role=spectator`. v2 lobby
144	    /// snapshots omit this field entirely; the `serde(default)` keeps v2
145	    /// clients deserializing a v3 `RoomSummary` cleanly (they just see 0).
146	    #[serde(default)]
147	    pub spectators: u16,
148	    pub has_password: bool,
149	    pub status: RoomStatus,
150	    /// Whether this room was created with `?hints=1` — i.e., AI hints
151	    /// (the in-game debug panel + PV overlay) are sanctioned by the
152	    /// room and visible to BOTH players + spectators. Frozen for the
153	    /// room's lifetime. v4 lobby snapshots omit this field; the
154	    /// `serde(default)` keeps v4 clients reading v5 summaries cleanly
155	    /// (treating the column as `false`).
156	    #[serde(default)]
157	    pub hints_allowed: bool,
158	}
159	
160	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]
161	#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]
162	pub enum RoomStatus {
163	    /// Game has not begun (≤1 seat occupied or ongoing with both seats).
164	    Lobby,
165	    /// Both seats occupied and the game is `Ongoing`.
166	    Playing,
167	    /// `GameStatus::Won` or `GameStatus::Drawn`. The room still exists but
168	    /// requires a rematch (or for both clients to disconnect → GC) to be
169	    /// reusable.
170	    Finished,
171	}
172	
173	/// Stable variant string for `RoomSummary.variant`. Uses kebab-case so the
174	/// JSON looks the same as the chess-tui `--style ascii` / `--style cjk`
175	/// arguments and the `--house` token list.
176	pub fn variant_label(rules: &RuleSet) -> &'static str {
177	    match rules.variant {
178	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
179	            if rules.xiangqi_allow_self_check {
180	                "xiangqi"
181	            } else {
182	                "xiangqi-strict"
183	            }
184	        }
185	        Variant::Banqi => "banqi",
186	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "three-kingdom",
187	    }
188	}
189
```

```text
1	//! Variant-slug parsing for `/local/:variant` plus URL-query rule encoding.
2	//!
3	//! Pure logic — native-testable. The picker page builds a query string from
4	//! its form state via [`build_local_query`]; the local page parses it back
5	//! into a [`RuleSet`] via [`parse_local_rules`] + [`build_rule_set`].
6	
7	use chess_ai::{Difficulty, Randomness, Strategy};
8	use chess_core::piece::Side;
9	use chess_core::rules::{
10	    house, HouseRules, RuleSet, Variant, PRESET_AGGRESSIVE, PRESET_PURIST, PRESET_TAIWAN,
11	};
12	
13	/// Local-page play mode. `Pvp` is the default pass-and-play; `VsAi`
14	/// drives the in-process alpha-beta engine (xiangqi only).
15	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Default)]
16	pub enum PlayMode {
17	    #[default]
18	    Pvp,
19	    VsAi,
20	}
21	
22	pub fn hosting_mode() -> &'static str {
23	    env!("CHESS_WEB_HOSTING")
24	}
25	
26	pub fn is_static_hosting() -> bool {
27	    hosting_mode() == "static"
28	}
29	
30	pub fn base_path() -> &'static str {
31	    env!("CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH")
32	}
33	
34	pub fn router_base() -> Option<&'static str> {
35	    let base = base_path();
36	    if base.is_empty() {
37	        None
38	    } else {
39	        Some(base)
40	    }
41	}
42	
43	pub fn app_href(path: &str) -> String {
44	    app_href_with_base(base_path(), path)
45	}
46	
47	pub fn app_href_with_base(base: &str, path: &str) -> String {
48	    let path = if path.is_empty() { "/" } else { path };
49	    let path = if path.starts_with('/') { path.to_string() } else { format!("/{path}") };
50	    let base = normalize_base_path(base);
51	    if base.is_empty() {
52	        path
53	    } else if path == "/" {
54	        format!("{base}/")
55	    } else {
56	        format!("{base}{path}")
57	    }
58	}
59	
60	fn normalize_base_path(base: &str) -> String {
61	    let trimmed = base.trim().trim_matches('/');
62	    if trimmed.is_empty() {
63	        String::new()
64	    } else {
65	        format!("/{trimmed}")
66	    }
67	}
68	
69	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
70	pub enum WsBaseError {
71	    Empty,
72	    BadScheme,
73	}
74	
75	pub fn normalize_ws_base(raw: &str) -> Result<String, WsBaseError> {
76	    let trimmed = raw.trim().trim_end_matches('/');
77	    if trimmed.is_empty() {
78	        return Err(WsBaseError::Empty);
79	    }
80	    if !(trimmed.starts_with("ws://") || trimmed.starts_with("wss://")) {
81	        return Err(WsBaseError::BadScheme);
82	    }
83	    Ok(trimmed.to_string())
84	}
85	
86	pub fn parse_variant_slug(slug: &str) -> Option<Variant> {
87	    match slug {
88	        "xiangqi" => Some(Variant::Xiangqi),
89	        "banqi" => Some(Variant::Banqi),
90	        "three-kingdom" => Some(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi),
91	        _ => None,
92	    }
93	}
94	
95	pub fn variant_slug(variant: Variant) -> &'static str {
96	    match variant {
97	        Variant::Xiangqi => "xiangqi",
98	        Variant::Banqi => "banqi",
99	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "three-kingdom",
100	    }
101	}
102	
103	/// URL-derived rule choices for `/local/:variant`. Used as an intermediate
104	/// between the picker form and the engine `RuleSet`.
105	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
106	pub struct LocalRulesParams {
107	    /// Xiangqi only. `false` (default) = casual / no self-check filter;
108	    /// `true` = standard rules where leaving your general in check is illegal.
109	    pub strict: bool,
110	    /// Banqi only. Bitflag set; `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` auto-implies
111	    /// `DARK_CAPTURE` after [`house::normalize`] in the parser.
112	    pub house: HouseRules,
113	    /// Banqi only. `None` = engine picks (non-deterministic on native;
114	    /// browser uses `getrandom` JS feature).
115	    pub seed: Option<u64>,
116	    /// Xiangqi only (banqi/three-kingdom ignore). Default `Pvp`.
117	    pub mode: PlayMode,
118	    /// `VsAi` only — which side the AI plays. Default Black (player is Red,
119	    /// the traditional first-mover). Ignored when `mode == Pvp`.
120	    pub ai_side: Side,
121	    /// `VsAi` only — search difficulty. Ignored when `mode == Pvp`.
122	    pub ai_difficulty: Difficulty,
123	    /// `VsAi` only — engine version. Defaults to [`Strategy::default`]
124	    /// (v2 since 2026-05-08). Versioned, switchable, non-overwriting:
125	    /// older strategies stay reachable via `?engine=v1`.
126	    pub ai_strategy: Strategy,
127	    /// `VsAi` only — randomness preset. `None` = use the difficulty
128	    /// default (`Difficulty::default_randomness`); `Some(_)` = explicit
129	    /// override (typically a [`Randomness`] preset like
130	    /// [`Randomness::STRICT`]). URL token: `&variation=strict|subtle|varied|chaotic`.
131	    pub ai_variation: Option<Randomness>,
132	    /// `VsAi` only — search depth override. `None` = use
133	    /// `Difficulty::default_depth` (Easy=1, Normal=3, Hard=4); `Some(N)`
134	    /// = explicit. Capped at `MAX_AI_DEPTH` to keep the worst-case
135	    /// browser response under ~10 s. URL token: `&depth=N`.
136	    pub ai_depth: Option<u8>,
137	    /// `VsAi` only — per-search node-count cap override. `None` = use
138	    /// the engine's default (v5 scales with depth via
139	    /// `chess_ai::search::node_budget_for_depth`; v1-v4 use the flat
140	    /// `NODE_BUDGET = 250_000`). `Some(N)` forces the cap regardless
141	    /// of strategy or depth. Capped at [`MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET`] so a
142	    /// typo'd 9-figure value can't lock the browser tab. URL token:
143	    /// `&budget=N`.
144	    pub ai_node_budget: Option<u32>,
145	    /// `VsAi` only — opens the AI debug panel showing the engine's full
146	    /// scored root-move list, hover-to-highlight on the board, and
147	    /// search metadata (depth, nodes, strategy, randomness). URL token:
148	    /// `&debug=1`. Default off (consumes ~no extra search cost — the
149	    /// scored list is computed regardless; `&debug=1` just exposes it).
150	    pub ai_debug: bool,
151	    /// User-facing alias for [`Self::ai_debug`]: opens the same AI
152	    /// hint / debug panel via the friendlier URL token `&hints=1`.
153	    /// Local mode treats `ai_debug || ai_hints` as the trigger to
154	    /// mount the panel — both flags are functionally equivalent
155	    /// offline (e.g. on the GitHub Pages build). In net mode (`/play/`)
156	    /// neither flag is enough on its own — the server's
157	    /// `hints_allowed` (set at room creation by the first joiner's
158	    /// `?hints=1`) gates the panel for fairness; see `pages/play.rs`.
159	    pub ai_hints: bool,
160	    /// Enables the live AI win-rate display: vertical eval bar attached
161	    /// to the right edge of the board, sidebar `紅 % • 黑 %` badge,
162	    /// per-ply samples cached for the post-game trend chart. Works in
163	    /// both vs-AI and pass-and-play (PvP).
164	    ///
165	    /// Reuses the `chess_ai::analyze` calls the AI move pump and hint
166	    /// pump already make — when this flag is on, the hint pump runs
167	    /// every turn (even without `?hints=1`) so each ply gets sampled.
168	    /// Costs ~100-300 ms WASM per turn at default Hard depth, on top
169	    /// of any existing AI/hint search work.
170	    ///
171	    /// URL token: `&evalbar=1`. Default off.
172	    /// Net mode (`/play/`) currently ignores this flag — see TODO.md
173	    /// "chess-net protocol v6" for the spectator-side broadcast follow-up.
174	    pub ai_evalbar: bool,
175	    /// Xiangqi pass-and-play only. When `true`, Black's piece glyphs are
176	    /// rendered rotated 180° so a player sitting on the opposite side of
177	    /// the device reads their pieces upright. Coordinate system is
178	    /// unchanged. Ignored when `mode == VsAi`, banqi, or three-kingdom.
179	    /// URL token: `&mirror=1`.
180	    pub mirror: bool,
181	}
182	
183	/// Hard cap on user-supplied depth. Picked so that browser WASM v3 Hard
184	/// at depth 8 (~5 s) is the worst tolerable response time. Higher than
185	/// 8 essentially demands iterative deepening + time budget — that's the
186	/// v5 roadmap entry, not a knob to twist.
187	pub const MAX_AI_DEPTH: u8 = 10;
188	
189	/// Hard cap on user-supplied node budget. Picked so the worst-case v5
190	/// search at MAX_AI_DEPTH stays under ~30 s on a typical 2024 laptop
191	/// running WASM (the engine's own auto-scaled cap is 16M, so 64M
192	/// gives the user 4× headroom for "go really deep on a big machine"
193	/// scenarios but still bounds the worst case). The picker's number
194	/// input clamps to this value at parse time.
195	pub const MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET: u32 = 64_000_000;
196	
197	impl Default for LocalRulesParams {
198	    fn default() -> Self {
199	        Self {
200	            strict: false,
201	            house: HouseRules::empty(),
202	            seed: None,
203	            mode: PlayMode::Pvp,
204	            ai_side: Side::BLACK,
205	            ai_difficulty: Difficulty::Normal,
206	            ai_strategy: Strategy::default(),
207	            ai_variation: None,
208	            ai_depth: None,
209	            ai_node_budget: None,
210	            ai_debug: false,
211	            ai_hints: false,
212	            ai_evalbar: false,
213	            mirror: false,
214	        }
215	    }
216	}
217	
218	/// Parse `?strict=1&house=chain,rush&preset=taiwan&seed=42` into a
219	/// [`LocalRulesParams`]. Unknown tokens are silently dropped — this is a
220	/// best-effort decoder for shareable URLs.
221	///
222	/// `house=` overrides `preset=` when both are present; the picker only
223	/// emits `house=` so this just covers manually-typed URLs.
224	pub fn parse_local_rules(get: impl Fn(&str) -> Option<String>) -> LocalRulesParams {
225	    let strict = matches!(get("strict").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true"));
226	    let preset = get("preset").as_deref().and_then(parse_preset_token);
227	    let house_csv = get("house").as_deref().map(parse_house_csv);
228	    let house = house_csv.or(preset).unwrap_or_else(HouseRules::empty);
229	    let seed = get("seed").and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());
230	    let mode = match get("mode").as_deref() {
231	        Some("ai") | Some("vsai") => PlayMode::VsAi,
232	        _ => PlayMode::Pvp,
233	    };
234	    let ai_side = match get("ai").as_deref() {
235	        Some("red") => Side::RED,
236	        _ => Side::BLACK,
237	    };
238	    let ai_difficulty =
239	        get("diff").as_deref().and_then(Difficulty::parse).unwrap_or(Difficulty::Normal);
240	    let ai_strategy = get("engine").as_deref().and_then(Strategy::parse).unwrap_or_default();
241	    let ai_variation = get("variation").as_deref().and_then(Randomness::parse);
242	    let ai_depth = get("depth")
243	        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())
244	        .map(|d| d.clamp(1, MAX_AI_DEPTH as u32) as u8);
245	    let ai_node_budget = get("budget").and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok()).map(|b| {
246	        // Parse as u64 so a typo'd 11-digit value clamps to the cap
247	        // rather than failing to parse and silently falling back to
248	        // None (`u32::parse` overflows above ~4.29B).
249	        b.clamp(1, MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET as u64) as u32
250	    });
251	    let ai_debug = matches!(get("debug").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
252	    let ai_hints = matches!(get("hints").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
253	    let ai_evalbar = matches!(get("evalbar").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
254	    let mirror = matches!(get("mirror").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
255	    LocalRulesParams {
256	        strict,
257	        house: house::normalize(house),
258	        seed,
259	        mode,
260	        ai_side,
261	        ai_difficulty,
262	        ai_strategy,
263	        ai_variation,
264	        ai_depth,
265	        ai_node_budget,
266	        ai_debug,
267	        ai_hints,
268	        ai_evalbar,
269	        mirror,
270	    }
271	}
272	
273	/// Inverse of [`parse_local_rules`] — emits a stable canonical query string
274	/// (no leading `?`). Empty result means "use defaults" — the picker drops the
275	/// `?` entirely so `/local/xiangqi` with no query is the default-casual URL.
276	pub fn build_local_query(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
277	    let mut parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
278	    match variant {
279	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
280	            if params.strict {
281	                parts.push("strict=1".to_string());
282	            }
283	            if params.mode == PlayMode::VsAi {
284	                parts.push("mode=ai".to_string());
285	                parts.push(format!(
286	                    "ai={}",
287	                    if params.ai_side == Side::RED { "red" } else { "black" }
288	                ));
289	                parts.push(format!("diff={}", params.ai_difficulty.as_str()));
290	                // Only emit engine= when the user picked a non-default
291	                // strategy. Keeps the canonical short URL unchanged for
292	                // the recommended setup.
293	                if params.ai_strategy != Strategy::default() {
294	                    parts.push(format!("engine={}", params.ai_strategy.as_str()));
295	                }
296	                // Only emit variation= when the user explicitly overrode
297	                // the difficulty default (and the override is one of the
298	                // known presets — custom Randomness values can't be
299	                // round-tripped through the URL).
300	                if let Some(name) = params.ai_variation.and_then(|r| r.preset_name()) {
301	                    parts.push(format!("variation={}", name));
302	                }
303	                // Only emit depth= when the user overrode the difficulty's
304	                // default. Allows the canonical short URL for the
305	                // recommended setup to stay short.
306	                if let Some(d) = params.ai_depth {
307	                    parts.push(format!("depth={}", d));
308	                }
309	                // Only emit budget= when the user explicitly overrode
310	                // the engine's default node-count cap. Keeps the
311	                // canonical "I'm using a preset difficulty" URL short.
312	                if let Some(b) = params.ai_node_budget {
313	                    parts.push(format!("budget={}", b));
314	                }
315	                // Debug panel toggle (vs-AI only — debug shows the AI's
316	                // own POV cached after each AI move; meaningless in PvP
317	                // where there's no AI move pump to fill the cache).
318	                if params.ai_debug {
319	                    parts.push("debug=1".to_string());
320	                }
321	            }
322	            // Hint mode toggle is emitted REGARDLESS of mode — hints
323	            // work in both vs-AI (analysis from human's POV when it's
324	            // their turn) AND pass-and-play (analysis from current
325	            // side-to-move's POV — both humans can ask the bot for
326	            // advice on every move). If we only emitted hints=1 inside
327	            // the VsAi block above, the picker's PvP-mode + checked
328	            // hints box would silently drop the flag and the player
329	            // would see no panel after clicking Start.
330	            if params.ai_hints {
331	                parts.push("hints=1".to_string());
332	            }
333	            // Win-rate display flag — same reason as hints=1 above:
334	            // works in both vs-AI and PvP, so emitted unconditionally
335	            // (when set). The local mode pages then thread it through
336	            // the same hint pump that powers `?hints=1`.
337	            if params.ai_evalbar {
338	                parts.push("evalbar=1".to_string());
339	            }
340	            // Mirror is pass-and-play only — vs-AI has no opponent on
341	            // the far side of the device, so it would just confuse the
342	            // single human. We still allow `?mirror=1` to round-trip
343	            // when set even with mode=ai (so URL hand-edits don't get
344	            // silently dropped on parse), but the picker only offers
345	            // the checkbox when mode == Pvp.
346	            if params.mirror && params.mode == PlayMode::Pvp {
347	                parts.push("mirror=1".to_string());
348	            }
349	        }
350	        Variant::Banqi => {
351	            let csv = house_csv(params.house);
352	            if !csv.is_empty() {
353	                parts.push(format!("house={}", csv));
354	            }
355	            if let Some(seed) = params.seed {
356	                parts.push(format!("seed={}", seed));
357	            }
358	        }
359	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {}
360	    }
361	    parts.join("&")
362	}
363	
364	/// Build the full path for the picker's "Start" link given the current form.
365	/// Always returns `"/local/<slug>"` with the query string appended only when
366	/// non-empty.
367	pub fn build_local_href(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
368	    let q = build_local_query(variant, params);
369	    if q.is_empty() {
370	        format!("/local/{}", variant_slug(variant))
371	    } else {
372	        format!("/local/{}?{}", variant_slug(variant), q)
373	    }
374	}
375	
376	/// Convert parsed [`LocalRulesParams`] into the engine `RuleSet` for the
377	/// chosen variant. The picker → URL → page flow is the only call site.
378	pub fn build_rule_set(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> RuleSet {
379	    match variant {
380	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
381	            if params.strict {
382	                RuleSet::xiangqi()
383	            } else {
384	                RuleSet::xiangqi_casual()
385	            }
386	        }
387	        Variant::Banqi => match params.seed {
388	            Some(seed) => RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(params.house, seed),
389	            None => RuleSet::banqi(params.house),
390	        },
391	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => RuleSet::three_kingdom(),
392	    }
393	}
394	
395	fn parse_house_csv(s: &str) -> HouseRules {
396	    let mut out = HouseRules::empty();
397	    for tok in s.split(',').map(str::trim).filter(|t| !t.is_empty()) {
398	        if let Some(flag) = parse_house_token(tok) {
399	            out.insert(flag);
400	        }
401	    }
402	    out
403	}
404	
405	fn house_csv(rules: HouseRules) -> String {
406	    let mut parts: Vec<&'static str> = Vec::new();
407	    for (flag, tok) in HOUSE_TOKENS {
408	        if rules.contains(*flag) {
409	            parts.push(tok);
410	        }
411	    }
412	    parts.join(",")
413	}
414	
415	fn parse_house_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
416	    HOUSE_TOKENS
417	        .iter()
418	        .chain(HOUSE_TOKEN_ALIASES.iter())
419	        .find(|(_, t)| t.eq_ignore_ascii_case(tok))
420	        .map(|(f, _)| *f)
421	}
422	
423	fn parse_preset_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
424	    match tok.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
425	        "purist" => Some(PRESET_PURIST),
426	        "taiwan" => Some(PRESET_TAIWAN),
427	        "aggressive" => Some(PRESET_AGGRESSIVE),
428	        _ => None,
429	    }
430	}
431	
432	const HOUSE_TOKENS: &[(HouseRules, &str)] = &[
433	    (HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, "chain"),
434	    (HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, "dark"),
435	    (HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH, "rush"),
436	    (HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, "horse"),
437	    (HouseRules::CANNON_FAST_MOVE, "cannon"),
438	    (HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE, "dark-trade"),
439	];
440	
441	/// Aliases recognised on parse only (older URLs / pre-rename snapshots).
442	/// Encoding uses the canonical `HOUSE_TOKENS` only.
443	const HOUSE_TOKEN_ALIASES: &[(HouseRules, &str)] = &[(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, "dark-chain")];
444	
445	#[cfg(test)]
446	mod tests {
447	    use super::*;
448	
449	    fn from_pairs<'a>(pairs: &'a [(&'a str, &'a str)]) -> impl Fn(&str) -> Option<String> + 'a {
450	        move |k: &str| pairs.iter().find(|(pk, _)| *pk == k).map(|(_, v)| v.to_string())
451	    }
452	
453	    #[test]
454	    fn known_slugs_parse() {
455	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("xiangqi"), Some(Variant::Xiangqi)));
456	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("banqi"), Some(Variant::Banqi)));
457	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("three-kingdom"), Some(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi)));
458	    }
459	
460	    #[test]
461	    fn unknown_slug_returns_none() {
462	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("chess").is_none());
463	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("").is_none());
464	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("XIANGQI").is_none());
465	    }
466	
467	    #[test]
468	    fn slug_round_trip() {
469	        for v in [Variant::Xiangqi, Variant::Banqi, Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi] {
470	            assert_eq!(parse_variant_slug(variant_slug(v)), Some(v));
471	        }
472	    }
473	
474	    #[test]
475	    fn xiangqi_default_is_casual() {
476	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[]));
477	        assert!(!p.strict);
478	        let r = build_rule_set(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
479	        assert!(r.xiangqi_allow_self_check);
480	    }
481	
482	    #[test]
483	    fn xiangqi_strict_query_yields_strict_ruleset() {
484	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("strict", "1")]));
485	        assert!(p.strict);

548	    #[test]
549	    fn app_href_adds_base_path() {
550	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("", "/local/xiangqi"), "/local/xiangqi");
551	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("/", "/local/xiangqi"), "/local/xiangqi");
552	        assert_eq!(
553	            app_href_with_base("/chinese-chess", "/local/xiangqi"),
554	            "/chinese-chess/local/xiangqi"
555	        );
556	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("chinese-chess", "lobby"), "/chinese-chess/lobby");
557	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("/chinese-chess/", "/"), "/chinese-chess/");
558	        assert_eq!(
559	            app_href_with_base("/chinese-chess", "/local/banqi?seed=7"),
560	            "/chinese-chess/local/banqi?seed=7"
561	        );
562	    }
563	
564	    #[test]
565	    fn ws_base_normalizes_websocket_urls_only() {
566	        assert_eq!(
567	            normalize_ws_base(" wss://example.com/ws-root/ "),
568	            Ok("wss://example.com/ws-root".to_string())
569	        );
570	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base("ws://127.0.0.1:7878"), Ok("ws://127.0.0.1:7878".to_string()));
571	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base(""), Err(WsBaseError::Empty));
572	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base("https://example.com"), Err(WsBaseError::BadScheme));
573	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base("/ws"), Err(WsBaseError::BadScheme));
574	    }

798	        assert!(p.ai_debug && p.ai_hints);

800	        assert!(q.contains("debug=1") && q.contains("hints=1"), "both kept: {}", q);

811	    #[test]
812	    fn xiangqi_pvp_hints_emitted_even_without_ai_mode() {
813	        // Regression: previously `hints=1` lived inside the
814	        // `if mode == VsAi` block in build_local_query, so a user
815	        // checking the picker's "🧠 Allow AI hints" box in PvP mode
816	        // would get a `/local/xiangqi` URL with no `hints=1`, and the
817	        // game page would show no panel. Hints work in PvP (analysis
818	        // for current side-to-move) so the flag must round-trip.
819	        let p = LocalRulesParams { mode: PlayMode::Pvp, ai_hints: true, ..Default::default() };
820	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
821	        assert!(q.contains("hints=1"), "PvP+hints must round-trip; got {:?}", q);
822	        // mode=ai must NOT appear (we're in Pvp).
823	        assert!(!q.contains("mode=ai"), "PvP must not emit mode=ai; got {:?}", q);
824	    }
825	
826	    #[test]
827	    fn xiangqi_pvp_hints_round_trips() {
828	        // Full encode → decode round-trip for PvP+hints (the no-AI
829	        // pass-and-play path that was previously broken).
830	        let p = LocalRulesParams { mode: PlayMode::Pvp, ai_hints: true, ..Default::default() };
831	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
832	        let pairs: Vec<(String, String)> = q
833	            .split('&')
834	            .filter_map(|kv| kv.split_once('=').map(|(k, v)| (k.to_string(), v.to_string())))
835	            .collect();
836	        let p2 =
837	            parse_local_rules(|k| pairs.iter().find(|(pk, _)| pk == k).map(|(_, v)| v.clone()));
838	        assert!(p2.ai_hints);
839	        assert_eq!(p2.mode, PlayMode::Pvp);
840	    }
```

## Inventory of Reusable Components & Logic

**Reusable Pure-Logic Functions in `routes.rs`:**

1. **`parse_local_rules(query_fn) → LocalRulesParams`** (lines 224-271)
   - Parses URL query string (`strict=`, `house=`, `preset=`, `seed=`, `mode=`, etc.) into a typed struct
   - Fully pure; takes closure for flexible query-string sourcing
   - Returns `LocalRulesParams` — a shareable intermediate struct with all rule + AI config

2. **`build_local_query(variant, params) → String`** (lines 276-362)
   - Inverse encoder: `LocalRulesParams` → canonical query string
   - Variant-aware (Xiangqi vs Banqi vs ThreeKingdom have different rules)
   - Pure logic; no state

3. **`build_rule_set(variant, params) → RuleSet`** (lines 378-393)
   - Converts `LocalRulesParams` directly to engine `RuleSet`
   - **Critical**: This is cleanly separable — you can call it directly from a LAN form without URL roundtripping
   - Handles seed-based banqi initialization and Xiangqi casual/strict branching

**Key Intermediate Type:**
- **`LocalRulesParams`** (lines 106-181) — a standalone struct with 13 fields covering all rule choices
  - Contains variant-agnostic flags (`strict`, `house`, `seed`, `mode`) and AI overrides (`ai_difficulty`, `ai_depth`, etc.)
  - **Shareable**: No signals, pure data; can be instantiated directly from UI form signals without URL parsing

---

**UI Structure in `picker.rs`:**

The picker is **NOT a reusable component**. It's heavily inline:
- **XiangqiCard** (lines 158-607): 450-line component with 15+ local signals; builds its own href via `build_local_href()`
- **BanqiCard** (lines 610-712): 100-line variant with 5 signals for house rules + seed
  - Variant + preset selector is **pure UI** — hardcoded buttons ("Purist", "Taiwan", "Aggressive") calling `apply_preset(PRESET_CONSTANT)` closures

**No Extracted Component**: Variant dropdown, rules checkboxes, and preset buttons live inline as JSX. No `VariantSelector` or `RulesForm` component exists.

---

**Display String Helpers:**

There are **no label-generation helpers** in the codebase. The picker uses:
- **Hardcoded strings**: "Taiwan", "Aggressive", "Purist" (lines 658–662)
- **Enum `.as_str()` methods**: `Difficulty::as_str()`, `Strategy::as_str()` (routes.rs lines 289, 294)
- **HouseRules → CSV tokens**: Checked programmatically via bitflags; no reverse lookup from flag → display name exists

---

**State Types for Sharing:**

1. **`LocalRulesParams`** — the sole shareable struct (pure data, no signals)
   - Covers: variant rules, house rule bitflags, seed, play mode, AI config (difficulty, depth, budget, strategy, variation)
   - **Recommendation**: Use directly to drive both picker AND LAN form signals

2. **Signals are NOT shareable** — picker owns 15+ `create_rw_signal()` instances per card
   - To embed rules in LAN setup, clone the **pattern not the signals**: create fresh `RwSignal`s in LAN form, collect them into `LocalRulesParams` on apply

---

**Smallest Extraction for Reuse:**

If you want to avoid duplication:
1. **Extract a `components/rules_form.rs` component** taking:
   - `variant: Signal<Variant>`
   - `on_apply: Callback<LocalRulesParams>`
   - Returns JSX for variant + house rules + seed inputs

2. **Embed in both picker and LAN host**:
   - Picker: collect form into `LocalRulesParams` → call `build_local_href()` to get URL
   - LAN host: collect form into `LocalRulesParams` → call `build_rule_set()` to get `RuleSet` directly (no URL roundtrip)

3. **Share nothing else** — each page owns its own signals and control flow

---

**Critical Separability:**

- `parse_local_rules()` + `build_rule_set()` are **100% decoupled from URLs**
- Call `build_rule_set(Variant::Xiangqi, &params)` directly; the LAN page never needs to generate a URL
- This is the clean cut: picker goes `form → LocalRulesParams → URL → game`, LAN goes `form → LocalRulesParams → RuleSet → HostRoom`

```text
158	            state: GameState::new(rules),
159	            seats: Vec::with_capacity(2),
160	            spectators: Vec::new(),
161	            rematch: Vec::with_capacity(2),
162	            password,
163	            hints_allowed,
164	            chat: VecDeque::with_capacity(CHAT_HISTORY_CAP),

181	        self.seats.len()
182	    }

185	        self.spectators.len()
186	    }
187	
188	    /// `true` when the room has no seated players AND no spectators. The
189	    /// transport uses this to GC user-created rooms (the v1 default room
190	    /// `"main"` is exempt).
191	    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
192	        self.seats.is_empty() && self.spectators.is_empty()
193	    }
194	
195	    /// Side that would be assigned to the next [`Room::join_player`] call,
196	    /// or `None` if the room is full. Useful for transports that want to
197	    /// preview "joinable as Red?" without mutating.
198	    pub fn next_seat(&self) -> Option<Side> {
199	        match self.seats.len() {
200	            0 => Some(Side::RED),
201	            1 => {
202	                let taken = self.seats[0].0;
203	                Some(if taken == Side::RED { Side::BLACK } else { Side::RED })
204	            }
205	            _ => None,
206	        }
207	    }
208	
209	    pub fn summary(&self, id: &str) -> RoomSummary {
210	        let status = match self.state.status {
211	            GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. } => RoomStatus::Finished,
212	            GameStatus::Ongoing => {
213	                if self.seats.len() >= 2 {
214	                    RoomStatus::Playing
215	                } else {
216	                    RoomStatus::Lobby
217	                }
218	            }
219	        };
220	        RoomSummary {
221	            id: id.to_string(),
222	            variant: variant_label(&self.state.rules).to_string(),
223	            seats: self.seats.len() as u8,
224	            spectators: self.spectators.len() as u16,
225	            has_password: self.password.is_some(),
226	            status,
227	            hints_allowed: self.hints_allowed,
228	        }
229	    }
230	
231	    fn chat_history(&self) -> Vec<ChatLine> {
232	        self.chat.iter().cloned().collect()
233	    }
234	
235	    /// What role is this peer in? `None` = unknown peer (e.g. already
236	    /// disconnected, or never joined this room).
237	    fn role_of(&self, peer: PeerId) -> Option<SeatRole> {
238	        if let Some((side, _)) = self.seats.iter().find(|(_, p)| *p == peer) {
239	            return Some(SeatRole::Player(*side));
240	        }
241	        if self.spectators.contains(&peer) {
242	            return Some(SeatRole::Spectator);
243	        }
244	        None
245	    }
246	}
```

```text
33	    OnlyOneSideHasPieces,
34	    Timeout,

36	    /// physically captured rather than ending in checkmate. In standard
37	    /// rules this state is unreachable because the legality filter rejects
38	    /// any move that would leave the general capturable.
39	    GeneralCaptured,
40	}

50	/// Side ↔ piece-color assignment for banqi (set after the first flip).

53	    /// `mapping[side.0 as usize]` = the piece-color that seat controls.

55	}

57	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
58	pub struct GameState {
59	    pub rules: RuleSet,
60	    pub board: Board,
61	    pub side_to_move: Side,
62	    pub turn_order: TurnOrder,
63	    pub history: Vec<MoveRecord>,
64	    pub status: GameStatus,
65	    /// Banqi only: established after first flip. `None` until then.
66	    pub side_assignment: Option<SideAssignment>,
67	    pub no_progress_plies: u16,
68	    /// Banqi 連吃 chain-mode lock. `Some(sq)` means the moving piece at
69	    /// `sq` just made a chain-eligible capture and may continue capturing
70	    /// — the turn does NOT auto-advance until the player issues
71	    /// `Move::EndChain { at: sq }` (or makes another chain-eligible
72	    /// capture which keeps the lock active). `#[serde(default)]` so
73	    /// pre-chain-mode snapshots load.
74	    #[serde(default)]
75	    pub chain_lock: Option<crate::coord::Square>,
76	    /// Zobrist hash of the current position — incrementally updated by
77	    /// [`Self::make_move`] / [`Self::unmake_move`]. Used by the v5+ AI
78	    /// search transposition table and (forthcoming) threefold-repetition
79	    /// detection. See [`zobrist`] module for what is and isn't hashed.
80	    /// `#[serde(default)]` for back-compat: deserialized snapshots get
81	    /// hash 0; call [`Self::recompute_position_hash`] to populate it.
82	    #[serde(default)]
83	    pub position_hash: u64,
84	}
85	
86	impl GameState {
87	    pub fn new(rules: RuleSet) -> Self {
88	        crate::setup::build_initial_state(rules)
89	    }
90	
91	    /// Recompute [`Self::position_hash`] from scratch by scanning the
92	    /// board. Call after constructing a `GameState` from a struct
93	    /// literal, after `serde` deserialization, or after any direct
94	    /// mutation of `board` / `side_to_move` that bypasses the
95	    /// `make_move`/`unmake_move` machinery.
96	    pub fn recompute_position_hash(&mut self) {
97	        self.position_hash = zobrist::compute_position_hash(self);
98	    }
99	
100	    /// XOR a single piece in/out of the running [`Self::position_hash`].
101	    /// Helper for the make/unmake hash maintenance below.
102	    #[inline]
103	    fn xor_piece(&mut self, piece: Piece, sq: crate::coord::Square) {
104	        self.position_hash ^= zobrist::zobrist_piece(piece.side, piece.kind, sq.0 as usize);
105	    }
106	
107	    /// Generate all legal moves for the side to move.
108	    ///
109	    /// When `chain_lock` is set (banqi 連吃 mid-chain), the legal-move
110	    /// list is filtered to capture-only moves originating from the
111	    /// locked square, plus `Move::EndChain` as the explicit terminator.
112	    pub fn legal_moves(&self) -> MoveList {
113	        let mut out = MoveList::new();
114	        crate::rules::generate_moves(self, &mut out);
115	        if let Some(lock) = self.chain_lock {
116	            out.retain(|m| match m {
117	                Move::Capture { from, .. }
118	                | Move::CannonJump { from, .. }
119	                | Move::DarkCapture { from, .. }
120	                | Move::ChainCapture { from, .. } => *from == lock,
121	                _ => false,
122	            });
123	            out.push(Move::EndChain { at: lock });
124	        }
125	        out
126	    }
127	
128	    /// Apply a move. Caller is responsible for legality (use `legal_moves`).
129	    /// Returns the move pushed to history (with `Reveal` payloads filled in).
130	    pub fn make_move(&mut self, m: &Move) -> Result<(), CoreError> {
131	        if matches!(self.status, GameStatus::Won { .. } | GameStatus::Drawn { .. }) {
132	            return Err(CoreError::GameOver);
133	        }
134	        let mover = self.side_to_move;
135	        let no_progress_before = self.no_progress_plies;
136	        let chain_lock_before = self.chain_lock;
137	
138	        // Normalize the move (fill in Reveal payload from board if missing).
139	        let normalized = self.normalize_for_apply(m)?;
140	        self.apply_inner(&normalized)?;
141	
142	        self.history.push(MoveRecord {
143	            mover,
144	            the_move: normalized.clone(),
145	            no_progress_before,
146	            chain_lock_before,
147	        });
148	
149	        if normalized.resets_no_progress() {
150	            self.no_progress_plies = 0;
```

```text
1	//! Initial position builders.
2	//!
3	//! Returns a fully-formed `GameState` for each variant. Banqi shuffling is
4	//! seeded via `ChaCha8Rng` so games are reproducible from `RuleSet::banqi_seed`.
5	
6	use rand::seq::SliceRandom;
7	use rand::SeedableRng;
8	use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng;
9	
10	use crate::board::{Board, BoardShape};
11	use crate::piece::{Piece, PieceKind, PieceOnSquare, Side};
12	use crate::rules::{RuleSet, Variant};
13	use crate::state::{GameState, GameStatus, TurnOrder};
14	
15	pub fn build_initial_state(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
16	    match rules.variant {
17	        Variant::Xiangqi => build_xiangqi(rules),
18	        Variant::Banqi => build_banqi(rules),
19	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => build_three_kingdom_stub(rules),
20	    }
21	}
22	
23	fn build_xiangqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
24	    let mut board = Board::new(BoardShape::Xiangqi9x10);
25	    place_xiangqi_initial(&mut board);
26	    let mut state = GameState {
27	        rules,
28	        board,
29	        side_to_move: Side::RED,
30	        turn_order: TurnOrder::two_player(),
31	        history: Vec::new(),
32	        status: GameStatus::Ongoing,
33	        side_assignment: None,
34	        no_progress_plies: 0,
35	        chain_lock: None,
36	        position_hash: 0,
37	    };
38	    state.recompute_position_hash();
39	    state
40	}
41	
42	/// Place the 32 standard xiangqi pieces. Files indexed 0..9 left-to-right
43	/// from Red's view; ranks 0..9 bottom-to-top with Red on the bottom.
44	fn place_xiangqi_initial(board: &mut Board) {
45	    use crate::coord::{File, Rank};
46	
47	    let mut back = |kind: PieceKind, side: Side, files: &[u8], rank: u8| {
48	        for &f in files {
49	            board.set(
50	                board.sq(File(f), Rank(rank)),
51	                Some(PieceOnSquare::revealed(Piece::new(side, kind))),
52	            );
53	        }
54	    };
55	
56	    // Red back rank (rank 0)
57	    back(PieceKind::Chariot, Side::RED, &[0, 8], 0);
58	    back(PieceKind::Horse, Side::RED, &[1, 7], 0);
59	    back(PieceKind::Elephant, Side::RED, &[2, 6], 0);
60	    back(PieceKind::Advisor, Side::RED, &[3, 5], 0);
61	    back(PieceKind::General, Side::RED, &[4], 0);
62	
63	    // Red cannons (rank 2)
64	    back(PieceKind::Cannon, Side::RED, &[1, 7], 2);
65	
66	    // Red soldiers (rank 3)
67	    back(PieceKind::Soldier, Side::RED, &[0, 2, 4, 6, 8], 3);
68	
69	    // Black mirrors at rank 9, 7, 6
70	    back(PieceKind::Chariot, Side::BLACK, &[0, 8], 9);
71	    back(PieceKind::Horse, Side::BLACK, &[1, 7], 9);
72	    back(PieceKind::Elephant, Side::BLACK, &[2, 6], 9);
73	    back(PieceKind::Advisor, Side::BLACK, &[3, 5], 9);
74	    back(PieceKind::General, Side::BLACK, &[4], 9);
75	    back(PieceKind::Cannon, Side::BLACK, &[1, 7], 7);
76	    back(PieceKind::Soldier, Side::BLACK, &[0, 2, 4, 6, 8], 6);
77	}
78	
79	fn build_banqi(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
80	    let mut board = Board::new(BoardShape::Banqi4x8);
81	    let seed = rules.banqi_seed.unwrap_or_else(|| {
82	        // Nondeterministic in the absence of a seed.
83	        use rand::RngCore;
84	        rand::thread_rng().next_u64()
85	    });
86	    let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(seed);
87	    let mut pieces = banqi_piece_set();
88	    pieces.shuffle(&mut rng);
89	    for (i, p) in pieces.into_iter().enumerate() {
90	        board.set(crate::coord::Square(i as u16), Some(PieceOnSquare::hidden(p)));
91	    }
92	    let mut state = GameState {
93	        rules,
94	        board,
95	        side_to_move: Side::RED,
96	        turn_order: TurnOrder::two_player(),
97	        history: Vec::new(),
98	        status: GameStatus::Ongoing,
99	        side_assignment: None,
100	        no_progress_plies: 0,
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-05-14 07:56:33Z)**_

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Code Map: LAN Game State Initialization & Variant Threading

**Initialization Sites (where GameState/RuleSet is currently hardcoded):**

1. **`clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs:258`** — The host-side GameState creation:
   ```rust
   let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(RuleSet::xiangqi(), None, /* hints */ false);
   ```
   This is the critical line where `Variant::Xiangqi` is hardcoded. The host constructs this after the DataChannel opens but BEFORE the joiner connects. The host immediately seats itself and queues `Hello` + `ChatHistory` into the incoming signal.

2. **`crates/chess-net/src/room.rs:156-166`** — The `Room::new` factory:
   ```rust
   pub fn new(rules: RuleSet, password: Option<String>, hints_allowed: bool) -> Self {
       Self {
           state: GameState::new(rules),
           ...
       }
   }
   ```
   Takes a `RuleSet` (which contains the variant enum and per-variant flags like `xiangqi_allow_self_check`, `banqi_seed`, etc.) and calls `GameState::new(rules)` to build the initial board.

3. **`crates/chess-core/src/setup.rs:15-21`** — The `build_initial_state` dispatcher:
   ```rust
   pub fn build_initial_state(rules: RuleSet) -> GameState {
       match rules.variant {
           Variant::Xiangqi => build_xiangqi(rules),
           Variant::Banqi => build_banqi(rules),
           Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => build_three_kingdom_stub(rules),
       }
   }
   ```
   Banqi shuffles via `ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(rules.banqi_seed.unwrap_or_else(|| rand::thread_rng().next_u64()))` — the seed is part of `RuleSet` and determines the layout deterministically.

---

**Where Variant/Rules Must Be Threaded:**

1. **Host setup page (`LanHostPage`, lines 119–440):** Currently has UI for "Open room" + offer/answer flow but NO variant picker. Must add a form field (dropdown or buttons) for variant + per-variant rule options (xiangqi strict, banqi house flags + optional seed). Store selection in a signal (e.g., `variant_signal`, `rules_params_signal`) and pass to `HostRoom::new()` at line 258.

2. **Join page (`LanJoinPage`, lines 441–717):** Currently receives the offer SDP and generates an answer. The joiner currently has NO visibility into what variant the host chose — it's not communicated until after the DataChannel opens. Must display the variant + rules after DC opens, read from `ServerMsg::Hello` (which already carries `RuleSet`).

3. **WebRTC handshake (`clients/chess-web/src/transport/webrtc.rs`):** The data channel setup (`install_dc_handlers_for_joiner` / `install_dc_state_handlers`) is variant-agnostic — it just shuttles JSON. No changes needed here; the variant info flows through the chess-net protocol message (`ServerMsg::Hello`).

4. **Routes layer (`clients/chess-web/src/app.rs:46-47`):** Currently routes to `/lan/host` and `/lan/join` with no query params. To support variant picker on the host page, either:
   - Add `?variant=xiangqi|banqi|three-kingdom` + rule params to the route (would need parsing in host page), OR
   - Keep the variant choice in-page (simpler; the host never leaves `/lan/host` until pairing succeeds).

---

**Protocol Envelope (Data Channel Payloads):**

- **Type:** `ServerMsg` and `ClientMsg` enums from `crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs`.
- **Variant already in the envelope:** `ServerMsg::Hello` carries:
  ```rust
  Hello {
      protocol: u16,
      observer: Side,
      rules: RuleSet,  // ← includes variant enum + all per-variant flags
      view: PlayerView,
      hints_allowed: bool,
  }
  ```
  The `rules: RuleSet` field includes `Variant` (enum) + variant-specific fields (`xiangqi_allow_self_check`, `banqi_seed`, `house` flags, etc.). **No new message variant needed** — the protocol already carries everything.

- **Joiner receives variant implicitly:** After the DC opens, the joiner gets `ServerMsg::Hello` from the host and deserializes the full `RuleSet`. The joiner's `Room` is never directly instantiated — it only receives `ServerMsg` updates from the host, so it mirrors the host's board state exactly (including banqi's hidden-tile layout, since the host already shuffled deterministically from the seed).

---

**Banqi Seed Handling (Critical for Correctness):**

- **Shuffle happens on the host only:** `build_banqi()` (line 81–91 of setup.rs) uses `rules.banqi_seed` to initialize a `ChaCha8Rng`, shuffles the 32-piece set, and places them face-down on the board.
- **Seed is part of `RuleSet`:** If the host chooses `RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(house_flags, seed_u64)`, both the host and joiner will have the same seed in their `RuleSet.banqi_seed: Option<u64>` field.
- **Joiner does NOT reconstruct:** The joiner receives no direct board state from the host initially — only the `ServerMsg::Hello` with `rules: RuleSet` (which includes the seed). But the joiner's client-side `Room` never calls `GameState::new()` for banqi. Instead, the joiner only receives `ServerMsg::Update` messages containing `PlayerView` (the host's board projected from each player's viewpoint, with hidden pieces opaque). Crucially, **both sides agree on the hidden layout because the host sent the seed in the `Hello` RuleSet`**, so if the joiner ever needed to reconstruct (e.g., for a local replay), it could call `GameState::new(rules)` with that same seed and get the same layout.
- **Current flow is correct:** The host is the single source of truth; the joiner mirrors its state via `Update` projections. No explicit seed-sharing message is needed — it's already in `Hello.rules.banqi_seed`.

---

**LAN Route Shape:**

- **Current routes:** `/lan/host` (Leptos route, no query params) and `/lan/join` (same).
- **No variant param today:** The routes are hardcoded in `clients/chess-web/src/app.rs:46-47`. If you add variant selection to the host page form (in-page signals), no route changes are needed. If you want the variant in the URL for deep-linking or shareable setup URIs, you'd add a query string parser (similar to the `parse_local_rules` in `routes.rs` for the `/local/:variant` pages) and handle it in `LanHostPage`.

---

**Summary — Extension Points:**

To extend LAN mode to all variants, modify **two sites only:**

1. **Host page form (line 119):** Add variant picker + rule options; store in signals; pass to `HostRoom::new(rules, ...)` instead of hardcoded `RuleSet::xiangqi()`.
2. **Join page display (line 441):** After DC opens and `ServerMsg::Hello` arrives, extract and display `rules` from the message so the joiner sees what they're playing.

The protocol and banqi seed handling are already correct — `ServerMsg::Hello` carries the full `RuleSet` with variant and all flags baked in. No new message types needed.
