```text
1	//! Variant-slug parsing for `/local/:variant` plus URL-query rule encoding.
2	//!
3	//! Pure logic — native-testable. The picker page builds a query string from
4	//! its form state via [`build_local_query`]; the local page parses it back
5	//! into a [`RuleSet`] via [`parse_local_rules`] + [`build_rule_set`].
6	
7	use chess_ai::{Difficulty, Randomness, Strategy};
8	use chess_core::piece::Side;
9	use chess_core::rules::{
10	    house, HouseRules, RuleSet, Variant, PRESET_AGGRESSIVE, PRESET_PURIST, PRESET_TAIWAN,
11	};
12	
13	/// Local-page play mode. `Pvp` is the default pass-and-play; `VsAi`
14	/// drives the in-process alpha-beta engine (xiangqi only).
15	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Default)]
16	pub enum PlayMode {
17	    #[default]
18	    Pvp,
19	    VsAi,
20	}
21	
22	pub fn hosting_mode() -> &'static str {
23	    env!("CHESS_WEB_HOSTING")
24	}
25	
26	pub fn is_static_hosting() -> bool {
27	    hosting_mode() == "static"
28	}
29	
30	pub fn base_path() -> &'static str {
31	    env!("CHESS_WEB_BASE_PATH")
32	}
33	
34	pub fn router_base() -> Option<&'static str> {
35	    let base = base_path();
36	    if base.is_empty() {
37	        None
38	    } else {
39	        Some(base)
40	    }
41	}
42	
43	pub fn app_href(path: &str) -> String {
44	    app_href_with_base(base_path(), path)
45	}
46	
47	pub fn app_href_with_base(base: &str, path: &str) -> String {
48	    let path = if path.is_empty() { "/" } else { path };
49	    let path = if path.starts_with('/') { path.to_string() } else { format!("/{path}") };
50	    let base = normalize_base_path(base);
51	    if base.is_empty() {
52	        path
53	    } else if path == "/" {
54	        format!("{base}/")
55	    } else {
56	        format!("{base}{path}")
57	    }
58	}
59	
60	fn normalize_base_path(base: &str) -> String {
61	    let trimmed = base.trim().trim_matches('/');
62	    if trimmed.is_empty() {
63	        String::new()
64	    } else {
65	        format!("/{trimmed}")
66	    }
67	}
68	
69	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
70	pub enum WsBaseError {
71	    Empty,
72	    BadScheme,
73	}
74	
75	pub fn normalize_ws_base(raw: &str) -> Result<String, WsBaseError> {
76	    let trimmed = raw.trim().trim_end_matches('/');
77	    if trimmed.is_empty() {
78	        return Err(WsBaseError::Empty);
79	    }
80	    if !(trimmed.starts_with("ws://") || trimmed.starts_with("wss://")) {
81	        return Err(WsBaseError::BadScheme);
82	    }
83	    Ok(trimmed.to_string())
84	}
85	
86	pub fn parse_variant_slug(slug: &str) -> Option<Variant> {
87	    match slug {
88	        "xiangqi" => Some(Variant::Xiangqi),
89	        "banqi" => Some(Variant::Banqi),
90	        "three-kingdom" => Some(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi),
91	        _ => None,
92	    }
93	}
94	
95	pub fn variant_slug(variant: Variant) -> &'static str {
96	    match variant {
97	        Variant::Xiangqi => "xiangqi",
98	        Variant::Banqi => "banqi",
99	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "three-kingdom",
100	    }
101	}
102	
103	/// URL-derived rule choices for `/local/:variant`. Used as an intermediate
104	/// between the picker form and the engine `RuleSet`.
105	#[derive(Clone, Eq, PartialEq, Debug)]
106	pub struct LocalRulesParams {
107	    /// Xiangqi only. `false` (default) = casual / no self-check filter;
108	    /// `true` = standard rules where leaving your general in check is illegal.
109	    pub strict: bool,
110	    /// Banqi only. Bitflag set; `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE` auto-implies
111	    /// `DARK_CAPTURE` after [`house::normalize`] in the parser.
112	    pub house: HouseRules,
113	    /// Banqi only. `None` = engine picks (non-deterministic on native;
114	    /// browser uses `getrandom` JS feature).
115	    pub seed: Option<u64>,
116	    /// Xiangqi only (banqi/three-kingdom ignore). Default `Pvp`.
117	    pub mode: PlayMode,
118	    /// `VsAi` only — which side the AI plays. Default Black (player is Red,
119	    /// the traditional first-mover). Ignored when `mode == Pvp`.
120	    pub ai_side: Side,
121	    /// `VsAi` only — search difficulty. Ignored when `mode == Pvp`.
122	    pub ai_difficulty: Difficulty,
123	    /// `VsAi` only — engine version. Defaults to [`Strategy::default`]
124	    /// (v2 since 2026-05-08). Versioned, switchable, non-overwriting:
125	    /// older strategies stay reachable via `?engine=v1`.
126	    pub ai_strategy: Strategy,
127	    /// `VsAi` only — randomness preset. `None` = use the difficulty
128	    /// default (`Difficulty::default_randomness`); `Some(_)` = explicit
129	    /// override (typically a [`Randomness`] preset like
130	    /// [`Randomness::STRICT`]). URL token: `&variation=strict|subtle|varied|chaotic`.
131	    pub ai_variation: Option<Randomness>,
132	    /// `VsAi` only — search depth override. `None` = use
133	    /// `Difficulty::default_depth` (Easy=1, Normal=3, Hard=4); `Some(N)`
134	    /// = explicit. Capped at `MAX_AI_DEPTH` to keep the worst-case
135	    /// browser response under ~10 s. URL token: `&depth=N`.
136	    pub ai_depth: Option<u8>,
137	    /// `VsAi` only — per-search node-count cap override. `None` = use
138	    /// the engine's default (v5 scales with depth via
139	    /// `chess_ai::search::node_budget_for_depth`; v1-v4 use the flat
140	    /// `NODE_BUDGET = 250_000`). `Some(N)` forces the cap regardless
141	    /// of strategy or depth. Capped at [`MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET`] so a
142	    /// typo'd 9-figure value can't lock the browser tab. URL token:
143	    /// `&budget=N`.
144	    pub ai_node_budget: Option<u32>,
145	    /// `VsAi` only — opens the AI debug panel showing the engine's full
146	    /// scored root-move list, hover-to-highlight on the board, and
147	    /// search metadata (depth, nodes, strategy, randomness). URL token:
148	    /// `&debug=1`. Default off (consumes ~no extra search cost — the
149	    /// scored list is computed regardless; `&debug=1` just exposes it).
150	    pub ai_debug: bool,
151	    /// User-facing alias for [`Self::ai_debug`]: opens the same AI
152	    /// hint / debug panel via the friendlier URL token `&hints=1`.
153	    /// Local mode treats `ai_debug || ai_hints` as the trigger to
154	    /// mount the panel — both flags are functionally equivalent
155	    /// offline (e.g. on the GitHub Pages build). In net mode (`/play/`)
156	    /// neither flag is enough on its own — the server's
157	    /// `hints_allowed` (set at room creation by the first joiner's
158	    /// `?hints=1`) gates the panel for fairness; see `pages/play.rs`.
159	    pub ai_hints: bool,
160	    /// Enables the live AI win-rate display: vertical eval bar attached
161	    /// to the right edge of the board, sidebar `紅 % • 黑 %` badge,
162	    /// per-ply samples cached for the post-game trend chart. Works in
163	    /// both vs-AI and pass-and-play (PvP).
164	    ///
165	    /// Reuses the `chess_ai::analyze` calls the AI move pump and hint
166	    /// pump already make — when this flag is on, the hint pump runs
167	    /// every turn (even without `?hints=1`) so each ply gets sampled.
168	    /// Costs ~100-300 ms WASM per turn at default Hard depth, on top
169	    /// of any existing AI/hint search work.
170	    ///
171	    /// URL token: `&evalbar=1`. Default off.
172	    /// Net mode (`/play/`) currently ignores this flag — see TODO.md
173	    /// "chess-net protocol v6" for the spectator-side broadcast follow-up.
174	    pub ai_evalbar: bool,
175	    /// Xiangqi pass-and-play only. When `true`, Black's piece glyphs are
176	    /// rendered rotated 180° so a player sitting on the opposite side of
177	    /// the device reads their pieces upright. Coordinate system is
178	    /// unchanged. Ignored when `mode == VsAi`, banqi, or three-kingdom.
179	    /// URL token: `&mirror=1`.
180	    pub mirror: bool,
181	}
182	
183	/// Hard cap on user-supplied depth. Picked so that browser WASM v3 Hard
184	/// at depth 8 (~5 s) is the worst tolerable response time. Higher than
185	/// 8 essentially demands iterative deepening + time budget — that's the
186	/// v5 roadmap entry, not a knob to twist.
187	pub const MAX_AI_DEPTH: u8 = 10;
188	
189	/// Hard cap on user-supplied node budget. Picked so the worst-case v5
190	/// search at MAX_AI_DEPTH stays under ~30 s on a typical 2024 laptop
191	/// running WASM (the engine's own auto-scaled cap is 16M, so 64M
192	/// gives the user 4× headroom for "go really deep on a big machine"
193	/// scenarios but still bounds the worst case). The picker's number
194	/// input clamps to this value at parse time.
195	pub const MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET: u32 = 64_000_000;
196	
197	impl Default for LocalRulesParams {
198	    fn default() -> Self {
199	        Self {
200	            strict: false,
201	            house: HouseRules::empty(),
202	            seed: None,
203	            mode: PlayMode::Pvp,
204	            ai_side: Side::BLACK,
205	            ai_difficulty: Difficulty::Normal,
206	            ai_strategy: Strategy::default(),
207	            ai_variation: None,
208	            ai_depth: None,
209	            ai_node_budget: None,
210	            ai_debug: false,
211	            ai_hints: false,
212	            ai_evalbar: false,
213	            mirror: false,
214	        }
215	    }
216	}
217	
218	/// Parse `?strict=1&house=chain,rush&preset=taiwan&seed=42` into a
219	/// [`LocalRulesParams`]. Unknown tokens are silently dropped — this is a
220	/// best-effort decoder for shareable URLs.
221	///
222	/// `house=` overrides `preset=` when both are present; the picker only
223	/// emits `house=` so this just covers manually-typed URLs.
224	pub fn parse_local_rules(get: impl Fn(&str) -> Option<String>) -> LocalRulesParams {
225	    let strict = matches!(get("strict").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true"));
226	    let preset = get("preset").as_deref().and_then(parse_preset_token);
227	    let house_csv = get("house").as_deref().map(parse_house_csv);
228	    let house = house_csv.or(preset).unwrap_or_else(HouseRules::empty);
229	    let seed = get("seed").and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok());
230	    let mode = match get("mode").as_deref() {
231	        Some("ai") | Some("vsai") => PlayMode::VsAi,
232	        _ => PlayMode::Pvp,
233	    };
234	    let ai_side = match get("ai").as_deref() {
235	        Some("red") => Side::RED,
236	        _ => Side::BLACK,
237	    };
238	    let ai_difficulty =
239	        get("diff").as_deref().and_then(Difficulty::parse).unwrap_or(Difficulty::Normal);
240	    let ai_strategy = get("engine").as_deref().and_then(Strategy::parse).unwrap_or_default();
241	    let ai_variation = get("variation").as_deref().and_then(Randomness::parse);
242	    let ai_depth = get("depth")
243	        .and_then(|s| s.parse::<u32>().ok())
244	        .map(|d| d.clamp(1, MAX_AI_DEPTH as u32) as u8);
245	    let ai_node_budget = get("budget").and_then(|s| s.parse::<u64>().ok()).map(|b| {
246	        // Parse as u64 so a typo'd 11-digit value clamps to the cap
247	        // rather than failing to parse and silently falling back to
248	        // None (`u32::parse` overflows above ~4.29B).
249	        b.clamp(1, MAX_AI_NODE_BUDGET as u64) as u32
250	    });
251	    let ai_debug = matches!(get("debug").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
252	    let ai_hints = matches!(get("hints").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
253	    let ai_evalbar = matches!(get("evalbar").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
254	    let mirror = matches!(get("mirror").as_deref(), Some("1") | Some("true") | Some("on"));
255	    LocalRulesParams {
256	        strict,
257	        house: house::normalize(house),
258	        seed,
259	        mode,
260	        ai_side,
261	        ai_difficulty,
262	        ai_strategy,
263	        ai_variation,
264	        ai_depth,
265	        ai_node_budget,
266	        ai_debug,
267	        ai_hints,
268	        ai_evalbar,
269	        mirror,
270	    }
271	}
272	
273	/// Inverse of [`parse_local_rules`] — emits a stable canonical query string
274	/// (no leading `?`). Empty result means "use defaults" — the picker drops the
275	/// `?` entirely so `/local/xiangqi` with no query is the default-casual URL.
276	pub fn build_local_query(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
277	    let mut parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
278	    match variant {
279	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
280	            if params.strict {
281	                parts.push("strict=1".to_string());
282	            }
283	            if params.mode == PlayMode::VsAi {
284	                parts.push("mode=ai".to_string());
285	                parts.push(format!(
286	                    "ai={}",
287	                    if params.ai_side == Side::RED { "red" } else { "black" }
288	                ));
289	                parts.push(format!("diff={}", params.ai_difficulty.as_str()));
290	                // Only emit engine= when the user picked a non-default
291	                // strategy. Keeps the canonical short URL unchanged for
292	                // the recommended setup.
293	                if params.ai_strategy != Strategy::default() {
294	                    parts.push(format!("engine={}", params.ai_strategy.as_str()));
295	                }
296	                // Only emit variation= when the user explicitly overrode
297	                // the difficulty default (and the override is one of the
298	                // known presets — custom Randomness values can't be
299	                // round-tripped through the URL).
300	                if let Some(name) = params.ai_variation.and_then(|r| r.preset_name()) {
301	                    parts.push(format!("variation={}", name));
302	                }
303	                // Only emit depth= when the user overrode the difficulty's
304	                // default. Allows the canonical short URL for the
305	                // recommended setup to stay short.
306	                if let Some(d) = params.ai_depth {
307	                    parts.push(format!("depth={}", d));
308	                }
309	                // Only emit budget= when the user explicitly overrode
310	                // the engine's default node-count cap. Keeps the
311	                // canonical "I'm using a preset difficulty" URL short.
312	                if let Some(b) = params.ai_node_budget {
313	                    parts.push(format!("budget={}", b));
314	                }
315	                // Debug panel toggle (vs-AI only — debug shows the AI's
316	                // own POV cached after each AI move; meaningless in PvP
317	                // where there's no AI move pump to fill the cache).
318	                if params.ai_debug {
319	                    parts.push("debug=1".to_string());
320	                }
321	            }
322	            // Hint mode toggle is emitted REGARDLESS of mode — hints
323	            // work in both vs-AI (analysis from human's POV when it's
324	            // their turn) AND pass-and-play (analysis from current
325	            // side-to-move's POV — both humans can ask the bot for
326	            // advice on every move). If we only emitted hints=1 inside
327	            // the VsAi block above, the picker's PvP-mode + checked
328	            // hints box would silently drop the flag and the player
329	            // would see no panel after clicking Start.
330	            if params.ai_hints {
331	                parts.push("hints=1".to_string());
332	            }
333	            // Win-rate display flag — same reason as hints=1 above:
334	            // works in both vs-AI and PvP, so emitted unconditionally
335	            // (when set). The local mode pages then thread it through
336	            // the same hint pump that powers `?hints=1`.
337	            if params.ai_evalbar {
338	                parts.push("evalbar=1".to_string());
339	            }
340	            // Mirror is pass-and-play only — vs-AI has no opponent on
341	            // the far side of the device, so it would just confuse the
342	            // single human. We still allow `?mirror=1` to round-trip
343	            // when set even with mode=ai (so URL hand-edits don't get
344	            // silently dropped on parse), but the picker only offers
345	            // the checkbox when mode == Pvp.
346	            if params.mirror && params.mode == PlayMode::Pvp {
347	                parts.push("mirror=1".to_string());
348	            }
349	        }
350	        Variant::Banqi => {
351	            let csv = house_csv(params.house);
352	            if !csv.is_empty() {
353	                parts.push(format!("house={}", csv));
354	            }
355	            if let Some(seed) = params.seed {
356	                parts.push(format!("seed={}", seed));
357	            }
358	        }
359	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {}
360	    }
361	    parts.join("&")
362	}
363	
364	/// Build the full path for the picker's "Start" link given the current form.
365	/// Always returns `"/local/<slug>"` with the query string appended only when
366	/// non-empty.
367	pub fn build_local_href(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> String {
368	    let q = build_local_query(variant, params);
369	    if q.is_empty() {
370	        format!("/local/{}", variant_slug(variant))
371	    } else {
372	        format!("/local/{}?{}", variant_slug(variant), q)
373	    }
374	}
375	
376	/// Convert parsed [`LocalRulesParams`] into the engine `RuleSet` for the
377	/// chosen variant. The picker → URL → page flow is the only call site.
378	pub fn build_rule_set(variant: Variant, params: &LocalRulesParams) -> RuleSet {
379	    match variant {
380	        Variant::Xiangqi => {
381	            if params.strict {
382	                RuleSet::xiangqi()
383	            } else {
384	                RuleSet::xiangqi_casual()
385	            }
386	        }
387	        Variant::Banqi => match params.seed {
388	            Some(seed) => RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(params.house, seed),
389	            None => RuleSet::banqi(params.house),
390	        },
391	        Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => RuleSet::three_kingdom(),
392	    }
393	}
394	
395	fn parse_house_csv(s: &str) -> HouseRules {
396	    let mut out = HouseRules::empty();
397	    for tok in s.split(',').map(str::trim).filter(|t| !t.is_empty()) {
398	        if let Some(flag) = parse_house_token(tok) {
399	            out.insert(flag);
400	        }
401	    }
402	    out
403	}
404	
405	fn house_csv(rules: HouseRules) -> String {
406	    let mut parts: Vec<&'static str> = Vec::new();
407	    for (flag, tok) in HOUSE_TOKENS {
408	        if rules.contains(*flag) {
409	            parts.push(tok);
410	        }
411	    }
412	    parts.join(",")
413	}
414	
415	fn parse_house_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
416	    HOUSE_TOKENS
417	        .iter()
418	        .chain(HOUSE_TOKEN_ALIASES.iter())
419	        .find(|(_, t)| t.eq_ignore_ascii_case(tok))
420	        .map(|(f, _)| *f)
421	}
422	
423	fn parse_preset_token(tok: &str) -> Option<HouseRules> {
424	    match tok.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
425	        "purist" => Some(PRESET_PURIST),
426	        "taiwan" => Some(PRESET_TAIWAN),
427	        "aggressive" => Some(PRESET_AGGRESSIVE),
428	        _ => None,
429	    }
430	}
431	
432	const HOUSE_TOKENS: &[(HouseRules, &str)] = &[
433	    (HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE, "chain"),
434	    (HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, "dark"),
435	    (HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH, "rush"),
436	    (HouseRules::HORSE_DIAGONAL, "horse"),
437	    (HouseRules::CANNON_FAST_MOVE, "cannon"),
438	    (HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE, "dark-trade"),
439	];
440	
441	/// Aliases recognised on parse only (older URLs / pre-rename snapshots).
442	/// Encoding uses the canonical `HOUSE_TOKENS` only.
443	const HOUSE_TOKEN_ALIASES: &[(HouseRules, &str)] = &[(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE, "dark-chain")];
444	
445	#[cfg(test)]
446	mod tests {
447	    use super::*;
448	
449	    fn from_pairs<'a>(pairs: &'a [(&'a str, &'a str)]) -> impl Fn(&str) -> Option<String> + 'a {
450	        move |k: &str| pairs.iter().find(|(pk, _)| *pk == k).map(|(_, v)| v.to_string())
451	    }
452	
453	    #[test]
454	    fn known_slugs_parse() {
455	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("xiangqi"), Some(Variant::Xiangqi)));
456	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("banqi"), Some(Variant::Banqi)));
457	        assert!(matches!(parse_variant_slug("three-kingdom"), Some(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi)));
458	    }
459	
460	    #[test]
461	    fn unknown_slug_returns_none() {
462	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("chess").is_none());
463	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("").is_none());
464	        assert!(parse_variant_slug("XIANGQI").is_none());
465	    }
466	
467	    #[test]
468	    fn slug_round_trip() {
469	        for v in [Variant::Xiangqi, Variant::Banqi, Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi] {
470	            assert_eq!(parse_variant_slug(variant_slug(v)), Some(v));
471	        }
472	    }
473	
474	    #[test]
475	    fn xiangqi_default_is_casual() {
476	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[]));
477	        assert!(!p.strict);
478	        let r = build_rule_set(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
479	        assert!(r.xiangqi_allow_self_check);
480	    }
481	
482	    #[test]
483	    fn xiangqi_strict_query_yields_strict_ruleset() {
484	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("strict", "1")]));
485	        assert!(p.strict);
486	        let r = build_rule_set(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
487	        assert!(!r.xiangqi_allow_self_check);
488	    }
489	
490	    #[test]
491	    fn banqi_house_csv_round_trips() {
492	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "chain,rush"), ("seed", "42")]));
493	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
494	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
495	        assert_eq!(p.seed, Some(42));
496	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Banqi, &p);
497	        assert!(q.contains("house=chain,rush"));
498	        assert!(q.contains("seed=42"));
499	    }
500	
501	    #[test]
502	    fn banqi_dark_capture_token_parses() {
503	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "dark")]));
504	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
505	    }
506	
507	    #[test]
508	    fn banqi_dark_chain_alias_still_parses_to_dark_capture() {
509	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "dark-chain")]));
510	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
511	    }
512	
513	    #[test]
514	    fn banqi_dark_trade_implies_dark_capture() {
515	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "dark-trade")]));
516	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE));
517	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::DARK_CAPTURE));
518	    }
519	
520	    #[test]
521	    fn banqi_preset_taiwan_picks_chain_and_rush() {
522	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("preset", "taiwan")]));
523	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
524	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
525	    }
526	
527	    #[test]
528	    fn banqi_house_overrides_preset_when_both_set() {
529	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("preset", "taiwan"), ("house", "")]));
530	        // Empty house= still wins → no flags.
531	        assert!(p.house.is_empty());
532	    }
533	
534	    #[test]
535	    fn unknown_house_token_is_dropped_silently() {
536	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("house", "chain,bogus,rush")]));
537	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE));
538	        assert!(p.house.contains(HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH));
539	    }
540	
541	    #[test]
542	    fn build_local_href_skips_query_when_defaults() {
543	        let p = LocalRulesParams::default();
544	        assert_eq!(build_local_href(Variant::Xiangqi, &p), "/local/xiangqi");
545	        assert_eq!(build_local_href(Variant::Banqi, &p), "/local/banqi");
546	    }
547	
548	    #[test]
549	    fn app_href_adds_base_path() {
550	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("", "/local/xiangqi"), "/local/xiangqi");
551	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("/", "/local/xiangqi"), "/local/xiangqi");
552	        assert_eq!(
553	            app_href_with_base("/chinese-chess", "/local/xiangqi"),
554	            "/chinese-chess/local/xiangqi"
555	        );
556	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("chinese-chess", "lobby"), "/chinese-chess/lobby");
557	        assert_eq!(app_href_with_base("/chinese-chess/", "/"), "/chinese-chess/");
558	        assert_eq!(
559	            app_href_with_base("/chinese-chess", "/local/banqi?seed=7"),
560	            "/chinese-chess/local/banqi?seed=7"
561	        );
562	    }
563	
564	    #[test]
565	    fn ws_base_normalizes_websocket_urls_only() {
566	        assert_eq!(
567	            normalize_ws_base(" wss://example.com/ws-root/ "),
568	            Ok("wss://example.com/ws-root".to_string())
569	        );
570	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base("ws://127.0.0.1:7878"), Ok("ws://127.0.0.1:7878".to_string()));
571	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base(""), Err(WsBaseError::Empty));
572	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base("https://example.com"), Err(WsBaseError::BadScheme));
573	        assert_eq!(normalize_ws_base("/ws"), Err(WsBaseError::BadScheme));
574	    }
575	
576	    #[test]
577	    fn build_local_href_includes_query_when_non_default() {
578	        let p = LocalRulesParams { strict: true, ..Default::default() };
579	        assert_eq!(build_local_href(Variant::Xiangqi, &p), "/local/xiangqi?strict=1");
580	        let p = LocalRulesParams {
581	            house: HouseRules::CHAIN_CAPTURE | HouseRules::CHARIOT_RUSH,
582	            seed: Some(7),
583	            ..Default::default()
584	        };
585	        assert_eq!(build_local_href(Variant::Banqi, &p), "/local/banqi?house=chain,rush&seed=7");
586	    }
587	
588	    #[test]
589	    fn xiangqi_vs_ai_query_round_trips() {
590	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("ai", "red"), ("diff", "hard")]));
591	        assert_eq!(p.mode, PlayMode::VsAi);
592	        assert_eq!(p.ai_side, Side::RED);
593	        assert_eq!(p.ai_difficulty, Difficulty::Hard);
594	        assert_eq!(p.ai_strategy, Strategy::default(), "missing engine= → default");
595	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
596	        assert!(q.contains("mode=ai"));
597	        assert!(q.contains("ai=red"));
598	        assert!(q.contains("diff=hard"));
599	        assert!(!q.contains("engine="), "default strategy should be omitted from query");
600	    }

602	    #[test]
603	    fn xiangqi_engine_v1_round_trips() {
604	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[
605	            ("mode", "ai"),
606	            ("ai", "black"),
607	            ("diff", "normal"),
608	            ("engine", "v1"),
609	        ]));
610	        assert_eq!(p.ai_strategy, Strategy::MaterialV1);
611	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
612	        assert!(q.contains("engine=v1"), "non-default strategy must round-trip; got {:?}", q);
613	    }

618	        assert_eq!(p.ai_strategy, Strategy::MaterialPstV2);

625	            ("engine", "material-king-safety-pst"),
626	        ]));

628	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("engine", "king-safety")]));
629	        assert_eq!(p.ai_strategy, Strategy::MaterialKingSafetyPstV3);

632	    #[test]
633	    fn xiangqi_engine_unknown_token_falls_back_to_default() {
634	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("engine", "v999")]));
635	        assert_eq!(p.ai_strategy, Strategy::default());

638	    #[test]
639	    fn xiangqi_variation_round_trips() {
640	        for (token, expected) in &[
641	            ("strict", Randomness::STRICT),
642	            ("subtle", Randomness::SUBTLE),
643	            ("varied", Randomness::VARIED),
644	            ("chaotic", Randomness::CHAOTIC),
645	        ] {
646	            let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("variation", token)]));
647	            assert_eq!(p.ai_variation, Some(*expected));
648	            let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
649	            assert!(
650	                q.contains(&format!("variation={}", token)),
651	                "round-trip failed for {}: {}",
652	                token,
653	                q
654	            );
655	        }
656	    }
657	
658	    #[test]
659	    fn xiangqi_variation_omitted_when_default() {
660	        let p = LocalRulesParams { mode: PlayMode::VsAi, ..Default::default() };
661	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
662	        assert!(
663	            !q.contains("variation="),
664	            "default (None) variation should not be emitted; got {}",
665	            q
666	        );
667	    }

670	    fn xiangqi_variation_unknown_token_falls_back_to_none() {
671	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("variation", "garbage")]));
672	        assert_eq!(p.ai_variation, None);
673	    }

678	        assert_eq!(p.ai_depth, Some(6));

686	        assert_eq!(p.ai_depth, Some(MAX_AI_DEPTH));

692	        assert_eq!(p.ai_depth, Some(1));

711	        assert_eq!(p.ai_node_budget, Some(2_000_000));

741	    /// Depth + budget together is the picker's "Custom" combo: both
742	    /// must round-trip independently.
743	    #[test]
744	    fn xiangqi_depth_and_node_budget_round_trip_together() {
745	        let p =
746	            parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("depth", "8"), ("budget", "4000000")]));
747	        assert_eq!(p.ai_depth, Some(8));
748	        assert_eq!(p.ai_node_budget, Some(4_000_000));
749	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
750	        assert!(q.contains("depth=8"));

757	        assert!(p.ai_debug);

759	        assert!(q.contains("debug=1"), "debug= round-trip: {}", q);

762	    #[test]
763	    fn xiangqi_debug_default_off_omitted_from_query() {
764	        let p = LocalRulesParams { mode: PlayMode::VsAi, ..Default::default() };
765	        assert!(!p.ai_debug);
766	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
767	        assert!(!q.contains("debug="), "off should not be emitted; got {}", q);
768	    }

770	    #[test]
771	    fn xiangqi_debug_accepts_truthy_aliases() {
772	        for token in ["1", "true", "on"] {
773	            let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("debug", token)]));
774	            assert!(p.ai_debug, "token {:?} should enable debug", token);
775	        }
776	        for token in ["0", "false", "off", "garbage"] {
777	            let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("debug", token)]));
778	            assert!(!p.ai_debug, "token {:?} should NOT enable debug", token);
779	        }
780	    }

782	    #[test]
783	    fn xiangqi_hints_round_trips() {
784	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("hints", "1")]));
785	        assert!(p.ai_hints);
786	        // ai_debug stays independent (hint is its own URL flag).
787	        assert!(!p.ai_debug);
788	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
789	        assert!(q.contains("hints=1"), "hints= round-trip: {}", q);
790	    }
791	
792	    #[test]
793	    fn xiangqi_hints_and_debug_can_coexist() {
794	        // Both URL tokens together → both flags set; canonical
795	        // emission preserves both. In local.rs the panel mounts when
796	        // either is true.
797	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("debug", "1"), ("hints", "1")]));
798	        assert!(p.ai_debug && p.ai_hints);
799	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
800	        assert!(q.contains("debug=1") && q.contains("hints=1"), "both kept: {}", q);
801	    }
802	
803	    #[test]
804	    fn xiangqi_hints_default_off_omitted_from_query() {
805	        let p = LocalRulesParams { mode: PlayMode::VsAi, ..Default::default() };
806	        assert!(!p.ai_hints);
807	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
808	        assert!(!q.contains("hints="), "off should not be emitted; got {}", q);
809	    }
810	
811	    #[test]
812	    fn xiangqi_pvp_hints_emitted_even_without_ai_mode() {
813	        // Regression: previously `hints=1` lived inside the
814	        // `if mode == VsAi` block in build_local_query, so a user
815	        // checking the picker's "🧠 Allow AI hints" box in PvP mode
816	        // would get a `/local/xiangqi` URL with no `hints=1`, and the
817	        // game page would show no panel. Hints work in PvP (analysis
818	        // for current side-to-move) so the flag must round-trip.
819	        let p = LocalRulesParams { mode: PlayMode::Pvp, ai_hints: true, ..Default::default() };
820	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
821	        assert!(q.contains("hints=1"), "PvP+hints must round-trip; got {:?}", q);
822	        // mode=ai must NOT appear (we're in Pvp).
823	        assert!(!q.contains("mode=ai"), "PvP must not emit mode=ai; got {:?}", q);
824	    }
825	
826	    #[test]
827	    fn xiangqi_pvp_hints_round_trips() {
828	        // Full encode → decode round-trip for PvP+hints (the no-AI
829	        // pass-and-play path that was previously broken).
830	        let p = LocalRulesParams { mode: PlayMode::Pvp, ai_hints: true, ..Default::default() };
831	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
832	        let pairs: Vec<(String, String)> = q
833	            .split('&')
834	            .filter_map(|kv| kv.split_once('=').map(|(k, v)| (k.to_string(), v.to_string())))
835	            .collect();
836	        let p2 =
837	            parse_local_rules(|k| pairs.iter().find(|(pk, _)| pk == k).map(|(_, v)| v.clone()));
838	        assert!(p2.ai_hints);
839	        assert_eq!(p2.mode, PlayMode::Pvp);
840	    }
841	
842	    #[test]
843	    fn xiangqi_evalbar_round_trips_in_vs_ai() {
844	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mode", "ai"), ("evalbar", "1")]));
845	        assert!(p.ai_evalbar);
846	        // Independent of hints / debug — three flags, no aliasing.
847	        assert!(!p.ai_hints);
848	        assert!(!p.ai_debug);
849	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
850	        assert!(q.contains("evalbar=1"), "evalbar= round-trip: {}", q);
851	    }
852	
853	    #[test]
854	    fn xiangqi_pvp_evalbar_emitted_even_without_ai_mode() {
855	        // Same shape as the PvP+hints regression — evalbar works in
856	        // PvP (samples come from the hint pump that runs every turn
857	        // when evalbar is on), so the flag must round-trip.
858	        let p = LocalRulesParams { mode: PlayMode::Pvp, ai_evalbar: true, ..Default::default() };
859	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
860	        assert!(q.contains("evalbar=1"), "PvP+evalbar must round-trip; got {:?}", q);
861	        assert!(!q.contains("mode=ai"));
862	    }
863	
864	    #[test]
865	    fn xiangqi_evalbar_default_off_omitted() {
866	        let p = LocalRulesParams { mode: PlayMode::VsAi, ..Default::default() };
867	        assert!(!p.ai_evalbar);
868	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
869	        assert!(!q.contains("evalbar="), "off should not be emitted; got {}", q);
870	    }
871	
872	    #[test]
873	    fn xiangqi_evalbar_truthy_aliases_parse() {
874	        for token in ["1", "true", "on"] {
875	            let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("evalbar", token)]));
876	            assert!(p.ai_evalbar, "token {:?} should enable evalbar", token);
877	        }
878	        for token in ["0", "false", "off", "garbage"] {
879	            let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("evalbar", token)]));
880	            assert!(!p.ai_evalbar, "token {:?} should NOT enable evalbar", token);
881	        }
882	    }
883	
884	    #[test]
885	    fn xiangqi_three_insight_flags_can_coexist() {
886	        // hints + debug + evalbar all set → all three preserved.
887	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[
888	            ("mode", "ai"),
889	            ("debug", "1"),
890	            ("hints", "1"),
891	            ("evalbar", "1"),
892	        ]));
893	        assert!(p.ai_debug && p.ai_hints && p.ai_evalbar);
894	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
895	        assert!(
896	            q.contains("debug=1") && q.contains("hints=1") && q.contains("evalbar=1"),
897	            "all three preserved: {}",
898	            q
899	        );
900	    }
901	
902	    #[test]
903	    fn xiangqi_default_mode_omits_ai_query() {
904	        let p = LocalRulesParams::default();
905	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
906	        assert!(q.is_empty(), "default mode should not emit query, got {:?}", q);
907	    }
908	
909	    #[test]
910	    fn xiangqi_mirror_round_trips_in_pvp() {
911	        let p = parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("mirror", "1")]));
912	        assert!(p.mirror);
913	        assert_eq!(p.mode, PlayMode::Pvp);
914	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
915	        assert!(q.contains("mirror=1"), "PvP+mirror must round-trip; got {:?}", q);
916	    }
917	
918	    #[test]
919	    fn xiangqi_mirror_default_off_omitted() {
920	        let p = LocalRulesParams::default();
921	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
922	        assert!(!q.contains("mirror="), "default off must not emit; got {:?}", q);
923	    }
924	
925	    #[test]
926	    fn xiangqi_mirror_dropped_in_vs_ai_emission() {
927	        let p = LocalRulesParams { mode: PlayMode::VsAi, mirror: true, ..Default::default() };
928	        let q = build_local_query(Variant::Xiangqi, &p);
929	        assert!(!q.contains("mirror="), "mirror must not be emitted in vs-AI; got {:?}", q);
930	    }
931	
932	    #[test]
933	    fn three_kingdom_ignores_query() {
934	        let p =
935	            parse_local_rules(from_pairs(&[("strict", "1"), ("house", "chain"), ("seed", "5")]));
936	        let r = build_rule_set(Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi, &p);
937	        assert_eq!(r.variant, Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi);

```md
# Extend WebRTC LAN mode: banqi support + host-side rule/variant picker

## Context

LAN mode (Phase 5 / 5.5) ships as a working WebRTC pairing flow but is
**xiangqi-only**, with **no rule customisation** at the host setup screen.
The variant is hardcoded at exactly one site:

\```rust
// clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs:258
let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(RuleSet::xiangqi(), None, /* hints */ false);
\```

The protocol envelope (`ServerMsg::Hello.rules: RuleSet` in
`crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs`) already carries variant + every flag
+ the banqi seed end-to-end — the joiner reads `Hello.rules` and renders
the host's authoritative state via `PlayerView` projections. No new
protocol message and no protocol version bump are needed.

Banqi-specific correctness is already handled by the existing protocol:
the host shuffles once via `ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(banqi_seed)` in
`crates/chess-core/src/setup.rs::build_banqi`, then projects per-side
`PlayerView` (hidden tiles stay opaque per ADR-0004). The joiner never
reconstructs the board locally; it just renders the projection. So
adding banqi to LAN requires **zero changes to** `host_room.rs` or
`transport/webrtc.rs` — only the host's choice of `RuleSet` and a small
joiner-side display.

Three-kingdom-banqi is **deliberately excluded** from the LAN dropdown —
it's a 3-player variant and WebRTC pairing here is 2-player only
(would need a 3-peer mesh redesign, not scoped).

### Intended outcome

Host clicks the variant dropdown on `/lan/host`, picks banqi, optionally
configures preset / house-rules / seed, and clicks "Open room". Joiner
scans QR or pastes offer. Both sides see the same variant + rules
summary and start playing banqi over WebRTC with hidden-tile flips
working correctly. Existing xiangqi LAN flow is preserved (the default
`/lan/host` still opens a casual xiangqi room with no extra clicks).

---

## Commit 1 — variant + rules picker on `/lan/host`

**Critical files (modify):**

- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs` — `LanHostPage` (lines ~119–270):
  add form controls before the "Open room" button; read selection on
  submit; pass to `HostRoom::new(rules, …)` at line 258.

**Reused helpers (no changes; just call):**

- `clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs:378` — `build_rule_set(variant, &LocalRulesParams) -> RuleSet`.
  Pure function; no URL roundtrip needed. Branches on variant, picks
  `xiangqi_casual` vs `xiangqi`, and threads `banqi_seed`.
- `clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs:106` — `LocalRulesParams` struct.
  Shareable form-state intermediate; 13 fields (most are AI-only and
  irrelevant to LAN — leave them at `Default::default()`).
- `clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs:224` — `parse_local_rules(get_fn)`.
  Used **only** for the optional URL pre-fill polish (see step 5).

### Changes (in order)

1. **Add signals scoped to `LanHostPage`** (above the existing
   `on_open`/`on_accept` closures):

   \```rust
   let variant = create_rw_signal(Variant::Xiangqi);
   let params  = create_rw_signal(LocalRulesParams::default());
   \```

   The xiangqi default is `xiangqi_casual` (matches `params.strict == false`),
   which is the same default the picker uses for `/local/xiangqi` per
   `routes.rs:475-480`. This preserves the current LAN-host behaviour
   (xiangqi casual room opens with one click).

2. **Render the form** as a single inline `<section class="lan-rules-form">`
   above the existing "Open room" button. Do **not** lift `picker.rs::XiangqiCard`
   or `BanqiCard` — they're 450 + 100 lines of inline JSX with AI options,
   board preview, side selector, and `build_local_href` URL generation
   that LAN doesn't need. The Plan agent confirmed there is no extracted
   `<RulesForm>` component to reuse. Duplicating the *pattern* (signals
   → `LocalRulesParams` → `build_rule_set`) is the right call here;
   extracting a shared component for 2 consumers of different shape is
   premature abstraction. Track `<RulesForm>` extraction in a follow-up
   if a third consumer appears.

   Form contents:

   \```rust
   <section class="lan-rules-form">
       <label>"Variant"</label>
       <select on:change=move |ev| {
           // Update variant signal from select value; reset params on switch
           // so xiangqi house flags don't leak into banqi and vice versa.
           let slug = event_target_value(&ev);
           if let Some(v) = parse_variant_slug(&slug) {
               variant.set(v);
               params.set(LocalRulesParams::default());
           }
       }>
           <option value="xiangqi">"Xiangqi (象棋)"</option>
           <option value="banqi">"Banqi (暗棋)"</option>
           // three-kingdom intentionally NOT listed; see Context.
       </select>

       <Show when=move || variant.get() == Variant::Xiangqi>
           <label>
               <input type="checkbox"
                   prop:checked=move || params.get().strict
                   on:change=move |ev| params.update(|p| p.strict = event_target_checked(&ev))/>
               "Strict (self-check forbidden)"
           </label>
       </Show>

       <Show when=move || variant.get() == Variant::Banqi>
           // Preset dropdown — Purist / Taiwan / Aggressive / Custom.
           // On change: set params.house = matching PRESET_* constant.
           // Custom = whatever the user toggles below.
           <label>"Preset"</label>
           <select on:change=move |ev| { /* set params.house from preset */ }>
               <option value="purist">"Purist (no house rules)"</option>
               <option value="taiwan">"Taiwan (chain + chariot rush)"</option>
               <option value="aggressive">"Aggressive (all flags on)"</option>
               <option value="custom">"Custom…"</option>
           </select>

           // Six house-rule checkboxes, each bound to a HouseRules bitflag.
           // Use HOUSE_TOKENS (routes.rs:432) for label ↔ flag mapping.
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Chain capture (chain)"</label>
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Dark capture (dark)"</label>
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Dark capture trade (dark-trade)"</label>
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Chariot rush (rush)"</label>
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Horse diagonal (horse) — UI flag, not yet wired"</label>
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Cannon fast move (cannon) — UI flag, not yet wired"</label>

           // Seed input — numeric, blank = engine picks random.
           <label>"Seed (optional)"</label>
           <input type="number" placeholder="random"
               on:input=move |ev| {
                   let v = event_target_value(&ev);
                   params.update(|p| p.seed = v.parse::<u64>().ok());
               }/>
       </Show>
   </section>
   \```

   Note on the two "not yet wired" flags: per CLAUDE.md, only `CHAIN_CAPTURE`,
   `DARK_CAPTURE`, `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE`, `CHARIOT_RUSH` are end-to-end in
   move-gen; `HORSE_DIAGONAL` and `CANNON_FAST_MOVE` parse but are no-op.
   Match `picker.rs::BanqiCard` exactly: show them and label them
   honestly. Don't silently hide — that hides behaviour drift from
   future users.

3. **Wire the room-open call** (currently line 258 of `lan.rs`):

   \```rust
   // before:
   let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(RuleSet::xiangqi(), None, false);
   // after:
   let rules = routes::build_rule_set(variant.get_untracked(), &params.get_untracked());
   let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(rules, None, false);
   \```

   `get_untracked()` is correct here: the open-room handler reads the
   form value once at click time and doesn't want to re-fire on later
   form edits (the room is locked once opened).

4. **Disable the form once the room is open.** After `on_open` succeeds
   (status moves past `Idle`), hide / `disabled` the variant + rules
   inputs so the user can't toggle them mid-pairing. Show the chosen
   rules as a read-only summary line ("Banqi · Taiwan · seed: 42") in
   the existing status area instead. Reuse the `describe_rules` helper
   from commit 2 (define it once, call from both pages).

5. **(Optional polish, still commit 1)** Parse query string on page load
   via `routes::parse_local_rules(...)`:

   \```rust
   // Wrap in use_query() / web_sys::window().location().search()
   let initial = parse_local_rules(|k| /* read query param */);
   params.set(initial);
   // Plus parse a top-level ?variant= via parse_variant_slug
   \```

   Gives free deep-linking — `/lan/host?variant=banqi&house=chain,rush&seed=42`
   pre-fills the form. Sets up a future picker entry ("Host LAN game" button
   per variant card) as a trivial follow-up.

6. **Add styles** in `clients/chess-web/style.css` — small section, ~10 lines:
   \```css
   .lan-rules-form { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px;
                     margin-bottom: 12px; padding: 8px;
                     border: 1px solid #eee; border-radius: 4px; }
   .lan-rules-form label { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; }
   \```
   Match the existing form aesthetic in `style.css` (`.chat-input`,
   `.lan-buttons` etc.).

7. **Unit tests** (native) in `routes.rs::tests` are already comprehensive
   (line 445–940). No new tests needed for `build_rule_set` / `parse_local_rules`
   themselves. The new `describe_rules` helper (added in commit 2) is the
   only new pure function and gets its own ~3 unit tests there.

---

## Commit 2 — joiner sees variant + rules on `/lan/join`

**Critical files:**

- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs` — `LanJoinPage` (lines ~441–717).
  Specifically, the `ServerMsg::Hello` handler (the joiner currently
  ignores `hello.rules`, so the user has no idea what they're about to
  play until the board renders).

**Changes:**

1. **Capture `rules` from the `Hello` message** in `LanJoinPage`. After
   pairing, the data-channel `onmessage` handler dispatches on
   `ServerMsg`. In the `Hello { rules, view, .. }` arm:

   \```rust
   set_rules_received.set(Some(rules.clone()));
   // existing handling: store view in board signal, set status…
   \```

   Add a `(rules_received, set_rules_received) = create_signal::<Option<RuleSet>>(None)`
   at the top of `LanJoinPage`.

2. **Display the summary** below the existing Status line:

   \```rust
   <Show when=move || rules_received.get().is_some()>
       <p>
           "Playing: " {move || describe_rules(rules_received.get().as_ref().unwrap())}
       </p>
   </Show>
   \```

3. **Add `describe_rules`** as a pure helper in `clients/chess-web/src/state.rs`
   (state.rs is native-buildable per `lib.rs:14` — keeps it out of the
   wasm32-only modules and unit-testable):

   \```rust
   pub fn describe_rules(rules: &RuleSet) -> String {
       let mut parts: Vec<String> = vec![variant_label(rules.variant).to_string()];
       match rules.variant {
           Variant::Xiangqi => {
               parts.push((if rules.xiangqi_allow_self_check { "casual" } else { "strict" }).into());
           }
           Variant::Banqi => {
               if !rules.house.is_empty() {
                   parts.push(format!("house: {}", house_csv_label(rules.house)));
               }
               if let Some(seed) = rules.banqi_seed {
                   parts.push(format!("seed: {seed}"));
               }
           }
           Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {}
       }
       parts.join(" · ")
   }

   fn variant_label(v: Variant) -> &'static str {
       match v {
           Variant::Xiangqi => "Xiangqi",
           Variant::Banqi  => "Banqi",
           Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "Three-Kingdom Banqi",
       }
   }
   \```

   `house_csv_label` reuses `routes.rs::house_csv` logic — either expose
   the existing private helper (`house_csv` at routes.rs:405) as `pub(crate)`
   and call it, OR copy the 8 lines into state.rs. **Prefer `pub(crate)`**:
   single source of truth for the canonical comma-separated form
   ("chain,rush"). Touch the existing function signature only — no
   behaviour change.

4. **Reuse in commit 1's form** — the host also calls `describe_rules`
   to render the read-only summary line after the room opens (step 4
   of commit 1). Single helper, two callers.

5. **Tests** in `state.rs::tests` (or `tests` mod near the helper):

   - `describe_rules(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual())` → `"Xiangqi · casual"`
   - `describe_rules(RuleSet::xiangqi())` → `"Xiangqi · strict"`
   - `describe_rules(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(PRESET_TAIWAN, 42))` →
     `"Banqi · house: chain,rush · seed: 42"`
   - `describe_rules(RuleSet::banqi(HouseRules::empty()))` →
     `"Banqi"` (no flags, no seed → no extra parts).

---

## Verification

\```bash
# 1. Workspace sanity (every crate still compiles, no leptos-into-pure-modules leak)
cargo check --workspace

# 2. Native tests — covers routes::build_rule_set + parse_local_rules
#    (existing 80+ tests) + the new describe_rules helper (3 new tests)
cargo test -p chess-web

# 3. Clippy clean
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings

# 4. Format clean (CI requires)
cargo fmt --check

# 5. WASM compile — the actual target
cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-web

# 6. Local xiangqi smoke (regression: ensure existing flow unchanged):
make play-web
# Tab A: http://localhost:8080/lan/host  (default = Xiangqi, no toggles)
#   → confirm form shows Xiangqi + Strict unchecked
#   → click "Open room"  → status shows "Playing: Xiangqi · casual"
# Tab B: http://localhost:8080/lan/join  → paste offer
#   → confirm joiner sees "Playing: Xiangqi · casual"
# Make 2-3 moves both sides; confirm board syncs as before.

# 7. Local banqi smoke (new path):
make play-web
# Tab A: /lan/host → variant dropdown → "Banqi (暗棋)"
#   → preset = Taiwan, seed = 42
#   → Open room → status: "Playing: Banqi · house: chain,rush · seed: 42"
# Tab B: /lan/join → paste/scan offer
#   → confirm joiner status: "Playing: Banqi · house: chain,rush · seed: 42"
#   → confirm both sides render the SAME 4×8 hidden layout
#     (since seed=42 forces deterministic shuffle; both sides should
#      agree on which 32 tiles are face-down where)
# Flip a tile from each side; confirm the flipped piece appears
# identically on both ends. Capture, chain capture, verify rules fire.

# 8. Banqi without seed (default random):
# Tab A: /lan/host → variant = Banqi → preset = Purist (no flags) → no seed
#   → Open room → status: "Playing: Banqi"  (no "house:" or "seed:" parts)
# Tab B: → confirm joiner sees same; board layouts agree (host shuffles
#   once, projection works as for xiangqi)

# 9. Deep-link smoke (commit 1's optional URL pre-fill):
open http://localhost:8080/lan/host?variant=banqi&house=chain,rush&seed=42
# Confirm form initialises with: variant dropdown = Banqi, preset = "Custom"
# (or auto-detect as Taiwan if exact match), checkboxes for chain + rush
# checked, seed input = 42.

# 10. Form-locking behaviour after open:
# Tab A: open a room (any variant)  → confirm variant dropdown is
# disabled (or hidden) and form values can't be changed mid-pairing.

# 11. iOS QR scan + banqi joiner — manual on iPhone if available:
# After cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-web,
# make build-web-static WEB_BASE=/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi, deploy.
# Confirm the banqi LAN flow works through QR scan + camera path
# (uses existing jsQR loader fixed in earlier commit).
\```

**Cannot verify locally:** physical-LAN pairing across two real devices
for banqi. Same caveat as Phase 5.5 — the in-browser two-tab flow uses
the WebRTC loopback path which already proved working for xiangqi, so
adding banqi to the same channel is mechanical (no new transport edges,
no protocol changes). If the user runs the cross-device test, the only
new failure mode is "banqi rules misbehave" — same risk as any banqi
session in `chess-net` today.

---

## Out of scope (call out in commit body)

- **Picker "Host LAN game" entry point.** A button on each picker
  variant card linking to `/lan/host?variant=...&house=...&seed=...`.
  Trivial once commit 1's deep-link parsing lands; track via
  `scripts/add-todo.sh` if user wants the picker integration.
- **In-game rule editing.** Once a room opens, rules are locked.
  Re-opening with different rules requires a new room. Matches
  chess-net `Room` semantics; rule editing would need a "rematch with
  new rules" message that doesn't exist in protocol v4 / v5.
- **Three-kingdom-banqi over WebRTC.** 3-player; doesn't fit 2-peer
  pairing. Would need a 3-peer mesh topology — separate redesign.
- **`<RulesForm>` component extraction.** Premature with 2 consumers
  (picker + LAN host) of differing shape. Re-evaluate when a third
  consumer appears or when the two drift in a confusing way.
- **Banqi `?role=spectator` over WebRTC.** Spectator role exists in
  chess-net protocol but WebRTC currently treats every peer as a
  player. Adding spectators needs a third peer + projection routing,
  separate from this work.
- **House-rule flags `HORSE_DIAGONAL` / `CANNON_FAST_MOVE` wired in
  move-gen.** UI form will show them (matching picker), but they
  remain no-op until `rules/banqi.rs::generate` consumes them. Already
  tracked in `TODO.md` as base-engine work.

## Approved Plan:
# Extend WebRTC LAN mode: banqi support + host-side rule/variant picker

## Context

LAN mode (Phase 5 / 5.5) ships as a working WebRTC pairing flow but is
**xiangqi-only**, with **no rule customisation** at the host setup screen.
The variant is hardcoded at exactly one site:

```rust
// clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs:258
let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(RuleSet::xiangqi(), None, /* hints */ false);
```

The protocol envelope (`ServerMsg::Hello.rules: RuleSet` in
`crates/chess-net/src/protocol.rs`) already carries variant + every flag
+ the banqi seed end-to-end — the joiner reads `Hello.rules` and renders
the host's authoritative state via `PlayerView` projections. No new
protocol message and no protocol version bump are needed.

Banqi-specific correctness is already handled by the existing protocol:
the host shuffles once via `ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(banqi_seed)` in
`crates/chess-core/src/setup.rs::build_banqi`, then projects per-side
`PlayerView` (hidden tiles stay opaque per ADR-0004). The joiner never
reconstructs the board locally; it just renders the projection. So
adding banqi to LAN requires **zero changes to** `host_room.rs` or
`transport/webrtc.rs` — only the host's choice of `RuleSet` and a small
joiner-side display.

Three-kingdom-banqi is **deliberately excluded** from the LAN dropdown —
it's a 3-player variant and WebRTC pairing here is 2-player only
(would need a 3-peer mesh redesign, not scoped).

### Intended outcome

Host clicks the variant dropdown on `/lan/host`, picks banqi, optionally
configures preset / house-rules / seed, and clicks "Open room". Joiner
scans QR or pastes offer. Both sides see the same variant + rules
summary and start playing banqi over WebRTC with hidden-tile flips
working correctly. Existing xiangqi LAN flow is preserved (the default
`/lan/host` still opens a casual xiangqi room with no extra clicks).

---

## Commit 1 — variant + rules picker on `/lan/host`

**Critical files (modify):**

- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs` — `LanHostPage` (lines ~119–270):
  add form controls before the "Open room" button; read selection on
  submit; pass to `HostRoom::new(rules, …)` at line 258.

**Reused helpers (no changes; just call):**

- `clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs:378` — `build_rule_set(variant, &LocalRulesParams) -> RuleSet`.
  Pure function; no URL roundtrip needed. Branches on variant, picks
  `xiangqi_casual` vs `xiangqi`, and threads `banqi_seed`.
- `clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs:106` — `LocalRulesParams` struct.
  Shareable form-state intermediate; 13 fields (most are AI-only and
  irrelevant to LAN — leave them at `Default::default()`).
- `clients/chess-web/src/routes.rs:224` — `parse_local_rules(get_fn)`.
  Used **only** for the optional URL pre-fill polish (see step 5).

### Changes (in order)

1. **Add signals scoped to `LanHostPage`** (above the existing
   `on_open`/`on_accept` closures):

   ```rust
   let variant = create_rw_signal(Variant::Xiangqi);
   let params  = create_rw_signal(LocalRulesParams::default());
   ```

   The xiangqi default is `xiangqi_casual` (matches `params.strict == false`),
   which is the same default the picker uses for `/local/xiangqi` per
   `routes.rs:475-480`. This preserves the current LAN-host behaviour
   (xiangqi casual room opens with one click).

2. **Render the form** as a single inline `<section class="lan-rules-form">`
   above the existing "Open room" button. Do **not** lift `picker.rs::XiangqiCard`
   or `BanqiCard` — they're 450 + 100 lines of inline JSX with AI options,
   board preview, side selector, and `build_local_href` URL generation
   that LAN doesn't need. The Plan agent confirmed there is no extracted
   `<RulesForm>` component to reuse. Duplicating the *pattern* (signals
   → `LocalRulesParams` → `build_rule_set`) is the right call here;
   extracting a shared component for 2 consumers of different shape is
   premature abstraction. Track `<RulesForm>` extraction in a follow-up
   if a third consumer appears.

   Form contents:

   ```rust
   <section class="lan-rules-form">
       <label>"Variant"</label>
       <select on:change=move |ev| {
           // Update variant signal from select value; reset params on switch
           // so xiangqi house flags don't leak into banqi and vice versa.
           let slug = event_target_value(&ev);
           if let Some(v) = parse_variant_slug(&slug) {
               variant.set(v);
               params.set(LocalRulesParams::default());
           }
       }>
           <option value="xiangqi">"Xiangqi (象棋)"</option>
           <option value="banqi">"Banqi (暗棋)"</option>
           // three-kingdom intentionally NOT listed; see Context.
       </select>

       <Show when=move || variant.get() == Variant::Xiangqi>
           <label>
               <input type="checkbox"
                   prop:checked=move || params.get().strict
                   on:change=move |ev| params.update(|p| p.strict = event_target_checked(&ev))/>
               "Strict (self-check forbidden)"
           </label>
       </Show>

       <Show when=move || variant.get() == Variant::Banqi>
           // Preset dropdown — Purist / Taiwan / Aggressive / Custom.
           // On change: set params.house = matching PRESET_* constant.
           // Custom = whatever the user toggles below.
           <label>"Preset"</label>
           <select on:change=move |ev| { /* set params.house from preset */ }>
               <option value="purist">"Purist (no house rules)"</option>
               <option value="taiwan">"Taiwan (chain + chariot rush)"</option>
               <option value="aggressive">"Aggressive (all flags on)"</option>
               <option value="custom">"Custom…"</option>
           </select>

           // Six house-rule checkboxes, each bound to a HouseRules bitflag.
           // Use HOUSE_TOKENS (routes.rs:432) for label ↔ flag mapping.
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Chain capture (chain)"</label>
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Dark capture (dark)"</label>
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Dark capture trade (dark-trade)"</label>
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Chariot rush (rush)"</label>
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Horse diagonal (horse) — UI flag, not yet wired"</label>
           <label><input type="checkbox" .../> "Cannon fast move (cannon) — UI flag, not yet wired"</label>

           // Seed input — numeric, blank = engine picks random.
           <label>"Seed (optional)"</label>
           <input type="number" placeholder="random"
               on:input=move |ev| {
                   let v = event_target_value(&ev);
                   params.update(|p| p.seed = v.parse::<u64>().ok());
               }/>
       </Show>
   </section>
   ```

   Note on the two "not yet wired" flags: per CLAUDE.md, only `CHAIN_CAPTURE`,
   `DARK_CAPTURE`, `DARK_CAPTURE_TRADE`, `CHARIOT_RUSH` are end-to-end in
   move-gen; `HORSE_DIAGONAL` and `CANNON_FAST_MOVE` parse but are no-op.
   Match `picker.rs::BanqiCard` exactly: show them and label them
   honestly. Don't silently hide — that hides behaviour drift from
   future users.

3. **Wire the room-open call** (currently line 258 of `lan.rs`):

   ```rust
   // before:
   let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(RuleSet::xiangqi(), None, false);
   // after:
   let rules = routes::build_rule_set(variant.get_untracked(), &params.get_untracked());
   let (room, session) = HostRoom::new(rules, None, false);
   ```

   `get_untracked()` is correct here: the open-room handler reads the
   form value once at click time and doesn't want to re-fire on later
   form edits (the room is locked once opened).

4. **Disable the form once the room is open.** After `on_open` succeeds
   (status moves past `Idle`), hide / `disabled` the variant + rules
   inputs so the user can't toggle them mid-pairing. Show the chosen
   rules as a read-only summary line ("Banqi · Taiwan · seed: 42") in
   the existing status area instead. Reuse the `describe_rules` helper
   from commit 2 (define it once, call from both pages).

5. **(Optional polish, still commit 1)** Parse query string on page load
   via `routes::parse_local_rules(...)`:

   ```rust
   // Wrap in use_query() / web_sys::window().location().search()
   let initial = parse_local_rules(|k| /* read query param */);
   params.set(initial);
   // Plus parse a top-level ?variant= via parse_variant_slug
   ```

   Gives free deep-linking — `/lan/host?variant=banqi&house=chain,rush&seed=42`
   pre-fills the form. Sets up a future picker entry ("Host LAN game" button
   per variant card) as a trivial follow-up.

6. **Add styles** in `clients/chess-web/style.css` — small section, ~10 lines:
   ```css
   .lan-rules-form { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px;
                     margin-bottom: 12px; padding: 8px;
                     border: 1px solid #eee; border-radius: 4px; }
   .lan-rules-form label { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; }
   ```
   Match the existing form aesthetic in `style.css` (`.chat-input`,
   `.lan-buttons` etc.).

7. **Unit tests** (native) in `routes.rs::tests` are already comprehensive
   (line 445–940). No new tests needed for `build_rule_set` / `parse_local_rules`
   themselves. The new `describe_rules` helper (added in commit 2) is the
   only new pure function and gets its own ~3 unit tests there.

---

## Commit 2 — joiner sees variant + rules on `/lan/join`

**Critical files:**

- `clients/chess-web/src/pages/lan.rs` — `LanJoinPage` (lines ~441–717).
  Specifically, the `ServerMsg::Hello` handler (the joiner currently
  ignores `hello.rules`, so the user has no idea what they're about to
  play until the board renders).

**Changes:**

1. **Capture `rules` from the `Hello` message** in `LanJoinPage`. After
   pairing, the data-channel `onmessage` handler dispatches on
   `ServerMsg`. In the `Hello { rules, view, .. }` arm:

   ```rust
   set_rules_received.set(Some(rules.clone()));
   // existing handling: store view in board signal, set status…
   ```

   Add a `(rules_received, set_rules_received) = create_signal::<Option<RuleSet>>(None)`
   at the top of `LanJoinPage`.

2. **Display the summary** below the existing Status line:

   ```rust
   <Show when=move || rules_received.get().is_some()>
       <p>
           "Playing: " {move || describe_rules(rules_received.get().as_ref().unwrap())}
       </p>
   </Show>
   ```

3. **Add `describe_rules`** as a pure helper in `clients/chess-web/src/state.rs`
   (state.rs is native-buildable per `lib.rs:14` — keeps it out of the
   wasm32-only modules and unit-testable):

   ```rust
   pub fn describe_rules(rules: &RuleSet) -> String {
       let mut parts: Vec<String> = vec![variant_label(rules.variant).to_string()];
       match rules.variant {
           Variant::Xiangqi => {
               parts.push((if rules.xiangqi_allow_self_check { "casual" } else { "strict" }).into());
           }
           Variant::Banqi => {
               if !rules.house.is_empty() {
                   parts.push(format!("house: {}", house_csv_label(rules.house)));
               }
               if let Some(seed) = rules.banqi_seed {
                   parts.push(format!("seed: {seed}"));
               }
           }
           Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => {}
       }
       parts.join(" · ")
   }

   fn variant_label(v: Variant) -> &'static str {
       match v {
           Variant::Xiangqi => "Xiangqi",
           Variant::Banqi  => "Banqi",
           Variant::ThreeKingdomBanqi => "Three-Kingdom Banqi",
       }
   }
   ```

   `house_csv_label` reuses `routes.rs::house_csv` logic — either expose
   the existing private helper (`house_csv` at routes.rs:405) as `pub(crate)`
   and call it, OR copy the 8 lines into state.rs. **Prefer `pub(crate)`**:
   single source of truth for the canonical comma-separated form
   ("chain,rush"). Touch the existing function signature only — no
   behaviour change.

4. **Reuse in commit 1's form** — the host also calls `describe_rules`
   to render the read-only summary line after the room opens (step 4
   of commit 1). Single helper, two callers.

5. **Tests** in `state.rs::tests` (or `tests` mod near the helper):

   - `describe_rules(RuleSet::xiangqi_casual())` → `"Xiangqi · casual"`
   - `describe_rules(RuleSet::xiangqi())` → `"Xiangqi · strict"`
   - `describe_rules(RuleSet::banqi_with_seed(PRESET_TAIWAN, 42))` →
     `"Banqi · house: chain,rush · seed: 42"`
   - `describe_rules(RuleSet::banqi(HouseRules::empty()))` →
     `"Banqi"` (no flags, no seed → no extra parts).

---

## Verification

```bash
# 1. Workspace sanity (every crate still compiles, no leptos-into-pure-modules leak)
cargo check --workspace

# 2. Native tests — covers routes::build_rule_set + parse_local_rules
#    (existing 80+ tests) + the new describe_rules helper (3 new tests)
cargo test -p chess-web

# 3. Clippy clean
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings

# 4. Format clean (CI requires)
cargo fmt --check

# 5. WASM compile — the actual target
cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-web

# 6. Local xiangqi smoke (regression: ensure existing flow unchanged):
make play-web
# Tab A: http://localhost:8080/lan/host  (default = Xiangqi, no toggles)
#   → confirm form shows Xiangqi + Strict unchecked
#   → click "Open room"  → status shows "Playing: Xiangqi · casual"
# Tab B: http://localhost:8080/lan/join  → paste offer
#   → confirm joiner sees "Playing: Xiangqi · casual"
# Make 2-3 moves both sides; confirm board syncs as before.

# 7. Local banqi smoke (new path):
make play-web
# Tab A: /lan/host → variant dropdown → "Banqi (暗棋)"
#   → preset = Taiwan, seed = 42
#   → Open room → status: "Playing: Banqi · house: chain,rush · seed: 42"
# Tab B: /lan/join → paste/scan offer
#   → confirm joiner status: "Playing: Banqi · house: chain,rush · seed: 42"
#   → confirm both sides render the SAME 4×8 hidden layout
#     (since seed=42 forces deterministic shuffle; both sides should
#      agree on which 32 tiles are face-down where)
# Flip a tile from each side; confirm the flipped piece appears
# identically on both ends. Capture, chain capture, verify rules fire.

# 8. Banqi without seed (default random):
# Tab A: /lan/host → variant = Banqi → preset = Purist (no flags) → no seed
#   → Open room → status: "Playing: Banqi"  (no "house:" or "seed:" parts)
# Tab B: → confirm joiner sees same; board layouts agree (host shuffles
#   once, projection works as for xiangqi)

# 9. Deep-link smoke (commit 1's optional URL pre-fill):
open http://localhost:8080/lan/host?variant=banqi&house=chain,rush&seed=42
# Confirm form initialises with: variant dropdown = Banqi, preset = "Custom"
# (or auto-detect as Taiwan if exact match), checkboxes for chain + rush
# checked, seed input = 42.

# 10. Form-locking behaviour after open:
# Tab A: open a room (any variant)  → confirm variant dropdown is
# disabled (or hidden) and form values can't be changed mid-pairing.

# 11. iOS QR scan + banqi joiner — manual on iPhone if available:
# After cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p chess-web,
# make build-web-static WEB_BASE=/Chinese-Chess_Xiangqi, deploy.
# Confirm the banqi LAN flow works through QR scan + camera path
# (uses existing jsQR loader fixed in earlier commit).
```

**Cannot verify locally:** physical-LAN pairing across two real devices
for banqi. Same caveat as Phase 5.5 — the in-browser two-tab flow uses
the WebRTC loopback path which already proved working for xiangqi, so
adding banqi to the same channel is mechanical (no new transport edges,
no protocol changes). If the user runs the cross-device test, the only
new failure mode is "banqi rules misbehave" — same risk as any banqi
session in `chess-net` today.

---

## Out of scope (call out in commit body)

- **Picker "Host LAN game" entry point.** A button on each picker
  variant card linking to `/lan/host?variant=...&house=...&seed=...`.
  Trivial once commit 1's deep-link parsing lands; track via
  `scripts/add-todo.sh` if user wants the picker integration.
- **In-game rule editing.** Once a room opens, rules are locked.
  Re-opening with different rules requires a new room. Matches
  chess-net `Room` semantics; rule editing would need a "rematch with
  new rules" message that doesn't exist in protocol v4 / v5.
- **Three-kingdom-banqi over WebRTC.** 3-player; doesn't fit 2-peer
  pairing. Would need a 3-peer mesh topology — separate redesign.
- **`<RulesForm>` component extraction.** Premature with 2 consumers
  (picker + LAN host) of differing shape. Re-evaluate when a third
  consumer appears or when the two drift in a confusing way.
- **Banqi `?role=spectator` over WebRTC.** Spectator role exists in
  chess-net protocol but WebRTC currently treats every peer as a
  player. Adding spectators needs a third peer + projection routing,
  separate from this work.
- **House-rule flags `HORSE_DIAGONAL` / `CANNON_FAST_MOVE` wired in
  move-gen.** UI form will show them (matching picker), but they
  remain no-op until `rules/banqi.rs::generate` consumes them. Already
  tracked in `TODO.md` as base-engine work.
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
